<sub>⏱️ 27s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

This is an **Expo / React Native** app. Here are your options to test on an iPad:

---

### Option 1: Expo Go (Easiest, no Mac needed)
1. Install **Expo Go** from the App Store on your iPad
2. Run `npm start` in this project directory
3. Scan the QR code with your iPad's camera (or the Expo Go app)

\> ⚠️ You're using `expo start --dev-client`, which means you may need a **custom dev client** (Option 2) if your app uses native modules beyond what Expo Go supports.

---

### Option 2: Custom Dev Client (requires a Mac with Xcode)
1. On a Mac, run: `npm run ios -- --device` to build and install directly to a connected iPad
2. Or build with EAS: `eas build --platform ios --profile development` and install the `.ipa` via TestFlight or direct install

---

### Option 3: iOS Simulator (Mac only)
Run `npm run ios` on a Mac — you can select an iPad simulator in Xcode.

---

**Quickest path:** Install Expo Go on the iPad → `npm start` → scan QR code. If you hit issues with native features (audio/haptics), you'll need the custom dev client route on a Mac.

---

1. import { router } from 'expo-router';
2. import React, { useState } from 'react';
3. import { ScrollView, StyleSheet, Text, TouchableOpacity, View } from 'react-native';
4. import LengthSelector from '../src/components/LengthSelector';
5. import ScaleSelector from '../src/components/ScaleSelector';
6. import { useExerciseStore } from '../src/store/exerciseStore';
7. import { NoteNameStr, NOTE_NAMES, rootMidi, SCALES } from '../src/utils/musicTheory';
8. 
9. export default function HomeScreen() {
10.   const store = useExerciseStore();
11.   const [rootName, setRootName] = useState<NoteNameStr>('C');
12.   const [rootOctave, setRootOctave] = useState(4);
13. 
14.   function handleStart() {
15.     store.setRoot(rootMidi(rootName, rootOctave));
16.     store.newSeed();
17.     store.resetDetected();
18.     router.push('/exercise');
19.   }
20. 
21.   return (
22.     <ScrollView contentContainerStyle={styles.container}>
23.       <Text style={styles.tagline}>Train your ear. Play it back.</Text>
24. 
25.       <View style={styles.card}>
26.         <ScaleSelector
27.           rootName={rootName}
28.           rootOctave={rootOctave}
29.           scaleId={store.scaleId}
30.           onRootChange={(name, oct) => {
31.             setRootName(name);
32.             setRootOctave(oct);
33.           }}
34.           onScaleChange={store.setScaleId}
35.         />
36.       </View>
37. 
38.       <View style={styles.card}>
39.         <LengthSelector value={store.sequenceLength} onChange={store.setSequenceLength} />
40.       </View>
41. 
42.       <TouchableOpacity style={styles.startBtn} onPress={handleStart}>
43.         <Text style={styles.startBtnText}>▶  Start Exercise</Text>
44.       </TouchableOpacity>
45. 
46.       <TouchableOpacity style={styles.progressBtn} onPress={() => router.push('/progress')}>
47.         <Text style={styles.progressBtnText}>📊  View Progress</Text>
48.       </TouchableOpacity>
49. 
50.       <TouchableOpacity style={styles.setupBtn} onPress={() => router.push('/setup')}>
51.         <Text style={styles.setupBtnText}>🎙  Mic Setup</Text>
52.       </TouchableOpacity>
53.     </ScrollView>
54.   );
55. }
56.

58.   container: { padding: 20, gap: 16 },
59.   tagline: { color: '#94a3b8', fontSize: 15, textAlign: 'center', marginBottom: 4 },
60.   card: {
61.     backgroundColor: '#1e293b',
62.     borderRadius: 16,
63.     padding: 16,
64.     borderWidth: 1,
65.     borderColor: '#334155',
66.   },

1. import React, { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
2. import { ScrollView, StyleSheet, Text, TouchableOpacity, View } from 'react-native';
3. import MusicStaff, { NoteColor } from '../src/components/MusicStaff';
4. import PitchMeter from '../src/components/PitchMeter';
5. import { usePitchDetection, PitchResult } from '../src/hooks/usePitchDetection';
6. import { staffPosition, freqToNote } from '../src/native/CoreBridge';
7. import { midiLabel } from '../src/utils/musicTheory';
8. 
9. const MAX_NOTES = 8;
10. 
11. // Notes to use for simulation testing (note name → frequency in Hz)
12. const TEST_NOTES: { label: string; hz: number }[] = [
13.   { label: 'C4',  hz: 261.63 },
14.   { label: 'E4',  hz: 329.63 },
15.   { label: 'G4',  hz: 392.00 },
16.   { label: 'A4',  hz: 440.00 },
17.   { label: 'C5',  hz: 523.25 },
18. ];
19. 
20. interface SetupNote {
21.   staffStep: number;
22.   color: NoteColor;
23.   label: string;
24.   accidental?: 'sharp' | 'flat';
25. }
26. 
27. export default function SetupScreen() {
28.   const [notes, setNotes] = useState<SetupNote[]>([]);
29.   const [lastLabel, setLastLabel] = useState<string | null>(null);
30.   const [simulating, setSimulating] = useState(false);
31. 
32.   const onNote = useCallback((result: PitchResult) => {
33.     const step = staffPosition(result.midi);
34.     const label = midiLabel(result.midi);
35.     const chroma = result.midi % 12;
36.     const isChromatic = [1, 3, 6, 8, 10].includes(chroma);
37.     const accidental = isChromatic ? 'sharp' as const : undefined;
38.     setLastLabel(label);
39.     setNotes((prev) => {
40.       const next = [...prev, { staffStep: step, color: 'white' as NoteColor, label, accidental }];
41.       // keep only the most recent MAX_NOTES
42.       return next.length > MAX_NOTES ? next.slice(next.length - MAX_NOTES) : next;
43.     });
44.   }, []);
45. 
46.   const pitchDetection = usePitchDetection(onNote);
47. 
48.   async function handleSimulate(hz: number, label: string) {
49.     if (simulating) return;
50.     setSimulating(true);
51.     await pitchDetection.simulatePitch(hz, 2000);
52.     setSimulating(false);
53.   }
54. 
55.   async function handleListen() {
56.     await pitchDetection.startListening();
57.   }
58. 
59.   function handleStop() {
60.     pitchDetection.stopListening();
61.   }
62. 
63.   function handleClear() {
64.     setNotes([]);
65.     setLastLabel(null);
66.   }
67. 
68.   const hz = pitchDetection.currentHz;
69.   const nr = hz > 0 ? freqToNote(hz) : null;
70. 
71.   return (
72.     <ScrollView contentContainerStyle={styles.container}>
73.       <Text style={styles.description}>
74.         Play notes on your instrument. Detected notes appear on the staff below.
75.       </Text>
76. 
77.       {/* Staff — always shown, starts empty */}
78.       <View style={styles.staffWrap}>
79.         <MusicStaff notes={notes} width={340} />
80.         {notes.length === 0 && (
81.           <Text style={styles.emptyHint}>No notes detected yet</Text>
82.         )}
83.       </View>
84. 
85.       {/* Last detected note name */}
86.       {lastLabel && (
87.         <Text style={styles.lastNote}>{lastLabel}</Text>
88.       )}
89. 
90.       {/* Live pitch meter while listening */}
91.       {pitchDetection.isListening && (
92.         <View style={styles.meterRow}>
93.           <PitchMeter hz={hz} midi={nr?.midi ?? -1} cents={nr?.cents ?? 0} />
94.           <Text style={styles.listenLabel}>Listening…</Text>
95.         </View>
96.       )}
97. 
98.       {/* Controls */}
99.       {!pitchDetection.isListening ? (
100.         <TouchableOpacity style={[styles.btn, styles.btnListen]} onPress={handleListen}>
101.           <Text style={styles.btnText}>🎤  Start Listening</Text>
102.         </TouchableOpacity>
103.       ) : (
104.         <TouchableOpacity style={[styles.btn, styles.btnStop]} onPress={handleStop}>
105.           <Text style={styles.btnText}>⏹  Stop</Text>
106.         </TouchableOpacity>
107.       )}
108. 
109.       <TouchableOpacity style={[styles.btn, styles.btnClear]} onPress={handleClear}>
110.         <Text style={styles.btnTextDim}>✕  Clear Staff</Text>
111.       </TouchableOpacity>
112. 
113.       {/* ── Simulation test section ─────────────────────────────────── */}
114.       <View style={styles.simSection}>
115.         <Text style={styles.simTitle}>🧪 Simulate note (for testing)</Text>
116.         <View style={styles.simRow}>
117.           {TEST_NOTES.map((n) => (
118.             <TouchableOpacity
119.               key={n.label}
120.               style={[styles.simBtn, simulating && styles.btnDisabled]}
121.               onPress={() => handleSimulate(n.hz, n.label)}
122.               disabled={simulating}
123.             >
124.               <Text style={styles.simBtnText}>{n.label}</Text>
125.             </TouchableOpacity>
126.           ))}
127.         </View>
128.         {simulating && <Text style={styles.simHint}>Simulating… (2 s)</Text>}
129.       </View>
130.     </ScrollView>
131.   );
132. }
133. 
134. const styles = StyleSheet.create({
135.   container: { padding: 20, gap: 16, alignItems: 'center' },
136.   description: {
137.     color: '#94a3b8',
138.     fontSize: 14,
139.     textAlign: 'center',
140.     lineHeight: 20,
141.   },
142.   staffWrap: { width: '100%' },
143.   emptyHint: {
144.     color: '#475569',
145.     fontSize: 13,
146.     textAlign: 'center',
147.     marginTop: 6,
148.   },
149.   lastNote: {
150.     color: '#e2e8f0',
151.     fontSize: 32,
152.     fontWeight: '700',
153.     letterSpacing: 2,
154.   },
155.   meterRow: { flexDirection: 'row', alignItems: 'center', gap: 16 },
156.   listenLabel: { color: '#94a3b8', fontSize: 14 },
157.   btn: {
158.     width: '100%',
159.     borderRadius: 14,
160.     paddingVertical: 16,
161.     alignItems: 'center',
162.   },
163.   btnListen: { backgroundColor: '#0ea5e9' },
164.   btnStop: { backgroundColor: '#ef4444' },
165.   btnClear: {
166.     backgroundColor: '#1e293b',
167.     borderWidth: 1,
168.     borderColor: '#334155',
169.   },
170.   btnText: { color: '#fff', fontSize: 17, fontWeight: '700' },
171.   btnTextDim: { color: '#94a3b8', fontSize: 16 },
172.   simSection: { width: '100%', gap: 10, marginTop: 4 },
173.   simTitle: { color: '#64748b', fontSize: 13, textAlign: 'center' },
174.   simRow: { flexDirection: 'row', justifyContent: 'center', gap: 8, flexWrap: 'wrap' },
175.   simBtn: {
176.     backgroundColor: '#1e293b',
177.     borderWidth: 1,
178.     borderColor: '#475569',
179.     borderRadius: 10,
180.     paddingVertical: 10,
181.     paddingHorizontal: 14,
182.   },
183.   simBtnText: { color: '#94a3b8', fontSize: 15, fontWeight: '600' },
184.   btnDisabled: { opacity: 0.4 },
185.   simHint: { color: '#6366f1', fontSize: 13, textAlign: 'center' },
186. });
187.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>288 lines</summary>

```
1. import * as Haptics from 'expo-haptics';
2. import React, { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react';
3. import { Platform, ScrollView, StyleSheet, Text, TouchableOpacity, View } from 'react-native';
4. import MusicStaff, { NoteColor } from '../src/components/MusicStaff';
5. import PitchMeter from '../src/components/PitchMeter';
6. import { useAudioPlayback } from '../src/hooks/useAudioPlayback';
7. import { usePitchDetection, PitchResult } from '../src/hooks/usePitchDetection';
8. import { useExerciseStore } from '../src/store/exerciseStore';
9. import { SCALES, midiLabel, isCorrect, getKeySignature } from '../src/utils/musicTheory';
10. import { staffPosition, generateSequence, freqToNote } from '../src/native/CoreBridge';
11. 
12. function triggerHaptic(correct: boolean) {
13.   if (Platform.OS === 'web') return;
14.   if (correct) {
15.     Haptics.notificationAsync(Haptics.NotificationFeedbackType.Success);
16.   } else {
17.     Haptics.notificationAsync(Haptics.NotificationFeedbackType.Error);
18.   }
19. }
20. 
21. // ── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22. 
23. type Phase = 'idle' | 'listening' | 'pausing';
24. 
25. interface StaffSlot {
26.   staffStep: number;
27.   color: NoteColor;
28.   accidental?: 'sharp' | 'flat';
29. }
30. 
31. function makeSlot(midi: number, color: NoteColor, keySigType: 'sharp' | 'flat' | 'none'): StaffSlot {
32.   const chroma = midi % 12;
33.   const isChromatic = [1, 3, 6, 8, 10].includes(chroma);
34.   return {
35.     staffStep: staffPosition(midi),
36.     color,
37.     accidental: isChromatic ? (keySigType === 'flat' ? 'flat' : 'sharp') : undefined,
38.   };
39. }
40. 
41. // ── Component ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42. 
43. export default function ExerciseScreen() {
44.   const store = useExerciseStore();
45.   const { playSequence, isPlaying, samplerLoading } = useAudioPlayback();
46. 
47.   // ── Display state ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48.   const [sequence, setSequence] = useState<number[]>([]);
49.   const [phase, setPhase] = useState<Phase>('idle');
50.   const [currentIdx, setCurrentIdx] = useState(0);
51.   const [confirmedNotes, setConfirmedNotes] = useState<StaffSlot[]>([]);
52.   const [currentDetected, setCurrentDetected] = useState<StaffSlot | null>(null);
53. 
54.   // ── Refs: mutable game state safe to read from async callbacks ───────────
55.   const seqRef = useRef<number[]>([]);
56.   const idxRef = useRef(0);
57.   const phaseRef = useRef<Phase>('idle');
58.   const keySigTypeRef = useRef<'sharp' | 'flat' | 'none'>('none');
59. 
60.   // ── Initialise (or re-initialise) a new sequence ─────────────────────────
61.   const initSequence = useCallback((seq: number[]) => {
62.     const keySigType = getKeySignature(store.rootMidi, store.scaleId).type;
63.     seqRef.current = seq;
64.     idxRef.current = 0;
65.     phaseRef.current = 'idle';
66.     keySigTypeRef.current = keySigType;
67.     setSequence(seq);
68.     setCurrentIdx(0);
69.     setConfirmedNotes([]);
70.     setCurrentDetected(null);
71.     setPhase('idle');
72.   }, [store.rootMidi, store.scaleId]);
73. 
74.   // Build first sequence on mount
75.   useEffect(() => {
76.     const seq = generateSequence(store.rootMidi, store.scaleId, store.sequenceLength, store.seed);
77.     store.setSequence(seq);
78.     store.resetDetected();
79.     initSequence(seq);
80.   }, []); // eslint-disable-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
81. 
82.   // ── Start a brand-new exercise (new seed) ────────────────────────────────
83.   const startFresh = useCallback(async () => {
84.     const newSeed = Math.floor(Math.random() * 2 ** 32);
85.     const seq = generateSequence(store.rootMidi, store.scaleId, store.sequenceLength, newSeed);
86.     store.setSequence(seq);
87.     store.resetDetected();
88.     initSequence(seq);
89.     await playSequence(seq);
90.     phaseRef.current = 'listening';
91.     setPhase('listening');
92.   }, [store.rootMidi, store.scaleId, store.sequenceLength, initSequence, playSequence]);
93. 
94.   // Stable ref so timeouts always call the latest version
95.   const startFreshRef = useRef(startFresh);
96.   useEffect(() => { startFreshRef.current = startFresh; }, [startFresh]);
97. 
98.   // ── Restart the same sequence (on mistake) ────────────────────────────────
99.   const restartSame = useCallback(async () => {
100.     idxRef.current = 0;
101.     phaseRef.current = 'idle';
102.     setCurrentIdx(0);
103.     setConfirmedNotes([]);
104.     setCurrentDetected(null);
105.     setPhase('idle');
106.     await playSequence(seqRef.current);
107.     phaseRef.current = 'listening';
108.     setPhase('listening');
109.   }, [playSequence]);
110. 
111.   const restartSameRef = useRef(restartSame);
112.   useEffect(() => { restartSameRef.current = restartSame; }, [restartSame]);
113. 
114.   // ── Note handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115.   const onNote = useCallback((result: PitchResult) => {
116.     if (phaseRef.current !== 'listening') return;
117.     const idx = idxRef.current;
118.     if (idx >= seqRef.current.length) return;
119. 
120.     phaseRef.current = 'pausing';
121.     setPhase('pausing');
122. 
123.     const expected = seqRef.current[idx];
124.     const correct = isCorrect(result.midi, expected, result.cents);
125.     const slot = makeSlot(result.midi, correct ? 'green' : 'red', keySigTypeRef.current);
126. 
127.     store.addDetected({ midi: result.midi, cents: result.cents });
128.     triggerHaptic(correct);
129.     setCurrentDetected(slot);
130. 
131.     if (correct) {
132.       const nextIdx = idx + 1;
133.       if (nextIdx < seqRef.current.length) {
134.         // Advance to next note after a brief green flash
135.         setTimeout(() => {
136.           setConfirmedNotes((prev) => [...prev, slot]);
137.           idxRef.current = nextIdx;
138.           setCurrentIdx(nextIdx);
139.           setCurrentDetected(null);
140.           phaseRef.current = 'listening';
141.           setPhase('listening');
142.         }, 500);
143.       } else {
144.         // All correct → celebrate briefly then start a fresh sequence
145.         setTimeout(() => {
146.           setConfirmedNotes((prev) => [...prev, slot]);
147.           setCurrentDetected(null);
148.           phaseRef.current = 'idle';
149.           setPhase('idle');
150.           setTimeout(() => startFreshRef.current(), 700);
151.         }, 500);
152.       }
153.     } else {
154.       // Wrong → show red, tell the user, replay same sequence after delay
155.       setTimeout(() => restartSameRef.current(), 2000);
156.     }
157.   }, [store]); // eslint-disable-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
158. 
159.   const pitchDetection = usePitchDetection(onNote);
160. 
161.   // ── Manual controls ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
162.   async function handlePlay() {
163.     if (isPlaying) return;
164.     await playSequence(sequence);
165.   }
166. 
167.   async function handleListen() {
168.     store.resetDetected();
169.     idxRef.current = 0;
170.     phaseRef.current = 'listening';
171.     setCurrentIdx(0);
172.     setConfirmedNotes([]);
173.     setCurrentDetected(null);
174.     setPhase('listening');
175.     await pitchDetection.startListening();
176.   }
177. 
178.   function handleStop() {
179.     pitchDetection.stopListening();
180.     phaseRef.current = 'idle';
181.     setPhase('idle');
182.     setCurrentDetected(null);
183.   }
184. 
185.   // ── Derived display values ───────────────────────────────────────────────
186.   const isActive = phase === 'listening' || phase === 'pausing';
187.   const staffNotes: StaffSlot[] = [...confirmedNotes, ...(currentDetected ? [currentDetected] : [])];
188.   const keySig = getKeySignature(store.rootMidi, store.scaleId);
189.   const scaleName = SCALES[store.scaleId]?.name ?? '';
190.   const rootLabel = midiLabel(store.rootMidi);
191. 
192.   // Feedback label shown during pausing phase
193.   const feedbackLabel =
194.     phase === 'pausing'
195.       ? currentDetected?.color === 'green'
196.         ? '✓ Correct!'
197.         : '✗ Wrong! Restarting…'
198.       : `Note ${currentIdx + 1} / ${sequence.length}`;
199. 
200.   return (
201.     <ScrollView contentContainerStyle={styles.container}>
202.       <Text style={styles.subtitle}>
203.         {rootLabel} {scaleName} · {sequence.length} notes
204.       </Text>
205. 
206.       {/* Music staff — starts empty, fills as notes are detected */}
207.       <View style={styles.staffWrap}>
208.         <MusicStaff notes={staffNotes} width={340} keySignature={keySig} />
209.       </View>
210. 
211.       {/* Status row: only shown while active */}
212.       {isActive && (
213.         <Text
214.           style={[
215.             styles.feedbackLabel,
216.             phase === 'pausing' && currentDetected?.color === 'green' && styles.feedbackGreen,
217.             phase === 'pausing' && currentDetected?.color === 'red' && styles.feedbackRed,
218.           ]}
219.         >
220.           {feedbackLabel}
221.         </Text>
222.       )}
223. 
224.       {/* Live pitch meter */}
225.       {isActive && (() => {
226.         const hz = pitchDetection.currentHz;
227.         const nr = hz > 0 ? freqToNote(hz) : null;
228.         return (
229.           <View style={styles.meterRow}>
230.             <PitchMeter hz={hz} midi={nr?.midi ?? -1} cents={nr?.cents ?? 0} />
231.           </View>
232.         );
233.       })()}
234. 
235.       {/* Action buttons */}
236.       <TouchableOpacity
237.         style={[styles.btn, styles.btnPlay, (isPlaying || samplerLoading) && styles.btnDisabled]}
238.         onPress={handlePlay}
239.         disabled={isPlaying || samplerLoading}
240.       >
241.         <Text style={styles.btnText}>
242.           {samplerLoading ? '⏳ Loading piano…' : isPlaying ? '♪ Playing…' : '▶  Play Sequence'}
243.         </Text>
244.       </TouchableOpacity>
245. 
246.       {!isActive ? (
247.         <TouchableOpacity style={[styles.btn, styles.btnListen]} onPress={handleListen}>
248.           <Text style={styles.btnText}>🎤  Start Listening</Text>
249.         </TouchableOpacity>
250.       ) : (
251.         <TouchableOpacity style={[styles.btn, styles.btnStop]} onPress={handleStop}>
252.           <Text style={styles.btnText}>⏹  Stop</Text>
253.         </TouchableOpacity>
254.       )}
255. 
256.       <TouchableOpacity style={styles.replayBtn} onPress={handlePlay} disabled={isPlaying || samplerLoading}>
257.         <Text style={styles.replayBtnText}>↺  Replay sequence</Text>
258.       </TouchableOpacity>
259.     </ScrollView>
260.   );
261. }
262. 
263. const styles = StyleSheet.create({
264.   container: { padding: 20, gap: 16, alignItems: 'center' },
265.   subtitle: { color: '#94a3b8', fontSize: 14 },
266.   staffWrap: { width: '100%' },
267.   feedbackLabel: {
268.     fontSize: 18,
269.     fontWeight: '600',
270.     color: '#94a3b8',
271.   },
272.   feedbackGreen: { color: '#4ade80' },
273.   feedbackRed: { color: '#f87171' },
274.   meterRow: { flexDirection: 'row', alignItems: 'center' },
275.   btn: {
276.     width: '100%',
277.     borderRadius: 14,
278.     paddingVertical: 16,
279.     alignItems: 'center',
280.   },
281.   btnPlay: { backgroundColor: '#6366f1' },
282.   btnListen: { backgroundColor: '#0ea5e9' },
283.   btnStop: { backgroundColor: '#ef4444' },
284.   btnDisabled: { opacity: 0.5 },
285.   btnText: { color: '#fff', fontSize: 17, fontWeight: '700' },
286.   replayBtn: { paddingVertical: 10 },
287.   replayBtnText: { color: '#94a3b8', fontSize: 14 },
288. });
```

38.

1. import { create } from 'zustand';
2. import { SCALES } from '../utils/musicTheory';
3. 
4. export type ExerciseStatus = 'idle' | 'playing' | 'listening' | 'done';
5. 
6. export interface DetectedNote {
7.   midi: number;
8.   cents: number;
9. }
10. 
11. interface ExerciseState {
12.   rootMidi: number;           // e.g. 60 = C4
13.   scaleId: number;            // index into SCALES
14.   sequenceLength: number;     // 2–8
15.   maxTries: number;           // tries per note before moving on
16.   sequence: number[];         // expected MIDI notes
17.   detected: DetectedNote[];   // notes collected from mic
18.   status: ExerciseStatus;
19.   seed: number;
20. 
21.   setRoot: (midi: number) => void;
22.   setScaleId: (id: number) => void;
23.   setSequenceLength: (len: number) => void;
24.   setMaxTries: (n: number) => void;
25.   setSequence: (seq: number[]) => void;
26.   setStatus: (s: ExerciseStatus) => void;
27.   addDetected: (note: DetectedNote) => void;
28.   resetDetected: () => void;
29.   newSeed: () => void;
30. }
31. 
32. export const useExerciseStore = create<ExerciseState>((set) => ({
33.   rootMidi: 60,   // C4
34.   scaleId: 0,     // Major
35.   sequenceLength: 4,
36.   maxTries: 3,
37.   sequence: [],
38.   detected: [],
39.   status: 'idle',
40.   seed: Math.floor(Math.random() * 2 ** 32),
41. 
42.   setRoot: (midi) => set({ rootMidi: midi }),
43.   setScaleId: (id) => set({ scaleId: id }),
44.   setSequenceLength: (len) => set({ sequenceLength: len }),
45.   setMaxTries: (n) => set({ maxTries: n }),
46.   setSequence: (seq) => set({ sequence: seq }),
47.   setStatus: (status) => set({ status }),
48.   addDetected: (note) => set((s) => ({ detected: [...s.detected, note] })),
49.   resetDetected: () => set({ detected: [] }),
50.   newSeed: () => set({ seed: Math.floor(Math.random() * 2 ** 32) }),
51. }));
52. 
53. export { SCALES };
54.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>68 lines</summary>

```
1. import { create } from 'zustand';
2. import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';
3. 
4. const STORAGE_KEY = '@ear_ring_history';
5. 
6. export interface SessionRecord {
7.   id: string;
8.   date: string;         // ISO timestamp
9.   scaleName: string;
10.   rootLabel: string;    // e.g. "C4"
11.   score: number;        // 0–100
12.   length: number;
13. }
14. 
15. interface ProgressState {
16.   history: SessionRecord[];
17.   streak: number;       // consecutive sessions with score ≥ 80
18.   bestByScale: Record<string, number>; // scaleName → best score
19. 
20.   loadHistory: () => Promise<void>;
21.   addSession: (record: SessionRecord) => Promise<void>;
22. }
23. 
24. function computeStreak(history: SessionRecord[]): number {
25.   let streak = 0;
26.   for (const rec of [...history].reverse()) {
27.     if (rec.score >= 80) streak++;
28.     else break;
29.   }
30.   return streak;
31. }
32. 
33. function computeBest(history: SessionRecord[]): Record<string, number> {
34.   const best: Record<string, number> = {};
35.   for (const rec of history) {
36.     if ((best[rec.scaleName] ?? -1) < rec.score) {
37.       best[rec.scaleName] = rec.score;
38.     }
39.   }
40.   return best;
41. }
42. 
43. export const useProgressStore = create<ProgressState>((set, get) => ({
44.   history: [],
45.   streak: 0,
46.   bestByScale: {},
47. 
48.   loadHistory: async () => {
49.     try {
50.       const raw = await AsyncStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY);
51.       const history: SessionRecord[] = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : [];
52.       set({ history, streak: computeStreak(history), bestByScale: computeBest(history) });
53.     } catch {
54.       // ignore storage errors
55.     }
56.   },
57. 
58.   addSession: async (record) => {
59.     const history = [...get().history, record];
60.     set({ history, streak: computeStreak(history), bestByScale: computeBest(history) });
61.     try {
62.       await AsyncStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(history));
63.     } catch {
64.       // ignore storage errors
65.     }
66.   },
67. }));
68.
```

</details>

1. /**
2.  * Music theory constants and helpers used by the UI layer.
3.  * Heavy computation is delegated to the Rust core via EarRingCoreModule.
4.  */
5. 
6. export const NOTE_NAMES = ['C', 'C#', 'D', 'D#', 'E', 'F', 'F#', 'G', 'G#', 'A', 'A#', 'B'] as const;
7. export type NoteNameStr = (typeof NOTE_NAMES)[number];
8. 
9. export interface ScaleDefinition {
10.   id: number;
11.   name: string;
12.   intervals: number[];
13. }
14. 
15. export const SCALES: ScaleDefinition[] = [
16.   { id: 0, name: 'Major',            intervals: [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11] },
17.   { id: 1, name: 'Natural Minor',    intervals: [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 10] },
18.   { id: 2, name: 'Harmonic Minor',   intervals: [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 11] },
19.   { id: 3, name: 'Pentatonic Major', intervals: [0, 2, 4, 7, 9] },
20.   { id: 4, name: 'Pentatonic Minor', intervals: [0, 3, 5, 7, 10] },
21.   { id: 5, name: 'Dorian',           intervals: [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10] },
22.   { id: 6, name: 'Mixolydian',       intervals: [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10] },
23.   { id: 7, name: 'Blues',            intervals: [0, 3, 5, 6, 7, 10] },
24. ];
25. 
26. /** Octave choices for root note selection (C3–C5 covers most instruments). */
27. export const ROOT_OCTAVES = [3, 4, 5] as const;
28. 
29. /** MIDI note number for a given root name + octave. */
30. export function rootMidi(noteName: NoteNameStr, octave: number): number {
31.   const chroma = NOTE_NAMES.indexOf(noteName as NoteNameStr);
32.   return (octave + 1) * 12 + chroma;
33. }
34. 
35. /** Human-readable label for a MIDI note, e.g. "A4". */
36. export function midiLabel(midi: number): string {
37.   const chroma = midi % 12;
38.   const octave = Math.floor(midi / 12) - 1;
39.   return `${NOTE_NAMES[chroma]}${octave}`;
40. }
41. 
42. /** Whether two MIDI notes are the same pitch class (ignoring octave). */
43. export function samePitchClass(a: number, b: number): boolean {
44.   return a % 12 === b % 12;
45. }
46. 
47. /**
48.  * Check if a detected MIDI note matches an expected MIDI note within tolerance.
49.  * Tolerance: same pitch class (ignore octave) with ≤ 50 cent deviation.
50.  */
51. export function isCorrect(detected: number, expected: number, cents: number): boolean {
52.   return samePitchClass(detected, expected) && Math.abs(cents) <= 50;
53. }
54. 
55. // ── Key signature ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
56. 
57. export type AccidentalType = 'sharp' | 'flat' | 'none';
58. 
59. export interface KeySignature {
60.   type: AccidentalType;
61.   count: number;
62. }
63. 
64. /** Major key signature for each chroma 0-11.
65.  *  Uses sharps for C, G, D, A, E, B, F#, C# and flats for F, Bb, Eb, Ab.
66.  *  D#/G#/A# use their flat enharmonic equivalents (Eb/Ab/Bb). */
67. const MAJOR_KEY_SIGS: Array<[AccidentalType, number]> = [
68.   ['none',  0], // C
69.   ['sharp', 7], // C#
70.   ['sharp', 2], // D
71.   ['flat',  3], // Eb (D#)
72.   ['sharp', 4], // E
73.   ['flat',  1], // F
74.   ['sharp', 6], // F#
75.   ['sharp', 1], // G
76.   ['flat',  4], // Ab (G#)
77.   ['sharp', 3], // A
78.   ['flat',  2], // Bb (A#)
79.   ['sharp', 5], // B
80. ];
81. 
82. /** Semitones to add to scale root to reach the parent major scale root. */
83. const SCALE_MAJOR_OFFSET: Record<number, number> = {
84.   0: 0,  // Major
85.   1: 3,  // Natural Minor (relative major = root + 3)
86.   2: 3,  // Harmonic Minor
87.   3: 0,  // Pentatonic Major
88.   4: 3,  // Pentatonic Minor
89.   5: 10, // Dorian (parent major = root − 2)
90.   6: 5,  // Mixolydian (parent major = root + 5)
91.   7: 3,  // Blues (minor-based)
92. };
93. 
94. /** Return the key signature (sharps or flats) for a given root MIDI note + scale. */
95. export function getKeySignature(rootMidi: number, scaleId: number): KeySignature {
96.   const offset = SCALE_MAJOR_OFFSET[scaleId] ?? 0;
97.   const majorChroma = (rootMidi % 12 + offset) % 12;
98.   const [type, count] = MAJOR_KEY_SIGS[majorChroma];
99.   return { type, count };
100. }
101.
```

</details>

1. import { isCorrect } from './musicTheory';
2. 
3. export interface NoteComparison {
4.   expected: number;   // MIDI
5.   detected: number;   // MIDI (-1 = not detected)
6.   cents: number;
7.   correct: boolean;
8. }
9. 
10. /**
11.  * Compare two sequences of MIDI notes and return per-note results.
12.  */
13. export function compareSequences(
14.   expected: number[],
15.   detected: Array<{ midi: number; cents: number }>
16. ): NoteComparison[] {
17.   return expected.map((exp, i) => {
18.     const det = detected[i] ?? { midi: -1, cents: 0 };
19.     const correct = det.midi !== -1 && isCorrect(det.midi, exp, det.cents);
20.     return { expected: exp, detected: det.midi, cents: det.cents, correct };
21.   });
22. }
23. 
24. /**
25.  * Compute a 0–100 score from a list of comparisons.
26.  */
27. export function computeScore(comparisons: NoteComparison[]): number {
28.   if (comparisons.length === 0) return 0;
29.   const correct = comparisons.filter((c) => c.correct).length;
30.   return Math.round((correct / comparisons.length) * 100);
31. }
32.
```

</details>

1. /**
2.  * EarRingCoreModule — React Native interface to the Rust ear_ring_core library.
3.  *
4.  * On iOS/Android this calls the native Turbo Module which wraps the compiled
5.  * Rust C FFI.  On Web the .web.ts platform-specific file is loaded instead.
6.  *
7.  * If the native module is unavailable (e.g. Rust JNI not yet built), falls
8.  * back to the pure-JS implementation so the app stays functional.
9.  */
10. 
11. import { NativeModules } from 'react-native';
12. import * as JSImpl from './EarRingCoreModule.web';
13. 
14. const { EarRingCore } = NativeModules;
15. 
16. if (!EarRingCore) {
17.   console.warn(
18.     'EarRingCore native module not found — falling back to JS implementation. ' +
19.     'Build the Rust JNI/XCFramework to enable native pitch detection.'
20.   );
21. }
22. 
23. export interface NoteResult {
24.   midi: number;
25.   cents: number;
26. }
27. 
28. /** Detect the fundamental pitch in a block of f32 PCM samples. Returns Hz or -1. */
29. export function detectPitch(samples: Float32Array, sampleRate: number): number {
30.   if (!EarRingCore) return JSImpl.detectPitch(samples, sampleRate);
31.   return EarRingCore.detectPitch(Array.from(samples), sampleRate);
32. }
33. 
34. /** Convert a frequency (Hz) to the nearest MIDI note + cents deviation. */
35. export function freqToNote(hz: number): NoteResult | null {
36.   if (!EarRingCore) return JSImpl.freqToNote(hz);
37.   return EarRingCore.freqToNote(hz);
38. }
39. 
40. /** Get the staff position for a MIDI note (0 = C4, +7 = C5, -1 = B3, etc.). */
41. export function staffPosition(midi: number): number {
42.   if (!EarRingCore) return JSImpl.staffPosition(midi);
43.   return EarRingCore.staffPosition(midi);
44. }
45. 
46. /**
47.  * Generate a random sequence of MIDI note numbers from a given scale.
48.  * @param rootMidi  Root note MIDI number (e.g. 60 = C4)
49.  * @param scaleId   0=Major 1=NatMinor 2=HarmMinor 3=PentMaj 4=PentMin 5=Dorian 6=Mixolydian 7=Blues
50.  * @param length    Number of notes (2–8)
51.  * @param seed      Random seed for reproducibility
52.  */
53. export function generateSequence(
54.   rootMidi: number,
55.   scaleId: number,
56.   length: number,
57.   seed: number
58. ): number[] {
59.   if (!EarRingCore) return JSImpl.generateSequence(rootMidi, scaleId, length, seed);
60.   return EarRingCore.generateSequence(rootMidi, scaleId, length, seed);
61. }
62.
```

```
1. /**
2.  * Web platform implementation of EarRingCoreModule.
3.  *
4.  * Implements all functions in pure JavaScript, mirroring the Rust core logic.
5.  * This avoids WASM loading complexity with Metro bundler and works immediately
6.  * in the browser. The Rust/WASM build remains available for future native use.
7.  */
8. 
9. import type { NoteResult } from './EarRingCoreModule';
10. 
11. // ── Music theory (mirrors rust/src/music_theory.rs) ──────────────────────────
12. 
13. /** Convert MIDI note number to frequency in Hz (A4 = 440 Hz, MIDI 69). */
14. function midiToFreq(midi: number): number {
15.   return 440 * Math.pow(2, (midi - 69) / 12);
16. }
17. 
18. /** Convert frequency to nearest MIDI note + cents deviation. */
19. export function freqToNote(hz: number): NoteResult | null {
20.   if (hz <= 0) return null;
21.   const midiF = 69 + 12 * Math.log2(hz / 440);
22.   if (midiF < 0 || midiF > 127) return null;
23.   const midi = Math.round(midiF);
24.   const cents = Math.round((midiF - midi) * 100);
25.   return { midi, cents };
26. }
27. 
28. /** Diatonic step offset (C=0, D=1, E=2, F=3, G=4, A=5, B=6) per chroma. */
29. const DIATONIC = [0, 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 3, 4, 4, 5, 5, 6];
30. 
31. /** Get staff position: diatonic steps above middle C (C4 = 0). */
32. export function staffPosition(midi: number): number {
33.   const chroma = midi % 12;
34.   const octave = Math.floor(midi / 12) - 1;
35.   return (octave - 4) * 7 + DIATONIC[chroma];
36. }
37. 
38. const SCALE_INTERVALS: Record<number, number[]> = {
39.   0: [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11],      // Major
40.   1: [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 10],      // Natural Minor
41.   2: [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 11],      // Harmonic Minor
42.   3: [0, 2, 4, 7, 9],             // Pentatonic Major
43.   4: [0, 3, 5, 7, 10],            // Pentatonic Minor
44.   5: [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10],      // Dorian
45.   6: [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10],      // Mixolydian
46.   7: [0, 3, 5, 6, 7, 10],         // Blues
47. };
48. 
49. /** LCG random number generator (matches Rust implementation). */
50. function lcgNext(seed: bigint): bigint {
51.   return (seed * 6364136223846793005n + 1442695040888963407n) & 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFn;
52. }
53. 
54. /** Generate a sequence of MIDI note numbers from the given scale. */
55. export function generateSequence(
56.   rootMidi: number,
57.   scaleId: number,
58.   length: number,
59.   seed: number
60. ): number[] {
61.   const intervals = SCALE_INTERVALS[scaleId] ?? SCALE_INTERVALS[0];
62.   const notes = intervals.map((i) => rootMidi + i);
63.   const n = BigInt(notes.length);
64.   let rng = BigInt(seed);
65.   const result: number[] = [];
66.   for (let i = 0; i < length; i++) {
67.     rng = lcgNext(rng);
68.     const idx = Number((rng >> 33n) % n);
69.     result.push(notes[idx]);
70.   }
71.   return result;
72. }
73. 
74. // ── Pitch detection (YIN algorithm — mirrors rust/src/pitch_detection.rs) ────
75. 
76. const YIN_THRESHOLD = 0.25;
77. 
78. /** Detect fundamental frequency via YIN algorithm. */
79. export function detectPitch(samples: Float32Array, sampleRate: number): number {
80.   const n = samples.length;
81.   if (n < 2) return -1;
82.   const maxLag = Math.min(Math.floor(sampleRate / 20), Math.floor(n / 2));
83.   const minLag = Math.max(Math.floor(sampleRate / 1200), 2);
84.   if (maxLag <= minLag) return -1;
85. 
86.   // Difference function
87.   const diff = new Float32Array(maxLag + 1);
88.   for (let tau = 1; tau <= maxLag; tau++) {
89.     let sum = 0;
90.     for (let j = 0; j < n - tau; j++) {
91.       const delta = samples[j] - samples[j + tau];
92.       sum += delta * delta;
93.     }
94.     diff[tau] = sum;
95.   }
96. 
97.   // CMNDF
98.   const cmndf = new Float32Array(maxLag + 1);
99.   cmndf[0] = 1;
100.   let running = 0;
101.   for (let tau = 1; tau <= maxLag; tau++) {
102.     running += diff[tau];
103.     cmndf[tau] = running < 1e-10 ? 1 : (diff[tau] * tau) / running;
104.   }
105. 
106.   // Find first dip below threshold
107.   let tauOpt = -1;
108.   for (let tau = minLag; tau <= maxLag; tau++) {
109.     if (cmndf[tau] < YIN_THRESHOLD) {
110.       while (tau + 1 <= maxLag && cmndf[tau + 1] < cmndf[tau]) tau++;
111.       tauOpt = tau;
112.       break;
113.     }
114.   }
115.   if (tauOpt === -1) {
116.     // Fallback: global minimum
117.     let minVal = Infinity;
118.     for (let tau = minLag; tau <= maxLag; tau++) {
119.       if (cmndf[tau] < minVal) { minVal = cmndf[tau]; tauOpt = tau; }
120.     }
121.   }
122.   if (tauOpt <= 0 || cmndf[tauOpt] > 0.5) return -1;
123. 
124.   // Parabolic interpolation
125.   if (tauOpt > 0 && tauOpt < maxLag) {
126.     const y0 = cmndf[tauOpt - 1], y1 = cmndf[tauOpt], y2 = cmndf[tauOpt + 1];
127.     const denom = 2 * (2 * y1 - y2 - y0);
128.     if (Math.abs(denom) > 1e-10) {
129.       tauOpt = tauOpt + (y2 - y0) / denom;
130.     }
131.   }
132. 
133.   return sampleRate / tauOpt;
134. }
135.
```

</details>

<details>

```
1. import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react';
2. import { NativeEventEmitter, NativeModules, PermissionsAndroid, Platform } from 'react-native';
3. 
4. export interface PitchResult {
5.   hz: number;
6.   midi: number;
7.   cents: number;
8.   confidence: 'high' | 'low';
9. }
10. 
11. const BUFFER_SIZE = 4096;
12. const STABILITY_MS = 450;    // how long a pitch must be stable before firing onNote
13. const SILENCE_RMS = 0.003;
14. const SILENCE_HOLD_MS = 250; // how long silence must last to end the current note
15. // How many consecutive audio frames a *different* pitch must persist before we
16. // reset the stability timer.  One frame ≈ 4096/44100 ≈ 93 ms on Android.
17. // Setting this to 2 means a brief single-frame glitch/harmonic won't derail detection.
18. const GLITCH_FRAMES = 2;
19. 
20. async function getCoreModule() {
21.   if (Platform.OS === 'web') {
22.     return import('../native/CoreBridge.web');
23.   }
24.   return import('../native/CoreBridge');
25. }
26. 
27. /** Decode a base64-encoded little-endian Float32 buffer. */
28. function decodeFloat32Base64(b64: string, count: number): Float32Array {
29.   const binary = atob(b64);
30.   const bytes = new Uint8Array(binary.length);
31.   for (let i = 0; i < binary.length; i++) bytes[i] = binary.charCodeAt(i);
32.   return new Float32Array(bytes.buffer, 0, count);
33. }
34. 
35. // RMS level below which we treat the buffer as silence
36. const SILENCE_RMS_THRESH = SILENCE_RMS;
37. 
38. export function usePitchDetection(onNote: (result: PitchResult) => void) {
39.   const [isListening, setIsListening] = useState(false);
40.   const [currentHz, setCurrentHz] = useState(0);
41.   const stopRef = useRef<(() => void) | null>(null);
42.   const onNoteRef = useRef(onNote);
43.   useEffect(() => { onNoteRef.current = onNote; }, [onNote]);
44. 
45.   const startListening = useCallback(async () => {
46.     setIsListening(true);
47.     const core = await getCoreModule();
48. 
49.     // ── Shared pitch processing state ──────────────────────────────────────
50.     let lastMidi = -1;       // MIDI of the note currently being tracked
51.     let stableStart = 0;     // timestamp when lastMidi first became stable
52.     let reported = false;    // true once onNote has fired for lastMidi
53.     let silenceStart = 0;    // timestamp when silence began (0 = not silent)
54.     let potentialMidi = -1;  // candidate next note seen while reported=true
55.     let potentialStart = 0;  // timestamp when potentialMidi was first seen
56.     // Glitch filtering: track how many consecutive frames show a *different* pitch
57.     // before we commit to treating it as a real pitch change.
58.     let otherMidi = -1;      // the different pitch we're seeing
59.     let otherFrames = 0;     // consecutive frames of otherMidi
60. 
61.     function processSamples(buf: Float32Array, sampleRate: number) {
62.       let rms = 0;
63.       for (let i = 0; i < buf.length; i++) rms += buf[i] * buf[i];
64.       rms = Math.sqrt(rms / buf.length);
65. 
66.       // ── Silence ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67.       if (rms < SILENCE_RMS_THRESH) {
68.         setCurrentHz(0);
69.         if (silenceStart === 0) silenceStart = Date.now();
70.         if (Date.now() - silenceStart >= SILENCE_HOLD_MS) {
71.           // Sustained silence → note ended; ready for a new note
72.           reported = false;
73.           lastMidi = -1;
74.           potentialMidi = -1;
75.           otherMidi = -1;
76.           otherFrames = 0;
77.         }
78.         return;
79.       }
80. 
81.       // Sound detected → reset silence timer
82.       silenceStart = 0;
83. 
84.       const hz = core.detectPitch(buf, sampleRate);
85.       setCurrentHz(hz > 0 ? hz : 0);
86.       if (hz <= 0) return;
87. 
88.       const noteResult = core.freqToNote(hz);
89.       if (!noteResult) return;
90.       const { midi, cents } = noteResult;
91. 
92.       if (!reported) {
93.         // ── Waiting for first stable note ──────────────────────────────────
94.         if (midi === lastMidi) {
95.           // Still on the same pitch — clear any glitch tracking
96.           otherMidi = -1;
97.           otherFrames = 0;
98.         } else {
99.           // Different pitch detected; apply glitch filter before resetting
100.           if (midi === otherMidi) {
101.             otherFrames++;
102.           } else {
103.             otherMidi = midi;
104.             otherFrames = 1;
105.           }
106.           if (otherFrames >= GLITCH_FRAMES) {
107.             // Sustained different pitch → treat as real note change
108.             lastMidi = midi;
109.             stableStart = Date.now();
110.             otherMidi = -1;
111.             otherFrames = 0;
112.           }
113.         }
114.         if (lastMidi === midi && Date.now() - stableStart >= STABILITY_MS) {
115.           reported = true;
116.           potentialMidi = -1;
117.           onNoteRef.current({ hz, midi, cents, confidence: Math.abs(cents) <= 30 ? 'high' : 'low' });
118.         }
119.       } else {
120.         // ── Note already reported; watch for a genuinely new stable pitch ──
121.         if (midi === lastMidi) {
122.           // Still on the same note — reset any candidate we were tracking
123.           potentialMidi = -1;
124.           otherMidi = -1;
125.           otherFrames = 0;
126.         } else {
127.           // Different MIDI: apply glitch filter before starting stability timer
128.           if (midi === otherMidi) {
129.             otherFrames++;
130.           } else {
131.             otherMidi = midi;
132.             otherFrames = 1;
133.           }
134.           if (otherFrames >= GLITCH_FRAMES) {
135.             // Committed to a new pitch — start tracking it as potential next note
136.             if (midi !== potentialMidi) {
137.               potentialMidi = midi;
138.               potentialStart = Date.now();
139.             } else if (Date.now() - potentialStart >= STABILITY_MS) {
140.               // New pitch is stable — accept it as the next note
141.               reported = false;
142.               lastMidi = midi;
143.               stableStart = potentialStart;
144.               potentialMidi = -1;
145.               otherMidi = -1;
146.               otherFrames = 0;
147.               // reported=false → will fire onNote on next call after stableStart check
148.             }
149.           }
150.         }
151.       }
152.     }
153. 
154.     // ── Web: Web Audio API ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
155.     if (Platform.OS === 'web' && typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.AudioContext) {
156.       let stream: MediaStream;
157.       try {
158.         stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true, video: false });
159.       } catch (err) {
160.         console.error('Microphone permission denied or unavailable:', err);
161.         setIsListening(false);
162.         return;
163.       }
164.       const ctx = new window.AudioContext();
165.       const actualRate = ctx.sampleRate;
166.       const source = ctx.createMediaStreamSource(stream);
167.       const processor = ctx.createScriptProcessor(BUFFER_SIZE, 1, 1);
168.       source.connect(processor);
169.       const gainNode = ctx.createGain();
170.       gainNode.gain.value = 0;
171.       processor.connect(gainNode);
172.       gainNode.connect(ctx.destination);
173.       processor.onaudioprocess = (e: AudioProcessingEvent) => {
174.         processSamples(e.inputBuffer.getChannelData(0), actualRate);
175.       };
176.       stopRef.current = () => {
177.         processor.disconnect();
178.         gainNode.disconnect();
179.         source.disconnect();
180.         stream.getTracks().forEach((t: MediaStreamTrack) => t.stop());
181.         ctx.close();
182.       };
183. 
184.     // ── Android: AudioCapture native module ────────────────────────────────
185.     } else if (Platform.OS === 'android') {
186.       const SAMPLE_RATE = 44100;
187.       const AudioCapture = NativeModules.AudioCapture;
188.       if (!AudioCapture) {
189.         console.error('AudioCapture native module not found');
190.         setIsListening(false);
191.         return;
192.       }
193.       // Request RECORD_AUDIO permission at runtime
194.       const granted = await PermissionsAndroid.request(
195.         PermissionsAndroid.PERMISSIONS.RECORD_AUDIO,
196.         {
197.           title: 'Microphone Permission',
198.           message: 'Ear Ring needs your microphone to detect the notes you play.',
199.           buttonPositive: 'Allow',
200.         }
201.       );
202.       if (granted !== PermissionsAndroid.RESULTS.GRANTED) {
203.         console.error('RECORD_AUDIO permission denied');
204.         setIsListening(false);
205.         return;
206.       }
207.       const emitter = new NativeEventEmitter(AudioCapture);
208.       const subscription = emitter.addListener(
209.         'onAudioData',
210.         ({ data, sampleRate, count }: { data: string; sampleRate: number; count: number }) => {
211.           const samples = decodeFloat32Base64(data, count);
212.           processSamples(samples, sampleRate);
213.         }
214.       );
215.       await AudioCapture.startRecording(SAMPLE_RATE, BUFFER_SIZE);
216.       stopRef.current = async () => {
217.         subscription.remove();
218.         await AudioCapture.stopRecording();
219.       };
220. 
221.     // ── iOS / other: stub ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
222.     } else {
223.       console.warn('Pitch detection not implemented on this platform.');
224.       stopRef.current = () => {};
225.     }
226.   }, []);
227. 
228.   const stopListening = useCallback(() => {
229.     const stop = stopRef.current;
230.     stopRef.current = null;
231.     if (stop) Promise.resolve(stop()).catch(() => {});
232.     setIsListening(false);
233.     setCurrentHz(0);
234.   }, []);
235. 
236.   useEffect(() => {
237.     return () => { stopRef.current?.(); };
238.   }, []);
239. 
240.   // ── Simulate a pitch for testing (feeds synthetic sine wave through the
241.   //    same detection pipeline without needing a physical microphone) ────────
242.   const simulatePitch = useCallback(async (freq: number, durationMs = 2000) => {
243.     const core = await getCoreModule();
244.     const SAMPLE_RATE = 44100;
245. 
246.     // Own copy of the state machine (same logic as processSamples above)
247.     let lastMidi = -1;
248.     let stableStart = 0;
249.     let reported = false;
250.     let silenceStart = 0;
251.     let potentialMidi = -1;
252.     let potentialStart = 0;
253.     let otherMidi = -1;
254.     let otherFrames = 0;
255. 
256.     function processSim(buf: Float32Array) {
257.       let rms = 0;
258.       for (let i = 0; i < buf.length; i++) rms += buf[i] * buf[i];
259.       rms = Math.sqrt(rms / buf.length);
260.       if (rms < SILENCE_RMS_THRESH) { silenceStart = silenceStart || Date.now(); return; }
261.       silenceStart = 0;
262.       const hz = core.detectPitch(buf, SAMPLE_RATE);
263.       setCurrentHz(hz > 0 ? hz : 0);
264.       if (hz <= 0) return;
265.       const noteResult = core.freqToNote(hz);
266.       if (!noteResult) return;
267.       const { midi, cents } = noteResult;
268.       if (!reported) {
269.         if (midi === lastMidi) { otherMidi = -1; otherFrames = 0; }
270.         else {
271.           midi === otherMidi ? otherFrames++ : (otherMidi = midi, otherFrames = 1);
272.           if (otherFrames >= GLITCH_FRAMES) { lastMidi = midi; stableStart = Date.now(); otherMidi = -1; otherFrames = 0; }
273.         }
274.         if (lastMidi === midi && Date.now() - stableStart >= STABILITY_MS) {
275.           reported = true;
276.           onNoteRef.current({ hz, midi, cents, confidence: Math.abs(cents) <= 30 ? 'high' : 'low' });
277.         }
278.       }
279.     }
280. 
281.     const totalFrames = Math.ceil((durationMs / 1000) * SAMPLE_RATE / BUFFER_SIZE);
282.     let frame = 0;
283.     const intervalMs = (BUFFER_SIZE / SAMPLE_RATE) * 1000; // real-time pace ≈ 93ms
284.     const timer = setInterval(() => {
285.       if (frame >= totalFrames) { clearInterval(timer); setCurrentHz(0); return; }
286.       const buf = new Float32Array(BUFFER_SIZE);
287.       const t0 = frame * BUFFER_SIZE;
288.       for (let i = 0; i < BUFFER_SIZE; i++) {
289.         buf[i] = 0.5 * Math.sin(2 * Math.PI * freq * (t0 + i) / SAMPLE_RATE);
290.       }
291.       processSim(buf);
292.       frame++;
293.     }, intervalMs);
294.   }, []);
295. 
296.   return { startListening, stopListening, simulatePitch, isListening, currentHz };
297. }
298.
```

```
1. import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react';
2. 
3. /**
4.  * useAudioPlayback — plays note sequences using Tone.js Sampler loaded with
5.  * real Salamander grand piano samples (hosted on tonejs.github.io CDN).
6.  *
7.  * Falls back to a synthesized piano-like sound (AM oscillator) if the CDN
8.  * samples haven't loaded yet.
9.  */
10. 
11. // Salamander piano samples hosted by Tone.js — covers notes from A0 to C8.
12. const SAMPLE_URLS: Record<string, string> = {
13.   A0: 'A0.mp3',
14.   C1: 'C1.mp3',
15.   'D#1': 'Ds1.mp3',
16.   'F#1': 'Fs1.mp3',
17.   A1: 'A1.mp3',
18.   C2: 'C2.mp3',
19.   'D#2': 'Ds2.mp3',
20.   'F#2': 'Fs2.mp3',
21.   A2: 'A2.mp3',
22.   C3: 'C3.mp3',
23.   'D#3': 'Ds3.mp3',
24.   'F#3': 'Fs3.mp3',
25.   A3: 'A3.mp3',
26.   C4: 'C4.mp3',
27.   'D#4': 'Ds4.mp3',
28.   'F#4': 'Fs4.mp3',
29.   A4: 'A4.mp3',
30.   C5: 'C5.mp3',
31.   'D#5': 'Ds5.mp3',
32.   'F#5': 'Fs5.mp3',
33.   A5: 'A5.mp3',
34.   C6: 'C6.mp3',
35.   'D#6': 'Ds6.mp3',
36.   'F#6': 'Fs6.mp3',
37.   A6: 'A6.mp3',
38.   C7: 'C7.mp3',
39.   'D#7': 'Ds7.mp3',
40.   'F#7': 'Fs7.mp3',
41.   A7: 'A7.mp3',
42.   C8: 'C8.mp3',
43. };
44. 
45. const BASE_URL = 'https://tonejs.github.io/audio/salamander/';
46. const NOTE_DURATION = '4n';      // quarter note
47. const NOTE_GAP_SEC = 0.55;       // seconds between note onsets
48. 
49. // Tone.js is loaded lazily so Metro doesn't try to bundle native modules
50. let toneLoaded = false;
51. let Tone: typeof import('tone') | null = null;
52. let sampler: import('tone').Sampler | null = null;
53. let samplerReady = false;
54. 
55. async function getTone() {
56.   if (!toneLoaded) {
57.     Tone = await import('tone');
58.     toneLoaded = true;
59.   }
60.   return Tone!;
61. }
62. 
63. async function getSampler() {
64.   if (sampler && samplerReady) return sampler;
65.   const T = await getTone();
66.   return new Promise<import('tone').Sampler>((resolve) => {
67.     sampler = new T.Sampler({
68.       urls: SAMPLE_URLS,
69.       baseUrl: BASE_URL,
70.       onload: () => {
71.         samplerReady = true;
72.         resolve(sampler!);
73.       },
74.     }).toDestination();
75.     // If samples load quickly, they may already be ready
76.     if (sampler.loaded) {
77.       samplerReady = true;
78.       resolve(sampler);
79.     }
80.   });
81. }
82. 
83. /** Convert MIDI note number to Tone.js note name, e.g. 60 → "C4". */
84. const NOTE_NAMES = ['C', 'C#', 'D', 'D#', 'E', 'F', 'F#', 'G', 'G#', 'A', 'A#', 'B'];
85. function midiToToneName(midi: number): string {
86.   const chroma = midi % 12;
87.   const octave = Math.floor(midi / 12) - 1;
88.   return `${NOTE_NAMES[chroma]}${octave}`;
89. }
90. 
91. export function useAudioPlayback() {
92.   const [isPlaying, setIsPlaying] = useState(false);
93.   const [samplerLoading, setSamplerLoading] = useState(false);
94.   const cancelRef = useRef(false);
95. 
96.   // Pre-warm the sampler so it's ready when the user hits Play
97.   useEffect(() => {
98.     setSamplerLoading(true);
99.     getSampler().then(() => setSamplerLoading(false)).catch(() => setSamplerLoading(false));
100.   }, []);
101. 
102.   const playSequence = useCallback(async (midiNotes: number[]) => {
103.     cancelRef.current = false;
104.     setIsPlaying(true);
105. 
106.     try {
107.       const T = await getTone();
108.       // Resume AudioContext (browsers require user gesture first)
109.       await T.start();
110.       const piano = await getSampler();
111. 
112.       const now = T.now();
113.       for (let i = 0; i < midiNotes.length; i++) {
114.         if (cancelRef.current) break;
115.         const noteName = midiToToneName(midiNotes[i]);
116.         piano.triggerAttackRelease(noteName, NOTE_DURATION, now + i * NOTE_GAP_SEC);
117.       }
118. 
119.       // Wait for all notes to finish before clearing isPlaying
120.       const totalDuration = midiNotes.length * NOTE_GAP_SEC + 1.0;
121.       await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
122.         const check = setInterval(() => {
123.           if (cancelRef.current) { clearInterval(check); resolve(); }
124.         }, 100);
125.         setTimeout(() => { clearInterval(check); resolve(); }, totalDuration * 1000);
126.       });
127.     } catch (err) {
128.       console.warn('Audio playback error:', err);
129.     }
130. 
131.     setIsPlaying(false);
132.   }, []);
133. 
134.   const stop = useCallback(() => {
135.     cancelRef.current = true;
136.     sampler?.releaseAll();
137.     setIsPlaying(false);
138.   }, []);
139. 
140.   return { playSequence, stop, isPlaying, samplerLoading };
141. }
142. 
143.
```

</details>

1. import React from 'react';
2. import { StyleSheet, View } from 'react-native';
3. import Svg, { Line, G, Ellipse, Text as SvgText } from 'react-native-svg';
4. import type { KeySignature } from '../utils/musicTheory';
5. 
6. export type NoteColor = 'white' | 'green' | 'red';
7. export type { KeySignature };
8. 
9. interface NoteHeadProps {
10.   /** Diatonic steps above middle C (C4=0, D4=1, … C5=7, B3=-1, …) */
11.   staffStep: number;
12.   color: NoteColor;
13.   /** Horizontal x position on the staff */
14.   x: number;
15.   /** Whether to draw a ledger line for this note */
16.   needsLedger?: boolean;
17.   /** Accidental to show to the left of this note head */
18.   accidental?: 'sharp' | 'flat';
19. }
20. 
21. const LINE_SPACING = 10; // px between staff lines
22. const STAFF_TOP = 40;    // y of top staff line (E5 in treble)
23. 
24. /** Convert a staff step (relative to C4) to a y pixel coordinate.
25.  *  staffPosition() returns: C4=0, D4=1, E4=2, F4=3, G4=4, A4=5, B4=6, C5=7, …
26.  *  Treble staff lines (bottom→top): E4(2), G4(4), B4(6), D5(8), F5(10)
27.  *  Middle C (C4, step 0) sits one ledger line below the staff.
28.  *  Each step = LINE_SPACING/2 pixels vertically.
29.  */
30. function staffY(step: number): number {
31.   // Bottom staff line = E4 = step 2.
32.   const bottomLineY = STAFF_TOP + 4 * LINE_SPACING;
33.   return bottomLineY - (step - 2) * (LINE_SPACING / 2);
34. }
35. 
36. export function NoteHead({ staffStep, color, x, needsLedger, accidental }: NoteHeadProps) {
37.   const y = staffY(staffStep);
38.   const fill = color === 'white' ? '#ffffff' : color === 'green' ? '#4ade80' : '#f87171';
39.   const stroke = color === 'white' ? '#94a3b8' : fill;
40. 
41.   return (
42.     <G>
43.       {/* Ledger line for notes outside the staff (middle C, etc.) */}
44.       {needsLedger && (
45.         <Line
46.           x1={x - 14}
47.           y1={y}
48.           x2={x + 14}
49.           y2={y}
50.           stroke="#94a3b8"
51.           strokeWidth={1.5}
52.         />
53.       )}
54.       {/* Accidental symbol to the left of the note head */}
55.       {accidental && (
56.         <SvgText
57.           x={x - 14}
58.           y={y + (accidental === 'flat' ? 7 : 5)}
59.           fontSize={20}
60.           fill={fill}
61.           fontFamily="serif"
62.           textAnchor="middle"
63.         >
64.           {accidental === 'sharp' ? '♯' : '♭'}
65.         </SvgText>
66.       )}
67.       <Ellipse
68.         cx={x}
69.         cy={y}
70.         rx={7}
71.         ry={5}
72.         fill={fill}
73.         stroke={stroke}
74.         strokeWidth={1.5}
75.         transform={`rotate(-20, ${x}, ${y})`}
76.       />
77.     </G>
78.   );
79. }
80. 
81. // ── MusicStaff ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
82. 
83. interface DisplayNote {
84.   staffStep: number;
85.   color: NoteColor;
86.   accidental?: 'sharp' | 'flat';
87. }
88. 
89. interface MusicStaffProps {
90.   notes: DisplayNote[];
91.   width?: number;
92.   keySignature?: KeySignature;
93. }
94. 
95. const STAFF_HEIGHT = STAFF_TOP + 4 * LINE_SPACING + 40; // some padding below
96. const CLEF_WIDTH = 48;
97. const NOTE_SPACING = 50;
98. const ACCIDENTAL_WIDTH = 16; // px per accidental symbol
99. 
100. /** Treble-clef staff steps for each sharp in circle-of-fifths order: F C G D A E B */
101. const SHARP_STEPS = [10, 7, 11, 8, 5, 9, 6];
102. /** Treble-clef staff steps for each flat in order: B E A D G C F */
103. const FLAT_STEPS  = [6, 9, 5, 8, 4, 7, 3];
104. 
105. function needsLedgerLine(step: number): boolean {
106.   // C4 (step 0) needs a ledger line below the staff.
107.   // A5 (step 12) and above need a ledger line above the staff.
108.   // Notes in the space above/below the staff (steps 1, 11) do not need one.
109.   return step <= 0 || step >= 12;
110. }
111. 
112. export default function MusicStaff({ notes, width = 360, keySignature }: MusicStaffProps) {
113.   const accCount = (keySignature && keySignature.type !== 'none') ? keySignature.count : 0;
114.   const keySigWidth = accCount > 0 ? accCount * ACCIDENTAL_WIDTH + 6 : 0;
115.   const noteStartX = CLEF_WIDTH + keySigWidth + 20;
116.   const staffWidth = Math.max(width, noteStartX + notes.length * NOTE_SPACING + 30);
117. 
118.   const accSteps = keySignature?.type === 'flat' ? FLAT_STEPS : SHARP_STEPS;
119.   const accChar  = keySignature?.type === 'flat' ? '♭' : '♯';
120.   const keySigX  = CLEF_WIDTH + 4; // x of first accidental symbol (centered)
121. 
122.   return (
123.     <View style={styles.container}>
124.       <Svg width={staffWidth} height={STAFF_HEIGHT}>
125.         {/* 5 staff lines */}
126.         {[0, 1, 2, 3, 4].map((i) => (
127.           <Line
128.             key={i}
129.             x1={0}
130.             y1={STAFF_TOP + i * LINE_SPACING}
131.             x2={staffWidth}
132.             y2={STAFF_TOP + i * LINE_SPACING}
133.             stroke="#94a3b8"
134.             strokeWidth={1.5}
135.           />
136.         ))}
137. 
138.         {/* Treble clef symbol (simplified as text; swap for SVG path in production) */}
139.         <SvgText
140.           x={6}
141.           y={STAFF_TOP + 3 * LINE_SPACING + 4}
142.           fontSize={52}
143.           fill="#94a3b8"
144.           fontFamily="serif"
145.         >
146.           𝄞
147.         </SvgText>
148. 
149.         {/* Key signature accidentals */}
150.         {Array.from({ length: accCount }, (_, i) => {
151.           const step = accSteps[i];
152.           const x = keySigX + i * ACCIDENTAL_WIDTH + ACCIDENTAL_WIDTH / 2;
153.           // y offset: place accidental visually centered on the staff step
154.           const y = staffY(step) + (keySignature?.type === 'flat' ? 6 : 4);
155.           return (
156.             <SvgText
157.               key={`acc-${i}`}
158.               x={x}
159.               y={y}
160.               fontSize={20}
161.               fill="#94a3b8"
162.               fontFamily="serif"
163.               textAnchor="middle"
164.             >
165.               {accChar}
166.             </SvgText>
167.           );
168.         })}
169. 
170.         {/* Note heads */}
171.         {notes.map((n, i) => (
172.           <NoteHead
173.             key={i}
174.             staffStep={n.staffStep}
175.             color={n.color}
176.             x={noteStartX + i * NOTE_SPACING}
177.             needsLedger={needsLedgerLine(n.staffStep)}
178.             accidental={n.accidental}
179.           />
180.         ))}
181.       </Svg>
182.     </View>
183.   );
184. }
185. 
186. const styles = StyleSheet.create({
187.   container: {
188.     backgroundColor: '#1e293b',
189.     borderRadius: 12,
190.     padding: 8,
191.     overflow: 'hidden',
192.   },
193. });
194.
```

1. import React from 'react';
2. import { StyleSheet, Text, TouchableOpacity, View } from 'react-native';
3. import { NOTE_NAMES, NoteNameStr, SCALES } from '../utils/musicTheory';
4. 
5. interface ScaleSelectorProps {
6.   rootName: NoteNameStr;
7.   rootOctave: number;
8.   scaleId: number;
9.   onRootChange: (name: NoteNameStr, octave: number) => void;
10.   onScaleChange: (id: number) => void;
11. }
12. 
13. export default function ScaleSelector({
14.   rootName,
15.   rootOctave,
16.   scaleId,
17.   onRootChange,
18.   onScaleChange,
19. }: ScaleSelectorProps) {
20.   return (
21.     <View style={styles.container}>
22.       <Text style={styles.label}>Key</Text>
23.       {/* Note name row */}
24.       <View style={styles.row}>
25.         {NOTE_NAMES.map((name) => (
26.           <TouchableOpacity
27.             key={name}
28.             style={[styles.chip, rootName === name && styles.chipActive]}
29.             onPress={() => onRootChange(name, rootOctave)}
30.           >
31.             <Text style={[styles.chipText, rootName === name && styles.chipTextActive]}>
32.               {name}
33.             </Text>
34.           </TouchableOpacity>
35.         ))}
36.       </View>
37. 
38.       {/* Octave row */}
39.       <Text style={styles.label}>Octave</Text>
40.       <View style={styles.row}>
41.         {[3, 4, 5].map((oct) => (
42.           <TouchableOpacity
43.             key={oct}
44.             style={[styles.chip, rootOctave === oct && styles.chipActive]}
45.             onPress={() => onRootChange(rootName, oct)}
46.           >
47.             <Text style={[styles.chipText, rootOctave === oct && styles.chipTextActive]}>
48.               {oct}
49.             </Text>
50.           </TouchableOpacity>
51.         ))}
52.       </View>
53. 
54.       {/* Scale type */}
55.       <Text style={styles.label}>Scale</Text>
56.       <View style={styles.row}>
57.         {SCALES.map((s) => (
58.           <TouchableOpacity
59.             key={s.id}
60.             style={[styles.chip, scaleId === s.id && styles.chipActive]}
61.             onPress={() => onScaleChange(s.id)}
62.           >
63.             <Text style={[styles.chipText, scaleId === s.id && styles.chipTextActive]}>
64.               {s.name}
65.             </Text>
66.           </TouchableOpacity>
67.         ))}
68.       </View>
69.     </View>
70.   );
71. }
72.

1. pub mod music_theory;
2. pub mod pitch_detection;
3. 
4. pub use music_theory::{
5.     freq_to_note, generate_sequence, midi_to_freq, scale_notes, staff_position, Note, NoteName,
6.     ScaleType,
7. };
8. pub use pitch_detection::detect_pitch;
9. 
10. // ── C-compatible FFI surface ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
11. // These thin wrappers are called from the React Native Turbo Native Module
12. // (Swift / Kotlin) without requiring uniffi code-gen at this stage.
13. 
14. use std::os::raw::{c_float, c_int, c_uchar, c_uint};
15. 
16. /// Detect pitch from a raw f32 PCM buffer.
17. ///
18. /// * `samples`       – pointer to f32 samples
19. /// * `num_samples`   – number of samples
20. /// * `sample_rate`   – sample rate in Hz
21. /// * `out_hz`        – out-parameter: detected frequency, 0.0 if not detected
22. ///
23. /// Returns 1 if a pitch was detected, 0 otherwise.
24. #[no_mangle]
25. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_detect_pitch(
26.     samples: *const c_float,
27.     num_samples: c_uint,
28.     sample_rate: c_uint,
29.     out_hz: *mut c_float,
30. ) -> c_int {
31.     if samples.is_null() || out_hz.is_null() {
32.         return 0;
33.     }
34.     let slice = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts(samples, num_samples as usize) };
35.     match detect_pitch(slice, sample_rate) {
36.         Some(hz) => {
37.             unsafe { *out_hz = hz };
38.             1
39.         }
40.         None => {
41.             unsafe { *out_hz = 0.0 };
42.             0
43.         }
44.     }
45. }
46. 
47. /// Convert a frequency to MIDI note number and cents deviation.
48. ///
49. /// * `hz`        – frequency in Hz
50. /// * `out_midi`  – out-parameter: MIDI note number (0–127)
51. /// * `out_cents` – out-parameter: deviation in cents (-50 to +50)
52. ///
53. /// Returns 1 on success, 0 if frequency is out of range.
54. #[no_mangle]
55. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_freq_to_note(
56.     hz: c_float,
57.     out_midi: *mut c_uchar,
58.     out_cents: *mut c_int,
59. ) -> c_int {
60.     if out_midi.is_null() || out_cents.is_null() {
61.         return 0;
62.     }
63.     match freq_to_note(hz) {
64.         Some((note, cents)) => {
65.             unsafe {
66.                 *out_midi = note.midi();
67.                 *out_cents = cents;
68.             }
69.             1
70.         }
71.         None => 0,
72.     }
73. }
74. 
75. /// Get the staff position for a MIDI note number.
76. ///
77. /// Returns diatonic steps above middle C (C4 = 0, D4 = 1, C5 = 7, B3 = -1, …).
78. #[no_mangle]
79. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_staff_position(midi: c_uchar) -> c_int {
80.     staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi))
81. }
82. 
83. /// Generate a sequence of MIDI note numbers.
84. ///
85. /// * `root_midi`   – MIDI number of the root note
86. /// * `scale_id`    – 0=Major, 1=NaturalMinor, 2=HarmonicMinor, 3=PentatonicMajor,
87. ///                   4=PentatonicMinor, 5=Dorian, 6=Mixolydian, 7=Blues
88. /// * `length`      – number of notes to generate
89. /// * `seed`        – random seed for reproducibility
90. /// * `out_buf`     – caller-allocated buffer of at least `length` bytes
91. ///
92. /// Returns the number of notes written, or -1 on error.
93. #[no_mangle]
94. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_generate_sequence(
95.     root_midi: c_uchar,
96.     scale_id: c_uchar,
97.     length: c_uchar,
98.     seed: u64,
99.     out_buf: *mut c_uchar,
100. ) -> c_int {
101.     if out_buf.is_null() {
102.         return -1;
103.     }
104.     let scale = match scale_id {
105.         0 => ScaleType::Major,
106.         1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
107.         2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
108.         3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
109.         4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
110.         5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
111.         6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
112.         7 => ScaleType::Blues,
113.         _ => return -1,
114.     };
115.     let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi);
116.     let notes = generate_sequence(root, scale, length, seed);
117.     let out = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(out_buf, length as usize) };
118.     for (i, note) in notes.iter().enumerate() {
119.         out[i] = note.midi();
120.     }
121.     notes.len() as c_int
122. }
123. 
124. // ── Android JNI exports ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125. // Exported with the exact symbol names the Kotlin EarRingCoreModule expects.
126. #[cfg(target_os = "android")]
127. mod android_jni {
128.     use jni::objects::{JClass, JFloatArray, JIntArray};
129.     use jni::sys::{jfloat, jfloatArray, jint, jintArray, jlong};
130.     use jni::JNIEnv;
131. 
132.     use super::{detect_pitch, freq_to_note, generate_sequence, staff_position, Note, ScaleType};
133. 
134.     #[no_mangle]
135.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeDetectPitch(
136.         mut env: JNIEnv,
137.         _class: JClass,
138.         samples: jfloatArray,
139.         sample_rate: jint,
140.     ) -> jfloat {
141.         let arr = unsafe { JFloatArray::from_raw(samples) };
142.         let len = match env.get_array_length(&arr) {
143.             Ok(l) => l as usize,
144.             Err(_) => return -1.0,
145.         };
146.         let mut buf = vec![0f32; len];
147.         if env.get_float_array_region(&arr, 0, &mut buf).is_err() {
148.             return -1.0;
149.         }
150.         match detect_pitch(&buf, sample_rate as u32) {
151.             Some(hz) => hz,
152.             None => -1.0,
153.         }
154.     }
155. 
156.     #[no_mangle]
157.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeFreqToMidi(
158.         _env: JNIEnv,
159.         _class: JClass,
160.         hz: jfloat,
161.     ) -> jint {
162.         match freq_to_note(hz) {
163.             Some((note, _)) => note.midi() as jint,
164.             None => -1,
165.         }
166.     }
167. 
168.     #[no_mangle]
169.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeFreqToCents(
170.         _env: JNIEnv,
171.         _class: JClass,
172.         hz: jfloat,
173.     ) -> jint {
174.         match freq_to_note(hz) {
175.             Some((_, cents)) => cents as jint,
176.             None => 0,
177.         }
178.     }
179. 
180.     #[no_mangle]
181.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeStaffPosition(
182.         _env: JNIEnv,
183.         _class: JClass,
184.         midi: jint,
185.     ) -> jint {
186.         staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi as u8)) as jint
187.     }
188. 
189.     #[no_mangle]
190.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeGenerateSequence(
191.         mut env: JNIEnv,
192.         _class: JClass,
193.         root_midi: jint,
194.         scale_id: jint,
195.         length: jint,
196.         seed: jlong,
197.     ) -> jintArray {
198.         let scale = match scale_id {
199.             0 => ScaleType::Major,
200.             1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
201.             2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
202.             3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
203.             4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
204.             5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
205.             6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
206.             7 => ScaleType::Blues,
207.             _ => ScaleType::Major,
208.         };
209.         let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi as u8);
210.         let notes = generate_sequence(root, scale, length as u8, seed as u64);
211.         let midi_vals: Vec<jint> = notes.iter().map(|n| n.midi() as jint).collect();
212. 
213.         let arr: JIntArray = match env.new_int_array(midi_vals.len() as i32) {
214.             Ok(a) => a,
215.             Err(_) => return std::ptr::null_mut(),
216.         };
217.         let _ = env.set_int_array_region(&arr, 0, &midi_vals);
218.         arr.into_raw()
219.     }
220. }
221.
