```
1. use std::fmt;
2. 
3. // ── Note ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4. 
5. /// Chromatic note names (12 pitch classes, using sharps).
6. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
7. pub enum NoteName {
8.     C,
9.     Cs, // C#
10.     D,
11.     Ds, // D#
12.     E,
13.     F,
14.     Fs, // F#
15.     G,
16.     Gs, // G#
17.     A,
18.     As, // A#
19.     B,
20. }
21. 
22. impl NoteName {
23.     /// All 12 pitch classes in chromatic order.
24.     pub const ALL: [NoteName; 12] = [
25.         NoteName::C,
26.         NoteName::Cs,
27.         NoteName::D,
28.         NoteName::Ds,
29.         NoteName::E,
30.         NoteName::F,
31.         NoteName::Fs,
32.         NoteName::G,
33.         NoteName::Gs,
34.         NoteName::A,
35.         NoteName::As,
36.         NoteName::B,
37.     ];
38. 
39.     /// Chromatic index 0–11 (C = 0, C# = 1, …, B = 11).
40.     pub fn chroma(self) -> u8 {
41.         self as u8
42.     }
43. 
44.     /// Display name suitable for UI labels.
45.     pub fn display_name(self) -> &'static str {
46.         match self {
47.             NoteName::C => "C",
48.             NoteName::Cs => "C#",
49.             NoteName::D => "D",
50.             NoteName::Ds => "D#",
51.             NoteName::E => "E",
52.             NoteName::F => "F",
53.             NoteName::Fs => "F#",
54.             NoteName::G => "G",
55.             NoteName::Gs => "G#",
56.             NoteName::A => "A",
57.             NoteName::As => "A#",
58.             NoteName::B => "B",
59.         }
60.     }
61. 
62.     /// From chromatic index 0–11.
63.     pub fn from_chroma(c: u8) -> Self {
64.         NoteName::ALL[(c % 12) as usize]
65.     }
66. }
67. 
68. impl fmt::Display for NoteName {
69.     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
70.         write!(f, "{}", self.display_name())
71.     }
72. }
73. 
74. // ── Note (name + octave) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75. 
76. /// A specific note: pitch class + octave (C4 = middle C, MIDI 60).
77. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
78. pub struct Note {
79.     pub name: NoteName,
80.     pub octave: i8,
81. }
82. 
83. impl Note {
84.     pub fn new(name: NoteName, octave: i8) -> Self {
85.         Self { name, octave }
86.     }
87. 
88.     /// MIDI number: C4 = 60, A4 = 69, etc.
89.     pub fn midi(self) -> u8 {
90.         let semitone = (self.octave as i16 + 1) * 12 + self.name.chroma() as i16;
91.         semitone.clamp(0, 127) as u8
92.     }
93. 
94.     /// Frequency in Hz: A4 = 440 Hz.
95.     pub fn frequency(self) -> f32 {
96.         midi_to_freq(self.midi())
97.     }
98. 
99.     /// Build from MIDI number.
100.     pub fn from_midi(midi: u8) -> Self {
101.         let chroma = midi % 12;
102.         let octave = (midi as i8 / 12) - 1;
103.         Note {
104.             name: NoteName::from_chroma(chroma),
105.             octave,
106.         }
107.     }
108. }
109. 
110. impl fmt::Display for Note {
111.     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
112.         write!(f, "{}{}", self.name, self.octave)
113.     }
114. }
115. 
116. // ── Frequency ↔ MIDI conversions ──────────────────────────────────────────────
117. 
118. /// Convert MIDI note number to frequency in Hz (A4 = 440 Hz, MIDI 69).
119. pub fn midi_to_freq(midi: u8) -> f32 {
120.     440.0 * 2.0_f32.powf((midi as f32 - 69.0) / 12.0)
121. }
122. 
123. /// Convert frequency to the nearest note and the deviation in cents.
124. /// Returns `None` if the frequency is outside the audible/MIDI range.
125. pub fn freq_to_note(hz: f32) -> Option<(Note, i32)> {
126.     if hz <= 0.0 {
127.         return None;
128.     }
129.     // MIDI float
130.     let midi_f = 69.0 + 12.0 * (hz / 440.0).log2();
131.     if !(0.0..=127.0).contains(&midi_f) {
132.         return None;
133.     }
134.     let midi = midi_f.round() as u8;
135.     let cents = ((midi_f - midi as f32) * 100.0).round() as i32;
136.     Some((Note::from_midi(midi), cents))
137. }
138. 
139. // ── Scale ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
140. 
141. /// Available scale types.
142. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
143. pub enum ScaleType {
144.     Major,
145.     NaturalMinor,
146.     HarmonicMinor,
147.     PentatonicMajor,
148.     PentatonicMinor,
149.     Dorian,
150.     Mixolydian,
151.     Blues,
152. }
153. 
154. impl ScaleType {
155.     /// Interval pattern in semitones (relative to root, not including octave).
156.     pub fn intervals(self) -> &'static [u8] {
157.         match self {
158.             ScaleType::Major => &[0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11],
159.             ScaleType::NaturalMinor => &[0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 10],
160.             ScaleType::HarmonicMinor => &[0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 11],
161.             ScaleType::PentatonicMajor => &[0, 2, 4, 7, 9],
162.             ScaleType::PentatonicMinor => &[0, 3, 5, 7, 10],
163.             ScaleType::Dorian => &[0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10],
164.             ScaleType::Mixolydian => &[0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10],
165.             ScaleType::Blues => &[0, 3, 5, 6, 7, 10],
166.         }
167.     }
168. 
169.     pub fn display_name(self) -> &'static str {
170.         match self {
171.             ScaleType::Major => "Major",
172.             ScaleType::NaturalMinor => "Natural Minor",
173.             ScaleType::HarmonicMinor => "Harmonic Minor",
174.             ScaleType::PentatonicMajor => "Pentatonic Major",
175.             ScaleType::PentatonicMinor => "Pentatonic Minor",
176.             ScaleType::Dorian => "Dorian",
177.             ScaleType::Mixolydian => "Mixolydian",
178.             ScaleType::Blues => "Blues",
179.         }
180.     }
181. }
182. 
183. // ── Scale generation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184. 
185. /// Generate scale notes starting from `root` (e.g. C4) for one octave.
186. /// Returns the notes in ascending order (root included, octave excluded).
187. pub fn scale_notes(root: Note, scale: ScaleType) -> Vec<Note> {
188.     let root_midi = root.midi();
189.     scale
190.         .intervals()
191.         .iter()
192.         .map(|&interval| Note::from_midi(root_midi + interval))
193.         .collect()
194. }
195. 
196. /// Generate a random sequence of `length` notes drawn from the given scale.
197. /// Uses a simple LCG seeded by the provided `seed` for reproducibility.
198. pub fn generate_sequence(root: Note, scale: ScaleType, length: u8, seed: u64) -> Vec<Note> {
199.     let notes = scale_notes(root, scale);
200.     let n = notes.len() as u64;
201.     let mut rng = seed;
202.     (0..length)
203.         .map(|_| {
204.             // LCG parameters (Knuth)
205.             rng = rng.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);
206.             let idx = ((rng >> 33) % n) as usize;
207.             notes[idx]
208.         })
209.         .collect()
210. }
211. 
212. // ── Staff position ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
213. 
214. /// Treble-clef staff position for a note.
215. /// Returns the number of staff steps above middle C (C4).
216. /// Each step = one line or one space. Positive = higher, negative = lower.
217. /// Used by the UI to place note heads on the staff.
218. pub fn staff_position(note: Note) -> i32 {
219.     // Diatonic step offset within an octave for each pitch class (C=0, D=1, E=2, …)
220.     let diatonic = match note.name {
221.         NoteName::C => 0,
222.         NoteName::Cs => 0,
223.         NoteName::D => 1,
224.         NoteName::Ds => 1,
225.         NoteName::E => 2,
226.         NoteName::F => 3,
227.         NoteName::Fs => 3,
228.         NoteName::G => 4,
229.         NoteName::Gs => 4,
230.         NoteName::A => 5,
231.         NoteName::As => 5,
232.         NoteName::B => 6,
233.     };
234.     // Middle C (C4) is at staff position 0
235.     let octave_offset = (note.octave as i32 - 4) * 7;
236.     octave_offset + diatonic
237. }
238. 
239. // ── Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
240.

247.         let a4 = Note::new(NoteName::A, 4);
248.         assert_eq!(a4.midi(), 69);
249.         let freq = a4.frequency();
250.         assert!((freq - 440.0).abs() < 0.01, "A4 should be ~440Hz, got {freq}");
251.     }

297.         assert_eq!(staff_position(Note::new(NoteName::D, 4)), 1);
298.         assert_eq!(staff_position(Note::new(NoteName::C, 5)), 7);
299.         assert_eq!(staff_position(Note::new(NoteName::B, 3)), -1);
300.     }
301. }
```

```
1. /// YIN pitch detection algorithm.
2. ///
3. /// Reference: de Cheveigné & Kawahara (2002), "YIN, a fundamental frequency
4. /// estimator for speech and music", JASA 111(4).
5. ///
6. /// Input: mono f32 PCM samples at `sample_rate` Hz.
7. /// Output: fundamental frequency in Hz, or `None` if not detected / low confidence.
8. 
9. const YIN_THRESHOLD: f32 = 0.15;
10. 
11. /// Detect the fundamental frequency of a monophonic signal.
12. ///
13. /// * `samples`     – slice of f32 PCM samples in the range [-1.0, 1.0]
14. /// * `sample_rate` – recording sample rate in Hz (e.g. 44100)
15. ///
16. /// Returns `Some(hz)` when a confident pitch is found, `None` otherwise.
17. pub fn detect_pitch(samples: &[f32], sample_rate: u32) -> Option<f32> {
18.     let n = samples.len();
19.     if n < 2 {
20.         return None;
21.     }
22.     // Maximum lag to check: lowest detectable pitch ~= sample_rate / max_lag.
23.     // We cap at 20 Hz (infrasound), so max_lag = sample_rate / 20.
24.     let max_lag = (sample_rate / 20).min(n as u32 / 2) as usize;
25.     // Minimum lag for ~1200 Hz upper bound.
26.     let min_lag = (sample_rate / 1200).max(2) as usize;
27. 
28.     if max_lag <= min_lag {
29.         return None;
30.     }
31. 
32.     // Step 1 & 2: difference function d(τ)
33.     let mut diff = vec![0.0f32; max_lag + 1];
34.     for tau in 1..=max_lag {
35.         let mut sum = 0.0f32;
36.         for j in 0..(n - tau) {
37.             let delta = samples[j] - samples[j + tau];
38.             sum += delta * delta;
39.         }
40.         diff[tau] = sum;
41.     }
42. 
43.     // Step 3: cumulative mean normalised difference function (CMNDF)
44.     let mut cmndf = vec![0.0f32; max_lag + 1];
45.     cmndf[0] = 1.0;
46.     let mut running_sum = 0.0f32;
47.     for tau in 1..=max_lag {
48.         running_sum += diff[tau];
49.         if running_sum.abs() < f32::EPSILON {
50.             cmndf[tau] = 1.0;
51.         } else {
52.             cmndf[tau] = diff[tau] * tau as f32 / running_sum;
53.         }
54.     }
55. 
56.     // Step 4: absolute threshold — find first local minimum below threshold.
57.     let mut tau_opt: Option<usize> = None;
58.     let mut tau = min_lag;
59.     while tau <= max_lag {
60.         if cmndf[tau] < YIN_THRESHOLD {
61.             // Find the local minimum within this dip.
62.             while tau + 1 <= max_lag && cmndf[tau + 1] < cmndf[tau] {
63.                 tau += 1;
64.             }
65.             tau_opt = Some(tau);
66.             break;
67.         }
68.         tau += 1;
69.     }
70. 
71.     // Fallback: pick global minimum if nothing below threshold.
72.     let tau_opt = tau_opt.unwrap_or_else(|| {
73.         (min_lag..=max_lag)
74.             .min_by(|&a, &b| cmndf[a].partial_cmp(&cmndf[b]).unwrap())
75.             .unwrap_or(min_lag)
76.     });
77. 
78.     if cmndf[tau_opt] > 0.5 {
79.         // Very low confidence — bail out.
80.         return None;
81.     }
82. 
83.     // Step 5: parabolic interpolation for sub-sample accuracy.
84.     let tau_f = parabolic_interpolation(&cmndf, tau_opt);
85. 
86.     if tau_f <= 0.0 {
87.         return None;
88.     }
89. 
90.     Some(sample_rate as f32 / tau_f)
91. }
92. 
93. /// Parabolic interpolation around the minimum at index `tau`.
94. fn parabolic_interpolation(cmndf: &[f32], tau: usize) -> f32 {
95.     if tau == 0 || tau >= cmndf.len() - 1 {
96.         return tau as f32;
97.     }
98.     let y0 = cmndf[tau - 1];
99.     let y1 = cmndf[tau];
100.     let y2 = cmndf[tau + 1];
101.     let denom = 2.0 * (2.0 * y1 - y2 - y0);
102.     if denom.abs() < f32::EPSILON {
103.         return tau as f32;
104.     }
105.     tau as f32 + (y2 - y0) / denom
106. }
107. 
108. // ── Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109. 
110. #[cfg(test)]
111. mod tests {
112.     use super::*;
113.     use std::f32::consts::PI;
114. 
115.     fn sine_wave(freq: f32, sample_rate: u32, num_samples: usize) -> Vec<f32> {
116.         (0..num_samples)
117.             .map(|i| (2.0 * PI * freq * i as f32 / sample_rate as f32).sin())
118.             .collect()
119.     }
120. 
121.     #[test]
122.     fn test_detect_a4() {
123.         let samples = sine_wave(440.0, 44100, 4096);
124.         let hz = detect_pitch(&samples, 44100).expect("Should detect A4");
125.         assert!(
126.             (hz - 440.0).abs() < 5.0,
127.             "Expected ~440 Hz, got {hz:.1} Hz"
128.         );
129.     }
130. 
131.     #[test]
132.     fn test_detect_c4() {
133.         let samples = sine_wave(261.63, 44100, 4096);
134.         let hz = detect_pitch(&samples, 44100).expect("Should detect C4");
135.         assert!((hz - 261.63).abs() < 5.0, "Expected ~261.6 Hz, got {hz:.1} Hz");
136.     }
137. 
138.     #[test]
139.     fn test_silence_returns_none() {
140.         let samples = vec![0.0f32; 4096];
141.         assert!(detect_pitch(&samples, 44100).is_none());
142.     }
143. }
144.
```

```
1. use ear_ring_core::{detect_pitch, freq_to_note, generate_sequence, staff_position, Note, ScaleType};
2. use wasm_bindgen::prelude::*;
3. 
4. /// Detect pitch from a Float32Array of PCM samples.
5. /// Returns the detected frequency in Hz, or -1.0 if not detected.
6. #[wasm_bindgen]
7. pub fn wasm_detect_pitch(samples: &[f32], sample_rate: u32) -> f32 {
8.     detect_pitch(samples, sample_rate).unwrap_or(-1.0)
9. }
10. 
11. /// Convert frequency (Hz) to MIDI note number.
12. /// Returns -1 if the frequency is out of range.
13. #[wasm_bindgen]
14. pub fn wasm_freq_to_midi(hz: f32) -> i32 {
15.     freq_to_note(hz).map(|(n, _)| n.midi() as i32).unwrap_or(-1)
16. }
17. 
18. /// Get cents deviation for a given frequency from the nearest semitone.
19. /// Returns 0 if out of range.
20. #[wasm_bindgen]
21. pub fn wasm_freq_to_cents(hz: f32) -> i32 {
22.     freq_to_note(hz).map(|(_, c)| c).unwrap_or(0)
23. }
24. 
25. /// Get staff position (diatonic steps above C4) for a MIDI note number.
26. #[wasm_bindgen]
27. pub fn wasm_staff_position(midi: u8) -> i32 {
28.     staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi))
29. }
30. 
31. /// Generate a note sequence; returns a Uint8Array of MIDI note numbers.
32. ///
33. /// * `root_midi` – root note MIDI number
34. /// * `scale_id`  – 0=Major, 1=NatMinor, 2=HarmMinor, 3=PentMaj, 4=PentMin,
35. ///                 5=Dorian, 6=Mixolydian, 7=Blues
36. /// * `length`    – number of notes
37. /// * `seed`      – random seed
38. #[wasm_bindgen]
39. pub fn wasm_generate_sequence(root_midi: u8, scale_id: u8, length: u8, seed: u64) -> Vec<u8> {
40.     let scale = match scale_id {
41.         0 => ScaleType::Major,
42.         1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
43.         2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
44.         3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
45.         4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
46.         5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
47.         6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
48.         7 => ScaleType::Blues,
49.         _ => ScaleType::Major,
50.     };
51.     let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi);
52.     generate_sequence(root, scale, length, seed)
53.         .iter()
54.         .map(|n| n.midi())
55.         .collect()
56. }
57.
```

```
1. import React from 'react';
2. import { StyleSheet, Text, TouchableOpacity, View } from 'react-native';

4. interface LengthSelectorProps {
5.   value: number;
6.   onChange: (n: number) => void;
7.   label?: string;
8.   min?: number;
9.   max?: number;
10. }
11. 
12. export default function LengthSelector({ value, onChange, label = 'Sequence length', min = 2, max = 8 }: LengthSelectorProps) {
13.   return (
14.     <View style={styles.container}>
15.       <Text style={styles.label}>{label}</Text>
16.       <View style={styles.row}>
17.         {Array.from({ length: max - min + 1 }, (_, i) => i + min).map((n) => (
18.           <TouchableOpacity
19.             key={n}
20.             style={[styles.chip, value === n && styles.chipActive]}
21.             onPress={() => onChange(n)}
22.           >
23.             <Text style={[styles.chipText, value === n && styles.chipTextActive]}>{n}</Text>
24.           </TouchableOpacity>
25.         ))}
26.       </View>
27.     </View>
28.   );
29. }
30.

32.   container: { gap: 6 },
33.   label: { color: '#94a3b8', fontSize: 12, textTransform: 'uppercase' },
```

C:\work\ear_ring/src/hooks/useAudioPlayback.native.ts

<details>
<summary>128 lines</summary>

```
1. import { createAudioPlayer, setAudioModeAsync } from 'expo-audio';
2. import type { AudioPlayer } from 'expo-audio';
3. import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react';
4. 
5. /**
6.  * useAudioPlayback — native (Android/iOS) implementation using expo-audio.
7.  *
8.  * Streams Salamander piano samples from the CDN via createAudioPlayer.
9.  * Pitch-shifts playback rate (2^(Δsemitones/12)) with shouldCorrectPitch=false
10.  * so the pitch changes along with the speed.
11.  *
12.  * Metro resolves this file over useAudioPlayback.ts on native builds.
13.  */
14. 
15. const BASE_URL = 'https://tonejs.github.io/audio/salamander/';
16. const NOTE_GAP_MS = 600;
17. 
18. // Available samples and their MIDI numbers
19. const SAMPLES: [string, number][] = [
20.   ['A0', 21], ['C1', 24], ['Ds1', 27], ['Fs1', 30],
21.   ['A1', 33], ['C2', 36], ['Ds2', 39], ['Fs2', 42],
22.   ['A2', 45], ['C3', 48], ['Ds3', 51], ['Fs3', 54],
23.   ['A3', 57], ['C4', 60], ['Ds4', 63], ['Fs4', 66],
24.   ['A4', 69], ['C5', 72], ['Ds5', 75], ['Fs5', 78],
25.   ['A5', 81], ['C6', 84], ['Ds6', 87], ['Fs6', 90],
26.   ['A6', 93], ['C7', 96], ['Ds7', 99], ['Fs7', 102],
27.   ['A7', 105], ['C8', 108],
28. ];
29. 
30. function nearestSample(midi: number): { url: string; semitones: number } {
31.   let best = SAMPLES[0];
32.   let bestDist = Infinity;
33.   for (const s of SAMPLES) {
34.     const d = Math.abs(s[1] - midi);
35.     if (d < bestDist) { bestDist = d; best = s; }
36.   }
37.   return { url: `${BASE_URL}${best[0]}.mp3`, semitones: midi - best[1] };
38. }
39. 
40. // Module-level cache — players survive re-renders
41. const playerCache = new Map<number, AudioPlayer>();
42. 
43. async function getPlayer(midi: number): Promise<AudioPlayer> {
44.   const cached = playerCache.get(midi);
45.   if (cached) {
46.     await cached.seekTo(0);
47.     return cached;
48.   }
49.   const { url, semitones } = nearestSample(midi);
50.   const player = createAudioPlayer({ uri: url });
51.   // Disable pitch correction so rate change = pitch change (2^(Δsemitones/12))
52.   player.shouldCorrectPitch = false;
53.   if (semitones !== 0) {
54.     player.setPlaybackRate(Math.pow(2, semitones / 12));
55.   }
56.   // Wait up to 8s for the audio to load
57.   if (!player.isLoaded) {
58.     await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
59.       const timeout = setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Audio load timeout')), 8000);
60.       const sub = player.addListener('playbackStatusUpdate', (status) => {
61.         if (status.isLoaded) {
62.           clearTimeout(timeout);
63.           sub.remove();
64.           resolve();
65.         }
66.       });
67.     });
68.   }
69.   playerCache.set(midi, player);
70.   return player;
71. }
72. 
73. export function useAudioPlayback() {
74.   const [isPlaying, setIsPlaying] = useState(false);
75.   const [samplerLoading, setSamplerLoading] = useState(false);
76.   const cancelRef = useRef(false);
77. 
78.   useEffect(() => {
79.     setAudioModeAsync({ playsInSilentMode: true, shouldPlayInBackground: false }).catch(() => {});
80.   }, []);
81. 
82.   const playSequence = useCallback(async (midiNotes: number[]) => {
83.     if (midiNotes.length === 0) return;
84.     cancelRef.current = false;
85.     setIsPlaying(true);
86.     setSamplerLoading(true);
87. 
88.     try {
89.       // Preload all notes in parallel before playback starts
90.       const players = await Promise.all(midiNotes.map(getPlayer));
91.       setSamplerLoading(false);
92. 
93.       for (let i = 0; i < players.length; i++) {
94.         if (cancelRef.current) break;
95.         await players[i].seekTo(0);
96.         players[i].play();
97. 
98.         if (i < players.length - 1) {
99.           // Interruptible gap
100.           await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
101.             const t = setTimeout(resolve, NOTE_GAP_MS);
102.             const iv = setInterval(() => {
103.               if (cancelRef.current) { clearTimeout(t); clearInterval(iv); resolve(); }
104.             }, 50);
105.             setTimeout(() => clearInterval(iv), NOTE_GAP_MS + 100);
106.           });
107.         }
108.       }
109.       // Let last note ring
110.       if (!cancelRef.current) {
111.         await new Promise<void>((r) => setTimeout(r, 1000));
112.       }
113.     } catch (err) {
114.       console.warn('Native audio playback error:', err);
115.       setSamplerLoading(false);
116.     }
117. 
118.     setIsPlaying(false);
119.   }, []);
120. 
121.   const stop = useCallback(() => {
122.     cancelRef.current = true;
123.     setIsPlaying(false);
124.   }, []);
125. 
126.   return { playSequence, stop, isPlaying, samplerLoading };
127. }
128.
```

</details>

<details>

```
1. package com.earring
2. 
3. import android.media.AudioFormat
4. import android.media.AudioRecord
5. import android.media.MediaRecorder
6. import android.util.Base64
7. import com.facebook.react.bridge.*
8. import com.facebook.react.module.annotations.ReactModule
9. import com.facebook.react.modules.core.DeviceEventManagerModule
10. import java.nio.ByteBuffer
11. import java.nio.ByteOrder
12. 
13. /**
14.  * AudioCaptureModule — streams real-time PCM audio from the microphone to JS.
15.  *
16.  * JS usage:
17.  *   const { NativeModules, NativeEventEmitter } = require('react-native');
18.  *   const emitter = new NativeEventEmitter(NativeModules.AudioCapture);
19.  *   emitter.addListener('onAudioData', ({ data, sampleRate, count }) => { ... });
20.  *   await NativeModules.AudioCapture.startRecording(44100, 4096);
21.  *   await NativeModules.AudioCapture.stopRecording();
22.  *
23.  * 'data' is a base64-encoded little-endian Float32 array of length 'count'.
24.  */
25. @ReactModule(name = AudioCaptureModule.NAME)
26. class AudioCaptureModule(reactContext: ReactApplicationContext) :
27.     ReactContextBaseJavaModule(reactContext) {
28. 
29.     companion object {
30.         const val NAME = "AudioCapture"
31.         const val EVENT_AUDIO_DATA = "onAudioData"
32.     }
33. 
34.     private var audioRecord: AudioRecord? = null
35.     private var recordingThread: Thread? = null
36.     @Volatile private var isRecording = false
37. 
38.     override fun getName() = NAME
39. 
40.     private fun sendEvent(params: WritableMap) {
41.         reactApplicationContext
42.             .getJSModule(DeviceEventManagerModule.RCTDeviceEventEmitter::class.java)
43.             .emit(EVENT_AUDIO_DATA, params)
44.     }
45. 
46.     @ReactMethod
47.     fun startRecording(sampleRate: Int, bufferSize: Int, promise: Promise) {
48.         if (isRecording) {
49.             promise.reject("ALREADY_RECORDING", "Already recording")
50.             return
51.         }
52. 
53.         val minBuf = AudioRecord.getMinBufferSize(
54.             sampleRate,
55.             AudioFormat.CHANNEL_IN_MONO,
56.             AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT
57.         )
58.         if (minBuf == AudioRecord.ERROR_BAD_VALUE || minBuf == AudioRecord.ERROR) {
59.             promise.reject("INIT_ERROR", "AudioRecord.getMinBufferSize failed")
60.             return
61.         }
62. 
63.         val byteBufSize = maxOf(minBuf, bufferSize * 2) // 2 bytes per sample (16-bit)
64. 
65.         try {
66.             audioRecord = AudioRecord(
67.                 MediaRecorder.AudioSource.MIC,
68.                 sampleRate,
69.                 AudioFormat.CHANNEL_IN_MONO,
70.                 AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT,
71.                 byteBufSize
72.             )
73.         } catch (e: Exception) {
74.             promise.reject("INIT_ERROR", "AudioRecord constructor failed: ${e.message}")
75.             return
76.         }
77. 
78.         if (audioRecord?.state != AudioRecord.STATE_INITIALIZED) {
79.             audioRecord?.release()
80.             audioRecord = null
81.             promise.reject("INIT_ERROR", "AudioRecord failed to initialize (check RECORD_AUDIO permission)")
82.             return
83.         }
84. 
85.         isRecording = true
86.         audioRecord?.startRecording()
87. 
88.         val shortBuf = ShortArray(bufferSize)
89.         val floatBuf = FloatArray(bufferSize)
90.         val byteBuffer = ByteBuffer.allocate(bufferSize * 4).order(ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN)
91. 
92.         recordingThread = Thread {
93.             while (isRecording) {
94.                 val read = audioRecord?.read(shortBuf, 0, bufferSize) ?: 0
95.                 if (read > 0) {
96.                     // Convert 16-bit PCM to float32 [-1.0, 1.0]
97.                     for (i in 0 until read) {
98.                         floatBuf[i] = shortBuf[i] / 32768.0f
99.                     }
100.                     // Pack as little-endian float32 bytes and base64-encode
101.                     byteBuffer.clear()
102.                     for (i in 0 until read) byteBuffer.putFloat(floatBuf[i])
103.                     val b64 = Base64.encodeToString(byteBuffer.array(), 0, read * 4, Base64.NO_WRAP)
104. 
105.                     val params = Arguments.createMap()
106.                     params.putString("data", b64)
107.                     params.putInt("sampleRate", sampleRate)
108.                     params.putInt("count", read)
109.                     sendEvent(params)
110.                 }
111.             }
112.         }.apply {
113.             name = "AudioCaptureThread"
114.             start()
115.         }
116. 
117.         promise.resolve(null)
118.     }
119. 
120.     @ReactMethod
121.     fun stopRecording(promise: Promise) {
122.         isRecording = false
123.         recordingThread?.join(2000)
124.         recordingThread = null
125.         try {
126.             audioRecord?.stop()
127.             audioRecord?.release()
128.         } catch (_: Exception) {}
129.         audioRecord = null
130.         promise.resolve(null)
131.     }
132. 
133.     // Required by NativeEventEmitter on the JS side
134.     @ReactMethod
135.     fun addListener(eventName: String) {}
136. 
137.     @ReactMethod
138.     fun removeListeners(count: Int) {}
139. }
140.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>81 lines</summary>

```
1. /**
2.  * EarRingCoreModule — Turbo Native Module (Kotlin/Android)
3.  *
4.  * Setup instructions:
5.  * 1. Build the Rust library for Android targets using cargo-ndk:
6.  *      cargo install cargo-ndk
7.  *      cargo ndk -t arm64-v8a -t x86_64 -o android/app/src/main/jniLibs build --release -p ear_ring_core
8.  *
9.  * 2. Add this file to the project (it should already be in the right package).
10.  *
11.  * 3. Register the module in MainApplication.kt by adding it to the package list:
12.  *      packages.add(EarRingCorePackage())
13.  *
14.  * The JNI bridge calls the C FFI functions defined in rust/src/lib.rs.
15.  * Java signatures are auto-generated from the function names.
16.  */
17. 
18. package com.earring
19. 
20. import com.facebook.react.bridge.*
21. import com.facebook.react.module.annotations.ReactModule
22. 
23. @ReactModule(name = EarRingCoreModule.NAME)
24. class EarRingCoreModule(reactContext: ReactApplicationContext) :
25.     ReactContextBaseJavaModule(reactContext) {
26. 
27.     companion object {
28.         const val NAME = "EarRingCore"
29. 
30.         private var libraryLoaded = false
31. 
32.         init {
33.             try {
34.                 System.loadLibrary("ear_ring_core")
35.                 libraryLoaded = true
36.             } catch (e: UnsatisfiedLinkError) {
37.                 android.util.Log.w(NAME, "ear_ring_core native library not found: ${e.message}")
38.             }
39.         }
40. 
41.         // JNI declarations — implemented in rust/src/lib.rs via C FFI
42.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeDetectPitch(samples: FloatArray, sampleRate: Int): Float
43.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToMidi(hz: Float): Int
44.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToCents(hz: Float): Int
45.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeStaffPosition(midi: Int): Int
46.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeGenerateSequence(
47.             rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Long
48.         ): IntArray
49.     }
50. 
51.     override fun getName() = NAME
52. 
53.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
54.     fun detectPitch(samples: ReadableArray, sampleRate: Int): Float {
55.         val floats = FloatArray(samples.size()) { samples.getDouble(it).toFloat() }
56.         return nativeDetectPitch(floats, sampleRate)
57.     }
58. 
59.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
60.     fun freqToNote(hz: Double): WritableMap? {
61.         val midi = nativeFreqToMidi(hz.toFloat())
62.         if (midi < 0) return null
63.         val cents = nativeFreqToCents(hz.toFloat())
64.         val result = WritableNativeMap()
65.         result.putInt("midi", midi)
66.         result.putInt("cents", cents)
67.         return result
68.     }
69. 
70.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
71.     fun staffPosition(midi: Int): Int = nativeStaffPosition(midi)
72. 
73.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
74.     fun generateSequence(rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Double): WritableArray {
75.         val arr = nativeGenerateSequence(rootMidi, scaleId, length, seed.toLong())
76.         val result = WritableNativeArray()
77.         arr.forEach { result.pushInt(it) }
78.         return result
79.     }
80. }
81.
```

</details>

C:\work\ear_ring/rust/Cargo.toml

<details>
<summary>15 lines</summary>

```
1. [package]
2. name = "ear_ring_core"
3. version = "0.1.0"
4. edition = "2021"
5. 
6. [lib]
7. crate-type = ["cdylib", "staticlib", "rlib"]
8. 
9. [dependencies]
10. 
11. [target.'cfg(target_os = "android")'.dependencies]
12. jni = { version = "0.21", default-features = false }
13. 
14. [dev-dependencies]
15.
```

</details>

# **Comprehensive Ear Ring Codebase Summary**

## **1. Project Overview**
**Ear Ring** is a cross-platform mobile app (iOS/Android/Web) built with **React Native + Expo** that helps users train their ear by playing musical note sequences and detecting what notes they sing/play via the microphone. It uses **Rust core libraries** for pitch detection and music theory calculations, with platform-specific native modules for audio capture.

---

## **2. Full Directory Structure**

```
C:\work\ear_ring/
├── app/                          # Expo Router screens (main navigation)
│   ├── _layout.tsx              # Root navigation layout (Stack)
│   ├── index.tsx                # Home screen - exercise setup
│   ├── exercise.tsx             # Main exercise gameplay
│   ├── setup.tsx                # Microphone setup/testing
│   ├── results.tsx              # Exercise results & scoring
│   └── progress.tsx             # Historical progress & statistics
│
├── src/
│   ├── components/              # React Native UI components
│   │   ├── MusicStaff.tsx       # SVG treble clef staff renderer
│   │   ├── PitchMeter.tsx       # Live pitch detection display (circular)
│   │   ├── ScaleSelector.tsx    # Root note + scale type selector
│   │   └── LengthSelector.tsx   # Sequence length selector (2-8 notes)
│   │
│   ├── hooks/                   # Custom React hooks
│   │   ├── usePitchDetection.ts # Pitch detection from mic (YIN algorithm)
│   │   ├── useAudioPlayback.ts  # Audio playback via Tone.js (web)
│   │   └── useAudioPlayback.native.ts  # Audio playback via expo-audio (native)
│   │

│   │   ├── CoreBridge.ts        # Platform-agnostic re-export
│   │   ├── EarRingCoreModule.ts # Native Turbo Module wrapper (iOS/Android)
│   │   ├── EarRingCoreModule.web.ts  # Pure JS implementation (web fallback)

│   │

│   │   └── progressStore.ts     # Historical session records (AsyncStorage-backed)
│   │

│       ├── musicTheory.ts       # Music theory constants & helpers

│

│   │   └── pitch_detection.rs  # YIN pitch detection algorithm

│
├── rust_wasm/                   # WebAssembly bindings (via wasm-bindgen)

│

│       ├── MainApplication.kt   # RN app setup & module registration
│       ├── AudioCaptureModule.kt # Real-time PCM audio streaming from mic
│       ├── AudioCapturePackage.kt
│       ├── EarRingCoreModule.kt # JNI bridge to Rust core library
│       └── EarRingCorePackage.kt
│
├── ios/                         # iOS native code (Objective-C) - not yet examined
```

---

## **3. What the App Does**

### **Core Functionality:**
The app trains users to recognize and sing/play musical notes within a selected scale by:

1. **Setup Phase** (`index.tsx`):
   - User selects a **root note** (C, C#, D, etc.) and **octave** (3, 4, or 5)
   - Selects a **musical scale** (Major, Natural Minor, Harmonic Minor, Pentatonic Major, Pentatonic Minor, Dorian, Mixolydian, Blues)
   - Selects a **sequence length** (2–8 notes)

2. **Exercise Phase** (`exercise.tsx`):
   - App plays a **random sequence of notes** from the selected scale via piano samples
   - User **listens and sings/plays back** each note on their instrument
   - App detects pitch via microphone using **YIN algorithm** (Rust/native)
   - Each detected note is **validated** (correct pitch class + ≤50 cent deviation)
   - Visual feedback: **music staff displays expected notes** (white) and **detected notes** (green ✓ / red ✗)
   - On correct sequence → **automatically generates a new sequence** with increased difficulty/variation
   - On mistake → **replays the same sequence**
   - Haptic feedback on correct/incorrect answers (phone vibration)

3. **Mic Setup** (`setup.tsx`):
   - Users can **test their microphone** by playing notes
   - **Live pitch meter** shows detected frequency
   - **Simulation mode** for testing without a real instrument
   - Visual feedback on music staff

4. **Results Screen** (`results.tsx`):
   - Shows **percentage score** (0–100%) based on notes correctly played
   - Displays expected vs played notes side-by-side
   - Saves session to persistent history

5. **Progress Screen** (`progress.tsx`):
   - **Streak counter** (consecutive sessions ≥80% score)
   - **Best score per scale** (tracks improvement)
   - **Session history** with timestamps, scores, scale, and sequence length

---

## **4. Rust Core Code**

### **File: `rust/src/lib.rs`**
- **Exports via C FFI** (for native iOS/Android via JNI/FFI)
- **Exports via JNI** (Android-specific bindings)
- Functions:
  - `ear_ring_detect_pitch()` – pitch detection from PCM buffer
  - `ear_ring_freq_to_note()` – frequency → MIDI conversion
  - `ear_ring_staff_position()` – MIDI → music staff position
  - `ear_ring_generate_sequence()` – random sequence generator

### **File: `rust/src/music_theory.rs`** (9,985 bytes)
**Core Music Theory System:**

- **`NoteName` enum** – 12 chromatic pitches (C, C#, D, D#, E, F, F#, G, G#, A, A#, B)
- **`Note` struct** – pitch class + octave (C4 = middle C = MIDI 60)
- **Frequency conversions:**
  - `midi_to_freq()` – MIDI number → Hz (A4 = 440 Hz standard)
  - `freq_to_note()` – Hz → MIDI + cents deviation
- **`ScaleType` enum** – 8 supported scales with semitone intervals:
  - Major: [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11]
  - Natural Minor: [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 10]
  - Harmonic Minor: [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 11]
  - Pentatonic Major: [0, 2, 4, 7, 9]
  - Pentatonic Minor: [0, 3, 5, 7, 10]
  - Dorian: [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10]
  - Mixolydian: [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10]
  - Blues: [0, 3, 5, 6, 7, 10]
- **`scale_notes()`** – generates scale notes (e.g., C Major = C, D, E, F, G, A, B)
- **`generate_sequence()`** – random note selector using **LCG RNG** (Linear Congruential Generator) seeded for reproducibility
- **`staff_position()`** – maps MIDI → diatonic steps on treble clef (C4 = 0, D4 = 1, ..., C5 = 7)

### **File: `rust/src/pitch_detection.rs`** (4,684 bytes)
**YIN Pitch Detection Algorithm** (de Cheveigné & Kawahara 2002):

1. **Difference Function**: Calculates squared differences between samples at varying lags
2. **CMNDF (Cumulative Mean Normalized Difference Function)**: Normalizes by running sum
3. **Threshold Detection**: Finds first dip below threshold (0.15)
4. **Parabolic Interpolation**: Sub-sample accuracy refinement
5. **Output**: Fundamental frequency in Hz, or `None` if low confidence (CMNDF \> 0.5)

**Range**: Detects pitches from ~20 Hz (bass) to ~1200 Hz (treble) based on sample rate.

### **File: `rust_wasm/src/lib.rs`**
WebAssembly bindings using `wasm-bindgen`:
- `wasm_detect_pitch()`, `wasm_freq_to_midi()`, `wasm_freq_to_cents()`, `wasm_staff_position()`, `wasm_generate_sequence()`
- Currently **not used in active builds** but remains available for future WASM integration

---

## **5. Android Native Code**

### **`android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/AudioCaptureModule.kt`**
**Real-time microphone audio streaming:**
- Captures 16-bit PCM at configurable sample rate (typically 44100 Hz)
- Converts to **normalized float32** (-1.0 to +1.0)
- **Base64-encodes** and emits via **React Native event emitter**
- JS hook (`usePitchDetection.ts`) receives `onAudioData` events with samples + metadata
- Runs on dedicated thread to avoid blocking UI

### **`android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/EarRingCoreModule.kt`**
**JNI bridge to Rust:**
- Loads native library: `System.loadLibrary("ear_ring_core")`
- Exposes Rust functions as Turbo Native Module methods:
  - `detectPitch(samples: FloatArray, sampleRate: Int): Float`
  - `freqToNote(hz: Double): WritableMap` (returns `{midi, cents}`)
  - `staffPosition(midi: Int): Int`
  - `generateSequence(rootMidi, scaleId, length, seed): IntArray`
- Synchronous blocking methods (`isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true`) for real-time performance

### **iOS Native Code**
- **Not yet examined** but would follow similar pattern with Objective-C/Swift
- Uses FFI/XCFramework wrapper to Rust C library

---

## **6. Key Business Logic – State Management**

### **Exercise Store** (`src/store/exerciseStore.ts`)
**Zustand state for current exercise:**
```typescript
{
  rootMidi: number;           // Root note MIDI (e.g., 60 = C4)
  scaleId: number;            // Scale type index (0-7)
  sequenceLength: number;     // 2-8 notes
  maxTries: number;           // Tries per note
  sequence: number[];         // Expected MIDI notes
  detected: DetectedNote[];   // {midi, cents} array
  status: 'idle' | 'playing' | 'listening' | 'done';
  seed: number;               // RNG seed for reproducibility
  
  // Methods
  setRoot(), setScaleId(), setSequenceLength(), setMaxTries()
  setSequence(), setStatus()
  addDetected(), resetDetected()
  newSeed()  // Generates new random seed
}
```

### **Progress Store** (`src/store/progressStore.ts`)
**AsyncStorage-backed Zustand store:**
```typescript
{
  history: SessionRecord[];  // [{id, date, scaleName, rootLabel, score, length}]
  streak: number;            // Consecutive sessions ≥80%
  bestByScale: Record\<string, number\>;  // Best score per scale
  
  loadHistory()  // Load from AsyncStorage on app boot
  addSession()   // Save new session
}
```

**Computed Values:**
- `streak`: Counts reverse from latest session until a session \<80%
- `bestByScale`: Max score per scale name

---

## **7. Audio Processing & Playback**

### **Pitch Detection Hook** (`src/hooks/usePitchDetection.ts`)
**State Machine for Stable Pitch Detection:**

```
STATE: idle → listening → pausing → back to listening

Detection Pipeline:
1. Microphone → 4096-sample PCM buffers
2. RMS analysis → silence detection (threshold: 0.003)
3. YIN pitch detection (via native Rust or JS fallback)
4. MIDI conversion (freq → MIDI + cents)

Stability Mechanism:
- Requires pitch to be stable for 450ms before firing onNote
- Glitch filter: ignores single-frame pitch anomalies
  (requires 2+ consecutive frames of different pitch to switch)
- Silence hold: 250ms of silence ends current note

Output: {hz, midi, cents, confidence}
```

**Web Implementation** (navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia + ScriptProcessor):
- Uses Web Audio API directly
- Falls back to synthetic note simulation for testing

**Android Implementation** (AudioCapture module):
- Real-time audio streaming via NativeEventEmitter
- Base64-encoded float32 samples

**iOS Implementation**:
- Stub (not yet implemented)

### **Audio Playback Hook** (`src/hooks/useAudioPlayback.ts` & `.native.ts`)

**Web (Tone.js):**
- Lazy-loads Tone.js to avoid Metro bundling issues
- **Salamander Grand Piano samples** from CDN (tonejs.github.io)
- Each note: quarter note (1/4 beat) with 0.55-second spacing between onsets
- Polyphonic playback with timing via Tone.js scheduler

**Native (expo-audio):**
- Pre-loads players in parallel from CDN
- **Pitch-shifts** playback rate to transpose samples:
  - Rate = 2^(Δsemitones / 12)
  - Maps requested MIDI to nearest sample (A0, C1, Ds1, Fs1, ..., C8)
  - Playback rate adjusted without pitch correction
- 600ms gap between note onsets
- Allows interruption (cancel playback)

---

## **8. UI Screens & Components**

### **Screen: Home (`app/index.tsx`)**
- **ScaleSelector**: Choose root note (C–B), octave (3–5), scale (0–7)
- **LengthSelector**: Sequence length (2–8)
- **Buttons**: 
  - ▶ Start Exercise
  - 📊 View Progress
  - 🎙 Mic Setup

### **Screen: Exercise (`app/exercise.tsx`)**
**Core gameplay loop:**
- **MusicStaff**: Displays expected sequence + detected notes in real-time
- **PitchMeter**: Circular indicator of current detected frequency
- **Feedback Label**: "Note N / M" or "✓ Correct!" or "✗ Wrong! Restarting…"
- **Buttons**:
  - ▶ Play Sequence (playback)
  - 🎤 Start Listening (activate pitch detection)
  - ⏹ Stop
  - ↺ Replay Sequence

**Note Validation** (`isCorrect()` from `musicTheory.ts`):
```typescript
isCorrect(detected, expected, cents) {
  return samePitchClass(detected, expected) && Math.abs(cents) \<= 50
}
```
- Allows octave transposition (C4 vs C5 both valid)
- Max ±50 cents deviation (half a semitone)

### **Screen: Setup (`app/setup.tsx`)**
- **Live note display** on staff
- **Live pitch meter**
- **Test buttons**: Play simulated notes (C4, E4, G4, A4, C5)
- Used to verify microphone & pitch detection before exercise

### **Screen: Results (`app/results.tsx`)**
- **Score display** with emoji (🏆 100%, 🎉 80%+, 👍 50%+, 💪 \<50%)
- **Visual staff** showing expected (white) vs played (green/red)
- **Per-note breakdown**: Expected note, played note, ✓/✗ status
- **Buttons**: Try Again, New Exercise, View Progress

### **Screen: Progress (`app/progress.tsx`)**
- **Streak card** with 🔥 icon
- **Best scores by scale** (e.g., "C Major: 95%")
- **Session history** (latest first):
  - Date/time, scale, root note, length, score
  - Color-coded badges: green (≥80%), yellow (50–79%), red (\<50%)

### **Components:**

**MusicStaff** (`src/components/MusicStaff.tsx`):
- Treble clef (𝄞 symbol)
- 5 staff lines + ledger lines for notes outside staff
- Key signature accidentals (sharps/flats)
- Note heads (white ○, green ✓, red ✗)
- Rendered with `react-native-svg`

**PitchMeter** (`src/components/PitchMeter.tsx`):
- Circular indicator (green when detecting, gray when silent)
- Displays detected MIDI note name (e.g., "A4")

**ScaleSelector** (`src/components/ScaleSelector.tsx`):
- Chip buttons for each note name (C–B)
- Chip buttons for octaves (3, 4, 5)
- Chip buttons for scale types (8 options)
- Active state: indigo background + white text

**LengthSelector** (`src/components/LengthSelector.tsx`):
- Numbered chips (2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8)
- Highlights selected length

---

## **9. Utilities & Shared Logic**

### **Music Theory Utils** (`src/utils/musicTheory.ts`)
**Constants:**
- `NOTE_NAMES`: ['C', 'C#', 'D', 'D#', 'E', 'F', 'F#', 'G', 'G#', 'A', 'A#', 'B']
- `ROOT_OCTAVES`: [3, 4, 5]
- `SCALES`: 8 scale definitions with intervals

**Functions:**
- `rootMidi(noteName, octave)` → MIDI number
- `midiLabel(midi)` → "A4" format
- `samePitchClass(a, b)` → true if same note (octave-agnostic)
- `isCorrect(detected, expected, cents)` → tolerance check
- `getKeySignature(rootMidi, scaleId)` → {type: 'sharp'|'flat'|'none', count: number}

### **Score Calculator** (`src/utils/scoreCalculator.ts`)
```typescript
compareSequences(expected: number[], detected: {midi, cents}[])
  → [{expected, detected, cents, correct}]

computeScore(comparisons: NoteComparison[])
  → (correctCount / totalCount) * 100
```

### **Platform-Agnostic Bridge** (`src/native/CoreBridge.ts`)
```typescript
detectPitch(samples, sampleRate): number
freqToNote(hz): {midi, cents} | null
staffPosition(midi): number
generateSequence(rootMidi, scaleId, length, seed): number[]
```
- Falls back to JS implementation if native module unavailable
- Metro auto-selects `.web.ts` for web platform

---

## **10. Technology Stack**

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| **Framework** | React Native 0.83.2, Expo 55.0.5 |
| **Navigation** | Expo Router (file-based) |
| **State Management** | Zustand 5.0.11 |
| **Audio Playback** | Tone.js 15.1.22 (web), expo-audio (native) |
| **Audio Capture** | Web Audio API (web), AudioCapture module (Android) |
| **UI Rendering** | react-native, react-native-svg 15.15.3 |
| **Storage** | AsyncStorage 2.2.0 (persistent progress) |
| **Core Algorithms** | Rust (music theory, pitch detection via YIN) |
| **Native Integration** | Turbo Native Modules (Android/iOS via JNI/FFI) |
| **Language** | TypeScript 5.9.3, Kotlin (Android), (Swift - iOS) |
| **Build** | Metro bundler, Cargo (Rust) |

---

## **11. Data Flow Summary**

### **Exercise Flow:**
```
User Setup
  ↓
Generate Random Sequence (Rust RNG)
  ↓
Play Audio (Tone.js / expo-audio)
  ↓
Listen for Microphone Input
  ↓
YIN Pitch Detection (Rust / JS)
  ↓
MIDI Conversion + Cents Calculation
  ↓
Validate Against Expected Note
  ↓
Update UI (staff, feedback, meter)
  ↓
If Correct → Advance to Next Note
If Wrong → Replay Same Sequence
If Complete → Generate New Sequence
  ↓
Save Session to AsyncStorage
```

---

## **Summary Table**

| Component | Purpose | Technology |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| **App Shell** | Navigation & layout | Expo Router (file-based) |
| **Home Screen** | Setup parameters | React Native UI |
| **Exercise Screen** | Gameplay loop | Hooks + Zustand + Native modules |
| **Pitch Detection** | Mic → Frequency | YIN (Rust) + Web Audio (JS) |
| **Audio Playback** | Play sequences | Tone.js (web) / expo-audio (native) |
| **Music Staff** | Visual feedback | react-native-svg |
| **Note Validation** | Check correctness | Pure JS (musicTheory utils) |
| **Scoring** | Grade user | Pure JS (scoreCalculator) |
| **Progress Tracking** | Persistence | Zustand + AsyncStorage |
| **Rust Core** | Heavy computation | C FFI (native) + JS fallback |

This is a well-architected educational music app with strong separation of concerns: UI layer (React Native), business logic layer (Zustand + utils), and compute layer (Rust + native modules).

### 💭 Reasoning

*This is a comprehensive project. The user wants to:
1. Commit the current state
2. Create a new branch called "native"
3. Convert to native Android (Kotlin) and iOS (Swift) — removing the React Native/Expo layer
4. Retain as much common logic as possible in Rust

This is a **massive** architectural change. Let me think about the scope:

Current architecture:
- React Native/Expo for UI
- Rust for pitch detection and music theory
- Kotlin for Android native module bridge
- iOS (likely Objective-C/Swift) for native module bridge
- Tone.js for audio playback (web)


- expo-audio for native audio playback
- Zustand for state management
- AsyncStorage for persistence

Target architecture:
- **Android**: Pure Kotlin app with native Android UI
- **iOS**: Pure Swift app with native iOS UI
- **Rust**: Shared core for pitch detection, music theory, sequence generation
- Remove all React Native/Expo/Node.js dependencies

This is a very significant undertaking. I need to set up separate Kotlin and Swift project structures, maintain the existing Rust core with its JNI bindings for Android, and rebuild the entire UI layer in native code for both platforms.

I should approach this incrementally—first committing the current state and creating a branch, then examining what already exists in the android/ and ios/ directories before planning the full conversion. The native modules like AudioCaptureModule.kt and EarRingCoreModule.kt are already in place, so the main work is converting the React Native UI to native Kotlin and Swift implementations.*

```
13. class MainActivity : ReactActivity() {
14.   override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
15.     // Set the theme to AppTheme BEFORE onCreate to support
16.     // coloring the background, status bar, and navigation bar.
17.     // This is required for expo-splash-screen.
18.     setTheme(R.style.AppTheme);
19.     super.onCreate(null)
20.   }
21. 
22.   /**
23.    * Returns the name of the main component registered from JavaScript. This is used to schedule
24.    * rendering of the component.
25.    */
26.   override fun getMainComponentName(): String = "main"
27. 
28.   /**
29.    * Returns the instance of the [ReactActivityDelegate]. We use [DefaultReactActivityDelegate]
30.    * which allows you to enable New Architecture with a single boolean flags [fabricEnabled]
31.    */
32.   override fun createReactActivityDelegate(): ReactActivityDelegate {
33.     return ReactActivityDelegateWrapper(
34.           this,
35.           BuildConfig.IS_NEW_ARCHITECTURE_ENABLED,
36.           object : DefaultReactActivityDelegate(
37.               this,
38.               mainComponentName,
39.               fabricEnabled
40.           ){})
41.   }
42. 
43.   /**
44.     * Align the back button behavior with Android S
45.     * where moving root activities to background instead of finishing activities.
46.     * @see <a href="https://developer.android.com/reference/android/app/Activity#onBackPressed()">onBackPressed</a>
47.     */
48.   override fun invokeDefaultOnBackPressed() {
49.       if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT <= Build.VERSION_CODES.R) {
50.           if (!moveTaskToBack(false)) {
51.               // For non-root activities, use the default implementation to finish them.
52.               super.invokeDefaultOnBackPressed()
53.           }
54.           return
55.       }
56. 
57.       // Use the default back button implementation on Android S
58.       // because it's doing more than [Activity.moveTaskToBack] in fact.
59.       super.invokeDefaultOnBackPressed()
60.   }
61. }
```

```
18.

20.     ExpoReactHostFactory.getDefaultReactHost(
21.       context = applicationContext,
22.       packageList =

25.           add(EarRingCorePackage())

34.     } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
35.       ReleaseLevel.STABLE
36.     }
37.     loadReactNative(this)
38.     ApplicationLifecycleDispatcher.onApplicationCreate(this)
39.   }
```

<summary>81 lines</summary>

```
1. /**
2.  * EarRingCoreModule — Turbo Native Module (Kotlin/Android)
3.  *
4.  * Setup instructions:
5.  * 1. Build the Rust library for Android targets using cargo-ndk:
6.  *      cargo install cargo-ndk
7.  *      cargo ndk -t arm64-v8a -t x86_64 -o android/app/src/main/jniLibs build --release -p ear_ring_core
8.  *
9.  * 2. Add this file to the project (it should already be in the right package).
10.  *
11.  * 3. Register the module in MainApplication.kt by adding it to the package list:
12.  *      packages.add(EarRingCorePackage())
13.  *
14.  * The JNI bridge calls the C FFI functions defined in rust/src/lib.rs.
15.  * Java signatures are auto-generated from the function names.
16.  */
17. 
18. package com.earring
19. 
20. import com.facebook.react.bridge.*
21. import com.facebook.react.module.annotations.ReactModule
22. 
23. @ReactModule(name = EarRingCoreModule.NAME)
24. class EarRingCoreModule(reactContext: ReactApplicationContext) :
25.     ReactContextBaseJavaModule(reactContext) {
26. 
27.     companion object {
28.         const val NAME = "EarRingCore"
29. 
30.         private var libraryLoaded = false
31. 
32.         init {
33.             try {
34.                 System.loadLibrary("ear_ring_core")
35.                 libraryLoaded = true
36.             } catch (e: UnsatisfiedLinkError) {
37.                 android.util.Log.w(NAME, "ear_ring_core native library not found: ${e.message}")
38.             }
39.         }
40. 
41.         // JNI declarations — implemented in rust/src/lib.rs via C FFI
42.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeDetectPitch(samples: FloatArray, sampleRate: Int): Float
43.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToMidi(hz: Float): Int
44.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToCents(hz: Float): Int
45.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeStaffPosition(midi: Int): Int
46.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeGenerateSequence(
47.             rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Long
48.         ): IntArray
49.     }
50. 
51.     override fun getName() = NAME
52. 
53.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
54.     fun detectPitch(samples: ReadableArray, sampleRate: Int): Float {
55.         val floats = FloatArray(samples.size()) { samples.getDouble(it).toFloat() }
56.         return nativeDetectPitch(floats, sampleRate)
57.     }
58. 
59.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
60.     fun freqToNote(hz: Double): WritableMap? {
61.         val midi = nativeFreqToMidi(hz.toFloat())
62.         if (midi < 0) return null
63.         val cents = nativeFreqToCents(hz.toFloat())
64.         val result = WritableNativeMap()
65.         result.putInt("midi", midi)
66.         result.putInt("cents", cents)
67.         return result
68.     }
69. 
70.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
71.     fun staffPosition(midi: Int): Int = nativeStaffPosition(midi)
72. 
73.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
74.     fun generateSequence(rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Double): WritableArray {
75.         val arr = nativeGenerateSequence(rootMidi, scaleId, length, seed.toLong())
76.         val result = WritableNativeArray()
77.         arr.forEach { result.pushInt(it) }
78.         return result
79.     }
80. }
81.
```

</details>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\work\ear_ring/android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/AudioCaptureModule.kt.**

C:\work\ear_ring/android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/AudioCaptureModule.kt

<details>
<summary>140 lines</summary>

```
1. package com.earring
2. 
3. import android.media.AudioFormat
4. import android.media.AudioRecord
5. import android.media.MediaRecorder
6. import android.util.Base64
7. import com.facebook.react.bridge.*
8. import com.facebook.react.module.annotations.ReactModule
9. import com.facebook.react.modules.core.DeviceEventManagerModule
10. import java.nio.ByteBuffer
11. import java.nio.ByteOrder
12. 
13. /**
14.  * AudioCaptureModule — streams real-time PCM audio from the microphone to JS.
15.  *
16.  * JS usage:
17.  *   const { NativeModules, NativeEventEmitter } = require('react-native');
18.  *   const emitter = new NativeEventEmitter(NativeModules.AudioCapture);
19.  *   emitter.addListener('onAudioData', ({ data, sampleRate, count }) => { ... });
20.  *   await NativeModules.AudioCapture.startRecording(44100, 4096);
21.  *   await NativeModules.AudioCapture.stopRecording();
22.  *
23.  * 'data' is a base64-encoded little-endian Float32 array of length 'count'.
24.  */
25. @ReactModule(name = AudioCaptureModule.NAME)
26. class AudioCaptureModule(reactContext: ReactApplicationContext) :
27.     ReactContextBaseJavaModule(reactContext) {
28. 
29.     companion object {
30.         const val NAME = "AudioCapture"
31.         const val EVENT_AUDIO_DATA = "onAudioData"
32.     }
33. 
34.     private var audioRecord: AudioRecord? = null
35.     private var recordingThread: Thread? = null
36.     @Volatile private var isRecording = false
37. 
38.     override fun getName() = NAME
39. 
40.     private fun sendEvent(params: WritableMap) {
41.         reactApplicationContext
42.             .getJSModule(DeviceEventManagerModule.RCTDeviceEventEmitter::class.java)
43.             .emit(EVENT_AUDIO_DATA, params)
44.     }
45. 
46.     @ReactMethod
47.     fun startRecording(sampleRate: Int, bufferSize: Int, promise: Promise) {
48.         if (isRecording) {
49.             promise.reject("ALREADY_RECORDING", "Already recording")
50.             return
51.         }
52. 
53.         val minBuf = AudioRecord.getMinBufferSize(
54.             sampleRate,
55.             AudioFormat.CHANNEL_IN_MONO,
56.             AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT
57.         )
58.         if (minBuf == AudioRecord.ERROR_BAD_VALUE || minBuf == AudioRecord.ERROR) {
59.             promise.reject("INIT_ERROR", "AudioRecord.getMinBufferSize failed")
60.             return
61.         }
62. 
63.         val byteBufSize = maxOf(minBuf, bufferSize * 2) // 2 bytes per sample (16-bit)
64. 
65.         try {
66.             audioRecord = AudioRecord(
67.                 MediaRecorder.AudioSource.MIC,
68.                 sampleRate,
69.                 AudioFormat.CHANNEL_IN_MONO,
70.                 AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT,
71.                 byteBufSize
72.             )
73.         } catch (e: Exception) {
74.             promise.reject("INIT_ERROR", "AudioRecord constructor failed: ${e.message}")
75.             return
76.         }
77. 
78.         if (audioRecord?.state != AudioRecord.STATE_INITIALIZED) {
79.             audioRecord?.release()
80.             audioRecord = null
81.             promise.reject("INIT_ERROR", "AudioRecord failed to initialize (check RECORD_AUDIO permission)")
82.             return
83.         }
84. 
85.         isRecording = true
86.         audioRecord?.startRecording()
87. 
88.         val shortBuf = ShortArray(bufferSize)
89.         val floatBuf = FloatArray(bufferSize)
90.         val byteBuffer = ByteBuffer.allocate(bufferSize * 4).order(ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN)
91. 
92.         recordingThread = Thread {
93.             while (isRecording) {
94.                 val read = audioRecord?.read(shortBuf, 0, bufferSize) ?: 0
95.                 if (read > 0) {
96.                     // Convert 16-bit PCM to float32 [-1.0, 1.0]
97.                     for (i in 0 until read) {
98.                         floatBuf[i] = shortBuf[i] / 32768.0f
99.                     }
100.                     // Pack as little-endian float32 bytes and base64-encode
101.                     byteBuffer.clear()
102.                     for (i in 0 until read) byteBuffer.putFloat(floatBuf[i])
103.                     val b64 = Base64.encodeToString(byteBuffer.array(), 0, read * 4, Base64.NO_WRAP)
104. 
105.                     val params = Arguments.createMap()
106.                     params.putString("data", b64)
107.                     params.putInt("sampleRate", sampleRate)
108.                     params.putInt("count", read)
109.                     sendEvent(params)
110.                 }
111.             }
112.         }.apply {
113.             name = "AudioCaptureThread"
114.             start()
115.         }
116. 
117.         promise.resolve(null)
118.     }
119. 
120.     @ReactMethod
121.     fun stopRecording(promise: Promise) {
122.         isRecording = false
123.         recordingThread?.join(2000)
124.         recordingThread = null
125.         try {
126.             audioRecord?.stop()
127.             audioRecord?.release()
128.         } catch (_: Exception) {}
129.         audioRecord = null
130.         promise.resolve(null)
131.     }
132. 
133.     // Required by NativeEventEmitter on the JS side
134.     @ReactMethod
135.     fun addListener(eventName: String) {}
136. 
137.     @ReactMethod
138.     fun removeListeners(count: Int) {}
139. }
140.
```

</details>

```
1. apply plugin: "com.android.application"
2. apply plugin: "org.jetbrains.kotlin.android"
3. apply plugin: "com.facebook.react"
4. 
5. def projectRoot = rootDir.getAbsoluteFile().getParentFile().getAbsolutePath()
6. 
7. /**
8.  * This is the configuration block to customize your React Native Android app.
9.  * By default you don't need to apply any configuration, just uncomment the lines you need.
10.  */
11. react {
12.     entryFile = file(["node", "-e", "require('expo/scripts/resolveAppEntry')", projectRoot, "android", "absolute"].execute(null, rootDir).text.trim())
13.     reactNativeDir = new File(["node", "--print", "require.resolve('react-native/package.json')"].execute(null, rootDir).text.trim()).getParentFile().getAbsoluteFile()
14.     hermesCommand = new File(["node", "--print", "require.resolve('hermes-compiler/package.json', { paths: [require.resolve('react-native/package.json')] })"].execute(null, rootDir).text.trim()).getParentFile().getAbsolutePath() + "/hermesc/%OS-BIN%/hermesc"
15.     codegenDir = new File(["node", "--print", "require.resolve('@react-native/codegen/package.json', { paths: [require.resolve('react-native/package.json')] })"].execute(null, rootDir).text.trim()).getParentFile().getAbsoluteFile()
16. 
17.     enableBundleCompression = (findProperty('android.enableBundleCompression') ?: false).toBoolean()
18.     // Use Expo CLI to bundle the app, this ensures the Metro config
19.     // works correctly with Expo projects.
20.     cliFile = new File(["node", "--print", "require.resolve('@expo/cli', { paths: [require.resolve('expo/package.json')] })"].execute(null, rootDir).text.trim())
21.     bundleCommand = "export:embed"
22. 
23.     /* Folders */
24.      //   The root of your project, i.e. where "package.json" lives. Default is '../..'
25.     // root = file("../../")
26.     //   The folder where the react-native NPM package is. Default is ../../node_modules/react-native
27.     // reactNativeDir = file("../../node_modules/react-native")
28.     //   The folder where the react-native Codegen package is. Default is ../../node_modules/@react-native/codegen
29.     // codegenDir = file("../../node_modules/@react-native/codegen")
30. 
31.     /* Variants */
32.     //   The list of variants to that are debuggable. For those we're going to
33.     //   skip the bundling of the JS bundle and the assets. By default is just 'debug'.
34.     //   If you add flavors like lite, prod, etc. you'll have to list your debuggableVariants.
35.     //   Set to empty so debug builds always include the bundled JS (no Metro dependency).
36.     debuggableVariants = []
37. 
38.     /* Bundling */
39.     //   A list containing the node command and its flags. Default is just 'node'.
40.     // nodeExecutableAndArgs = ["node"]
41. 
42.     //
43.     //   The path to the CLI configuration file. Default is empty.
44.     // bundleConfig = file(../rn-cli.config.js)
45.     //
46.     //   The name of the generated asset file containing your JS bundle
47.     // bundleAssetName = "MyApplication.android.bundle"
48.     //
49.     //   The entry file for bundle generation. Default is 'index.android.js' or 'index.js'
50.     // entryFile = file("../js/MyApplication.android.js")
51.     //
52.     //   A list of extra flags to pass to the 'bundle' commands.
53.     //   See https://github.com/react-native-community/cli/blob/main/docs/commands.md#bundle
54.     // extraPackagerArgs = []
55. 
56.     /* Hermes Commands */
57.     //   The hermes compiler command to run. By default it is 'hermesc'
58.     // hermesCommand = "$rootDir/my-custom-hermesc/bin/hermesc"
59.     //
60.     //   The list of flags to pass to the Hermes compiler. By default is "-O", "-output-source-map"
61.     // hermesFlags = ["-O", "-output-source-map"]
62. 
63.     /* Autolinking */
64.     autolinkLibrariesWithApp()
65. }
66. 
67. /**
68.  * Set this to true in release builds to optimize the app using [R8](https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/app-optimization/enable-app-optimization).
69.  */
70. def enableMinifyInReleaseBuilds = (findProperty('android.enableMinifyInReleaseBuilds') ?: false).toBoolean()
71. 
72. /**
73.  * The preferred build flavor of JavaScriptCore (JSC)
74.  *
75.  * For example, to use the international variant, you can use:
76.  * `def jscFlavor = 'org.webkit:android-jsc-intl:+'`
77.  *
78.  * The international variant includes ICU i18n library and necessary data
79.  * allowing to use e.g. `Date.toLocaleString` and `String.localeCompare` that
80.  * give correct results when using with locales other than en-US. Note that
81.  * this variant is about 6MiB larger per architecture than default.
82.  */
83. def jscFlavor = 'io.github.react-native-community:jsc-android:2026004.+'
84. 
85. android {
86.     ndkVersion rootProject.ext.ndkVersion
87. 
88.     buildToolsVersion rootProject.ext.buildToolsVersion
89.     compileSdk rootProject.ext.compileSdkVersion
90. 
91.     namespace "com.earring"
92.     defaultConfig {
93.         applicationId "com.earring"
94.         minSdkVersion rootProject.ext.minSdkVersion
95.         targetSdkVersion rootProject.ext.targetSdkVersion
96.         versionCode 1
97.         versionName "1.0"
98. 
99.         buildConfigField "String", "REACT_NATIVE_RELEASE_LEVEL", "\"${findProperty('reactNativeReleaseLevel') ?: 'stable'}\""
100.     }
101.     signingConfigs {
102.         debug {
103.             storeFile file('debug.keystore')
104.             storePassword 'android'
105.             keyAlias 'androiddebugkey'
106.             keyPassword 'android'
107.         }
108.     }
109.     buildTypes {
110.         debug {
111.             signingConfig signingConfigs.debug
112.         }
113.         release {
114.             // Caution! In production, you need to generate your own keystore file.
115.             // see https://reactnative.dev/docs/signed-apk-android.
116.             signingConfig signingConfigs.debug
117.             def enableShrinkResources = findProperty('android.enableShrinkResourcesInReleaseBuilds') ?: 'false'
118.             shrinkResources enableShrinkResources.toBoolean()
119.             minifyEnabled enableMinifyInReleaseBuilds
120.             proguardFiles getDefaultProguardFile("proguard-android.txt"), "proguard-rules.pro"
121.             def enablePngCrunchInRelease = findProperty('android.enablePngCrunchInReleaseBuilds') ?: 'true'
122.             crunchPngs enablePngCrunchInRelease.toBoolean()
123.         }
124.     }
125.     packagingOptions {
126.         jniLibs {
127.             def enableLegacyPackaging = findProperty('expo.useLegacyPackaging') ?: 'false'
128.             useLegacyPackaging enableLegacyPackaging.toBoolean()
129.         }
130.     }
131.     androidResources {
132.         ignoreAssetsPattern '!.svn:!.git:!.ds_store:!*.scc:!CVS:!thumbs.db:!picasa.ini:!*~'
133.     }
134. }
135. 
136. // Apply static values from `gradle.properties` to the `android.packagingOptions`
137. // Accepts values in comma delimited lists, example:
138. // android.packagingOptions.pickFirsts=/LICENSE,**/picasa.ini
139. ["pickFirsts", "excludes", "merges", "doNotStrip"].each { prop ->
140.     // Split option: 'foo,bar' -> ['foo', 'bar']
141.     def options = (findProperty("android.packagingOptions.$prop") ?: "").split(",");
142.     // Trim all elements in place.
143.     for (i in 0..<options.size()) options[i] = options[i].trim();
144.     // `[] - ""` is essentially `[""].filter(Boolean)` removing all empty strings.
145.     options -= ""
146. 
147.     if (options.length > 0) {
148.         println "android.packagingOptions.$prop += $options ($options.length)"
149.         // Ex: android.packagingOptions.pickFirsts += '**/SCCS/**'
150.         options.each {
151.             android.packagingOptions[prop] += it
152.         }
153.     }
154. }
155. 
156. dependencies {
157.     // The version of react-native is set by the React Native Gradle Plugin
158.     implementation("com.facebook.react:react-android")
159. 
160.     def isGifEnabled = (findProperty('expo.gif.enabled') ?: "") == "true";
161.     def isWebpEnabled = (findProperty('expo.webp.enabled') ?: "") == "true";
162.     def isWebpAnimatedEnabled = (findProperty('expo.webp.animated') ?: "") == "true";
163. 
164.     if (isGifEnabled) {
165.         // For animated gif support
166.         implementation("com.facebook.fresco:animated-gif:${expoLibs.versions.fresco.get()}")
167.     }
168. 
169.     if (isWebpEnabled) {
170.         // For webp support
171.         implementation("com.facebook.fresco:webpsupport:${expoLibs.versions.fresco.get()}")
172.         if (isWebpAnimatedEnabled) {
173.             // Animated webp support
174.             implementation("com.facebook.fresco:animated-webp:${expoLibs.versions.fresco.get()}")
175.         }
176.     }
177. 
178.     if (hermesEnabled.toBoolean()) {
179.         implementation("com.facebook.react:hermes-android")
180.     } else {
181.         implementation jscFlavor
182.     }
183. }
184.
```

```
1. internal import Expo
2. import React
3. import ReactAppDependencyProvider
4. 
5. @main
6. class AppDelegate: ExpoAppDelegate {
7.   var window: UIWindow?
8. 
9.   var reactNativeDelegate: ExpoReactNativeFactoryDelegate?
10.   var reactNativeFactory: RCTReactNativeFactory?
11. 
12.   public override func application(
13.     _ application: UIApplication,
14.     didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]? = nil
15.   ) -> Bool {
16.     let delegate = ReactNativeDelegate()
17.     let factory = ExpoReactNativeFactory(delegate: delegate)
18.     delegate.dependencyProvider = RCTAppDependencyProvider()
19. 
20.     reactNativeDelegate = delegate
21.     reactNativeFactory = factory
22. 
23. #if os(iOS) || os(tvOS)
24.     window = UIWindow(frame: UIScreen.main.bounds)
25.     factory.startReactNative(
26.       withModuleName: "main",
27.       in: window,
28.       launchOptions: launchOptions)
29. #endif
30. 
31.     return super.application(application, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions: launchOptions)
32.   }
33. 
34.   // Linking API
35.   public override func application(
36.     _ app: UIApplication,
37.     open url: URL,
38.     options: [UIApplication.OpenURLOptionsKey: Any] = [:]
39.   ) -> Bool {
40.     return super.application(app, open: url, options: options) || RCTLinkingManager.application(app, open: url, options: options)
41.   }
42. 
43.   // Universal Links
44.   public override func application(
45.     _ application: UIApplication,
46.     continue userActivity: NSUserActivity,
47.     restorationHandler: @escaping ([UIUserActivityRestoring]?) -> Void
48.   ) -> Bool {
49.     let result = RCTLinkingManager.application(application, continue: userActivity, restorationHandler: restorationHandler)
50.     return super.application(application, continue: userActivity, restorationHandler: restorationHandler) || result
51.   }
52. }
53. 
54. class ReactNativeDelegate: ExpoReactNativeFactoryDelegate {
55.   // Extension point for config-plugins
56. 
57.   override func sourceURL(for bridge: RCTBridge) -> URL? {
58.     // needed to return the correct URL for expo-dev-client.
59.     bridge.bundleURL ?? bundleURL()
60.   }
61. 
62.   override func bundleURL() -> URL? {
63. #if DEBUG

65. #else

```
```
1. /**
2.  * EarRingCoreModule — Turbo Native Module (Swift)
3.  *
4.  * Setup instructions:
5.  * 1. Build the Rust library for iOS targets:
6.  *      cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p ear_ring_core
7.  *      cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios-sim --release -p ear_ring_core
8.  *      cargo build --target x86_64-apple-ios --release -p ear_ring_core
9.  *
10.  * 2. Create an XCFramework:
11.  *      xcodebuild -create-xcframework \
12.  *        -library target/aarch64-apple-ios/release/libear_ring_core.a \
13.  *        -headers rust/include/ \
14.  *        -library target/aarch64-apple-ios-sim/release/libear_ring_core.a \
15.  *        -headers rust/include/ \
16.  *        -output ios/EarRingCore.xcframework
17.  *
18.  * 3. Add EarRingCore.xcframework to the Xcode project (drag into Frameworks).
19.  *
20.  * 4. Add this file to the Xcode project and register the module in
21.  *    your AppDelegate:
22.  *      RCTBridge.moduleClasses.append(EarRingCoreModule.self)
23.  *
24.  * The C header (ear_ring_core.h) is generated from lib.rs — run:
25.  *   cbindgen --config cbindgen.toml --crate ear_ring_core --output rust/include/ear_ring_core.h
26.  */
27. 
28. import Foundation
29. import React
30. 
31. @objc(EarRingCore)
32. class EarRingCoreModule: NSObject, RCTBridgeModule {
33. 
34.   static func moduleName() -> String! { "EarRingCore" }
35.   static func requiresMainQueueSetup() -> Bool { false }
36. 
37.   /// Detect pitch in a PCM sample array.
38.   /// - Parameters:
39.   ///   - samples: Array of Float32 values in [-1.0, 1.0]
40.   ///   - sampleRate: Recording sample rate (e.g. 44100)
41.   /// - Returns: Detected frequency in Hz, or -1.0 if not detected
42.   @objc func detectPitch(_ samples: [NSNumber], sampleRate: Int) -> NSNumber {
43.     var floats = samples.map { $0.floatValue }
44.     var outHz: Float = 0
45.     let detected = ear_ring_detect_pitch(&floats, UInt32(floats.count), UInt32(sampleRate), &outHz)
46.     return detected == 1 ? NSNumber(value: outHz) : NSNumber(value: -1.0)
47.   }
48. 
49.   /// Convert frequency to nearest MIDI note and cents deviation.
50.   @objc func freqToNote(_ hz: Double, resolver resolve: RCTPromiseResolveBlock, rejecter reject: RCTPromiseRejectBlock) {
51.     var outMidi: UInt8 = 0
52.     var outCents: Int32 = 0
53.     let ok = ear_ring_freq_to_note(Float(hz), &outMidi, &outCents)
54.     if ok == 1 {
55.       resolve(["midi": outMidi, "cents": outCents])
56.     } else {
57.       resolve(nil)
58.     }
59.   }
60. 
61.   /// Get staff position for a MIDI note (diatonic steps above C4).
62.   @objc func staffPosition(_ midi: Int) -> NSNumber {
63.     return NSNumber(value: ear_ring_staff_position(UInt8(midi)))
64.   }
65. 
66.   /// Generate a sequence of MIDI note numbers.
67.   @objc func generateSequence(_ rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Double) -> [NSNumber] {
68.     var buf = [UInt8](repeating: 0, count: length)
69.     let count = ear_ring_generate_sequence(UInt8(rootMidi), UInt8(scaleId), UInt8(length), UInt64(seed), &buf)
70.     guard count >= 0 else { return [] }
71.     return buf.prefix(Int(count)).map { NSNumber(value: $0) }
72.   }
73. }
74.
```

</details>

```
1. require File.join(File.dirname(`node --print "require.resolve('expo/package.json')"`), "scripts/autolinking")
2. require File.join(File.dirname(`node --print "require.resolve('react-native/package.json')"`), "scripts/react_native_pods")
3. 
4. require 'json'
5. podfile_properties = JSON.parse(File.read(File.join(__dir__, 'Podfile.properties.json'))) rescue {}
6. 
7. def ccache_enabled?(podfile_properties)
8.   # Environment variable takes precedence
9.   return ENV['USE_CCACHE'] == '1' if ENV['USE_CCACHE']
10. 
11.   # Fall back to Podfile properties
12.   podfile_properties['apple.ccacheEnabled'] == 'true'
13. end
14. 
15. ENV['EX_DEV_CLIENT_NETWORK_INSPECTOR'] ||= podfile_properties['EX_DEV_CLIENT_NETWORK_INSPECTOR']
16. ENV['RCT_USE_RN_DEP'] ||= '1' if podfile_properties['ios.buildReactNativeFromSource'] != 'true'
17. ENV['RCT_USE_PREBUILT_RNCORE'] ||= '1' if podfile_properties['ios.buildReactNativeFromSource'] != 'true'
18. ENV['RCT_HERMES_V1_ENABLED'] ||= '1' if podfile_properties['expo.useHermesV1'] == 'true'
19. platform :ios, podfile_properties['ios.deploymentTarget'] || '15.1'
20. 
21. prepare_react_native_project!
22. 
23. target 'earring' do
24.   use_expo_modules!
25. 
26.   if ENV['EXPO_USE_COMMUNITY_AUTOLINKING'] == '1'
27.     config_command = ['node', '-e', "process.argv=['', '', 'config'];require('@react-native-community/cli').run()"];
28.   else
29.     config_command = [
30.       'node',
31.       '--no-warnings',
32.       '--eval',
33.       'require(\'expo/bin/autolinking\')',
34.       'expo-modules-autolinking',
35.       'react-native-config',
36.       '--json',
37.       '--platform',
38.       'ios'
39.     ]
40.   end
41. 
42.   config = use_native_modules!(config_command)
43. 
44.   use_frameworks! :linkage => podfile_properties['ios.useFrameworks'].to_sym if podfile_properties['ios.useFrameworks']
45.   use_frameworks! :linkage => ENV['USE_FRAMEWORKS'].to_sym if ENV['USE_FRAMEWORKS']
46. 
47.   use_react_native!(
48.     :path => config[:reactNativePath],
49.     :hermes_enabled => podfile_properties['expo.jsEngine'] == nil || podfile_properties['expo.jsEngine'] == 'hermes',
50.     # An absolute path to your application root.
51.     :app_path => "#{Pod::Config.instance.installation_root}/..",
52.     :privacy_file_aggregation_enabled => podfile_properties['apple.privacyManifestAggregationEnabled'] != 'false',
53.   )
54. 
55.   post_install do |installer|
56.     react_native_post_install(
57.       installer,
58.       config[:reactNativePath],
59.       :mac_catalyst_enabled => false,
60.       :ccache_enabled => ccache_enabled?(podfile_properties),
61.     )
62.   end
63. end
64.
```
