<details>
<summary>221 lines</summary>

```
1. pub mod music_theory;
2. pub mod pitch_detection;
3. 
4. pub use music_theory::{
5.     freq_to_note, generate_sequence, midi_to_freq, scale_notes, staff_position, Note, NoteName,
6.     ScaleType,
7. };
8. pub use pitch_detection::detect_pitch;
9. 
10. // ── C-compatible FFI surface ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
11. // These thin wrappers are called from the React Native Turbo Native Module
12. // (Swift / Kotlin) without requiring uniffi code-gen at this stage.
13. 
14. use std::os::raw::{c_float, c_int, c_uchar, c_uint};
15. 
16. /// Detect pitch from a raw f32 PCM buffer.
17. ///
18. /// * `samples`       – pointer to f32 samples
19. /// * `num_samples`   – number of samples
20. /// * `sample_rate`   – sample rate in Hz
21. /// * `out_hz`        – out-parameter: detected frequency, 0.0 if not detected
22. ///
23. /// Returns 1 if a pitch was detected, 0 otherwise.
24. #[no_mangle]
25. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_detect_pitch(
26.     samples: *const c_float,
27.     num_samples: c_uint,
28.     sample_rate: c_uint,
29.     out_hz: *mut c_float,
30. ) -> c_int {
31.     if samples.is_null() || out_hz.is_null() {
32.         return 0;
33.     }
34.     let slice = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts(samples, num_samples as usize) };
35.     match detect_pitch(slice, sample_rate) {
36.         Some(hz) => {
37.             unsafe { *out_hz = hz };
38.             1
39.         }
40.         None => {
41.             unsafe { *out_hz = 0.0 };
42.             0
43.         }
44.     }
45. }
46. 
47. /// Convert a frequency to MIDI note number and cents deviation.
48. ///
49. /// * `hz`        – frequency in Hz
50. /// * `out_midi`  – out-parameter: MIDI note number (0–127)
51. /// * `out_cents` – out-parameter: deviation in cents (-50 to +50)
52. ///
53. /// Returns 1 on success, 0 if frequency is out of range.
54. #[no_mangle]
55. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_freq_to_note(
56.     hz: c_float,
57.     out_midi: *mut c_uchar,
58.     out_cents: *mut c_int,
59. ) -> c_int {
60.     if out_midi.is_null() || out_cents.is_null() {
61.         return 0;
62.     }
63.     match freq_to_note(hz) {
64.         Some((note, cents)) => {
65.             unsafe {
66.                 *out_midi = note.midi();
67.                 *out_cents = cents;
68.             }
69.             1
70.         }
71.         None => 0,
72.     }
73. }
74. 
75. /// Get the staff position for a MIDI note number.
76. ///
77. /// Returns diatonic steps above middle C (C4 = 0, D4 = 1, C5 = 7, B3 = -1, …).
78. #[no_mangle]
79. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_staff_position(midi: c_uchar) -> c_int {
80.     staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi))
81. }
82. 
83. /// Generate a sequence of MIDI note numbers.
84. ///
85. /// * `root_midi`   – MIDI number of the root note
86. /// * `scale_id`    – 0=Major, 1=NaturalMinor, 2=HarmonicMinor, 3=PentatonicMajor,
87. ///                   4=PentatonicMinor, 5=Dorian, 6=Mixolydian, 7=Blues
88. /// * `length`      – number of notes to generate
89. /// * `seed`        – random seed for reproducibility
90. /// * `out_buf`     – caller-allocated buffer of at least `length` bytes
91. ///
92. /// Returns the number of notes written, or -1 on error.
93. #[no_mangle]
94. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_generate_sequence(
95.     root_midi: c_uchar,
96.     scale_id: c_uchar,
97.     length: c_uchar,
98.     seed: u64,
99.     out_buf: *mut c_uchar,
100. ) -> c_int {
101.     if out_buf.is_null() {
102.         return -1;
103.     }
104.     let scale = match scale_id {
105.         0 => ScaleType::Major,
106.         1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
107.         2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
108.         3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
109.         4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
110.         5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
111.         6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
112.         7 => ScaleType::Blues,
113.         _ => return -1,
114.     };
115.     let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi);
116.     let notes = generate_sequence(root, scale, length, seed);
117.     let out = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(out_buf, length as usize) };
118.     for (i, note) in notes.iter().enumerate() {
119.         out[i] = note.midi();
120.     }
121.     notes.len() as c_int
122. }
123. 
124. // ── Android JNI exports ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125. // Exported with the exact symbol names the Kotlin EarRingCoreModule expects.
126. #[cfg(target_os = "android")]
127. mod android_jni {
128.     use jni::objects::{JClass, JFloatArray, JIntArray};
129.     use jni::sys::{jfloat, jfloatArray, jint, jintArray, jlong};
130.     use jni::JNIEnv;
131. 
132.     use super::{detect_pitch, freq_to_note, generate_sequence, staff_position, Note, ScaleType};
133. 
134.     #[no_mangle]
135.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeDetectPitch(
136.         mut env: JNIEnv,
137.         _class: JClass,
138.         samples: jfloatArray,
139.         sample_rate: jint,
140.     ) -> jfloat {
141.         let arr = unsafe { JFloatArray::from_raw(samples) };
142.         let len = match env.get_array_length(&arr) {
143.             Ok(l) => l as usize,
144.             Err(_) => return -1.0,
145.         };
146.         let mut buf = vec![0f32; len];
147.         if env.get_float_array_region(&arr, 0, &mut buf).is_err() {
148.             return -1.0;
149.         }
150.         match detect_pitch(&buf, sample_rate as u32) {
151.             Some(hz) => hz,
152.             None => -1.0,
153.         }
154.     }
155. 
156.     #[no_mangle]
157.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeFreqToMidi(
158.         _env: JNIEnv,
159.         _class: JClass,
160.         hz: jfloat,
161.     ) -> jint {
162.         match freq_to_note(hz) {
163.             Some((note, _)) => note.midi() as jint,
164.             None => -1,
165.         }
166.     }
167. 
168.     #[no_mangle]
169.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeFreqToCents(
170.         _env: JNIEnv,
171.         _class: JClass,
172.         hz: jfloat,
173.     ) -> jint {
174.         match freq_to_note(hz) {
175.             Some((_, cents)) => cents as jint,
176.             None => 0,
177.         }
178.     }
179. 
180.     #[no_mangle]
181.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeStaffPosition(
182.         _env: JNIEnv,
183.         _class: JClass,
184.         midi: jint,
185.     ) -> jint {
186.         staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi as u8)) as jint
187.     }
188. 
189.     #[no_mangle]
190.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeGenerateSequence(
191.         mut env: JNIEnv,
192.         _class: JClass,
193.         root_midi: jint,
194.         scale_id: jint,
195.         length: jint,
196.         seed: jlong,
197.     ) -> jintArray {
198.         let scale = match scale_id {
199.             0 => ScaleType::Major,
200.             1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
201.             2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
202.             3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
203.             4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
204.             5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
205.             6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
206.             7 => ScaleType::Blues,
207.             _ => ScaleType::Major,
208.         };
209.         let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi as u8);
210.         let notes = generate_sequence(root, scale, length as u8, seed as u64);
211.         let midi_vals: Vec<jint> = notes.iter().map(|n| n.midi() as jint).collect();
212. 
213.         let arr: JIntArray = match env.new_int_array(midi_vals.len() as i32) {
214.             Ok(a) => a,
215.             Err(_) => return std::ptr::null_mut(),
216.         };
217.         let _ = env.set_int_array_region(&arr, 0, &midi_vals);
218.         arr.into_raw()
219.     }
220. }
221.
```

```
1. /// YIN pitch detection algorithm.
2. ///
3. /// Reference: de Cheveigné & Kawahara (2002), "YIN, a fundamental frequency
4. /// estimator for speech and music", JASA 111(4).
5. ///
6. /// Input: mono f32 PCM samples at `sample_rate` Hz.
7. /// Output: fundamental frequency in Hz, or `None` if not detected / low confidence.
8. 
9. const YIN_THRESHOLD: f32 = 0.15;
10. 
11. /// Detect the fundamental frequency of a monophonic signal.
12. ///
13. /// * `samples`     – slice of f32 PCM samples in the range [-1.0, 1.0]
14. /// * `sample_rate` – recording sample rate in Hz (e.g. 44100)
15. ///
16. /// Returns `Some(hz)` when a confident pitch is found, `None` otherwise.
17. pub fn detect_pitch(samples: &[f32], sample_rate: u32) -> Option<f32> {
18.     let n = samples.len();
19.     if n < 2 {
20.         return None;
21.     }
22.     // Maximum lag to check: lowest detectable pitch ~= sample_rate / max_lag.
23.     // We cap at 20 Hz (infrasound), so max_lag = sample_rate / 20.
24.     let max_lag = (sample_rate / 20).min(n as u32 / 2) as usize;
25.     // Minimum lag for ~1200 Hz upper bound.
26.     let min_lag = (sample_rate / 1200).max(2) as usize;
27. 
28.     if max_lag <= min_lag {
29.         return None;
30.     }
31. 
32.     // Step 1 & 2: difference function d(τ)
33.     let mut diff = vec![0.0f32; max_lag + 1];
34.     for tau in 1..=max_lag {
35.         let mut sum = 0.0f32;
36.         for j in 0..(n - tau) {
37.             let delta = samples[j] - samples[j + tau];
38.             sum += delta * delta;
39.         }
40.         diff[tau] = sum;
41.     }
42. 
43.     // Step 3: cumulative mean normalised difference function (CMNDF)
44.     let mut cmndf = vec![0.0f32; max_lag + 1];
45.     cmndf[0] = 1.0;
46.     let mut running_sum = 0.0f32;
47.     for tau in 1..=max_lag {
48.         running_sum += diff[tau];
49.         if running_sum.abs() < f32::EPSILON {
50.             cmndf[tau] = 1.0;
51.         } else {
52.             cmndf[tau] = diff[tau] * tau as f32 / running_sum;
53.         }
54.     }
55. 
56.     // Step 4: absolute threshold — find first local minimum below threshold.
57.     let mut tau_opt: Option<usize> = None;
58.     let mut tau = min_lag;
59.     while tau <= max_lag {
60.         if cmndf[tau] < YIN_THRESHOLD {
61.             // Find the local minimum within this dip.
62.             while tau + 1 <= max_lag && cmndf[tau + 1] < cmndf[tau] {
63.                 tau += 1;
64.             }
65.             tau_opt = Some(tau);
66.             break;
67.         }
68.         tau += 1;
69.     }
70. 
71.     // Fallback: pick global minimum if nothing below threshold.
72.     let tau_opt = tau_opt.unwrap_or_else(|| {
73.         (min_lag..=max_lag)
74.             .min_by(|&a, &b| cmndf[a].partial_cmp(&cmndf[b]).unwrap())
75.             .unwrap_or(min_lag)
76.     });
77. 
78.     if cmndf[tau_opt] > 0.5 {
79.         // Very low confidence — bail out.
80.         return None;
81.     }
82. 
83.     // Step 5: parabolic interpolation for sub-sample accuracy.
84.     let tau_f = parabolic_interpolation(&cmndf, tau_opt);
85. 
86.     if tau_f <= 0.0 {
87.         return None;
88.     }
89. 
90.     Some(sample_rate as f32 / tau_f)
91. }
92. 
93. /// Parabolic interpolation around the minimum at index `tau`.
94. fn parabolic_interpolation(cmndf: &[f32], tau: usize) -> f32 {
95.     if tau == 0 || tau >= cmndf.len() - 1 {
96.         return tau as f32;
97.     }
98.     let y0 = cmndf[tau - 1];
99.     let y1 = cmndf[tau];
100.     let y2 = cmndf[tau + 1];
101.     let denom = 2.0 * (2.0 * y1 - y2 - y0);
102.     if denom.abs() < f32::EPSILON {
103.         return tau as f32;
104.     }
105.     tau as f32 + (y2 - y0) / denom
106. }
107. 
108. // ── Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109. 
110. #[cfg(test)]
111. mod tests {
112.     use super::*;
113.     use std::f32::consts::PI;
114. 
115.     fn sine_wave(freq: f32, sample_rate: u32, num_samples: usize) -> Vec<f32> {
116.         (0..num_samples)
117.             .map(|i| (2.0 * PI * freq * i as f32 / sample_rate as f32).sin())
118.             .collect()
119.     }
120. 
121.     #[test]
122.     fn test_detect_a4() {
123.         let samples = sine_wave(440.0, 44100, 4096);
124.         let hz = detect_pitch(&samples, 44100).expect("Should detect A4");
125.         assert!(
126.             (hz - 440.0).abs() < 5.0,
127.             "Expected ~440 Hz, got {hz:.1} Hz"
128.         );
129.     }
130. 
131.     #[test]
132.     fn test_detect_c4() {
133.         let samples = sine_wave(261.63, 44100, 4096);
134.         let hz = detect_pitch(&samples, 44100).expect("Should detect C4");
135.         assert!((hz - 261.63).abs() < 5.0, "Expected ~261.6 Hz, got {hz:.1} Hz");
136.     }
137. 
138.     #[test]
139.     fn test_silence_returns_none() {
140.         let samples = vec![0.0f32; 4096];
141.         assert!(detect_pitch(&samples, 44100).is_none());
142.     }
143. }
144.
```

```
1. use std::fmt;
2. 
3. // ── Note ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4. 
5. /// Chromatic note names (12 pitch classes, using sharps).
6. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
7. pub enum NoteName {
8.     C,
9.     Cs, // C#
10.     D,
11.     Ds, // D#
12.     E,
13.     F,
14.     Fs, // F#
15.     G,
16.     Gs, // G#
17.     A,
18.     As, // A#
19.     B,
20. }
21. 
22. impl NoteName {
23.     /// All 12 pitch classes in chromatic order.
24.     pub const ALL: [NoteName; 12] = [
25.         NoteName::C,
26.         NoteName::Cs,
27.         NoteName::D,
28.         NoteName::Ds,
29.         NoteName::E,
30.         NoteName::F,
31.         NoteName::Fs,
32.         NoteName::G,
33.         NoteName::Gs,
34.         NoteName::A,
35.         NoteName::As,
36.         NoteName::B,
37.     ];
38. 
39.     /// Chromatic index 0–11 (C = 0, C# = 1, …, B = 11).
40.     pub fn chroma(self) -> u8 {
41.         self as u8
42.     }
43. 
44.     /// Display name suitable for UI labels.
45.     pub fn display_name(self) -> &'static str {
46.         match self {
47.             NoteName::C => "C",
48.             NoteName::Cs => "C#",
49.             NoteName::D => "D",
50.             NoteName::Ds => "D#",
51.             NoteName::E => "E",
52.             NoteName::F => "F",
53.             NoteName::Fs => "F#",
54.             NoteName::G => "G",
55.             NoteName::Gs => "G#",
56.             NoteName::A => "A",
57.             NoteName::As => "A#",
58.             NoteName::B => "B",
59.         }
60.     }
61. 
62.     /// From chromatic index 0–11.
63.     pub fn from_chroma(c: u8) -> Self {
64.         NoteName::ALL[(c % 12) as usize]
65.     }
66. }
67. 
68. impl fmt::Display for NoteName {
69.     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
70.         write!(f, "{}", self.display_name())
71.     }
72. }
73. 
74. // ── Note (name + octave) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75. 
76. /// A specific note: pitch class + octave (C4 = middle C, MIDI 60).
77. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
78. pub struct Note {
79.     pub name: NoteName,
80.     pub octave: i8,
81. }
82. 
83. impl Note {
84.     pub fn new(name: NoteName, octave: i8) -> Self {
85.         Self { name, octave }
86.     }
87. 
88.     /// MIDI number: C4 = 60, A4 = 69, etc.
89.     pub fn midi(self) -> u8 {
90.         let semitone = (self.octave as i16 + 1) * 12 + self.name.chroma() as i16;
91.         semitone.clamp(0, 127) as u8
92.     }
93. 
94.     /// Frequency in Hz: A4 = 440 Hz.
95.     pub fn frequency(self) -> f32 {
96.         midi_to_freq(self.midi())
97.     }
98. 
99.     /// Build from MIDI number.
100.     pub fn from_midi(midi: u8) -> Self {
101.         let chroma = midi % 12;
102.         let octave = (midi as i8 / 12) - 1;
103.         Note {
104.             name: NoteName::from_chroma(chroma),
105.             octave,
106.         }
107.     }
108. }
109. 
110. impl fmt::Display for Note {
111.     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
112.         write!(f, "{}{}", self.name, self.octave)
113.     }
114. }
115. 
116. // ── Frequency ↔ MIDI conversions ──────────────────────────────────────────────
117. 
118. /// Convert MIDI note number to frequency in Hz (A4 = 440 Hz, MIDI 69).
119. pub fn midi_to_freq(midi: u8) -> f32 {
120.     440.0 * 2.0_f32.powf((midi as f32 - 69.0) / 12.0)
121. }
122. 
123. /// Convert frequency to the nearest note and the deviation in cents.
124. /// Returns `None` if the frequency is outside the audible/MIDI range.
125. pub fn freq_to_note(hz: f32) -> Option<(Note, i32)> {
126.     if hz <= 0.0 {
127.         return None;
128.     }
129.     // MIDI float
130.     let midi_f = 69.0 + 12.0 * (hz / 440.0).log2();
131.     if !(0.0..=127.0).contains(&midi_f) {
132.         return None;
133.     }
134.     let midi = midi_f.round() as u8;
135.     let cents = ((midi_f - midi as f32) * 100.0).round() as i32;
136.     Some((Note::from_midi(midi), cents))
137. }
138. 
139. // ── Scale ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
140. 
141. /// Available scale types.
142. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
143. pub enum ScaleType {
144.     Major,
145.     NaturalMinor,
146.     HarmonicMinor,
147.     PentatonicMajor,
148.     PentatonicMinor,
149.     Dorian,
150.     Mixolydian,
151.     Blues,
152. }
153. 
154. impl ScaleType {
155.     /// Interval pattern in semitones (relative to root, not including octave).
156.     pub fn intervals(self) -> &'static [u8] {
157.         match self {
158.             ScaleType::Major => &[0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11],
159.             ScaleType::NaturalMinor => &[0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 10],
160.             ScaleType::HarmonicMinor => &[0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 11],
161.             ScaleType::PentatonicMajor => &[0, 2, 4, 7, 9],
162.             ScaleType::PentatonicMinor => &[0, 3, 5, 7, 10],
163.             ScaleType::Dorian => &[0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10],
164.             ScaleType::Mixolydian => &[0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10],
165.             ScaleType::Blues => &[0, 3, 5, 6, 7, 10],
166.         }
167.     }
168. 
169.     pub fn display_name(self) -> &'static str {
170.         match self {
171.             ScaleType::Major => "Major",
172.             ScaleType::NaturalMinor => "Natural Minor",
173.             ScaleType::HarmonicMinor => "Harmonic Minor",
174.             ScaleType::PentatonicMajor => "Pentatonic Major",
175.             ScaleType::PentatonicMinor => "Pentatonic Minor",
176.             ScaleType::Dorian => "Dorian",
177.             ScaleType::Mixolydian => "Mixolydian",
178.             ScaleType::Blues => "Blues",
179.         }
180.     }
181. }
182. 
183. // ── Scale generation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184. 
185. /// Generate scale notes starting from `root` (e.g. C4) for one octave.
186. /// Returns the notes in ascending order (root included, octave excluded).
187. pub fn scale_notes(root: Note, scale: ScaleType) -> Vec<Note> {
188.     let root_midi = root.midi();
189.     scale
190.         .intervals()
191.         .iter()
192.         .map(|&interval| Note::from_midi(root_midi + interval))
193.         .collect()
194. }
195. 
196. /// Generate a random sequence of `length` notes drawn from the given scale.
197. /// Uses a simple LCG seeded by the provided `seed` for reproducibility.
198. pub fn generate_sequence(root: Note, scale: ScaleType, length: u8, seed: u64) -> Vec<Note> {
199.     let notes = scale_notes(root, scale);
200.     let n = notes.len() as u64;
201.     let mut rng = seed;
202.     (0..length)
203.         .map(|_| {
204.             // LCG parameters (Knuth)
205.             rng = rng.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);
206.             let idx = ((rng >> 33) % n) as usize;
207.             notes[idx]
208.         })
209.         .collect()
210. }
211. 
212. // ── Staff position ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
213. 
214. /// Treble-clef staff position for a note.
215. /// Returns the number of staff steps above middle C (C4).
216. /// Each step = one line or one space. Positive = higher, negative = lower.
217. /// Used by the UI to place note heads on the staff.
218. pub fn staff_position(note: Note) -> i32 {
219.     // Diatonic step offset within an octave for each pitch class (C=0, D=1, E=2, …)
220.     let diatonic = match note.name {
221.         NoteName::C => 0,
222.         NoteName::Cs => 0,
223.         NoteName::D => 1,
224.         NoteName::Ds => 1,
225.         NoteName::E => 2,
226.         NoteName::F => 3,
227.         NoteName::Fs => 3,
228.         NoteName::G => 4,
229.         NoteName::Gs => 4,
230.         NoteName::A => 5,
231.         NoteName::As => 5,
232.         NoteName::B => 6,
233.     };
234.     // Middle C (C4) is at staff position 0
235.     let octave_offset = (note.octave as i32 - 4) * 7;
236.     octave_offset + diatonic
237. }
238. 
239. // ── Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
240. 
241. #[cfg(test)]
242. mod tests {
243.     use super::*;
244. 
245.     #[test]
246.     fn test_midi_freq_round_trip() {
247.         let a4 = Note::new(NoteName::A, 4);
248.         assert_eq!(a4.midi(), 69);
249.         let freq = a4.frequency();
250.         assert!((freq - 440.0).abs() < 0.01, "A4 should be ~440Hz, got {freq}");
251.     }
252. 
253.     #[test]
254.     fn test_freq_to_note() {
255.         let (note, cents) = freq_to_note(440.0).unwrap();
256.         assert_eq!(note.name, NoteName::A);
257.         assert_eq!(note.octave, 4);
258.         assert_eq!(cents, 0);
259.     }
260. 
261.     #[test]
262.     fn test_middle_c() {
263.         let c4 = Note::new(NoteName::C, 4);
264.         assert_eq!(c4.midi(), 60);
265.         assert!((c4.frequency() - 261.63).abs() < 0.1);
266.     }
267. 
268.     #[test]
269.     fn test_major_scale_c4() {
270.         let root = Note::new(NoteName::C, 4);
271.         let notes = scale_notes(root, ScaleType::Major);
272.         let names: Vec<_> = notes.iter().map(|n| n.name).collect();
273.         assert_eq!(
274.             names,
275.             vec![
276.                 NoteName::C,
277.                 NoteName::D,
278.                 NoteName::E,
279.                 NoteName::F,
280.                 NoteName::G,
281.                 NoteName::A,
282.                 NoteName::B,
283.             ]
284.         );
285.     }
286. 
287.     #[test]
288.     fn test_generate_sequence_length() {
289.         let root = Note::new(NoteName::C, 4);
290.         let seq = generate_sequence(root, ScaleType::Major, 5, 42);
291.         assert_eq!(seq.len(), 5);
292.     }
293. 
294.     #[test]
295.     fn test_staff_position() {
296.         assert_eq!(staff_position(Note::new(NoteName::C, 4)), 0); // middle C
297.         assert_eq!(staff_position(Note::new(NoteName::D, 4)), 1);
298.         assert_eq!(staff_position(Note::new(NoteName::C, 5)), 7);
299.         assert_eq!(staff_position(Note::new(NoteName::B, 3)), -1);
300.     }
301. }
302.
```

## Complete Native Infrastructure Overview

I've explored the entire native codebase. Here's the complete picture:

---

## **1. ANDROID INFRASTRUCTURE** (`C:\work\ear_ring/android/`)

### **Kotlin Native Modules** (`android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/`)

#### **MainActivity.kt**
- React Native activity with Expo support
- Handles splash screen (AppTheme before onCreate)
- Uses DefaultReactActivityDelegate with Fabric New Architecture support
- Implements back button handling (Android S compatibility)
- Main component name: "main"

#### **MainApplication.kt**
- Extends `Application` and implements `ReactApplication`
- Uses `ExpoReactHostFactory` for React initialization
- Registers custom packages: `AudioCapturePackage()` and `EarRingCorePackage()`
- Application lifecycle dispatcher for Expo modules

#### **EarRingCorePackage.kt & EarRingCoreModule.kt**
**Purpose:** JNI bridge to Rust core library

**Exported Native Methods:**
- `detectPitch(samples: FloatArray, sampleRate: Int): Float` — detects pitch using YIN algorithm
- `freqToNote(hz: Double): WritableMap?` — returns `{midi, cents}` for a frequency
- `staffPosition(midi: Int): Int` — diatonic staff position above C4
- `generateSequence(rootMidi, scaleId, length, seed): IntArray` — random scale sequence

**JNI Bridge:** Loads `libear_ring_core.so` native library
```kotlin
System.loadLibrary("ear_ring_core")
```

**External JNI Declarations:**
```kotlin
@JvmStatic external fun nativeDetectPitch(samples: FloatArray, sampleRate: Int): Float
@JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToMidi(hz: Float): Int
@JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToCents(hz: Float): Int
@JvmStatic external fun nativeStaffPosition(midi: Int): Int
@JvmStatic external fun nativeGenerateSequence(rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Long): IntArray
```

#### **AudioCapturePackage.kt & AudioCaptureModule.kt**
**Purpose:** Real-time PCM audio streaming from microphone to JS

**Features:**
- **Events:** `onAudioData` — emitted on each audio frame capture
- **API:**
  - `startRecording(sampleRate: Int, bufferSize: Int, promise: Promise)` — starts async recording thread
  - `stopRecording(promise: Promise)` — stops recording
- **Audio Format:** PCM 16-bit mono, converted to Float32 [-1.0, 1.0]
- **Delivery:** Base64-encoded little-endian Float32 bytes
- **Event Structure:** `{data: string (b64), sampleRate: int, count: int}`

**Permissions Required:**
- `RECORD_AUDIO`
- `MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS`

### **Android Manifest** (`android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`)
```xml
Permissions:
- android.permission.INTERNET
- android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO
- android.permission.MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS
- android.permission.SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW (optional)
- android.permission.VIBRATE (optional)

App Name: ear_ring
Package: com.earring
Minimum Theme: AppTheme
Splash Screen: Theme.App.SplashScreen
```

### **Build Configuration**

**gradle.properties:**
- JVM Memory: `-Xmx2048m -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=512m`
- Parallel builds enabled
- AndroidX support enabled
- **Architecture targets:** `armeabi-v7a, arm64-v8a, x86, x86_64`
- **New Architecture (Fabric/TurboModules):** Enabled
- **JS Engine:** Hermes enabled
- **Edge-to-edge display:** Enabled
- GIF/WebP support: Enabled

**app/build.gradle:**
- Min SDK: (from rootProject.ext.minSdkVersion)
- Target SDK: (from rootProject.ext.targetSdkVersion)
- Compile SDK: (from rootProject.ext.compileSdkVersion)
- App ID: `com.earring`
- Version: 1.0 (code: 1)
- JSC flavor: `org.webkit:android-jsc-intl:+`
- Uses Expo CLI for bundling (Metro config)

**settings.gradle:**
- Plugin management via Expo and React Native gradle plugins
- Autolink libraries from Expo/React Native
- Project name: `earring`

---

## **2. iOS INFRASTRUCTURE** (`C:\work\ear_ring/ios/`)

### **Swift Native Module** (`ios/earring/EarRingCoreModule.swift`)

**Exported Methods:**
- `detectPitch(_ samples: [NSNumber], sampleRate: Int) -\> NSNumber` — sync detection
- `freqToNote(_ hz: Double, resolver, rejecter)` — async promise-based, returns `{midi, cents}`
- `staffPosition(_ midi: Int) -\> NSNumber` — sync staff position
- `generateSequence(_ rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Double) -\> [NSNumber]` — sync

**C FFI Integration:**
- Direct calls to Rust C functions: `ear_ring_detect_pitch`, `ear_ring_freq_to_note`, `ear_ring_staff_position`, `ear_ring_generate_sequence`
- Module name: "EarRingCore"
- Does NOT require main queue setup

**Build Instructions (in comments):**
```bash
# Build for all targets
cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p ear_ring_core
cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios-sim --release -p ear_ring_core
cargo build --target x86_64-apple-ios --release -p ear_ring_core

# Create XCFramework
xcodebuild -create-xcframework \
  -library target/aarch64-apple-ios/release/libear_ring_core.a \
  -headers rust/include/ \
  -library target/aarch64-apple-ios-sim/release/libear_ring_core.a \
  -headers rust/include/ \
  -output ios/EarRingCore.xcframework
```

### **AppDelegate.swift**
- Extends `ExpoAppDelegate`
- Creates `ExpoReactNativeFactory` with `ReactNativeDelegate`
- Starts React Native with module name "main"
- Implements Linking API (URL handling)
- Implements Universal Links support

**ReactNativeDelegate:**
- Returns bundle URL for Expo dev client (DEBUG) or main.jsbundle (RELEASE)
- Source URL resolution for bridge

### **Info.plist**
```

Bundle Identifier: $(PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER)
Supported Orientations: Portrait, LandscapeLeft, LandscapeRight
Status Bar: Default style
ATS: Allows local networking, no arbitrary loads
Splash Screen: SplashScreen.storyboard
```

### **Podfile**
- Platform: iOS 15.1 (from properties)
- Uses Expo modules: `use_expo_modules!`
- React Native with Hermes enabled
- Network inspector enabled (dev)
- Uses frameworks with specified linkage
- Post-install: React Native pod setup with ccache support

### **Podfile.properties.json**

```json
{
  "expo.jsEngine": "hermes",
  "EX_DEV_CLIENT_NETWORK_INSPECTOR": "true"
}
```

### **Bridging Header** (`earring-Bridging-Header.h`)
- Empty (no Objective-C imports currently exposed to Swift)

## **3. RUST CORE LIBRARY** (`C:\work\ear_ring/rust/`)

### **Cargo.toml**
```toml
Package: ear_ring_core
Version: 0.1.0
Edition: 2021

Crate Types: cdylib, staticlib, rlib
  - cdylib: Shared library for NDK (Android)
  - staticlib: Static library for iOS
  - rlib: Rust library

Android Dependencies: jni 0.21 (no default features)
```

### **cbindgen.toml**
- Auto-generates C header from Rust code
- Header guard: `EAR_RING_CORE_H`
- Exported functions:
  - `ear_ring_detect_pitch`
  - `ear_ring_freq_to_note`
  - `ear_ring_staff_position`
  - `ear_ring_generate_sequence`

---

### **lib.rs** — C FFI Surface + JNI Bridge

#### **C FFI Functions** (callable from Swift/Kotlin)

1. **`ear_ring_detect_pitch(samples: *const f32, num_samples: u32, sample_rate: u32, out_hz: *mut f32) -\> i32`**
   - Detects pitch from PCM samples using YIN algorithm
   - Returns: 1 (detected) or 0 (not detected)
   - Out-parameter: `out_hz` contains detected frequency

2. **`ear_ring_freq_to_note(hz: f32, out_midi: *mut u8, out_cents: *mut i32) -\> i32`**
   - Converts frequency to MIDI note + cents deviation
   - MIDI range: 0–127, Cents: ±50
   - Returns: 1 (success) or 0 (out of range)

3. **`ear_ring_staff_position(midi: u8) -\> i32`**
   - Returns diatonic steps above C4 (staff position)
   - C4 = 0, D4 = 1, C5 = 7, B3 = -1, etc.

4. **`ear_ring_generate_sequence(root_midi: u8, scale_id: u8, length: u8, seed: u64, out_buf: *mut u8) -\> i32`**
   - Generates random MIDI note sequence from scale
   - Scale IDs: 0=Major, 1=NaturalMinor, 2=HarmonicMinor, 3=PentatonicMajor, 4=PentatonicMinor, 5=Dorian, 6=Mixolydian, 7=Blues
   - Returns: number of notes written (or -1 on error)

#### **Android JNI Exports** (`#[cfg(target_os = "android")]`)

Maps Kotlin external functions directly:
- `Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeDetectPitch`
- `Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeFreqToMidi`
- `Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeFreqToCents`
- `Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeStaffPosition`
- `Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeGenerateSequence`

Uses `jni` crate for array marshaling and JNI environment interaction.

---

### **pitch_detection.rs** — YIN Algorithm

**Implements:** `pub fn detect_pitch(samples: &[f32], sample_rate: u32) -\> Option\<f32\>`

**Algorithm:**
1. Computes difference function d(τ) — sum of squared differences at lag τ
2. Cumulative mean normalized difference function (CMNDF)
3. Absolute threshold search (YIN_THRESHOLD = 0.15)
4. Parabolic interpolation for sub-sample accuracy
5. Confidence check: rejects if CMNDF \> 0.5

**Lag Bounds:**
- Min lag: sample_rate / 1200 (~37 samples @ 44.1kHz for upper ~1200 Hz)
- Max lag: sample_rate / 20 (~2205 samples @ 44.1kHz for lower ~20 Hz)

**Output:** Frequency in Hz, or None if not detected

**Tests:**
- Detects A4 (440 Hz) within ±5 Hz
- Detects C4 (261.63 Hz) within ±5 Hz
- Returns None for silence

---

### **music_theory.rs** — Music Fundamentals

#### **NoteName Enum**
12 pitch classes (C, C#, D, D#, E, F, F#, G, G#, A, A#, B)
- `chroma()` → chromatic index 0–11
- `display_name()` → "C#", "D#", etc.

#### **Note Struct**
- `name: NoteName`
- `octave: i8` (C4 = middle C)
- `midi()` → MIDI number 0–127 (C4 = 60, A4 = 69)
- `frequency()` → Hz (A4 = 440 Hz)
- `from_midi(u8)` ← build from MIDI number

#### **Frequency ↔ MIDI Conversion**
- `midi_to_freq(midi: u8) -\> f32` — Equal temperament (A4 = 440 Hz)
- `freq_to_note(hz: f32) -\> Option\<(Note, i32)\>` — Nearest MIDI + cents deviation

#### **ScaleType Enum**
8 scale types with interval patterns (in semitones):
- **Major:** [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11]
- **Natural Minor:** [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 10]
- **Harmonic Minor:** [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 11]
- **Pentatonic Major:** [0, 2, 4, 7, 9]
- **Pentatonic Minor:** [0, 3, 5, 7, 10]
- **Dorian:** [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10]
- **Mixolydian:** [0, 2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10]
- **Blues:** [0, 3, 5, 6, 7, 10]

#### **Sequence Generation**
- `scale_notes(root, scale) -\> Vec\<Note\>` — notes in one octave
- `generate_sequence(root, scale, length, seed) -\> Vec\<Note\>` — random sequence using LCG (Knuth parameters)
  - Reproducible via seed

#### **Staff Position**
- `staff_position(note) -\> i32` — diatonic steps above C4
- Used by UI for treble clef positioning
- C4=0, D4=1, C5=7, B3=-1, etc.

---

## **Build Instructions Summary**

### **Android**
```bash
# Build Rust libraries for Android targets
cargo install cargo-ndk
cargo ndk -t arm64-v8a -t x86_64 -o android/app/src/main/jniLibs build --release -p ear_ring_core

# Build APK
cd android
./gradlew build
```

### **iOS**
```bash
# Build Rust libraries
cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p ear_ring_core
cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios-sim --release -p ear_ring_core
cargo build --target x86_64-apple-ios --release -p ear_ring_core

# Generate C header
cbindgen --config cbindgen.toml --crate ear_ring_core --output rust/include/ear_ring_core.h

# Create XCFramework
xcodebuild -create-xcframework \
  -library target/aarch64-apple-ios/release/libear_ring_core.a \
  -headers rust/include/ \
  -library target/aarch64-apple-ios-sim/release/libear_ring_core.a \
  -headers rust/include/ \
  -output ios/EarRingCore.xcframework

# Install pods and build
cd ios
pod install
xcodebuild build -workspace earring.xcworkspace -scheme earring
```

---

## **Architecture Summary**

```
React Native (JS)
    ↓
[Android] Kotlin Turbo Native Modules ← [iOS] Swift Native Modules
    ↓                                         ↓
JNI Bridge                                  C FFI
    ↓                                         ↓
Rust FFI (lib.rs)
    ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│ pitch_detection.rs (YIN algo)   │
│ music_theory.rs (scales, MIDI)  │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## **Key Integration Points**

1. **AudioCaptureModule** (Android only) → streams PCM via events
2. **EarRingCoreModule** (both) → Rust core algorithms (pitch detection, music theory)
3. **JNI/FFI Bridge** → Rust C interface for both platforms
4. **Expo Integration** → React Native dev tooling, splash screen, linking
5. **Build Configuration** → Cross-platform (4 Android archs, 3 iOS archs)

<sub>⏱️ 20m 32s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me plan out the conversion:

**Current Architecture:**
- React Native/Expo for UI
- Kotlin native modules (AudioCapture, EarRingCore) - for bridging
- Swift native module (EarRingCore) - for bridging
- Rust core for pitch detection and music theory

**Target Architecture:**
- **Android**: Pure Kotlin app with native Android Views/Compose UI
- **iOS**: Pure Swift app with SwiftUI
- **Rust**: Same core, but now called directly via JNI (Android) and C FFI (iOS)


- No React Native, no Expo, no Node.js/Metro

I'll keep the Rust modules for music theory and pitch detection unchanged, just extending the JNI and FFI exports. For Android, I need to build a new Kotlin app with Jetpack Compose for the UI—starting with a MainActivity and Application class, then creating Compose screens for the home setup, exercise selection, and playback.

I'm also building native audio capture with AudioRecord and playback with MediaPlayer or ExoPlayer for the piano samples, connecting everything through the JNI bridge to Rust. For persistence and state management, I'll use ViewModel with StateFlow and either SharedPreferences or Room for storing progress.

On iOS, I'm mirroring the same structure with Swift and SwiftUI screens, using AVAudioEngine for both capture and playback, and connecting through the FFI bridge. I'll handle persistence with UserDefaults or CoreData and manage state similarly to the Android side.

The Rust core stays untouched since it's already handling the computation, and both platforms already have their respective JNI and FFI exports ready. My plan is to commit the current state, branch off to `native`, then build out fresh Android and iOS app structures while keeping the existing android/ directory's Kotlin files and replacing them with pure native versions rather than starting completely from scratch.

Now I'm mapping out the UI screens I need to build for Android using Jetpack Compose—a home screen for selecting scales and exercise length, an exercise screen with music staff and pitch feedback, a setup screen for calibration, results and progress tracking screens...

and the same five screens for iOS in SwiftUI. The music staff rendering will be the trickiest part since I need to draw treble clefs, staff lines, ledger lines, and note heads using Canvas APIs on both platforms. For audio, I'm looking at AudioRecord and ExoPlayer on Android, AVAudioEngine and AVAudioPlayer on iOS, plus I'll need to handle piano samples either from a CDN or bundled with the app. This is realistically thousands of lines of code across both platforms, so I'm going to be strategic—start with a fresh branch, focus on building out the Android app first, then tackle iOS.

For the build configuration, I need to strip out React Native and Expo dependencies from the Android gradle files and bring in Jetpack Compose, ExoPlayer, and keep the NDK for Rust JNI bindings. On iOS, I'll remove the Podfile dependencies and set up SPM or CocoaPods for what I need while keeping the XCFramework for Rust. I think the best move is to use a code generation agent with full context to write out all of this at once rather than piecemeal.

I'll start by committing my current work and creating a branch, then map out the architecture: Android gets Jetpack Compose with AudioRecord for capture and ExoPlayer for playback, iOS gets SwiftUI with AVAudioEngine and AVAudioPlayer, both using ViewModel/StateFlow and ObservableObject patterns respectively, and I'll keep the Rust core untouched while handling audio samples the same way through runtime CDN downloads.*

```
142.     // Trim all elements in place.
```

<sub>⏱️ 21m 59s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Now I have a complete picture. Let me create the plan and set up the todos, then start the implementation.

**Plan:**

The conversion involves:

1. **Android (Kotlin + Jetpack Compose)**:
   - Replace `build.gradle` to remove React Native / Expo / Hermes, add Compose + ExoPlayer
   - Replace `settings.gradle` to remove React Native plugins
   - Replace `gradle.properties` to remove RN-specific properties
   - Replace `MainActivity.kt` with a pure Compose Activity
   - Replace `MainApplication.kt` with a pure Android Application class
   - Keep `EarRingCoreModule.kt` and `EarRingCorePackage.kt` but remove the React Native interfaces → just use JNI directly
   - Convert `AudioCaptureModule.kt` to a service/repository pattern instead of a React Native module
   - Build out the UI screens (Home, Exercise, Setup, Results, Progress) using Compose
   - Integrate audio playback with ExoPlayer for the piano samples
   - Set up ViewModel and StateFlow for state management
   - Use SharedPreferences to save progress data
   - Remove the React Native Application class entirely

2. **iOS (Swift + SwiftUI)**:
   - Replace `AppDelegate.swift` to remove React Native / Expo
   - Replace `Podfile` to remove RN dependencies and Expo pods
   - Update `Info.plist` to clean out Expo/RN entries
   - Keep `EarRingCoreModule.swift` but remove the RN bridge decorators and call the C FFI directly
   - Build SwiftUI views for Home, Exercise, Setup, Results, and Progress
   - Add audio capture with AVAudioEngine and playback with AVAudioPlayer/AVAudioEngine
   - Use ObservableObject and ViewModel for state management

3. **Rust** (keep unchanged):
   - The Rust code is already well-suited for this - C FFI for iOS, JNI for Android
   - No changes needed to rust/src/

4. **Clean up React Native artifacts**:
   - Remove node_modules references from the build system while keeping node_modules itself for build tools
   - Keep the JS/TS files in the repo since this is a branch - the native directories become standalone apps while the original code stays intact

So the approach is to keep the JavaScript/TypeScript code as-is in the repository, but replace the android/ and ios/ directories with proper native applications that don't depend on React Native. The Rust core stays untouched. Now I'll write out the full plan and implementation steps.

For Android, I'm going to use Jetpack Compose for the UI, capture audio with AudioRecord, play piano samples from a CDN using MediaPlayer, and call into the Rust library through JNI for the pitch detection and music theory logic. I'll manage state with ViewModel and StateFlow, and persist progress using SharedPreferences. The main files I need to create or rewrite are the gradle configuration files to remove React Native dependencies, the main Activity as a pure Compose component, and the supporting Kotlin files for audio handling and Rust interop.

Still writing Kotlin files... I'm also going to need custom Canvas components for the music staff and pitch meter visualization, plus updates to the Android manifest. For iOS, I'll need to completely rewrite the Podfile and AppDelegate while stripping out React Native dependencies, then create new SwiftUI files. This is a substantial undertaking, so I should delegate the Android and iOS implementations to specialized agents rather than trying to handle everything myself. about the scope more carefully. This is a full conversion of a reasonably complex app. I'll need to write:

**Android (new files):**
- ~15-20 Kotlin files 
- Updated build files

**iOS (new files):**
- ~10-15 Swift files
- Updated Podfile/project structure

This is probably 3000-5000 lines of new code. I should use task agents to write this.

Let me break this into manageable pieces:
1. First do the Android build system changes (modify build.gradle, settings.gradle, etc.)
2. Write Android core files (JNI, AudioCapture, AudioPlayback)
3. Write Android UI screens
4. Write iOS files

Let me use one general-purpose agent to write the Android app, and another for iOS.

Now I need to review the existing Kotlin files to see what I can reuse and what needs to change.*

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\work\ear_ring\android\app\src\main\java\com\earring\EarRingCoreModule.kt.**

C:\work\ear_ring\android\app\src\main\java\com\earring\EarRingCoreModule.kt

<details>
<summary>81 lines</summary>

```
1. /**
2.  * EarRingCoreModule — Turbo Native Module (Kotlin/Android)
3.  *
4.  * Setup instructions:
5.  * 1. Build the Rust library for Android targets using cargo-ndk:
6.  *      cargo install cargo-ndk
7.  *      cargo ndk -t arm64-v8a -t x86_64 -o android/app/src/main/jniLibs build --release -p ear_ring_core
8.  *
9.  * 2. Add this file to the project (it should already be in the right package).
10.  *
11.  * 3. Register the module in MainApplication.kt by adding it to the package list:
12.  *      packages.add(EarRingCorePackage())
13.  *
14.  * The JNI bridge calls the C FFI functions defined in rust/src/lib.rs.
15.  * Java signatures are auto-generated from the function names.
16.  */
17. 
18. package com.earring
19. 
20. import com.facebook.react.bridge.*
21. import com.facebook.react.module.annotations.ReactModule
22. 
23. @ReactModule(name = EarRingCoreModule.NAME)
24. class EarRingCoreModule(reactContext: ReactApplicationContext) :
25.     ReactContextBaseJavaModule(reactContext) {
26. 
27.     companion object {
28.         const val NAME = "EarRingCore"
29. 
30.         private var libraryLoaded = false
31. 
32.         init {
33.             try {
34.                 System.loadLibrary("ear_ring_core")
35.                 libraryLoaded = true
36.             } catch (e: UnsatisfiedLinkError) {
37.                 android.util.Log.w(NAME, "ear_ring_core native library not found: ${e.message}")
38.             }
39.         }
40. 
41.         // JNI declarations — implemented in rust/src/lib.rs via C FFI
42.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeDetectPitch(samples: FloatArray, sampleRate: Int): Float
43.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToMidi(hz: Float): Int
44.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToCents(hz: Float): Int
45.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeStaffPosition(midi: Int): Int
46.         @JvmStatic external fun nativeGenerateSequence(
47.             rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Long
48.         ): IntArray
49.     }
50. 
51.     override fun getName() = NAME
52. 
53.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
54.     fun detectPitch(samples: ReadableArray, sampleRate: Int): Float {
55.         val floats = FloatArray(samples.size()) { samples.getDouble(it).toFloat() }
56.         return nativeDetectPitch(floats, sampleRate)
57.     }
58. 
59.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
60.     fun freqToNote(hz: Double): WritableMap? {
61.         val midi = nativeFreqToMidi(hz.toFloat())
62.         if (midi < 0) return null
63.         val cents = nativeFreqToCents(hz.toFloat())
64.         val result = WritableNativeMap()
65.         result.putInt("midi", midi)
66.         result.putInt("cents", cents)
67.         return result
68.     }
69. 
70.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
71.     fun staffPosition(midi: Int): Int = nativeStaffPosition(midi)
72. 
73.     @ReactMethod(isBlockingSynchronousMethod = true)
74.     fun generateSequence(rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Double): WritableArray {
75.         val arr = nativeGenerateSequence(rootMidi, scaleId, length, seed.toLong())
76.         val result = WritableNativeArray()
77.         arr.forEach { result.pushInt(it) }
78.         return result
79.     }
80. }
81.
```

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\work\ear_ring\android\app\src\main\java\com\earring\AudioCaptureModule.kt.**

C:\work\ear_ring\android\app\src\main\java\com\earring\AudioCaptureModule.kt

<details>
<summary>140 lines</summary>

```
1. package com.earring
2. 
3. import android.media.AudioFormat
4. import android.media.AudioRecord
5. import android.media.MediaRecorder
6. import android.util.Base64
7. import com.facebook.react.bridge.*
8. import com.facebook.react.module.annotations.ReactModule
9. import com.facebook.react.modules.core.DeviceEventManagerModule
10. import java.nio.ByteBuffer
11. import java.nio.ByteOrder
12. 
13. /**
14.  * AudioCaptureModule — streams real-time PCM audio from the microphone to JS.
15.  *
16.  * JS usage:
17.  *   const { NativeModules, NativeEventEmitter } = require('react-native');
18.  *   const emitter = new NativeEventEmitter(NativeModules.AudioCapture);
19.  *   emitter.addListener('onAudioData', ({ data, sampleRate, count }) => { ... });
20.  *   await NativeModules.AudioCapture.startRecording(44100, 4096);
21.  *   await NativeModules.AudioCapture.stopRecording();
22.  *
23.  * 'data' is a base64-encoded little-endian Float32 array of length 'count'.
24.  */
25. @ReactModule(name = AudioCaptureModule.NAME)
26. class AudioCaptureModule(reactContext: ReactApplicationContext) :
27.     ReactContextBaseJavaModule(reactContext) {
28. 
29.     companion object {
30.         const val NAME = "AudioCapture"
31.         const val EVENT_AUDIO_DATA = "onAudioData"
32.     }
33. 
34.     private var audioRecord: AudioRecord? = null
35.     private var recordingThread: Thread? = null
36.     @Volatile private var isRecording = false
37. 
38.     override fun getName() = NAME
39. 
40.     private fun sendEvent(params: WritableMap) {
41.         reactApplicationContext
42.             .getJSModule(DeviceEventManagerModule.RCTDeviceEventEmitter::class.java)
43.             .emit(EVENT_AUDIO_DATA, params)
44.     }
45. 
46.     @ReactMethod
47.     fun startRecording(sampleRate: Int, bufferSize: Int, promise: Promise) {
48.         if (isRecording) {
49.             promise.reject("ALREADY_RECORDING", "Already recording")
50.             return
51.         }
52. 
53.         val minBuf = AudioRecord.getMinBufferSize(
54.             sampleRate,
55.             AudioFormat.CHANNEL_IN_MONO,
56.             AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT
57.         )
58.         if (minBuf == AudioRecord.ERROR_BAD_VALUE || minBuf == AudioRecord.ERROR) {
59.             promise.reject("INIT_ERROR", "AudioRecord.getMinBufferSize failed")
60.             return
61.         }
62. 
63.         val byteBufSize = maxOf(minBuf, bufferSize * 2) // 2 bytes per sample (16-bit)
64. 
65.         try {
66.             audioRecord = AudioRecord(
67.                 MediaRecorder.AudioSource.MIC,
68.                 sampleRate,
69.                 AudioFormat.CHANNEL_IN_MONO,
70.                 AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT,
71.                 byteBufSize
72.             )
73.         } catch (e: Exception) {
74.             promise.reject("INIT_ERROR", "AudioRecord constructor failed: ${e.message}")
75.             return
76.         }
77. 
78.         if (audioRecord?.state != AudioRecord.STATE_INITIALIZED) {
79.             audioRecord?.release()
80.             audioRecord = null
81.             promise.reject("INIT_ERROR", "AudioRecord failed to initialize (check RECORD_AUDIO permission)")
82.             return
83.         }
84. 
85.         isRecording = true
86.         audioRecord?.startRecording()
87. 
88.         val shortBuf = ShortArray(bufferSize)
89.         val floatBuf = FloatArray(bufferSize)
90.         val byteBuffer = ByteBuffer.allocate(bufferSize * 4).order(ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN)
91. 
92.         recordingThread = Thread {
93.             while (isRecording) {
94.                 val read = audioRecord?.read(shortBuf, 0, bufferSize) ?: 0
95.                 if (read > 0) {
96.                     // Convert 16-bit PCM to float32 [-1.0, 1.0]
97.                     for (i in 0 until read) {
98.                         floatBuf[i] = shortBuf[i] / 32768.0f
99.                     }
100.                     // Pack as little-endian float32 bytes and base64-encode
101.                     byteBuffer.clear()
102.                     for (i in 0 until read) byteBuffer.putFloat(floatBuf[i])
103.                     val b64 = Base64.encodeToString(byteBuffer.array(), 0, read * 4, Base64.NO_WRAP)
104. 
105.                     val params = Arguments.createMap()
106.                     params.putString("data", b64)
107.                     params.putInt("sampleRate", sampleRate)
108.                     params.putInt("count", read)
109.                     sendEvent(params)
110.                 }
111.             }
112.         }.apply {
113.             name = "AudioCaptureThread"
114.             start()
115.         }
116. 
117.         promise.resolve(null)
118.     }
119. 
120.     @ReactMethod
121.     fun stopRecording(promise: Promise) {
122.         isRecording = false
123.         recordingThread?.join(2000)
124.         recordingThread = null
125.         try {
126.             audioRecord?.stop()
127.             audioRecord?.release()
128.         } catch (_: Exception) {}
129.         audioRecord = null
130.         promise.resolve(null)
131.     }
132. 
133.     // Required by NativeEventEmitter on the JS side
134.     @ReactMethod
135.     fun addListener(eventName: String) {}
136. 
137.     @ReactMethod
138.     fun removeListeners(count: Int) {}
139. }
140.
```

</details>

```
31.    */
32.   override fun createReactActivityDelegate(): ReactActivityDelegate {
33.     return ReactActivityDelegateWrapper(
34.           this,
35.           BuildConfig.IS_NEW_ARCHITECTURE_ENABLED,
36.           object : DefaultReactActivityDelegate(
37.               this,
38.               mainComponentName,

40.           ){})
41.   }
42. 
43.   /**
44.     * Align the back button behavior with Android S
45.     * where moving root activities to background instead of finishing activities.
46.     * @see <a href="https://developer.android.com/reference/android/app/Activity#onBackPressed()">onBackPressed</a>
47.     */
48.   override fun invokeDefaultOnBackPressed() {
49.       if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT <= Build.VERSION_CODES.R) {
50.           if (!moveTaskToBack(false)) {
51.               // For non-root activities, use the default implementation to finish them.
52.               super.invokeDefaultOnBackPressed()
53.           }
54.           return
55.       }
56. 
57.       // Use the default back button implementation on Android S
58.       // because it's doing more than [Activity.moveTaskToBack] in fact.
59.       super.invokeDefaultOnBackPressed()
60.   }
```

```
5. @main
6. class AppDelegate: ExpoAppDelegate {
7.   var window: UIWindow?
8. 
9.   var reactNativeDelegate: ExpoReactNativeFactoryDelegate?
10.   var reactNativeFactory: RCTReactNativeFactory?
11. 
12.   public override func application(
13.     _ application: UIApplication,
14.     didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]? = nil
15.   ) -> Bool {
16.     let delegate = ReactNativeDelegate()
17.     let factory = ExpoReactNativeFactory(delegate: delegate)
18.     delegate.dependencyProvider = RCTAppDependencyProvider()
19. 
20.     reactNativeDelegate = delegate
21.     reactNativeFactory = factory
22. 
23. #if os(iOS) || os(tvOS)
24.     window = UIWindow(frame: UIScreen.main.bounds)
25.     factory.startReactNative(
26.       withModuleName: "main",
27.       in: window,
28.       launchOptions: launchOptions)
29. #endif
30. 
31.     return super.application(application, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions: launchOptions)
32.   }
33. 
34.   // Linking API
35.   public override func application(
36.     _ app: UIApplication,
37.     open url: URL,
38.     options: [UIApplication.OpenURLOptionsKey: Any] = [:]
39.   ) -> Bool {
40.     return super.application(app, open: url, options: options) || RCTLinkingManager.application(app, open: url, options: options)
41.   }
42. 
43.   // Universal Links
44.   public override func application(
45.     _ application: UIApplication,
46.     continue userActivity: NSUserActivity,
47.     restorationHandler: @escaping ([UIUserActivityRestoring]?) -> Void
48.   ) -> Bool {
49.     let result = RCTLinkingManager.application(application, continue: userActivity, restorationHandler: restorationHandler)
50.     return super.application(application, continue: userActivity, restorationHandler: restorationHandler) || result
51.   }
52. }
53.

58.     // needed to return the correct URL for expo-dev-client.

60.   }

63. #if DEBUG
```

<details>

```
1. /**
2.  * EarRingCoreModule — Turbo Native Module (Swift)
3.  *
4.  * Setup instructions:
5.  * 1. Build the Rust library for iOS targets:
6.  *      cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p ear_ring_core
7.  *      cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios-sim --release -p ear_ring_core
8.  *      cargo build --target x86_64-apple-ios --release -p ear_ring_core
9.  *
10.  * 2. Create an XCFramework:
11.  *      xcodebuild -create-xcframework \
12.  *        -library target/aarch64-apple-ios/release/libear_ring_core.a \
13.  *        -headers rust/include/ \
14.  *        -library target/aarch64-apple-ios-sim/release/libear_ring_core.a \
15.  *        -headers rust/include/ \
16.  *        -output ios/EarRingCore.xcframework
17.  *
18.  * 3. Add EarRingCore.xcframework to the Xcode project (drag into Frameworks).
19.  *
20.  * 4. Add this file to the Xcode project and register the module in
21.  *    your AppDelegate:
22.  *      RCTBridge.moduleClasses.append(EarRingCoreModule.self)
23.  *
24.  * The C header (ear_ring_core.h) is generated from lib.rs — run:
25.  *   cbindgen --config cbindgen.toml --crate ear_ring_core --output rust/include/ear_ring_core.h
26.  */
27. 
28. import Foundation
29. import React
30. 
31. @objc(EarRingCore)
32. class EarRingCoreModule: NSObject, RCTBridgeModule {
33. 
34.   static func moduleName() -> String! { "EarRingCore" }
35.   static func requiresMainQueueSetup() -> Bool { false }
36. 
37.   /// Detect pitch in a PCM sample array.
38.   /// - Parameters:
39.   ///   - samples: Array of Float32 values in [-1.0, 1.0]
40.   ///   - sampleRate: Recording sample rate (e.g. 44100)
41.   /// - Returns: Detected frequency in Hz, or -1.0 if not detected
42.   @objc func detectPitch(_ samples: [NSNumber], sampleRate: Int) -> NSNumber {
43.     var floats = samples.map { $0.floatValue }
44.     var outHz: Float = 0
45.     let detected = ear_ring_detect_pitch(&floats, UInt32(floats.count), UInt32(sampleRate), &outHz)
46.     return detected == 1 ? NSNumber(value: outHz) : NSNumber(value: -1.0)
47.   }
48. 
49.   /// Convert frequency to nearest MIDI note and cents deviation.
50.   @objc func freqToNote(_ hz: Double, resolver resolve: RCTPromiseResolveBlock, rejecter reject: RCTPromiseRejectBlock) {
51.     var outMidi: UInt8 = 0
52.     var outCents: Int32 = 0
53.     let ok = ear_ring_freq_to_note(Float(hz), &outMidi, &outCents)
54.     if ok == 1 {
55.       resolve(["midi": outMidi, "cents": outCents])
56.     } else {
57.       resolve(nil)
58.     }
59.   }
60. 
61.   /// Get staff position for a MIDI note (diatonic steps above C4).
62.   @objc func staffPosition(_ midi: Int) -> NSNumber {
63.     return NSNumber(value: ear_ring_staff_position(UInt8(midi)))
64.   }
65. 
66.   /// Generate a sequence of MIDI note numbers.
67.   @objc func generateSequence(_ rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Double) -> [NSNumber] {
68.     var buf = [UInt8](repeating: 0, count: length)
69.     let count = ear_ring_generate_sequence(UInt8(rootMidi), UInt8(scaleId), UInt8(length), UInt64(seed), &buf)
70.     guard count >= 0 else { return [] }
71.     return buf.prefix(Int(count)).map { NSNumber(value: $0) }
72.   }
73. }
74.
```

</details>

```
1. require File.join(File.dirname(`node --print "require.resolve('expo/package.json')"`), "scripts/autolinking")
2. require File.join(File.dirname(`node --print "require.resolve('react-native/package.json')"`), "scripts/react_native_pods")
3. 
4. require 'json'
5. podfile_properties = JSON.parse(File.read(File.join(__dir__, 'Podfile.properties.json'))) rescue {}
6. 
7. def ccache_enabled?(podfile_properties)
8.   # Environment variable takes precedence
9.   return ENV['USE_CCACHE'] == '1' if ENV['USE_CCACHE']
10. 
11.   # Fall back to Podfile properties
12.   podfile_properties['apple.ccacheEnabled'] == 'true'
13. end
14. 
15. ENV['EX_DEV_CLIENT_NETWORK_INSPECTOR'] ||= podfile_properties['EX_DEV_CLIENT_NETWORK_INSPECTOR']
16. ENV['RCT_USE_RN_DEP'] ||= '1' if podfile_properties['ios.buildReactNativeFromSource'] != 'true'
17. ENV['RCT_USE_PREBUILT_RNCORE'] ||= '1' if podfile_properties['ios.buildReactNativeFromSource'] != 'true'
18. ENV['RCT_HERMES_V1_ENABLED'] ||= '1' if podfile_properties['expo.useHermesV1'] == 'true'
19. platform :ios, podfile_properties['ios.deploymentTarget'] || '15.1'
20. 
21. prepare_react_native_project!
22. 
23. target 'earring' do
24.   use_expo_modules!
25. 
26.   if ENV['EXPO_USE_COMMUNITY_AUTOLINKING'] == '1'
27.     config_command = ['node', '-e', "process.argv=['', '', 'config'];require('@react-native-community/cli').run()"];
28.   else
29.     config_command = [
30.       'node',
31.       '--no-warnings',
32.       '--eval',
33.       'require(\'expo/bin/autolinking\')',
34.       'expo-modules-autolinking',
35.       'react-native-config',
36.       '--json',
37.       '--platform',
38.       'ios'
39.     ]
40.   end
41. 
42.   config = use_native_modules!(config_command)
43. 
44.   use_frameworks! :linkage => podfile_properties['ios.useFrameworks'].to_sym if podfile_properties['ios.useFrameworks']
45.   use_frameworks! :linkage => ENV['USE_FRAMEWORKS'].to_sym if ENV['USE_FRAMEWORKS']
46. 
47.   use_react_native!(
48.     :path => config[:reactNativePath],
49.     :hermes_enabled => podfile_properties['expo.jsEngine'] == nil || podfile_properties['expo.jsEngine'] == 'hermes',
50.     # An absolute path to your application root.
51.     :app_path => "#{Pod::Config.instance.installation_root}/..",
52.     :privacy_file_aggregation_enabled => podfile_properties['apple.privacyManifestAggregationEnabled'] != 'false',
53.   )
54. 
55.   post_install do |installer|
56.     react_native_post_install(
57.       installer,
58.       config[:reactNativePath],
59.       :mac_catalyst_enabled => false,
60.       :ccache_enabled => ccache_enabled?(podfile_properties),
61.     )
62.   end
63. end
64.
```

<sub>⏱️ 23m 10s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Now I have all the information I need. Let me launch two parallel general-purpose agents:
1. One to write the complete Android Kotlin/Compose app
2. One to write the complete iOS Swift/SwiftUI app

Meanwhile, I'll write the Android build system files (build.gradle, settings.gradle, etc.) myself since those are more straightforward.

Let me think about what the agents should write:

**Android agent** - complete native Android app:
- `android/settings.gradle` - pure Gradle without RN/Expo
- `android/build.gradle` - pure Android/Kotlin without RN


- `android/app/build.gradle` - Compose + NDK
- `android/gradle.properties` - clean
- `android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` - clean
- `android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/MainActivity.kt` - Compose UI
- `android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/MainApplication.kt` - plain Android
- `android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/EarRingCore.kt` - JNI bindings
- Audio processing files... I'm mapping out the remaining UI screens for the Android app—SetupScreen, ResultsScreen, ProgressScreen, and custom components like MusicStaff and PitchMeter. For iOS, I'm building a native SwiftUI app from scratch with no React Native or Expo dependencies, starting with the main app entry point and AppDelegate configuration.

Setting up the core iOS infrastructure with EarRingCore handling the C FFI bindings, AudioCapture using AVAudioEngine, and AudioPlayback with AVAudioPlayer, plus the data models for exercises and progress tracking backed by UserDefaults. Then I'm building out the SwiftUI views to mirror the Android screens.

Now I'm reconsidering the approach—should I spin up parallel agents for Android and iOS, or have one agent handle the entire conversion to keep everything consistent? Since the code needs to be coherent across both platforms, I think a single comprehensive agent makes more sense, even if it takes longer sequentially.

Let me define the core data models that both platforms will need: Note objects with MIDI values and cent offsets, a fixed set of scale names like Major and Natural Minor, scale intervals matching the original musicTheory logic, and SessionRecord objects to track user performance with id, date, scale name, root note, score, and sequence length.

Now I'm drafting detailed prompts for the implementation—I need to be very specific about what code to generate so the agents produce real, functional code rather than placeholders. For the Android side, I'm laying out the full context of what Ear Ring does: it plays note sequences, captures user input, and evaluates their accuracy. detects pitch via microphone using YIN algorithm (implemented in Rust)
4. Scores user on accuracy

Key features:
- 8 scales (Major, Natural Minor, Harmonic Minor, Pentatonic Major, Pentatonic Minor, Dorian, Mixolydian, Blues)
- Sequence length 2-8 notes
- Root note selection (any chromatic pitch, octave 3-5)
- Music staff display (treble clef)
- Pitch meter
- Progress tracking with streak
- Note tolerance: ±50 cents, same pitch class (octave-agnostic)

The Rust library provides pitch detection, frequency-to-MIDI conversion, cent deviation calculation, staff position mapping, and sequence generation based on scale and root note. Piano samples are sourced from Tone.js's Salamander library covering notes from A0 through A6.

Now I need to craft detailed prompts for the agent to implement the full codebase, providing the native function signatures, sample URLs, pitch shifting calculations, and existing code structure.*

**Write Android native app**

<details>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Write Android native app",
  "mode": "background",
  "prompt": "You are converting an Expo/React Native app called \"Ear Ring\" to a pure native Android app using Kotlin + Jetpack Compose. The app is at C:\\work\\ear_ring. We are working on the `native` git branch.\n\n## What the app does\nEar Ring is an ear training app that:\n1. Plays random sequences of musical notes from a selected scale (piano samples)\n2. User sings/plays the notes back\n3. App detects pitch via microphone (YIN algorithm, implemented in Rust via JNI)\n4. Shows detected notes on a music staff, scores accuracy\n5. Tracks progress over sessions\n\n## App screens:\n- **Home**: Select root note (C–B), octave (3–5), scale type (8 options), sequence length (2–8). Buttons: Start Exercise, Mic Setup, View Progress\n- **Exercise**: Shows music staff with expected note sequence. User clicks \"Play Sequence\" to hear notes, then \"Start Listening\" to sing. Detected notes appear on staff (green ✓ = correct, red ✗ = wrong). If wrong → replay same sequence. If correct entire sequence → generate new sequence. Haptic feedback on correct/wrong.\n- **Setup**: Test microphone. Live pitch meter + staff showing detected note. Buttons to play test notes (C4, E4, G4, A4, C5).\n- **Results**: Show score %, expected vs played notes, save to history.\n- **Progress**: Streak (consecutive ≥80% sessions), best score per scale, session history.\n\n## Music Theory\n- 12 chromatic notes: C, C#, D, D#, E, F, F#, G, G#, A, A#, B\n- Root MIDI = (octave + 1) * 12 + noteIndex (C4 = MIDI 60)\n- 8 scales:\n  - 0=Major [0,2,4,5,7,9,11], 1=Natural Minor [0,2,3,5,7,8,10]\n  - 2=Harmonic Minor [0,2,3,5,7,8,11], 3=Pentatonic Major [0,2,4,7,9]\n  - 4=Pentatonic Minor [0,3,5,7,10], 5=Dorian [0,2,3,5,7,9,10]\n  - 6=Mixolydian [0,2,4,5,7,9,10], 7=Blues [0,3,5,6,7,10]\n- MIDI to note name: midiNote % 12 → index into NOTE_NAMES. Octave = midiNote / 12 - 1\n- Note validation: correct if same pitch class (midi % 12 == expected % 12) AND abs(cents) <= 50\n- Staff position (C4=0, D4=1, E4=2, F4=3, G4=4, A4=5, B4=6, C5=7, B3=-1, etc.) — diatonic steps above C4\n\n## Rust JNI (library name: ear_ring_core, already built, .so in jniLibs)\n```kotlin\ncompanion object {\n    init { System.loadLibrary(\"ear_ring_core\") }\n    @JvmStatic external fun nativeDetectPitch(samples: FloatArray, sampleRate: Int): Float  // returns Hz or -1.0\n    @JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToMidi(hz: Float): Int   // returns MIDI 0-127 or -1\n    @JvmStatic external fun nativeFreqToCents(hz: Float): Int  // returns cents (-50 to +50)\n    @JvmStatic external fun nativeStaffPosition(midi: Int): Int // C4=0, D4=1, etc.\n    @JvmStatic external fun nativeGenerateSequence(rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: Long): IntArray\n}\n```\n\n## Audio Playback (piano samples)\nBase URL: `https://tonejs.github.io/audio/salamander/`\nAvailable samples: A0, C1, Ds1, Fs1, A1, C2, Ds2, Fs2, A2, C3, Ds3, Fs3, A3, C4, Ds4, Fs4, A4, C5, Ds5, Fs5, A5, C6, Ds6, Fs6, A6, C7, Ds7, Fs7, A7, C8\nFile extension: .mp3\nPitch shift: playback rate = 2^(deltaSemitones/12) where delta = requested_midi - nearest_sample_midi\nUse MediaPlayer to stream+cache and apply playback rate.\nPlay notes with ~600ms gap between onsets.\n\n## Pitch Detection Pipeline\n1. AudioRecord at 44100 Hz, 16-bit mono, ~4096 samples/buffer\n2. Convert shorts to float32 (divide by 32768)\n3. Call nativeDetectPitch(floats, 44100)\n4. If Hz > 0: call nativeFreqToMidi and nativeFreqToCents\n5. Stability: require same pitch class for 2+ frames before firing, 450ms stable = confirmed note\n6. Silence: if RMS < 0.003, reset stability counter\n\n## Files to CREATE (all new — pure Kotlin, no React Native imports)\n\n### Build system:\n\n**android/settings.gradle** - Simple Gradle settings, no RN/Expo:\n```groovy\npluginManagement {\n    repositories {\n        google()\n        mavenCentral()\n        gradlePluginPortal()\n    }\n}\ndependencyResolutionManagement {\n    repositoriesMode.set(RepositoriesMode.FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS)\n    repositories {\n        google()\n        mavenCentral()\n    }\n}\nrootProject.name = \"earring\"\ninclude ':app'\n```\n\n**android/build.gradle** - root build file, no RN:\n```groovy\nplugins {\n    id 'com.android.application' version '8.5.0' apply false\n    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.android' version '1.9.24' apply false\n}\n```\n\n**android/gradle.properties** - clean (keep JVM args, remove RN/Expo properties):\n```\norg.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx2048m -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=512m\norg.gradle.parallel=true\nandroid.useAndroidX=true\nandroid.enableJetifier=true\nkotlin.code.style=official\n```\n\n**android/app/build.gradle** - Compose + NDK, no RN:\n```groovy\nplugins {\n    id 'com.android.application'\n    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.android'\n}\n\nandroid {\n    namespace 'com.earring'\n    compileSdk 35\n    ndkVersion '27.0.12077973'\n\n    defaultConfig {\n        applicationId 'com.earring'\n        minSdk 26\n        targetSdk 35\n        versionCode 1\n        versionName \"1.0\"\n        ndk { abiFilters 'arm64-v8a', 'x86_64' }\n    }\n    buildFeatures { compose true }\n    composeOptions { kotlinCompilerExtensionVersion '1.5.14' }\n    buildTypes {\n        release {\n            minifyEnabled false\n            proguardFiles getDefaultProguardFile('proguard-android-optimize.txt'), 'proguard-rules.pro'\n        }\n    }\n    compileOptions {\n        sourceCompatibility JavaVersion.VERSION_17\n        targetCompatibility JavaVersion.VERSION_17\n    }\n    kotlinOptions { jvmTarget = '17' }\n    packagingOptions {\n        jniLibs { useLegacyPackaging = true }\n    }\n}\n\ndependencies {\n    def composeBom = platform('androidx.compose:compose-bom:2024.06.00')\n    implementation composeBom\n    implementation 'androidx.compose.ui:ui'\n    implementation 'androidx.compose.ui:ui-graphics'\n    implementation 'androidx.compose.ui:ui-tooling-preview'\n    implementation 'androidx.compose.material3:material3'\n    implementation 'androidx.compose.foundation:foundation'\n    implementation 'androidx.activity:activity-compose:1.9.0'\n    implementation 'androidx.navigation:navigation-compose:2.7.7'\n    implementation 'androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-viewmodel-compose:2.8.2'\n    implementation 'androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-runtime-ktx:2.8.2'\n    implementation 'androidx.core:core-ktx:1.13.1'\n    implementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.10.1'\n    debugImplementation 'androidx.compose.ui:ui-tooling'\n}\n```\n\n**android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml**:\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"utf-8\"?>\n<manifest xmlns:android=\"http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android\">\n    <uses-permission android:name=\"android.permission.INTERNET\" />\n    <uses-permission android:name=\"android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO\" />\n    <uses-permission android:name=\"android.permission.MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS\" />\n    <uses-permission android:name=\"android.permission.VIBRATE\" />\n    <application\n        android:name=\".MainApplication\"\n        android:label=\"Ear Ring\"\n        android:icon=\"@mipmap/ic_launcher\"\n        android:roundIcon=\"@mipmap/ic_launcher_round\"\n        android:theme=\"@style/Theme.EarRing\"\n        android:allowBackup=\"true\"\n        android:supportsRtl=\"true\"\n        android:usesCleartextTraffic=\"true\">\n        <activity\n            android:name=\".MainActivity\"\n            android:exported=\"true\"\n            android:theme=\"@style/Theme.EarRing\"\n            android:windowSoftInputMode=\"adjustResize\">\n            <intent-filter>\n                <action android:name=\"android.intent.action.MAIN\" />\n                <category android:name=\"android.intent.category.LAUNCHER\" />\n            </intent-filter>\n        </activity>\n    </application>\n</manifest>\n```\n\n**android/app/src/main/res/values/themes.xml** (create this):\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"utf-8\"?>\n<resources>\n    <style name=\"Theme.EarRing\" parent=\"android:Theme.Material.Light.NoActionBar\" />\n</resources>\n```\n\n### Kotlin source files (all in package com.earring):\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/MainApplication.kt**:\nPlain Android Application class, no RN.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/MainActivity.kt**:\nComponentActivity with setContent { EarRingApp() }. Request RECORD_AUDIO permission on start.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/EarRingCore.kt**:\nPure JNI wrapper (no RN). Companion object with System.loadLibrary(\"ear_ring_core\") and the external fun declarations. Public methods: detectPitch, freqToMidi, freqToCents, staffPosition, generateSequence. Handle UnsatisfiedLinkError gracefully.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/AudioCapture.kt**:\nPure AudioRecord-based class. Has `start(sampleRate: Int, bufferSize: Int, onAudio: (FloatArray) -> Unit)` and `stop()`. Converts PCM16 to float32. Runs on background thread using coroutines. No RN dependencies.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/AudioPlayback.kt**:\nDownloads and caches piano samples. Has `playNote(midi: Int)` that finds nearest sample, calculates pitch shift rate, and plays with MediaPlayer. Has `cancelPlayback()`. Has `playSequence(midiNotes: List<Int>, onEach: (Int) -> Unit, onDone: () -> Unit)` with 600ms spacing. Cache downloaded mp3s in cacheDir. Use coroutines.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/MusicTheory.kt**:\nConstants and helpers:\n- NOTE_NAMES list\n- SCALE_NAMES list  \n- SCALE_INTERVALS map (scaleId -> List<Int>)\n- midiToLabel(midi: Int): String (e.g. \"A4\")\n- midiPitchClass(midi: Int): Int\n- isCorrect(detectedMidi: Int, cents: Int, expectedMidi: Int): Boolean\n- Key signature helper for staff rendering\n- Staff position calculation\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ExerciseViewModel.kt**:\nViewModel with StateFlow. States:\n```kotlin\ndata class ExerciseState(\n    val rootMidi: Int = 60,\n    val scaleId: Int = 0,\n    val sequenceLength: Int = 4,\n    val sequence: List<Int> = emptyList(),\n    val detected: List<DetectedNote> = emptyList(),\n    val status: ExerciseStatus = ExerciseStatus.IDLE,\n    val currentNoteIndex: Int = 0,\n    val seed: Long = System.currentTimeMillis()\n)\ndata class DetectedNote(val midi: Int, val cents: Int)\nenum class ExerciseStatus { IDLE, PLAYING, LISTENING, DONE }\n```\nMethods: setRoot, setScaleId, setSequenceLength, startExercise (generates sequence via Rust), playSequence, startListening, stopListening, addDetectedNote (advances through sequence, checks correctness), newRound.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ProgressViewModel.kt**:\nViewModel backed by SharedPreferences + Gson. Stores List<SessionRecord>. Computes streak (consecutive ≥80%), bestByScale map. addSession(scaleName, rootLabel, score, length) saves to prefs.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ui/theme/Theme.kt**:\nSimple Material3 theme with indigo/purple colors matching the RN app's style.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ui/EarRingApp.kt**:\nNavHost with routes: home, exercise, setup, results, progress. Pass ViewModels.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ui/HomeScreen.kt**:\nCompose screen. Shows:\n- App title \"Ear Ring 🎵\"\n- Root note chip row (C through B)\n- Octave chip row (3, 4, 5)\n- Scale type chip row (8 scales, can wrap)\n- Length chip row (2–8)\n- Three buttons: \"▶ Start Exercise\", \"🎙 Mic Setup\", \"📊 Progress\"\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ui/ExerciseScreen.kt**:\nShows: MusicStaff, PitchMeter, current status text, control buttons (Play, Listen, Stop, Back).\nExercise flow:\n- Auto-generates sequence on enter\n- \"Play Sequence\" → calls viewModel.playSequence (plays audio)\n- \"Start Listening\" → calls viewModel.startListening (starts AudioCapture + pitch detection loop)\n- When note detected: call viewModel.addDetectedNote, vibrate on correct/wrong\n- If sequence done: navigate to results\n- Back button goes home\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ui/SetupScreen.kt**:\nLive pitch detection display. MusicStaff + PitchMeter. \"Start Listening\" / \"Stop\" buttons. Test note buttons (C4=60, E4=64, G4=67, A4=69, C5=72) that play audio. Back button.\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ui/ResultsScreen.kt**:\nShows score as percentage. Emoji: 100%=🏆, ≥80%=🎉, ≥50%=👍, <50%=💪\nList of notes: expected note label + detected label + ✓/✗\nButtons: \"Try Again\" (back to exercise), \"New Exercise\" (back to home), \"Progress\" (navigate to progress)\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ui/ProgressScreen.kt**:\nStreak display with 🔥. Best scores per scale. Scrollable session history (newest first) with date, scale, score badge (green/yellow/red).\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ui/components/MusicStaff.kt**:\nCanvas-based Compose component. Draw:\n- 5 horizontal staff lines (spaced 12.dp apart)\n- Treble clef symbol (use a \"𝄞\" text or stylized drawing)\n- Note heads as circles at correct vertical positions (staff position from Rust)\n  - White notes = expected (not yet played)\n  - Green notes = correctly played\n  - Red notes = incorrectly played\n  - A dot/circle = currently active note\n- Ledger lines above/below staff where needed\n- Key signature accidentals (sharps/flats based on scale)\n\nStaff position → y coordinate: y = staffCenterY - position * (lineSpacing / 2)\n\n**android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/ui/components/PitchMeter.kt**:\nCanvas circle. Shows detected frequency name (e.g. \"A4\"). Green circle border when detecting, gray when silent.\n\n## IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS:\n1. CREATE all these files using the create/edit tools\n2. Delete the old React Native files:\n   - android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/EarRingCoreModule.kt (replace with EarRingCore.kt)\n   - android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/EarRingCorePackage.kt\n   - android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/AudioCaptureModule.kt (replace with AudioCapture.kt)\n   - android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/AudioCapturePackage.kt\n3. Overwrite android/settings.gradle, android/build.gradle, android/gradle.properties, android/app/build.gradle\n4. Overwrite android/app/src/main/java/com/earring/MainActivity.kt and MainApplication.kt\n5. Write COMPLETE, WORKING Kotlin code — not stubs or TODOs\n6. Use kotlinx.coroutines for async work\n7. Handle all edge cases (permission denied, library not loaded, network errors)\n8. The code must compile with Kotlin 1.9 + Compose BOM 2024.06\n\nWrite all files now. Be thorough and write complete implementations."
````
