````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Write iOS Swift native app",
  "mode": "background",
  "prompt": "You are converting an Expo/React Native app called \"Ear Ring\" to a pure native iOS app using Swift + SwiftUI. The app is at C:\\work\\ear_ring. We are working on the `native` git branch.\n\n## What the app does\nEar Ring is an ear training app that:\n1. Plays random sequences of musical notes from a selected scale (piano samples)\n2. User sings/plays the notes back\n3. App detects pitch via microphone (YIN algorithm, implemented in Rust via C FFI)\n4. Shows detected notes on a music staff, scores accuracy\n5. Tracks progress over sessions\n\n## App screens:\n- **Home**: Select root note (C–B), octave (3–5), scale type (8 options), sequence length (2–8). Buttons: Start Exercise, Mic Setup, View Progress\n- **Exercise**: Shows music staff with expected note sequence. User clicks \"Play Sequence\" to hear notes, then \"Start Listening\" to sing. Detected notes appear on staff (green ✓ = correct, red ✗ = wrong). If wrong → replay same sequence. If correct entire sequence → generate new sequence. Haptic feedback.\n- **Setup**: Test microphone. Live pitch meter + staff showing detected note. Buttons to play test notes (C4, E4, G4, A4, C5).\n- **Results**: Show score %, expected vs played notes, save to history.\n- **Progress**: Streak (consecutive ≥80% sessions), best score per scale, session history.\n\n## Music Theory\n- 12 chromatic notes: C, C#, D, D#, E, F, F#, G, G#, A, A#, B\n- Root MIDI = (octave + 1) * 12 + noteIndex (C4 = MIDI 60)\n- 8 scales (by scaleId):\n  - 0=Major [0,2,4,5,7,9,11], 1=Natural Minor [0,2,3,5,7,8,10]\n  - 2=Harmonic Minor [0,2,3,5,7,8,11], 3=Pentatonic Major [0,2,4,7,9]\n  - 4=Pentatonic Minor [0,3,5,7,10], 5=Dorian [0,2,3,5,7,9,10]\n  - 6=Mixolydian [0,2,4,5,7,9,10], 7=Blues [0,3,5,6,7,10]\n- MIDI to note name: midiNote % 12 → index into noteNames. Octave = midiNote / 12 - 1\n- Note validation: correct if same pitch class (midi % 12 == expected % 12) AND abs(cents) <= 50\n- Staff position (C4=0, D4=1, E4=2, F4=3, G4=4, A4=5, B4=6, C5=7, B3=-1, etc.)\n\n## Rust C FFI (already built as XCFramework at ios/EarRingCore.xcframework)\nThe C functions are declared in rust/include/ear_ring_core.h:\n```c\nint32_t ear_ring_detect_pitch(const float *samples, uint32_t num_samples, uint32_t sample_rate, float *out_hz);\nint32_t ear_ring_freq_to_note(float hz, uint8_t *out_midi, int32_t *out_cents);\nint32_t ear_ring_staff_position(uint8_t midi);\nint32_t ear_ring_generate_sequence(uint8_t root_midi, uint8_t scale_id, uint8_t length, uint64_t seed, uint8_t *out_buf);\n```\nReturns 1 on success (detect_pitch, freq_to_note), count on success (generate_sequence), -1 on error.\nAccessed via the bridging header or direct import.\n\n## Audio Playback (piano samples)\nBase URL: `https://tonejs.github.io/audio/salamander/`\nAvailable samples: A0, C1, Ds1, Fs1, A1, C2, Ds2, Fs2, A2, C3, Ds3, Fs3, A3, C4, Ds4, Fs4, A4, C5, Ds5, Fs5, A5, C6, Ds6, Fs6, A6, C7, Ds7, Fs7, A7, C8\nFile extension: .mp3\nPitch shift: playback rate = 2^(deltaSemitones/12) where delta = requested_midi - nearest_sample_midi\nUse AVAudioPlayer. Cache downloaded data in caches directory.\nPlay notes with ~600ms spacing between onsets.\n\n## Pitch Detection Pipeline\n1. AVAudioEngine tap at 44100 Hz, mono float32\n2. Call ear_ring_detect_pitch with buffer\n3. If Hz > 0: call ear_ring_freq_to_note for midi + cents\n4. Stability: require same pitch class for 450ms before firing confirmed note\n5. Silence: if RMS < 0.003, reset stability\n\n## Existing file to REUSE (strip React Native, keep FFI calls):\n`ios/earring/EarRingCoreModule.swift` — has direct C FFI calls. Rewrite as a simple Swift struct/class with static methods, no @objc/RCT.\n\n## Files to CREATE/OVERWRITE:\n\n### `ios/Podfile` — minimal, no RN/Expo:\n```ruby\nplatform :ios, '16.0'\n\ntarget 'earring' do\n  use_frameworks!\n  # No pods needed — using system frameworks only\nend\n```\n\n### `ios/earring/EarRingApp.swift` — NEW file, @main SwiftUI App:\n```swift\nimport SwiftUI\n\n@main\nstruct EarRingApp: App {\n    var body: some Scene {\n        WindowGroup {\n            ContentView()\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### `ios/earring/AppDelegate.swift` — OVERWRITE with minimal UIKit delegate (or remove @main from it since EarRingApp.swift takes over):\n```swift\nimport UIKit\n\nclass AppDelegate: NSObject, UIApplicationDelegate {\n    func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]? = nil) -> Bool {\n        return true\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### `ios/earring/EarRingCore.swift` — OVERWRITE, pure Swift FFI wrapper:\nStrip the @objc/RCT stuff. Make it a struct with static methods.\n```swift\nimport Foundation\n\nstruct EarRingCore {\n    static func detectPitch(samples: [Float], sampleRate: UInt32) -> Float? {\n        var floats = samples\n        var outHz: Float = 0\n        let ok = ear_ring_detect_pitch(&floats, UInt32(floats.count), sampleRate, &outHz)\n        return ok == 1 ? outHz : nil\n    }\n    static func freqToNote(hz: Float) -> (midi: Int, cents: Int)? { ... }\n    static func staffPosition(midi: Int) -> Int { ... }\n    static func generateSequence(rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: UInt64) -> [Int] { ... }\n}\n```\n\n### `ios/earring/AudioCapture.swift` — NEW file:\nAVAudioEngine-based capture. Class with:\n- `start(onAudio: @escaping ([Float]) -> Void)`\n- `stop()`\n- Request microphone permission\n- Install tap on inputNode (44100 Hz, mono)\n- Convert buffer to [Float] array\n\n### `ios/earring/AudioPlayback.swift` — NEW file:\n- `playNote(midi: Int) async` — download/cache mp3, create AVAudioPlayer, set rate, play\n- `playSequence(notes: [Int], onEach: @escaping (Int) -> Void) async`\n- `cancelPlayback()`\n- Cache files in FileManager.default.urls(for: .cachesDirectory)\n\n### `ios/earring/MusicTheory.swift` — NEW file:\nSame constants as above. Struct with static methods:\n- NOTE_NAMES, SCALE_NAMES, SCALE_INTERVALS\n- midiToLabel(_ midi: Int) -> String\n- pitchClass(_ midi: Int) -> Int\n- isCorrect(detectedMidi: Int, cents: Int, expectedMidi: Int) -> Bool\n- staffPosition(_ midi: Int) -> Int (use EarRingCore or compute in Swift)\n\n### `ios/earring/ExerciseModel.swift` — NEW file:\n@MainActor class ExerciseModel: ObservableObject:\n```swift\n@Published var rootMidi: Int = 60\n@Published var scaleId: Int = 0\n@Published var sequenceLength: Int = 4\n@Published var sequence: [Int] = []\n@Published var detected: [DetectedNote] = []\n@Published var status: ExerciseStatus = .idle\n@Published var currentNoteIndex: Int = 0\n\nstruct DetectedNote { var midi: Int; var cents: Int }\nenum ExerciseStatus { case idle, playing, listening, done }\n```\nMethods: generateSequence(), playSequence(), startListening(), stopListening(), addDetectedNote(_:cents:), newRound()\n\n### `ios/earring/ProgressModel.swift` — NEW file:\n@MainActor class ProgressModel: ObservableObject backed by UserDefaults:\n```swift\n@Published var history: [SessionRecord] = []\nvar streak: Int { computed }\nvar bestByScale: [String: Int] { computed }\nfunc addSession(scaleName: String, rootLabel: String, score: Int, length: Int)\n```\nSessionRecord: Codable struct with id, date, scaleName, rootLabel, score, length.\n\n### `ios/earring/ContentView.swift` — NEW file:\nNavigationStack with routes to all screens.\n\n### `ios/earring/views/HomeView.swift` — NEW:\nSwiftUI view matching the Home screen description. Use LazyVGrid or ScrollView for note chips. Styled with indigo/purple accent.\n\n### `ios/earring/views/ExerciseView.swift` — NEW:\nFull exercise gameplay. MusicStaffView + PitchMeterView + status text + control buttons. Triggers audio and pitch detection.\n\n### `ios/earring/views/SetupView.swift` — NEW:\nMic test. MusicStaffView + PitchMeterView + live detection. Test note buttons.\n\n### `ios/earring/views/ResultsView.swift` — NEW:\nScore display + note list + action buttons.\n\n### `ios/earring/views/ProgressView.swift` — NEW:\nStreak + best by scale + history list.\n\n### `ios/earring/views/MusicStaffView.swift` — NEW:\nSwiftUI Canvas view. Draw:\n- 5 horizontal staff lines\n- Treble clef (\"𝄞\" text label)\n- Note circles at correct vertical positions (using staffPosition)\n  - Empty circle = expected (not yet played)\n  - Green filled = correct\n  - Red filled = wrong\n- Ledger lines for notes outside staff (-2 to -1 or 8 to 9)\n\n### `ios/earring/views/PitchMeterView.swift` — NEW:\nCircle with note name label. Green stroke when active, gray when silent.\n\n### `ios/earring/earring-Bridging-Header.h` — OVERWRITE:\n```c\n#include \"ear_ring_core.h\"\n```\n(The Rust header is at rust/include/ear_ring_core.h — but for the bridging header in the iOS project, just import the XCFramework header)\n\n## IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS:\n1. CREATE all new files. OVERWRITE existing ones using the edit tool.\n2. Write COMPLETE, WORKING Swift code — not stubs or placeholders.\n3. Use async/await for all async operations.\n4. Handle all permissions (microphone).\n5. The existing `ios/earring/EarRingCoreModule.swift` already has direct C FFI calls — reuse that pattern.\n6. The `EarRingApp.swift` should use `@main`. The existing `AppDelegate.swift` should NOT have `@main` (remove it).\n7. For the Bridging Header, since we're on a dev machine (Windows), just write the header correctly — it will be used when building on Mac.\n8. Target iOS 16.0+.\n9. Use `@EnvironmentObject` to pass ExerciseModel and ProgressModel down the view hierarchy.\n10. Write all files now. Be complete and thorough."
}
````

```
## What was created/changed

**Build system** — Rewrote `settings.gradle`, `build.gradle`, `gradle.properties`, and `app/build.gradle` to remove all React Native / Expo dependencies and configure Compose BOM 2024.06 + Kotlin 1.9.

**UI (Jetpack Compose):**
- `EarRingApp.kt` — NavHost with 5 routes
- `HomeScreen.kt` — Root note, octave, scale, length chip selectors + 3 action buttons
- `ExerciseScreen.kt` — Staff, live pitch meter, play/listen controls, per-note result indicators
- `SetupScreen.kt` — Live pitch detection, test note buttons (C4–C5)
- `ResultsScreen.kt` — Score %, emoji, note-by-note breakdown, navigation buttons
- `ProgressScreen.kt` — Streak 🔥, best-by-scale table, scrollable session history with color-coded badges
- `MusicStaff.kt` — Canvas-drawn 5-line staff, treble clef, colored note heads, ledger lines
- `PitchMeter.kt` — Circle indicator showing detected note label
```

```
All 22 files committed. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

**22 files created/modified** on the `native` branch:

### New Core Files
- **`EarRingApp.swift`** — `@main` SwiftUI App, injects `ExerciseModel` + `ProgressModel` as environment objects
- **`EarRingCore.swift`** — Static Swift wrappers over the 4 Rust C FFI functions
- **`AudioCapture.swift`** — AVAudioEngine mic tap using native hardware format (passes actual sample rate to FFI)
- **`AudioPlayback.swift`** — Downloads/caches Salamander .mp3 samples, pitch-shifts via `AVAudioPlayer.rate = 2^(Δsemitones/12)`, 600ms note spacing
- **`MusicTheory.swift`** — Note names, 8 scale definitions, `midiToLabel`, `isCorrect` (pitch class + ±50 cents)
- **`ExerciseModel.swift`** — `@MainActor ObservableObject` with 450ms stability tracking, haptic feedback, live pitch display, scoring, and separate live-only detection mode for SetupView
- **`ProgressModel.swift`** — Session history, streak (consecutive ≥80%), best-by-scale, UserDefaults persistence
- **`ContentView.swift`** — `NavigationStack` with `AppRoute` enum routing all 5 screens

### Views
- **`HomeView.swift`** — Chip selectors for root note, octave, scale, length; shared `ChipButtonStyle`/`PrimaryButtonStyle`/`SecondaryButtonStyle`/`cardStyle()`
- **`ExerciseView.swift`** — Staff + pitch meter + 4 status-driven button states
- **`SetupView.swift`** — Live mic detection + 5 test note buttons (C4–C5)
- **`ResultsView.swift`** — Animated score arc, note-by-note breakdown
- **`ProgressScreen.swift`** — Streak 🔥, best-by-scale table, session history list
- **`MusicStaffView.swift`** — Canvas-drawn treble clef staff, open circles (expected) / green/red filled (detected), ledger lines
- **`PitchMeterView.swift`** — Animated circle with note name, green when active

### Project Files
- **`project.pbxproj`** — All 15 new Swift files registered, RN build phases removed, deployment target → iOS 16.0
- **`Podfile`** — Minimal `platform :ios, '16.0'`, no pods
- **`earring-Bridging-Header.h`** — `#include "../../rust/include/ear_ring_core.h"`
- **`rust/include/ear_ring_core.h`** — C header declaring all 4 FFI functions
- **`Info.plist`** — Added `NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`, updated min version to 16.0

### To build on Mac
1. Build the Rust XCFramework: `cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release` then `xcodebuild -create-xcframework ...`
2. Add `EarRingCore.xcframework` to the Xcode project (drag into Frameworks)
3. Open `ios/earring.xcodeproj` — all Swift source files are pre-registered in the project
```

C:\work\ear_ring\ios\earring\EarRingCore.swift

<details>
<summary>39 lines</summary>

```
1. import Foundation
2. 
3. /// Pure Swift wrappers around the Rust C FFI exposed via the bridging header.
4. struct EarRingCore {
5. 
6.     /// Detect the fundamental pitch in a buffer of float PCM samples.
7.     /// - Returns: Frequency in Hz, or nil if no pitch was detected.
8.     static func detectPitch(samples: [Float], sampleRate: UInt32) -> Float? {
9.         var floats = samples
10.         var outHz: Float = 0
11.         let result = ear_ring_detect_pitch(&floats, UInt32(floats.count), sampleRate, &outHz)
12.         return result == 1 ? outHz : nil
13.     }
14. 
15.     /// Convert a frequency in Hz to the nearest MIDI note number and cents deviation.
16.     /// - Returns: A tuple (midi, cents), or nil if the frequency is out of range.
17.     static func freqToNote(hz: Float) -> (midi: Int, cents: Int)? {
18.         var outMidi: UInt8 = 0
19.         var outCents: Int32 = 0
20.         let result = ear_ring_freq_to_note(hz, &outMidi, &outCents)
21.         return result == 1 ? (Int(outMidi), Int(outCents)) : nil
22.     }
23. 
24.     /// Return the diatonic staff position for a MIDI note (C4 = 0, D4 = 1, …).
25.     static func staffPosition(midi: Int) -> Int {
26.         return Int(ear_ring_staff_position(UInt8(midi)))
27.     }
28. 
29.     /// Generate a sequence of MIDI notes from the given scale.
30.     /// - Returns: Array of MIDI note integers, empty on failure.
31.     static func generateSequence(rootMidi: Int, scaleId: Int, length: Int, seed: UInt64) -> [Int] {
32.         var buf = [UInt8](repeating: 0, count: length)
33.         let count = ear_ring_generate_sequence(
34.             UInt8(rootMidi), UInt8(scaleId), UInt8(length), seed, &buf)
35.         guard count > 0 else { return [] }
36.         return buf.prefix(Int(count)).map { Int($0) }
37.     }
38. }
39.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 52m 58s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Everything is committed. Here's the full summary:

---

**Branch `native` — 3 commits, all done ✅**

### What changed

| Area | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| Android build | React Native + Expo + Hermes Gradle plugins | Pure Android Gradle + Compose BOM 2024 |
| Android entry | `ReactActivity` + `ExpoAppDelegate` | `ComponentActivity` + Compose NavHost |
| Android audio | `AudioCaptureModule.kt` (RN event emitter) | `AudioCapture.kt` (pure `AudioRecord`) |
| Android core | `EarRingCoreModule.kt` (RN Turbo Module) | `EarRingCore.kt` (direct JNI object) |
| iOS build | Podfile with full RN/Expo pod tree | `platform :ios, '16.0'` — no pods |
| iOS entry | `ExpoAppDelegate` + `ReactNativeFactory` | `@main EarRingApp: App` (SwiftUI) |
| iOS core | `EarRingCoreModule.swift` (`@objc RCTBridgeModule`) | `EarRingCore.swift` (plain Swift struct + C FFI) |
| **Rust** | ✅ Unchanged | ✅ Unchanged |

### To build

**Android** (build Rust `.so` first, then Gradle):
```bash
cargo ndk -t arm64-v8a -t x86_64 -o android/app/src/main/jniLibs build --release -p ear_ring_core
cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug
```

**iOS** (requires a Mac):
```bash
cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p ear_ring_core
cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios-sim --release -p ear_ring_core
xcodebuild -create-xcframework \
  -library target/aarch64-apple-ios/release/libear_ring_core.a -headers rust/include/ \
  -library target/aarch64-apple-ios-sim/release/libear_ring_core.a -headers rust/include/ \
  -output ios/EarRingCore.xcframework
open ios/earring.xcodeproj
```

<sub>⏱️ 57m 39s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

No crashes — `ActivityTaskManager: Displayed com.earring/.MainActivity` confirms the app launched and rendered successfully. The warnings are normal first-run ART JIT compilation noise.

**✅ Build and install successful!**

- **Rust** compiled for `arm64-v8a` + `x86_64` in ~1s (already cached)
- **APK** built and installed in 36s  
- **App running** on `Medium_Phone_API_36.1` — no crashes, no React Native anywhere in the process

The `lock verification` warnings are harmless — that's ART complaining about how Compose's internal `SnapshotStateList` is compiled, nothing app-specific. The app is fully native Kotlin + Jetpack Compose running on the emulator.

```
1. package com.earring
2. 
3. import android.app.Application
4. import android.os.VibrationEffect
5. import android.os.Vibrator
6. import android.os.VibratorManager
7. import android.content.Context
8. import android.os.Build
9. import androidx.lifecycle.AndroidViewModel
10. import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
11. import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
12. import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
13. import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
14. import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
15. 
16. data class DetectedNote(val midi: Int, val cents: Int, val correct: Boolean)
17. 
18. enum class ExerciseStatus { IDLE, PLAYING, LISTENING, DONE }
19. 
20. data class ExerciseState(
21.     val rootNote: Int = 0,        // 0=C, 1=C#, ..., 11=B
22.     val octave: Int = 4,
23.     val scaleId: Int = 0,
24.     val sequenceLength: Int = 4,
25.     val sequence: List<Int> = emptyList(),
26.     val detected: List<DetectedNote> = emptyList(),
27.     val status: ExerciseStatus = ExerciseStatus.IDLE,
28.     val currentNoteIndex: Int = 0,
29.     val seed: Long = System.currentTimeMillis(),
30.     val highlightIndex: Int = -1
31. ) {
32.     val rootMidi: Int get() = (octave + 1) * 12 + rootNote
33.     val score: Float get() {
34.         if (detected.isEmpty()) return 0f
35.         return detected.count { it.correct }.toFloat() / detected.size
36.     }
37. }
38. 
39. class ExerciseViewModel(application: Application) : AndroidViewModel(application) {
40. 
41.     private val _state = MutableStateFlow(ExerciseState())
42.     val state: StateFlow<ExerciseState> = _state.asStateFlow()
43. 
44.     val audioCapture = AudioCapture()
45.     val audioPlayback = AudioPlayback(application)
46. 
47.     // Pitch stability tracking
48.     private var stableCount = 0
49.     private var stablePitchClass = -1
50.     private var lastConfirmedMs = 0L
51.     private val STABLE_FRAMES_REQUIRED = 2
52.     private val CONFIRM_HOLD_MS = 450L
53.     private val SILENCE_RMS_THRESHOLD = 0.003f
54. 
55.     private val vibrator: Vibrator by lazy {
56.         if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.S) {
57.             (application.getSystemService(Context.VIBRATOR_MANAGER_SERVICE) as VibratorManager).defaultVibrator
58.         } else {
59.             @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
60.             application.getSystemService(Context.VIBRATOR_SERVICE) as Vibrator
61.         }
62.     }
63. 
64.     fun setRootNote(note: Int) { _state.value = _state.value.copy(rootNote = note) }
65.     fun setOctave(octave: Int) { _state.value = _state.value.copy(octave = octave) }
66.     fun setScaleId(id: Int) { _state.value = _state.value.copy(scaleId = id) }
67.     fun setSequenceLength(len: Int) { _state.value = _state.value.copy(sequenceLength = len) }
68. 
69.     fun startExercise() {
70.         val s = _state.value
71.         val seed = System.currentTimeMillis()
72.         val seq = EarRingCore.generateSequence(s.rootMidi, s.scaleId, s.sequenceLength, seed)
73.         _state.value = s.copy(
74.             sequence = seq.toList(),
75.             detected = emptyList(),
76.             status = ExerciseStatus.IDLE,
77.             currentNoteIndex = 0,
78.             seed = seed,
79.             highlightIndex = -1
80.         )
81.         resetStability()
82.     }
83. 
84.     fun playSequence(onDone: () -> Unit = {}) {
85.         val seq = _state.value.sequence
86.         if (seq.isEmpty()) return
87.         _state.value = _state.value.copy(status = ExerciseStatus.PLAYING, highlightIndex = -1)
88.         audioPlayback.playSequence(
89.             midiNotes = seq,
90.             onEach = { idx -> _state.value = _state.value.copy(highlightIndex = idx) },
91.             onDone = {
92.                 _state.value = _state.value.copy(status = ExerciseStatus.IDLE, highlightIndex = -1)
93.                 onDone()
94.             }
95.         )
96.     }
97. 
98.     fun startListening() {
99.         resetStability()
100.         _state.value = _state.value.copy(status = ExerciseStatus.LISTENING)
101.         audioCapture.start(onAudio = { samples -> processSamples(samples) })
102.     }
103. 
104.     fun stopListening() {
105.         audioCapture.stop()
106.         _state.value = _state.value.copy(status = ExerciseStatus.IDLE)
107.         resetStability()
108.     }
109. 
110.     private fun processSamples(samples: FloatArray) {
111.         val rms = kotlin.math.sqrt(samples.map { it * it }.average().toFloat())
112.         if (rms < SILENCE_RMS_THRESHOLD) {
113.             resetStability()
114.             return
115.         }
116. 
117.         val hz = EarRingCore.detectPitch(samples, 44100)
118.         if (hz <= 0f) {
119.             resetStability()
120.             return
121.         }
122. 
123.         val midi = EarRingCore.freqToMidi(hz)
124.         if (midi < 0) { resetStability(); return }
125.         val pitchClass = midi % 12
126.         val cents = EarRingCore.freqToCents(hz)
127. 
128.         if (pitchClass == stablePitchClass) {
129.             stableCount++
130.         } else {
131.             stablePitchClass = pitchClass
132.             stableCount = 1
133.             lastConfirmedMs = 0L
134.         }
135. 
136.         if (stableCount >= STABLE_FRAMES_REQUIRED) {
137.             val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
138.             if (lastConfirmedMs == 0L) {
139.                 lastConfirmedMs = now
140.             } else if (now - lastConfirmedMs >= CONFIRM_HOLD_MS) {
141.                 confirmNote(midi, cents)
142.                 lastConfirmedMs = now + CONFIRM_HOLD_MS * 2  // debounce
143.             }
144.         }
145.     }
146. 
147.     private fun confirmNote(midi: Int, cents: Int) {
148.         val state = _state.value
149.         if (state.status != ExerciseStatus.LISTENING) return
150.         val idx = state.currentNoteIndex
151.         if (idx >= state.sequence.size) return
152. 
153.         val expected = state.sequence[idx]
154.         val correct = MusicTheory.isCorrect(midi, cents, expected)
155. 
156.         vibrate(correct)
157. 
158.         val detected = DetectedNote(midi, cents, correct)
159.         val newDetected = state.detected + detected
160. 
161.         if (correct) {
162.             val nextIdx = idx + 1
163.             if (nextIdx >= state.sequence.size) {
164.                 audioCapture.stop()
165.                 _state.value = state.copy(
166.                     detected = newDetected,
167.                     currentNoteIndex = nextIdx,
168.                     status = ExerciseStatus.DONE
169.                 )
170.             } else {
171.                 _state.value = state.copy(
172.                     detected = newDetected,
173.                     currentNoteIndex = nextIdx
174.                 )
175.                 resetStability()
176.             }
177.         } else {
178.             audioCapture.stop()
179.             _state.value = state.copy(
180.                 detected = newDetected,
181.                 currentNoteIndex = state.sequence.size,
182.                 status = ExerciseStatus.DONE
183.             )
184.         }
185.     }
186. 
187.     fun newRound() {
188.         startExercise()
189.     }
190. 
191.     private fun resetStability() {
192.         stableCount = 0
193.         stablePitchClass = -1
194.         lastConfirmedMs = 0L
195.     }
196. 
197.     private fun vibrate(success: Boolean) {
198.         try {
199.             if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
200.                 val effect = if (success)
201.                     VibrationEffect.createOneShot(80, VibrationEffect.DEFAULT_AMPLITUDE)
202.                 else
203.                     VibrationEffect.createWaveform(longArrayOf(0, 100, 80, 100), -1)
204.                 vibrator.vibrate(effect)
205.             } else {
206.                 @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
207.                 vibrator.vibrate(if (success) 80 else 200)
208.             }
209.         } catch (e: Exception) {
210.             android.util.Log.e("ExerciseViewModel", "Vibrate error: ${e.message}")
211.         }
212.     }
213. 
214.     override fun onCleared() {
215.         super.onCleared()
216.         audioCapture.stop()
217.         audioPlayback.cancelPlayback()
218.     }
219. }
220.
```

<sub>⏱️ 63m 41s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Looking at the code, I see a potential issue. In `ExerciseViewModel.startExercise()`, it calls `EarRingCore.generateSequence(...)`. The `EarRingCore` is an `object` (singleton), not a class, and it declares `external fun` methods directly on the object without a companion object.

Wait, looking at the EarRingCore.kt:
```kotlin
object EarRingCore {
    private var loaded = false
    init { System.loadLibrary("ear_ring_core") }
    @JvmStatic external fun nativeDetectPitch(...)


```

The issue is that `@JvmStatic external fun` on an `object` actually needs to be in a `companion object` for JNI to work correctly. But there's a bigger problem—the JNI function names in the Rust code are `Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeDetectPitch`, but the Kotlin class structure doesn't match that naming convention.

The Rust code expects `EarRingCoreModule`, but the new Kotlin class is `EarRingCore`, so the JNI function names need to be updated to `Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_nativeDetectPitch`. Additionally, for a Kotlin `object` singleton, the JNI naming includes special encoding for the class structure that I need to account for.

Actually, wait—with `@JvmStatic external fun` on a Kotlin `object`, the JNI binding should be directly on the class itself, so the name would be `Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_nativeDetectPitch`. The current Rust code has `Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeDetectPitch`, which won't match and is causing the crash. When `startExercise()` calls `EarRingCore.generateSequence()`, which then calls `nativeGenerateSequence()`, the JNI lookup fails because the method name doesn't exist, throwing an `UnsatisfiedLinkError`.

The `loaded` flag gets set to true because `System.loadLibrary("ear_ring_core")` succeeds—the .so file exists—but the actual JNI function lookup fails at runtime when `nativeGenerateSequence()` is invoked. The fix is to rename the Rust JNI functions to match the correct Kotlin class structure.

Let me check the Rust lib.rs file to see what the current function names are.*

```
1. pub mod music_theory;
2. pub mod pitch_detection;
3. 
4. pub use music_theory::{
5.     freq_to_note, generate_sequence, midi_to_freq, scale_notes, staff_position, Note, NoteName,
6.     ScaleType,
7. };
8. pub use pitch_detection::detect_pitch;
9. 
10. // ── C-compatible FFI surface ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
11. // These thin wrappers are called from the React Native Turbo Native Module
12. // (Swift / Kotlin) without requiring uniffi code-gen at this stage.
13. 
14. use std::os::raw::{c_float, c_int, c_uchar, c_uint};
15. 
16. /// Detect pitch from a raw f32 PCM buffer.
17. ///
18. /// * `samples`       – pointer to f32 samples
19. /// * `num_samples`   – number of samples
20. /// * `sample_rate`   – sample rate in Hz
21. /// * `out_hz`        – out-parameter: detected frequency, 0.0 if not detected
22. ///
23. /// Returns 1 if a pitch was detected, 0 otherwise.
24. #[no_mangle]
25. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_detect_pitch(
26.     samples: *const c_float,
27.     num_samples: c_uint,
28.     sample_rate: c_uint,
29.     out_hz: *mut c_float,
30. ) -> c_int {
31.     if samples.is_null() || out_hz.is_null() {
32.         return 0;
33.     }
34.     let slice = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts(samples, num_samples as usize) };
35.     match detect_pitch(slice, sample_rate) {
36.         Some(hz) => {
37.             unsafe { *out_hz = hz };
38.             1
39.         }
40.         None => {
41.             unsafe { *out_hz = 0.0 };
42.             0
43.         }
44.     }
45. }
46. 
47. /// Convert a frequency to MIDI note number and cents deviation.
48. ///
49. /// * `hz`        – frequency in Hz
50. /// * `out_midi`  – out-parameter: MIDI note number (0–127)
51. /// * `out_cents` – out-parameter: deviation in cents (-50 to +50)
52. ///
53. /// Returns 1 on success, 0 if frequency is out of range.
54. #[no_mangle]
55. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_freq_to_note(
56.     hz: c_float,
57.     out_midi: *mut c_uchar,
58.     out_cents: *mut c_int,
59. ) -> c_int {
60.     if out_midi.is_null() || out_cents.is_null() {
61.         return 0;
62.     }
63.     match freq_to_note(hz) {
64.         Some((note, cents)) => {
65.             unsafe {
66.                 *out_midi = note.midi();
67.                 *out_cents = cents;
68.             }
69.             1
70.         }
71.         None => 0,
72.     }
73. }
74. 
75. /// Get the staff position for a MIDI note number.
76. ///
77. /// Returns diatonic steps above middle C (C4 = 0, D4 = 1, C5 = 7, B3 = -1, …).
78. #[no_mangle]
79. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_staff_position(midi: c_uchar) -> c_int {
80.     staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi))
81. }
82. 
83. /// Generate a sequence of MIDI note numbers.
84. ///
85. /// * `root_midi`   – MIDI number of the root note
86. /// * `scale_id`    – 0=Major, 1=NaturalMinor, 2=HarmonicMinor, 3=PentatonicMajor,
87. ///                   4=PentatonicMinor, 5=Dorian, 6=Mixolydian, 7=Blues
88. /// * `length`      – number of notes to generate
89. /// * `seed`        – random seed for reproducibility
90. /// * `out_buf`     – caller-allocated buffer of at least `length` bytes
91. ///
92. /// Returns the number of notes written, or -1 on error.
93. #[no_mangle]
94. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_generate_sequence(
95.     root_midi: c_uchar,
96.     scale_id: c_uchar,
97.     length: c_uchar,
98.     seed: u64,
99.     out_buf: *mut c_uchar,
100. ) -> c_int {
101.     if out_buf.is_null() {
102.         return -1;
103.     }
104.     let scale = match scale_id {
105.         0 => ScaleType::Major,
106.         1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
107.         2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
108.         3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
109.         4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
110.         5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
111.         6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
112.         7 => ScaleType::Blues,
113.         _ => return -1,
114.     };
115.     let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi);
116.     let notes = generate_sequence(root, scale, length, seed);
117.     let out = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(out_buf, length as usize) };
118.     for (i, note) in notes.iter().enumerate() {
119.         out[i] = note.midi();
120.     }
121.     notes.len() as c_int
122. }
123. 
124. // ── Android JNI exports ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125. // Exported with the exact symbol names the Kotlin EarRingCoreModule expects.
126. #[cfg(target_os = "android")]
127. mod android_jni {
128.     use jni::objects::{JClass, JFloatArray, JIntArray};
129.     use jni::sys::{jfloat, jfloatArray, jint, jintArray, jlong};
130.     use jni::JNIEnv;
131. 
132.     use super::{detect_pitch, freq_to_note, generate_sequence, staff_position, Note, ScaleType};
133. 
134.     #[no_mangle]
135.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeDetectPitch(
136.         mut env: JNIEnv,
137.         _class: JClass,
138.         samples: jfloatArray,
139.         sample_rate: jint,
140.     ) -> jfloat {
141.         let arr = unsafe { JFloatArray::from_raw(samples) };
142.         let len = match env.get_array_length(&arr) {
143.             Ok(l) => l as usize,
144.             Err(_) => return -1.0,
145.         };
146.         let mut buf = vec![0f32; len];
147.         if env.get_float_array_region(&arr, 0, &mut buf).is_err() {
148.             return -1.0;
149.         }
150.         match detect_pitch(&buf, sample_rate as u32) {
151.             Some(hz) => hz,
152.             None => -1.0,
153.         }
154.     }
155. 
156.     #[no_mangle]
157.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeFreqToMidi(
158.         _env: JNIEnv,
159.         _class: JClass,
160.         hz: jfloat,
161.     ) -> jint {
162.         match freq_to_note(hz) {
163.             Some((note, _)) => note.midi() as jint,
164.             None => -1,
165.         }
166.     }
167. 
168.     #[no_mangle]
169.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeFreqToCents(
170.         _env: JNIEnv,
171.         _class: JClass,
172.         hz: jfloat,
173.     ) -> jint {
174.         match freq_to_note(hz) {
175.             Some((_, cents)) => cents as jint,
176.             None => 0,
177.         }
178.     }
179. 
180.     #[no_mangle]
181.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeStaffPosition(
182.         _env: JNIEnv,
183.         _class: JClass,
184.         midi: jint,
185.     ) -> jint {
186.         staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi as u8)) as jint
187.     }
188. 
189.     #[no_mangle]
190.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_nativeGenerateSequence(
191.         mut env: JNIEnv,
192.         _class: JClass,
193.         root_midi: jint,
194.         scale_id: jint,
195.         length: jint,
196.         seed: jlong,
197.     ) -> jintArray {
198.         let scale = match scale_id {
199.             0 => ScaleType::Major,
200.             1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
201.             2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
202.             3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
203.             4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
204.             5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
205.             6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
206.             7 => ScaleType::Blues,
207.             _ => ScaleType::Major,
208.         };
209.         let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi as u8);
210.         let notes = generate_sequence(root, scale, length as u8, seed as u64);
211.         let midi_vals: Vec<jint> = notes.iter().map(|n| n.midi() as jint).collect();
212. 
213.         let arr: JIntArray = match env.new_int_array(midi_vals.len() as i32) {
214.             Ok(a) => a,
215.             Err(_) => return std::ptr::null_mut(),
216.         };
217.         let _ = env.set_int_array_region(&arr, 0, &midi_vals);
218.         arr.into_raw()
219.     }
220. }
221.
```

</details>


---

<sub>⏱️ 64m 2s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Found the bug. The JNI functions are all named `Java_com_earring_EarRingCoreModule_*` but the new Kotlin class is `EarRingCore` (an object). The JNI will look for `Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_*`.

I need to update the Rust JNI function names to use `EarRingCore` instead of `EarRingCoreModule`.

Also, there's another issue - in the Kotlin `EarRingCore` object, the external functions are declared directly on the object (not in a companion object). For a Kotlin `object` with `@JvmStatic external fun`, the JNI lookup would be for `Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_nativeDetectPitch`. But actually in Kotlin, `object` declarations create a singleton class. 

The `@JvmStatic` annotation makes these accessible as static methods on the Java class, so the JNI naming convention is `Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_nativeDetectPitch`. Now I'm updating the Rust code to rename all the JNI functions from `EarRingCoreModule` to `EarRingCore`.*

---

<details>
<summary>194 lines</summary>

```
1. package com.earring.ui
2. 
3. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.*
4. import androidx.compose.material3.*
5. import androidx.compose.runtime.*
6. import androidx.compose.ui.Alignment
7. import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
8. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
9. import androidx.compose.ui.text.font.FontWeight
10. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
11. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.sp
12. import com.earring.ExerciseStatus
13. import com.earring.ExerciseViewModel
14. import com.earring.MusicTheory
15. import com.earring.ui.components.MusicStaff
16. import com.earring.ui.components.NoteState
17. import com.earring.ui.components.PitchMeter
18. import com.earring.ui.components.StaffNote
19. 
20. @Composable
21. fun ExerciseScreen(
22.     viewModel: ExerciseViewModel,
23.     onNavigateResults: () -> Unit,
24.     onBack: () -> Unit
25. ) {
26.     val state by viewModel.state.collectAsState()
27. 
28.     // Navigate to results when exercise is done
29.     LaunchedEffect(state.status) {
30.         if (state.status == ExerciseStatus.DONE) {
31.             viewModel.stopListening()
32.             onNavigateResults()
33.         }
34.     }
35. 
36.     // Live pitch detection state for meter display
37.     var liveHz by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(-1f) }
38.     var liveMidi by remember { mutableIntStateOf(-1) }
39. 
40.     // When listening: run pitch detection and update live display
41.     LaunchedEffect(state.status) {
42.         if (state.status != ExerciseStatus.LISTENING) {
43.             liveHz = -1f
44.             liveMidi = -1
45.         }
46.     }
47. 
48.     Column(
49.         modifier = Modifier
50.             .fillMaxSize()
51.             .padding(16.dp),
52.         horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally
53.     ) {
54.         Row(
55.             modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
56.             verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
57.         ) {
58.             TextButton(onClick = {
59.                 viewModel.stopListening()
60.                 viewModel.audioPlayback.cancelPlayback()
61.                 onBack()
62.             }) { Text("← Back") }
63.             Spacer(Modifier.weight(1f))
64.             Text(
65.                 text = "${MusicTheory.NOTE_NAMES[state.rootNote]}${state.octave} ${MusicTheory.SCALE_NAMES[state.scaleId]}",
66.                 style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium
67.             )
68.         }
69. 
70.         Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp))
71. 
72.         // Staff
73.         val staffNotes = buildStaffNotes(state.sequence, state.detected, state.currentNoteIndex, state.highlightIndex)
74.         MusicStaff(notes = staffNotes, modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth())
75. 
76.         Spacer(Modifier.height(12.dp))
77. 
78.         // Status text
79.         val statusText = when (state.status) {
80.             ExerciseStatus.IDLE -> "Press Play to hear the sequence"
81.             ExerciseStatus.PLAYING -> "Listen carefully…"
82.             ExerciseStatus.LISTENING -> {
83.                 val idx = state.currentNoteIndex
84.                 if (idx < state.sequence.size)
85.                     "Sing note ${idx + 1} of ${state.sequence.size}: ${MusicTheory.midiToLabel(state.sequence[idx])}"
86.                 else "Done!"
87.             }
88.             ExerciseStatus.DONE -> "Calculating score…"
89.         }
90.         Text(text = statusText, style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)
91. 
92.         Spacer(Modifier.height(16.dp))
93. 
94.         // Pitch meter
95.         PitchMeter(
96.             detectedMidi = liveMidi,
97.             detectedHz = liveHz
98.         )
99. 
100.         Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
101. 
102.         // Control buttons
103.         when (state.status) {
104.             ExerciseStatus.IDLE -> {
105.                 Button(
106.                     onClick = { viewModel.playSequence() },
107.                     modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(52.dp)
108.                 ) { Text("▶ Play Sequence", fontSize = 17.sp) }
109.                 Spacer(Modifier.height(10.dp))
110.                 OutlinedButton(
111.                     onClick = {
112.                         // Start listening and wire live pitch to meter
113.                         viewModel.audioCapture.start(onAudio = { samples ->
114.                             val hz = com.earring.EarRingCore.detectPitch(samples, 44100)
115.                             if (hz > 0f) {
116.                                 liveHz = hz
117.                                 liveMidi = com.earring.EarRingCore.freqToMidi(hz)
118.                             } else {
119.                                 liveHz = -1f
120.                                 liveMidi = -1
121.                             }
122.                         })
123.                         viewModel.startListening()
124.                     },
125.                     modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(48.dp)
126.                 ) { Text("🎙 Start Listening", fontSize = 16.sp) }
127.             }
128.             ExerciseStatus.PLAYING -> {
129.                 OutlinedButton(
130.                     onClick = { viewModel.audioPlayback.cancelPlayback() },
131.                     modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(48.dp)
132.                 ) { Text("⏹ Stop Playback", fontSize = 16.sp) }
133.             }
134.             ExerciseStatus.LISTENING -> {
135.                 Button(
136.                     onClick = { viewModel.stopListening(); liveHz = -1f; liveMidi = -1 },
137.                     colors = ButtonDefaults.buttonColors(containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error),
138.                     modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(52.dp)
139.                 ) { Text("⏹ Stop Listening", fontSize = 17.sp) }
140.             }
141.             ExerciseStatus.DONE -> {
142.                 CircularProgressIndicator()
143.             }
144.         }
145. 
146.         // Note progress display
147.         if (state.sequence.isNotEmpty()) {
148.             Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
149.             Text("Notes:", style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelLarge)
150.             Spacer(Modifier.height(4.dp))
151.             Row(horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(6.dp)) {
152.                 state.sequence.forEachIndexed { idx, midi ->
153.                     val det = state.detected.getOrNull(idx)
154.                     val chip = when {
155.                         det == null && idx == state.currentNoteIndex -> "→"
156.                         det == null -> "○"
157.                         det.correct -> "✓"
158.                         else -> "✗"
159.                     }
160.                     val color = when {
161.                         det?.correct == true -> Color(0xFF4CAF50)
162.                         det?.correct == false -> Color(0xFFF44336)
163.                         idx == state.currentNoteIndex -> MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary
164.                         else -> MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
165.                     }
166.                     Column(horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally) {
167.                         Text(text = chip, color = color, fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold)
168.                         Text(text = MusicTheory.midiToLabel(midi), fontSize = 10.sp, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)
169.                     }
170.                 }
171.             }
172.         }
173.     }
174. }
175. 
176. private fun buildStaffNotes(
177.     sequence: List<Int>,
178.     detected: List<com.earring.DetectedNote>,
179.     currentIdx: Int,
180.     highlightIdx: Int
181. ): List<StaffNote> {
182.     return sequence.mapIndexed { idx, midi ->
183.         val det = detected.getOrNull(idx)
184.         val noteState = when {
185.             highlightIdx == idx -> NoteState.ACTIVE
186.             det == null && idx == currentIdx -> NoteState.ACTIVE
187.             det == null -> NoteState.EXPECTED
188.             det.correct -> NoteState.CORRECT
189.             else -> NoteState.INCORRECT
190.         }
191.         StaffNote(midi = midi, state = noteState)
192.     }
193. }
194.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>147 lines</summary>

```
1. package com.earring
2. 
3. import android.content.Context
4. import android.media.MediaPlayer
5. import android.util.Log
6. import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
7. import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
8. import kotlinx.coroutines.Job
9. import kotlinx.coroutines.delay
10. import kotlinx.coroutines.isActive
11. import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
12. import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
13. import java.io.File
14. import java.io.FileOutputStream
15. import java.net.URL
16. import kotlin.math.pow
17. import kotlin.math.roundToInt
18. 
19. class AudioPlayback(private val context: Context) {
20. 
21.     private val scope = CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO)
22.     private var sequenceJob: Job? = null
23.     private val activePlayers = mutableListOf<MediaPlayer>()
24. 
25.     // Available piano sample notes as MIDI values
26.     // A0=21, C1=24, Ds1=27, Fs1=30, A1=33, C2=36, Ds2=39, Fs2=42, A2=45,
27.     // C3=48, Ds3=51, Fs3=54, A3=57, C4=60, Ds4=63, Fs4=66, A4=69, C5=72,
28.     // Ds5=75, Fs5=78, A5=81, C6=84, Ds6=87, Fs6=90, A6=93, C7=96, Ds7=99, Fs7=102, A7=105, C8=108
29.     private val sampleMidiNotes = listOf(
30.         21, 24, 27, 30, 33, 36, 39, 42, 45,
31.         48, 51, 54, 57, 60, 63, 66, 69, 72,
32.         75, 78, 81, 84, 87, 90, 93, 96, 99, 102, 105, 108
33.     )
34. 
35.     private val sampleNames = mapOf(
36.         21 to "A0", 24 to "C1", 27 to "Ds1", 30 to "Fs1", 33 to "A1",
37.         36 to "C2", 39 to "Ds2", 42 to "Fs2", 45 to "A2",
38.         48 to "C3", 51 to "Ds3", 54 to "Fs3", 57 to "A3",
39.         60 to "C4", 63 to "Ds4", 66 to "Fs4", 69 to "A4",
40.         72 to "C5", 75 to "Ds5", 78 to "Fs5", 81 to "A5",
41.         84 to "C6", 87 to "Ds6", 90 to "Fs6", 93 to "A6",
42.         96 to "C7", 99 to "Ds7", 102 to "Fs7", 105 to "A7", 108 to "C8"
43.     )
44. 
45.     private val BASE_URL = "https://tonejs.github.io/audio/salamander/"
46.     private val cacheDir = File(context.cacheDir, "piano_samples").also { it.mkdirs() }
47. 
48.     private fun nearestSampleMidi(midi: Int): Int {
49.         return sampleMidiNotes.minByOrNull { kotlin.math.abs(it - midi) } ?: 60
50.     }
51. 
52.     private fun pitchRate(requestedMidi: Int, sampleMidi: Int): Float {
53.         val delta = requestedMidi - sampleMidi
54.         return 2f.pow(delta / 12f)
55.     }
56. 
57.     private suspend fun getCachedSample(midi: Int): File? = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) {
58.         val name = sampleNames[midi] ?: return@withContext null
59.         val file = File(cacheDir, "$name.mp3")
60.         if (file.exists() && file.length() > 0) return@withContext file
61.         try {
62.             val url = URL("$BASE_URL${name}.mp3")
63.             val connection = url.openConnection().apply {
64.                 connectTimeout = 10_000
65.                 readTimeout = 30_000
66.             }
67.             connection.getInputStream().use { input ->
68.                 FileOutputStream(file).use { output ->
69.                     input.copyTo(output)
70.                 }
71.             }
72.             file
73.         } catch (e: Exception) {
74.             Log.e("AudioPlayback", "Failed to download $name: ${e.message}")
75.             file.delete()
76.             null
77.         }
78.     }
79. 
80.     fun playNote(midi: Int) {
81.         scope.launch {
82.             val nearestMidi = nearestSampleMidi(midi)
83.             val sampleFile = getCachedSample(nearestMidi) ?: return@launch
84.             val rate = pitchRate(midi, nearestMidi)
85.             withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
86.                 playFile(sampleFile, rate)
87.             }
88.         }
89.     }
90. 
91.     private fun playFile(file: File, rate: Float) {
92.         try {
93.             val player = MediaPlayer().apply {
94.                 setDataSource(file.absolutePath)
95.                 prepare()
96.                 playbackParams = playbackParams.setSpeed(rate.coerceIn(0.5f, 2.0f))
97.                 setOnCompletionListener {
98.                     it.release()
99.                     synchronized(activePlayers) { activePlayers.remove(it) }
100.                 }
101.                 setOnErrorListener { mp, _, _ ->
102.                     mp.release()
103.                     synchronized(activePlayers) { activePlayers.remove(mp) }
104.                     false
105.                 }
106.             }
107.             synchronized(activePlayers) { activePlayers.add(player) }
108.             player.start()
109.         } catch (e: Exception) {
110.             Log.e("AudioPlayback", "Error playing file: ${e.message}")
111.         }
112.     }
113. 
114.     fun cancelPlayback() {
115.         sequenceJob?.cancel()
116.         sequenceJob = null
117.         synchronized(activePlayers) {
118.             activePlayers.forEach { runCatching { it.stop(); it.release() } }
119.             activePlayers.clear()
120.         }
121.     }
122. 
123.     fun playSequence(
124.         midiNotes: List<Int>,
125.         onEach: (Int) -> Unit = {},
126.         onDone: () -> Unit = {}
127.     ) {
128.         cancelPlayback()
129.         sequenceJob = scope.launch {
130.             for ((index, midi) in midiNotes.withIndex()) {
131.                 if (!isActive) break
132.                 withContext(Dispatchers.Main) { onEach(index) }
133.                 val nearestMidi = nearestSampleMidi(midi)
134.                 val sampleFile = getCachedSample(nearestMidi)
135.                 if (sampleFile != null) {
136.                     val rate = pitchRate(midi, nearestMidi)
137.                     withContext(Dispatchers.Main) { playFile(sampleFile, rate) }
138.                 }
139.                 delay(600)
140.             }
141.             if (isActive) {
142.                 withContext(Dispatchers.Main) { onDone() }
143.             }
144.         }
145.     }
146. }
147.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 70m 41s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*I can see the problem. In `ExerciseScreen.kt`, the "Start Listening" button has a duplicate audio capture start:

```kotlin
ExerciseStatus.IDLE -\> {
    Button(
        onClick = { viewModel.playSequence() },
        ...
    ) { Text("▶ Play Sequence", ...) }
    Spacer(...)
    OutlinedButton(
        onClick = {
            // Start listening and wire live pitch to meter
            viewModel.audioCapture.start(onAudio = { samples -\>
                ...
            })
            viewModel.startListening()
        },
        ...
    ) { Text("🎙 Start Listening", ...) }
}
```

The issue is that `viewModel.startListening()` also calls `audioCapture.start()` internally, so there are two calls to `start()` happening. But looking deeper, when Play Sequence is clicked, the ViewModel is also starting audio capture internally, which could be conflicting with the listening state.

Actually, I think the real problem is that the "Start Listening" button is calling both `viewModel.audioCapture.start()` directly AND `viewModel.startListening()`, which means `start()` gets called twice. This double-call might be preventing the state from updating properly to LISTENING, which is why the UI still shows the IDLE buttons instead of switching to the listening state. Looking at the logcat timing and the UI state, the audio that played was from a previous tap—the current tap at (540, 1103) didn't actually trigger the Play Sequence action, which is why the status never changed from IDLE and the button text still shows "Press Play to hear the sequence."

Now I need to fix the duplicate `audioCapture.start()` call in ExerciseScreen, test the "Start Listening" functionality, and address the remaining issues like the blank root note chips and whether highlighting during playback is actually working or just too fast to see.

Looking at the HomeScreen's ChipRow, the 12 note chips are crammed into a single Row where each gets equal weight, leaving only about 80 pixels per chip—way too narrow for the text to display properly. I see the ChipRow component is using `weight(1f)` on each of the 12 note name chips, which should distribute them evenly across the available width—but on a 1080 pixel wide screen, that might be causing layout issues or making them too small to render properly.

Looking at the UI dump, there's a suspicious gap between the "Root Note" label and "Octave" section where the chips should appear, which suggests they might be rendering but either too narrow to display text or not showing up at all due to size constraints.

Examining the actual screenshot more carefully, I can see the note chips are indeed rendering as small white rectangles in the Root Note row, just without any visible text inside them. The octave chips below show text fine, which confirms the issue is specifically with the note name chips being too narrow. The solution is to apply the same WrappingChipRow approach to the note names that's already working for the octave chips.

I also need to address the duplicate audioCapture.start() call in ExerciseScreen while I'm making these layout fixes.*

````
# GitHub Copilot CLI Documentation

## README
# GitHub Copilot CLI

The power of GitHub Copilot, now in your terminal.

GitHub Copilot CLI brings AI-powered coding assistance directly to your command line, enabling you to build, debug, and understand code through natural language conversations. Powered by the same agentic harness as GitHub's Copilot coding agent, it provides intelligent assistance while staying deeply integrated with your GitHub workflow.

See [our official documentation](https://docs.github.com/copilot/concepts/agents/about-copilot-cli) for more information.

![Image of the splash screen for the Copilot CLI](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f40aa23d-09dd-499e-9457-1d57d3368887)


## 🚀 Introduction and Overview

We're bringing the power of GitHub Copilot coding agent directly to your terminal. With GitHub Copilot CLI, you can work locally and synchronously with an AI agent that understands your code and GitHub context.

- **Terminal-native development:** Work with Copilot coding agent directly in your command line — no context switching required.
- **GitHub integration out of the box:** Access your repositories, issues, and pull requests using natural language, all authenticated with your existing GitHub account.
- **Agentic capabilities:** Build, edit, debug, and refactor code with an AI collaborator that can plan and execute complex tasks.
- **MCP-powered extensibility:** Take advantage of the fact that the coding agent ships with GitHub's MCP server by default and supports custom MCP servers to extend capabilities.
- **Full control:** Preview every action before execution — nothing happens without your explicit approval.

We're still early in our journey, but with your feedback, we're rapidly iterating to make the GitHub Copilot CLI the best possible companion in your terminal.

## 📦 Getting Started

### Supported Platforms

- **Linux**
- **macOS**
- **Windows**

### Prerequisites

- (On Windows) **PowerShell** v6 or higher
- An **active Copilot subscription**. See [Copilot plans](https://github.com/features/copilot/plans?ref_cta=Copilot+plans+signup&ref_loc=install-copilot-cli&ref_page=docs).

If you have access to GitHub Copilot via your organization or enterprise, you cannot use GitHub Copilot CLI if your organization owner or enterprise administrator has disabled it in the organization or enterprise settings. See [Managing policies and features for GitHub Copilot in your organization](http://docs.github.com/copilot/managing-copilot/managing-github-copilot-in-your-organization/managing-github-copilot-features-in-your-organization/managing-policies-for-copilot-in-your-organization) for more information.

### Installation

Install with the install script (macOS and Linux):

```bash
curl -fsSL https://gh.io/copilot-install | bash
```

Or

```bash
wget -qO- https://gh.io/copilot-install | bash
```

Use `| sudo bash` to run as root and install to `/usr/local/bin`.

Set `PREFIX` to install to `$PREFIX/bin/` directory. Defaults to `/usr/local`
when run as root or `$HOME/.local` when run as a non-root user.

Set `VERSION` to install a specific version. Defaults to the latest version.

For example, to install version `v0.0.369` to a custom directory:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://gh.io/copilot-install | VERSION="v0.0.369" PREFIX="$HOME/custom" bash
```

Install with [Homebrew](https://formulae.brew.sh/cask/copilot-cli) (macOS and Linux):

```bash
brew install copilot-cli
```

```bash
brew install copilot-cli@prerelease
```


Install with [WinGet](https://github.com/microsoft/winget-cli) (Windows):

```bash
winget install GitHub.Copilot
```

```bash
winget install GitHub.Copilot.Prerelease
```


Install with [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot) (macOS, Linux, and Windows):

```bash
npm install -g @github/copilot
```

```bash
npm install -g @github/copilot@prerelease
```


### Launching the CLI

```bash
copilot
```

On first launch, you'll be greeted with our adorable animated banner! If you'd like to see this banner again, launch `copilot` with the `--banner` flag.

If you're not currently logged in to GitHub, you'll be prompted to use the `/login` slash command. Enter this command and follow the on-screen instructions to authenticate.

#### Authenticate with a Personal Access Token (PAT)

You can also authenticate using a fine-grained PAT with the "Copilot Requests" permission enabled.

1. Visit https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens/new
2. Under "Permissions," click "add permissions" and select "Copilot Requests"
3. Generate your token
4. Add the token to your environment via the environment variable `GH_TOKEN` or `GITHUB_TOKEN` (in order of precedence)

### Using the CLI

Launch `copilot` in a folder that contains code you want to work with.

By default, `copilot` utilizes Claude Sonnet 4.5. Run the `/model` slash command to choose from other available models, including Claude Sonnet 4 and GPT-5.

### Experimental Mode

Experimental mode enables access to new features that are still in development. You can activate experimental mode by:

- Launching with the `--experimental` flag: `copilot --experimental`
- Using the `/experimental` slash command from within the CLI

Once activated, the setting is persisted in your config, so the `--experimental` flag is no longer needed on subsequent launches.

#### Experimental Features

- **Autopilot mode:** Autopilot is a new mode (press `Shift+Tab` to cycle through modes), which encourages the agent to continue working until a task is completed.

Each time you submit a prompt to GitHub Copilot CLI, your monthly quota of premium requests is reduced by one. For information about premium requests, see [About premium requests](https://docs.github.com/copilot/managing-copilot/monitoring-usage-and-entitlements/about-premium-requests).

For more information about how to use the GitHub Copilot CLI, see [our official documentation](https://docs.github.com/copilot/concepts/agents/about-copilot-cli).

## 🔧 Configuring LSP Servers

GitHub Copilot CLI supports Language Server Protocol (LSP) for enhanced code intelligence. This feature provides intelligent code features like go-to-definition, hover information, and diagnostics.

### Installing Language Servers

Copilot CLI does not bundle LSP servers. You need to install them separately. For example, to set up TypeScript support:

```bash
npm install -g typescript-language-server
```

For other languages, install the corresponding LSP server and configure it following the same pattern shown below.

### Configuring LSP Servers

LSP servers are configured through a dedicated LSP configuration file. You can configure LSP servers at the user level or repository level:

**User-level configuration** (applies to all projects):
Edit `~/.copilot/lsp-config.json`

**Repository-level configuration** (applies to specific project):
Create `.github/lsp.json` in your repository root

Example configuration:

```json
{
  "lspServers": {
    "typescript": {
      "command": "typescript-language-server",
      "args": ["--stdio"],
      "fileExtensions": {
        ".ts": "typescript",
        ".tsx": "typescript"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Viewing LSP Server Status

Check configured LSP servers using the `/lsp` command in an interactive session, or view your configuration files directly.

For more information, see the [changelog](./changelog.md).

## 📢 Feedback and Participation

We're excited to have you join us early in the Copilot CLI journey.

We're building quickly. Expect frequent updates--please keep your client up to date for the latest features and fixes!

Your insights are invaluable! Open an issue in this repo, join Discussions, and run `/feedback` from the CLI to submit a confidential feedback survey!


## Help Command Output
Global
  @ - mention files, include contents in context
  ctrl+s - run command while preserving input
  shift+tab - cycle modes (interactive → plan)
  ctrl+t - toggle model reasoning display
  ctrl+o - expand recent timeline (when no input)
  ctrl+e - expand all timeline (when no input)
  ↑ ↓ - navigate command history
  ! - execute command in your local shell (bypass Copilot)
  Esc - cancel the current operation
  ctrl+c - cancel operation / clear input / exit
  ctrl+d - shutdown
  ctrl+l - clear the screen

Editing
  ctrl+a - move to beginning of line (when typing)
  ctrl+e - move to end of line (when typing)
  ctrl+h - delete previous character
  ctrl+w - delete previous word
  ctrl+u - delete from cursor to beginning of line
  ctrl+k - delete from cursor to end of line (joins lines at end of line)
  meta+← → - move cursor by word
  ctrl+g - edit prompt in external editor

Agent environment
  /init - Initialize Copilot instructions for this repository, or suppress the init suggestion
  /agent - Browse and select from available agents (if any)
  /skills - Manage skills for enhanced capabilities
  /mcp - Manage MCP server configuration
  /plugin - Manage plugins and plugin marketplaces

Models and subagents
  /model - Select AI model to use
  /fleet - Enable fleet mode for parallel subagent execution
  /tasks - View and manage background tasks (subagents and shell sessions)

Code
  /ide - Connect to an IDE workspace
  /diff - Review the changes made in the current directory
  /review - Run code review agent to analyze changes
  /lsp - Manage language server configuration
  /terminal-setup - Configure terminal for multiline input support (shift+enter)

Permissions
  /allow-all - Enable all permissions (tools, paths, and URLs)
  /add-dir - Add a directory to the allowed list for file access
  /list-dirs - Display all allowed directories for file access
  /cwd - Change working directory or show current directory
  /reset-allowed-tools - Reset the list of allowed tools

Session
  /resume - Switch to a different session (optionally specify session ID)
  /rename - Rename the current session (alias for /session rename)
  /context - Show context window token usage and visualization
  /usage - Display session usage metrics and statistics
  /session - Show session info and workspace summary. Use subcommands for details.
  /compact - Summarize conversation history to reduce context window usage
  /share - Share session or research report to markdown file or GitHub gist
  /copy - Copy the last response to the clipboard

Help and feedback
  /help - Show help for interactive commands
  /changelog - Display changelog for CLI versions
  /feedback - Provide feedback about the CLI
  /theme - View or configure terminal theme
  /update - Update the CLI to the latest version
  /experimental - Show available experimental features, or enable/disable experimental mode
  /clear - Clear the conversation history
  /instructions - View and toggle custom instruction files
  /streamer-mode - Toggle streamer mode (hides preview model names and quota details for streaming)

Other commands
  /exit, /quit - Exit the CLI
  /login - Log in to Copilot
  /logout - Log out of Copilot
  /plan - Create an implementation plan before coding
  /research - Run deep research investigation using GitHub search and web sources
  /user - Manage GitHub user list

Copilot respects instructions from these locations:
  CLAUDE.md
  GEMINI.md
  AGENTS.md (in git root & cwd)
  .github/instructions/**/*.instructions.md (in git root & cwd)
  .github/copilot-instructions.md
  $HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md
  COPILOT_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS_DIRS (additional directories via env var)

To learn about what I can do
  Ask me: "What can you do?"
  Or visit: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/use-copilot-cli

---

**Note**: If the above documentation doesn't answer the user's request, you can fetch additional documentation from the official GitHub Docs for more information.

Use the web_fetch tool to fetch https://docs.github.com/api/article/body?pathname=/en/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/use-copilot-cli

You can also follow hyperlinks to related docs pages using the same API pattern.
````

<details>

```
1. package com.earring
2. 
3. import android.app.Application
4. import android.os.VibrationEffect
5. import android.os.Vibrator
6. import android.os.VibratorManager
7. import android.content.Context
8. import android.os.Build

10. import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
11. import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
12. import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
13. import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
14. import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
15. 
16. data class DetectedNote(val midi: Int, val cents: Int, val correct: Boolean)
17. 
18. enum class ExerciseStatus { IDLE, PLAYING, LISTENING, DONE }
19. 
20. data class ExerciseState(
21.     val rootNote: Int = 0,        // 0=C, 1=C#, ..., 11=B
22.     val octave: Int = 4,
23.     val scaleId: Int = 0,
24.     val sequenceLength: Int = 4,
25.     val sequence: List<Int> = emptyList(),
26.     val detected: List<DetectedNote> = emptyList(),
27.     val status: ExerciseStatus = ExerciseStatus.IDLE,
28.     val currentNoteIndex: Int = 0,
29.     val seed: Long = System.currentTimeMillis(),
30.     val highlightIndex: Int = -1
31. ) {
32.     val rootMidi: Int get() = (octave + 1) * 12 + rootNote
33.     val score: Float get() {
34.         if (detected.isEmpty()) return 0f
35.         return detected.count { it.correct }.toFloat() / detected.size
36.     }
37. }
38. 
39. class ExerciseViewModel(application: Application) : AndroidViewModel(application) {
40. 
41.     private val _state = MutableStateFlow(ExerciseState())
42.     val state: StateFlow<ExerciseState> = _state.asStateFlow()
43. 
44.     val audioCapture = AudioCapture()
45.     val audioPlayback = AudioPlayback(application)
46. 
47.     // Live pitch for the meter display (updated on every audio frame while listening)
48.     private val _liveHz = MutableStateFlow(-1f)
49.     val liveHz: StateFlow<Float> = _liveHz.asStateFlow()
50. 
51.     // Pitch stability tracking
52.     private var stableCount = 0
53.     private var stablePitchClass = -1
54.     private var lastConfirmedMs = 0L
55.     private val STABLE_FRAMES_REQUIRED = 2
56.     private val CONFIRM_HOLD_MS = 450L
57.     private val SILENCE_RMS_THRESHOLD = 0.003f
58. 
59.     private val vibrator: Vibrator by lazy {
60.         if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.S) {
61.             (application.getSystemService(Context.VIBRATOR_MANAGER_SERVICE) as VibratorManager).defaultVibrator
62.         } else {
63.             @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
64.             application.getSystemService(Context.VIBRATOR_SERVICE) as Vibrator
65.         }
66.     }
67. 
68.     fun setRootNote(note: Int) { _state.value = _state.value.copy(rootNote = note) }
69.     fun setOctave(octave: Int) { _state.value = _state.value.copy(octave = octave) }
70.     fun setScaleId(id: Int) { _state.value = _state.value.copy(scaleId = id) }
71.     fun setSequenceLength(len: Int) { _state.value = _state.value.copy(sequenceLength = len) }
72. 
73.     fun startExercise() {
74.         val s = _state.value
75.         val seed = System.currentTimeMillis()
76.         val seq = EarRingCore.generateSequence(s.rootMidi, s.scaleId, s.sequenceLength, seed)
77.         _state.value = s.copy(
78.             sequence = seq.toList(),
79.             detected = emptyList(),
80.             status = ExerciseStatus.IDLE,
81.             currentNoteIndex = 0,
82.             seed = seed,
83.             highlightIndex = -1
84.         )
85.         resetStability()
86.     }
87. 
88.     fun playSequence(onDone: () -> Unit = {}) {
89.         val seq = _state.value.sequence
90.         if (seq.isEmpty()) return
91.         _state.value = _state.value.copy(status = ExerciseStatus.PLAYING, highlightIndex = -1)
92.         audioPlayback.playSequence(
93.             midiNotes = seq,
94.             onEach = { idx -> _state.value = _state.value.copy(highlightIndex = idx) },
95.             onDone = {
96.                 _state.value = _state.value.copy(status = ExerciseStatus.IDLE, highlightIndex = -1)
97.                 onDone()
98.             }
99.         )
100.     }
101. 
102.     fun startListening() {
103.         resetStability()
104.         _state.value = _state.value.copy(status = ExerciseStatus.LISTENING)
105.         _liveHz.value = -1f
106.         audioCapture.start(onAudio = { samples -> processSamples(samples) })
107.     }
108. 
109.     fun stopListening() {
110.         audioCapture.stop()
111.         _state.value = _state.value.copy(status = ExerciseStatus.IDLE)
112.         _liveHz.value = -1f
113.         resetStability()
114.     }
115. 
116.     private fun processSamples(samples: FloatArray) {
117.         val rms = kotlin.math.sqrt(samples.map { it * it }.average().toFloat())
118.         if (rms < SILENCE_RMS_THRESHOLD) {
119.             resetStability()
120.             return
121.         }
122. 
123.         val hz = EarRingCore.detectPitch(samples, 44100)
124.         if (hz <= 0f) {
125.             _liveHz.value = -1f
126.             resetStability()
127.             return
128.         }
129.         _liveHz.value = hz
130. 
131.         val midi = EarRingCore.freqToMidi(hz)
132.         if (midi < 0) { resetStability(); return }
133.         val pitchClass = midi % 12
134.         val cents = EarRingCore.freqToCents(hz)
135. 
136.         if (pitchClass == stablePitchClass) {
137.             stableCount++
138.         } else {
139.             stablePitchClass = pitchClass
140.             stableCount = 1
141.             lastConfirmedMs = 0L
142.         }
143. 
144.         if (stableCount >= STABLE_FRAMES_REQUIRED) {
145.             val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
146.             if (lastConfirmedMs == 0L) {
147.                 lastConfirmedMs = now
148.             } else if (now - lastConfirmedMs >= CONFIRM_HOLD_MS) {
149.                 confirmNote(midi, cents)
150.                 lastConfirmedMs = now + CONFIRM_HOLD_MS * 2  // debounce
151.             }
152.         }
153.     }
154. 
155.     private fun confirmNote(midi: Int, cents: Int) {
156.         val state = _state.value
157.         if (state.status != ExerciseStatus.LISTENING) return
158.         val idx = state.currentNoteIndex
159.         if (idx >= state.sequence.size) return
160. 
161.         val expected = state.sequence[idx]
162.         val correct = MusicTheory.isCorrect(midi, cents, expected)
163. 
164.         vibrate(correct)
165. 
166.         val detected = DetectedNote(midi, cents, correct)
167.         val newDetected = state.detected + detected
168. 
169.         if (correct) {
170.             val nextIdx = idx + 1
171.             if (nextIdx >= state.sequence.size) {
172.                 audioCapture.stop()
173.                 _state.value = state.copy(
174.                     detected = newDetected,
175.                     currentNoteIndex = nextIdx,
176.                     status = ExerciseStatus.DONE
177.                 )
178.             } else {
179.                 _state.value = state.copy(
180.                     detected = newDetected,
181.                     currentNoteIndex = nextIdx
182.                 )
183.                 resetStability()
184.             }
185.         } else {
186.             audioCapture.stop()
187.             _state.value = state.copy(
188.                 detected = newDetected,
189.                 currentNoteIndex = state.sequence.size,
190.                 status = ExerciseStatus.DONE
191.             )
192.         }
193.     }
194. 
195.     fun newRound() {
196.         startExercise()
197.     }
198. 
199.     private fun resetStability() {
200.         stableCount = 0
201.         stablePitchClass = -1
202.         lastConfirmedMs = 0L
203.     }
204. 
205.     private fun vibrate(success: Boolean) {
206.         try {
207.             if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
208.                 val effect = if (success)
209.                     VibrationEffect.createOneShot(80, VibrationEffect.DEFAULT_AMPLITUDE)
210.                 else
211.                     VibrationEffect.createWaveform(longArrayOf(0, 100, 80, 100), -1)
212.                 vibrator.vibrate(effect)
213.             } else {
214.                 @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
215.                 vibrator.vibrate(if (success) 80 else 200)
216.             }
217.         } catch (e: Exception) {
218.             android.util.Log.e("ExerciseViewModel", "Vibrate error: ${e.message}")
219.         }
220.     }
221. 
222.     override fun onCleared() {
223.         super.onCleared()
224.         audioCapture.stop()
225.         audioPlayback.cancelPlayback()
226.     }
227. }
228.
```

</details>


---

<sub>⏱️ 77m 23s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*The ViewModel logic looks correct. The `playSequence()` function:
1. Sets status to PLAYING
2. Calls `audioPlayback.playSequence()` with `onEach` (updates highlightIndex) and `onDone` (resets to IDLE)

The issue is that when we took the screenshot at 5 seconds, the sequence had already completed (4 notes × 600ms = 2.4s) and it was back to IDLE state.

The note highlighting not updating during playback - let me think. Looking at the `MusicStaff` composable and the `buildStaffNotes` function:


The highlight should update since `highlightIndex` is in the StateFlow and `onEach` is called to update it on the main thread via `withContext(Dispatchers.Main)`.

But I'm realizing the real issue: the audio samples are being downloaded from the CDN on first playback, which takes 10-15 seconds. So `getCachedSample()` blocks during that initial download, meaning the sequence doesn't actually start playing right away—the first note gets delayed significantly while waiting for the sample to download. Looking at the UI state and button labels, the status is definitely IDLE, which means the sequence either finished or never started. Since the samples like A4, D4, and E4 are likely cached from earlier interactions, the whole sequence would complete in about 2.4 seconds—well before the 5-second mark when the screenshot was taken. The real issue is that the wait time is too long to actually observe the PLAYING state in action. to IDLE. The UI is functioning correctly.

Now I need to test the actual pitch detection with Start Listening, which is the core functionality. The emulator doesn't have a microphone by default, so I'm not sure if audio input will work, but let me try tapping Start Listening and see what happens.*

---
