18. @Composable
19. fun ResultsScreen(
20.     viewModel: ExerciseViewModel,
21.     progressViewModel: ProgressViewModel,
22.     onTryAgain: () -> Unit,
23.     onNewExercise: () -> Unit,
24.     onProgress: () -> Unit
25. ) {
26.     val state by viewModel.state.collectAsState()
27. 
28.     // Save session on first composition
29.     var saved by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
30.     LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
31.         if (!saved) {
32.             saved = true
33.             val scaleName = MusicTheory.SCALE_NAMES[state.scaleId]
34.             val rootLabel = MusicTheory.NOTE_NAMES[state.rootNote] + state.octave.toString()
35.             progressViewModel.addSession(scaleName, rootLabel, state.score, state.sequenceLength)
36.         }
37.     }
38. 
39.     val scorePercent = (state.score * 100).toInt()
40.     val emoji = when {
41.         scorePercent == 100 -> "🏆"
42.         scorePercent >= 80 -> "🎉"
43.         scorePercent >= 50 -> "👍"
44.         else -> "💪"
45.     }
46. 
47.     Column(
48.         modifier = Modifier
49.             .fillMaxSize()
50.             .verticalScroll(rememberScrollState())
51.             .padding(16.dp),
52.         horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally
53.     ) {
54.         Spacer(Modifier.height(32.dp))
55.         Text(emoji, fontSize = 64.sp)
56.         Spacer(Modifier.height(12.dp))
57.         Text("$scorePercent%", fontSize = 56.sp, fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold, color = scoreColor(scorePercent))
58.         Text("Score", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)
59.         Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp))
60.         Text(
61.             text = "${MusicTheory.NOTE_NAMES[state.rootNote]}${state.octave}  ${MusicTheory.SCALE_NAMES[state.scaleId]}",
62.             style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge
63.         )
64. 
65.         Spacer(Modifier.height(24.dp))
66.         HorizontalDivider()
67.         Spacer(Modifier.height(16.dp))
68. 
69.         Text("Note by Note", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium, fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold)
70.         Spacer(Modifier.height(12.dp))
71. 
72.         state.sequence.forEachIndexed { idx, expectedMidi ->
73.             val det = state.detected.getOrNull(idx)
74.             NoteResultRow(
75.                 index = idx + 1,
76.                 expectedLabel = MusicTheory.midiToLabel(expectedMidi),
77.                 detectedLabel = if (det != null) MusicTheory.midiToLabel(det.midi) else "—",
78.                 correct = det?.correct ?: false,
79.                 wasAttempted = det != null
80.             )
81.             if (idx < state.sequence.lastIndex) HorizontalDivider(color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surfaceVariant)
82.         }
83. 
84.         Spacer(Modifier.height(28.dp))
85. 
86.         Button(
87.             onClick = onTryAgain,
88.             modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(52.dp)
89.         ) { Text("🔄 Try Again", fontSize = 17.sp) }
90.         Spacer(Modifier.height(10.dp))
91.         OutlinedButton(
92.             onClick = onNewExercise,
93.             modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(48.dp)
94.         ) { Text("🏠 New Exercise", fontSize = 16.sp) }
95.         Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp))
96.         OutlinedButton(
97.             onClick = onProgress,
98.             modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(48.dp)
99.         ) { Text("📊 View Progress", fontSize = 16.sp) }
100.         Spacer(Modifier.height(16.dp))
101.     }
102. }
103. 
104. @Composable
105. private fun NoteResultRow(
106.     index: Int,
107.     expectedLabel: String,
108.     detectedLabel: String,
109.     correct: Boolean,
110.     wasAttempted: Boolean
111. ) {
112.     Row(
113.         modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().padding(vertical = 10.dp),
114.         verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
115.     ) {
116.         Text("$index.", modifier = Modifier.width(28.dp), style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)
117.         Text("Expected: $expectedLabel", modifier = Modifier.weight(1f), style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium)
118.         Text("Sung: $detectedLabel", modifier = Modifier.weight(1f), style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium)
119.         Text(
120.             text = if (!wasAttempted) "—" else if (correct) "✓" else "✗",
121.             color = if (!wasAttempted) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant else if (correct) Color(0xFF4CAF50) else Color(0xFFF44336),
122.             fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold,
123.             fontSize = 20.sp
124.         )
125.     }
126. }
127.

132. }

<sub>⏱️ 471m 14s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*The user wants to know how to test the app on their real Android phone. Let me give them the steps.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 471m 15s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Here's how to sideload the debug APK onto your phone:

**1. Enable Developer Options on your phone**
- Go to **Settings → About phone**
- Tap **Build number** 7 times until you see "You are now a developer"
- Go back to **Settings → Developer options**
- Enable **USB debugging**

**2. Connect via USB**
- Plug your phone into your PC with a USB cable
- On your phone, tap **Allow** when prompted to trust this computer

**3. Verify adb sees your phone**
```
adb devices
```
You should see your device listed (not just the emulator).

**4. Install the APK**
```
adb install -r C:\work\ear_ring\android\app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk
```

**5. Launch it**
```
adb shell am start -n com.earring/.MainActivity
```

Or just find **Ear Ring** in your app drawer and tap it.

---

**If you'd rather go wireless (no USB after first setup):**
```
adb tcpip 5555
adb connect \<your-phone-ip\>:5555
```
Then unplug the cable and `adb install` works over Wi-Fi.

---

The APK is already built at `android\app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk` — no rebuild needed. Once on a real device the mic will work and you can test the full pitch detection → exercise completion → results flow.

---

<sub>⏱️ 473m 20s</sub>

### 👤 User

how do i tell adb  which device to use

---

<sub>⏱️ 473m 22s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Use the `-s` flag with the device serial from `adb devices`:

```
adb devices
```
Output looks like:
```
List of devices attached
emulator-5554          device
R3CN90ABCDE            device   ← your phone's serial
```

Then target a specific device:
```
adb -s R3CN90ABCDE install -r android\app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk
```

**Shortcut flags** if you only have one of each type connected:
- `-d` — target the only real USB device (ignores emulators)
- `-e` — target the only emulator

```
adb -d install -r android\app\build\outputs\apk\debug\app-debug.apk
```

That's the easiest option when you have one phone + one emulator running.

---

22. @Composable
23. fun EarRingApp() {
24.     val navController = rememberNavController()
25.     val exerciseViewModel: ExerciseViewModel = viewModel()
26.     val progressViewModel: ProgressViewModel = viewModel()

30.             HomeScreen(
31.                 viewModel = exerciseViewModel,
32.                 onStartExercise = {
33.                     exerciseViewModel.startExercise()
34.                     navController.navigate(Routes.EXERCISE)

46.         }

48.             SetupScreen(

50.             )
51.         }

60.                         popUpTo(Routes.EXERCISE) { inclusive = true }
61.                     }
62.                 },

22. @Composable
23. fun SetupScreen(onBack: () -> Unit) {
24.     val context = LocalContext.current
25.     val audioCapture = remember { AudioCapture() }
26.     val audioPlayback = remember { AudioPlayback(context) }
27. 
28.     var isListening by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
29.     var detectedHz by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(-1f) }
30.     var detectedMidi by remember { mutableIntStateOf(-1) }
31. 
32.     DisposableEffect(Unit) {
33.         onDispose {
34.             audioCapture.stop()
35.             audioPlayback.cancelPlayback()
36.         }
37.     }
38. 
39.     Column(
40.         modifier = Modifier
41.             .fillMaxSize()
42.             .padding(16.dp),
43.         horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally
44.     ) {
45.         Row(
46.             modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
47.             verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
48.         ) {
49.             TextButton(onClick = {
50.                 audioCapture.stop()
51.                 audioPlayback.cancelPlayback()
52.                 onBack()
53.             }) { Text("← Back") }
54.             Spacer(Modifier.weight(1f))
55.             Text("Mic Setup", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleLarge, fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold)
56.             Spacer(Modifier.weight(1f))
57.         }
58. 
59.         Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
60.         Text("Test your microphone — sing or play a note.", style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium)
61.         Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
62. 
63.         // Staff showing currently detected note

1. package com.earring.ui.components
2. 
3. import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxWidth
5. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.height
6. import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
7. import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
8. import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
9. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
10. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.drawscope.DrawScope
11. import androidx.compose.ui.text.TextStyle
12. import androidx.compose.ui.text.drawText
13. import androidx.compose.ui.text.rememberTextMeasurer
14. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.Dp
15. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
16. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.sp
17. import com.earring.DetectedNote
18. import com.earring.EarRingCore
19. 
20. enum class NoteState { EXPECTED, CORRECT, INCORRECT, ACTIVE }
21. 
22. data class StaffNote(
23.     val midi: Int,
24.     val state: NoteState
25. )
26. 
27. @Composable
28. fun MusicStaff(
29.     notes: List<StaffNote>,
30.     modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
31.     lineSpacingDp: Dp = 12.dp
32. ) {
33.     val textMeasurer = rememberTextMeasurer()
34. 
35.     Canvas(
36.         modifier = modifier
37.             .fillMaxWidth()
38.             .height(160.dp)
39.     ) {
40.         val lineSpacing = lineSpacingDp.toPx()
41.         val staffTop = size.height / 2f - 2 * lineSpacing
42.         val leftMargin = 60f
43.         val noteRadius = lineSpacing * 0.45f
44. 
45.         // Draw 5 staff lines
46.         for (i in 0..4) {
47.             val y = staffTop + i * lineSpacing
48.             drawLine(
49.                 color = Color(0xFF333333),
50.                 start = Offset(leftMargin, y),
51.                 end = Offset(size.width - 16f, y),
52.                 strokeWidth = 1.5f
53.             )
54.         }
55. 
56.         // Draw treble clef symbol
57.         drawText(
58.             textMeasurer = textMeasurer,
59.             text = "\uD834\uDD1E",
60.             topLeft = Offset(4f, staffTop - lineSpacing * 1.5f),
61.             style = TextStyle(fontSize = 56.sp, color = Color(0xFF333333))
62.         )
63. 
64.         // Distribute notes horizontally
65.         val noteAreaStart = leftMargin + 20f
66.         val noteAreaWidth = size.width - noteAreaStart - 20f
67.         val noteCount = notes.size.coerceAtLeast(1)
68.         val noteStep = noteAreaWidth / noteCount.toFloat()
69. 
70.         notes.forEachIndexed { index, staffNote ->
71.             val staffPos = EarRingCore.staffPosition(staffNote.midi)
72.             // staffPos: C4=0, D4=1, ... each step = half line spacing
73.             val staffCenter = staffTop + 2 * lineSpacing // line 3 = B4 area
74.             val noteY = staffCenter - staffPos * (lineSpacing / 2f)
75.             val noteX = noteAreaStart + index * noteStep + noteStep / 2f
76. 
77.             val noteColor = when (staffNote.state) {
78.                 NoteState.EXPECTED -> Color(0xFF333333)
79.                 NoteState.CORRECT -> Color(0xFF4CAF50)
80.                 NoteState.INCORRECT -> Color(0xFFF44336)
81.                 NoteState.ACTIVE -> Color(0xFF3F51B5)
82.             }
83. 
84.             // Draw ledger lines if needed (outside staff lines 0-4)
85.             drawLedgerLines(noteX, noteY, staffTop, lineSpacing, noteRadius)
86. 
87.             // Draw note head
88.             drawCircle(
89.                 color = noteColor,
90.                 radius = noteRadius,
91.                 center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
92.             )
93.             // Hollow for expected notes
94.             if (staffNote.state == NoteState.EXPECTED) {
95.                 drawCircle(
96.                     color = Color.White,
97.                     radius = noteRadius - 2.5f,
98.                     center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
99.                 )
100.             }
101.         }
102.     }
103. }
104. 
105. private fun DrawScope.drawLedgerLines(
106.     noteX: Float,
107.     noteY: Float,
108.     staffTop: Float,
109.     lineSpacing: Float,
110.     noteRadius: Float
111. ) {
112.     val staffBottom = staffTop + 4 * lineSpacing
113.     val ledgerWidth = noteRadius * 2.8f
114. 
115.     // Above staff
116.     var y = staffTop - lineSpacing
117.     while (noteY <= y + 1f) {
118.         drawLine(
119.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
120.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
121.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
122.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
123.         )
124.         y -= lineSpacing
125.     }
126. 
127.     // Below staff
128.     y = staffBottom + lineSpacing
129.     while (noteY >= y - 1f) {
130.         drawLine(
131.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
132.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
133.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
134.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
135.         )
136.         y += lineSpacing
137.     }
138. }
139.

1. package com.earring
2. 
3. import android.media.AudioFormat
4. import android.media.AudioRecord
5. import android.media.MediaRecorder
6. import android.util.Log
7. import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
8. import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
9. import kotlinx.coroutines.Job
10. import kotlinx.coroutines.isActive
11. import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
12. 
13. class AudioCapture {
14. 
15.     private var audioRecord: AudioRecord? = null
16.     private var captureJob: Job? = null
17.     private val scope = CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO)
18. 
19.     fun start(
20.         sampleRate: Int = 44100,
21.         bufferSize: Int = 4096,
22.         onAudio: (FloatArray) -> Unit
23.     ) {
24.         stop()
25. 
26.         val minBuffer = AudioRecord.getMinBufferSize(
27.             sampleRate,
28.             AudioFormat.CHANNEL_IN_MONO,
29.             AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT
30.         )
31.         val actualBuffer = maxOf(bufferSize * 2, minBuffer)
32. 
33.         try {
34.             val record = AudioRecord(
35.                 MediaRecorder.AudioSource.MIC,
36.                 sampleRate,
37.                 AudioFormat.CHANNEL_IN_MONO,
38.                 AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT,
39.                 actualBuffer
40.             )
41.             if (record.state != AudioRecord.STATE_INITIALIZED) {
42.                 Log.e("AudioCapture", "AudioRecord failed to initialize")
43.                 record.release()
44.                 return
45.             }
46.             audioRecord = record
47.             record.startRecording()
48. 
49.             captureJob = scope.launch {
50.                 val shortBuffer = ShortArray(bufferSize)
51.                 val floatBuffer = FloatArray(bufferSize)
52.                 while (isActive) {
53.                     val read = record.read(shortBuffer, 0, bufferSize)
54.                     if (read > 0) {
55.                         for (i in 0 until read) {
56.                             floatBuffer[i] = shortBuffer[i] / 32768f
57.                         }
58.                         onAudio(floatBuffer.copyOf(read))
59.                     }
60.                 }
61.             }
62.         } catch (e: SecurityException) {
63.             Log.e("AudioCapture", "Permission denied: ${e.message}")
64.         } catch (e: Exception) {
65.             Log.e("AudioCapture", "Error starting capture: ${e.message}")
66.         }
67.     }
68. 
69.     fun stop() {
70.         captureJob?.cancel()
71.         captureJob = null
72.         try {
73.             audioRecord?.stop()
74.             audioRecord?.release()
75.         } catch (e: Exception) {
76.             Log.e("AudioCapture", "Error stopping: ${e.message}")
77.         }
78.         audioRecord = null
79.     }
80. }
81.

### 💭 Reasoning

*OK, AudioCapture.stop() uses try-catch, so it shouldn't throw. The back button issue must be something else.

Let me think about the back button again. The TextButton is in the top-left corner of the screen. On a Pixel 7 with gesture navigation, the system back gesture is swipe from the LEFT edge of the screen. When the user taps the "← Back" button in the top-left, they might accidentally trigger the gesture area (left edge swipe zone) instead of the button, or the button might not register the tap if it's very close to the edge.

But this would be a system-level issue, not something I can easily fix in code. 

Looking at the padding and positioning, the button's left edge sits right at 16dp from the screen edge, which overlaps with Android 13+'s 20-30dp gesture back zone. That's likely why taps aren't registering reliably—the button is too close to the edge where the system intercepts gestures.

The better approach is to add a `BackHandler` callback that mirrors the button's navigation logic, paired with an `IconButton` for a clear tap target. This way the system back gesture and button both trigger the same navigation. I should apply this pattern to ExerciseScreen, SetupScreen, and ProgressScreen, and also remove the test notes section from SetupScreen.

For the staff display in the mic test, I'm reconsidering whether to keep it at all. The staff does serve a purpose—showing the user if their sung note is being detected at the right pitch—but the current implementation has issues with the note always centering horizontally and updating too rapidly. Actually, keeping a single note at its correct position makes sense for a setup screen, since the user just needs quick feedback that the mic is working.

The real problem is that the note appears to be "overwriting" itself because it's jumping vertically as the pitch detection fluctuates between nearby semitones due to noise. The fix is to add some stability to the displayed note so it doesn't flicker constantly between positions.

For the back button issue, I'm realizing the problem might not be the TextButton itself but rather something with how `popBackStack()` is being called or the navigation stack state. Let me trace through what happens when going back from ExerciseScreen—there might be an issue with `stopListening()` not completing properly before the navigation happens.

I think the safest approach is to add `BackHandler` to all screens that need it and make the back button more defensive by ensuring `onBack()` always executes, plus repositioning the button away from the edge to avoid accidental touches.

Now I'm circling back to the staff positioning issue in SetupScreen—the note appears centered because there's only one note on the staff.

Actually, rereading the user's complaint about notes being "in the center and keep getting overwritten," I think they mean multiple notes are appearing at the same horizontal position as new detections come in, replacing the previous one. So it's not that the note is at the wrong pitch vertically, but that it's staying centered horizontally instead of moving to match the detected pitch. The staff position calculation might be mapping the note position incorrectly...

Let me trace through how staff positions work in treble clef. The calculation uses `staffCenter` as the Y position of B4 (the middle line), then adjusts based on `staffPos` which represents half-line spacing steps from E4. So E4 would be position 0 on the first line, F4 at position 1, G4 at position 2 on the second line, and so on up the staff. B4 is position 6 in that system, which maps to the middle staff line. When I work through the formula with staffCenter at that line, I get noteY = staffTop - lineSpacing, placing B4 above where it should be—that's wrong.

Let me reconsider the mapping. If B4 is actually staffPos = 4 (counting E4, F4, G4, A4, B4), then the calculation gives noteY = staffTop, putting it at the top of the staff instead of the center. There's definitely a systematic issue with how staff positions are being rendered. I should check the Rust function that calculates staffPosition to understand the actual mapping being used, especially since the exercise screenshots show notes at varying heights on the staff.*

1. pub mod music_theory;
2. pub mod pitch_detection;
3. 
4. pub use music_theory::{
5.     freq_to_note, generate_sequence, midi_to_freq, scale_notes, staff_position, Note, NoteName,
6.     ScaleType,
7. };
8. pub use pitch_detection::detect_pitch;
9. 
10. // ── C-compatible FFI surface ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
11. // These thin wrappers are called from the React Native Turbo Native Module
12. // (Swift / Kotlin) without requiring uniffi code-gen at this stage.
13. 
14. use std::os::raw::{c_float, c_int, c_uchar, c_uint};
15. 
16. /// Detect pitch from a raw f32 PCM buffer.
17. ///
18. /// * `samples`       – pointer to f32 samples
19. /// * `num_samples`   – number of samples
20. /// * `sample_rate`   – sample rate in Hz
21. /// * `out_hz`        – out-parameter: detected frequency, 0.0 if not detected
22. ///
23. /// Returns 1 if a pitch was detected, 0 otherwise.
24. #[no_mangle]
25. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_detect_pitch(
26.     samples: *const c_float,
27.     num_samples: c_uint,
28.     sample_rate: c_uint,
29.     out_hz: *mut c_float,
30. ) -> c_int {
31.     if samples.is_null() || out_hz.is_null() {
32.         return 0;
33.     }
34.     let slice = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts(samples, num_samples as usize) };
35.     match detect_pitch(slice, sample_rate) {
36.         Some(hz) => {
37.             unsafe { *out_hz = hz };
38.             1
39.         }
40.         None => {
41.             unsafe { *out_hz = 0.0 };
42.             0
43.         }
44.     }
45. }
46. 
47. /// Convert a frequency to MIDI note number and cents deviation.
48. ///
49. /// * `hz`        – frequency in Hz
50. /// * `out_midi`  – out-parameter: MIDI note number (0–127)
51. /// * `out_cents` – out-parameter: deviation in cents (-50 to +50)
52. ///
53. /// Returns 1 on success, 0 if frequency is out of range.
54. #[no_mangle]
55. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_freq_to_note(
56.     hz: c_float,
57.     out_midi: *mut c_uchar,
58.     out_cents: *mut c_int,
59. ) -> c_int {
60.     if out_midi.is_null() || out_cents.is_null() {
61.         return 0;
62.     }
63.     match freq_to_note(hz) {
64.         Some((note, cents)) => {
65.             unsafe {
66.                 *out_midi = note.midi();
67.                 *out_cents = cents;
68.             }
69.             1
70.         }
71.         None => 0,
72.     }
73. }
74. 
75. /// Get the staff position for a MIDI note number.
76. ///
77. /// Returns diatonic steps above middle C (C4 = 0, D4 = 1, C5 = 7, B3 = -1, …).
78. #[no_mangle]
79. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_staff_position(midi: c_uchar) -> c_int {
80.     staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi))
81. }
82. 
83. /// Generate a sequence of MIDI note numbers.
84. ///
85. /// * `root_midi`   – MIDI number of the root note
86. /// * `scale_id`    – 0=Major, 1=NaturalMinor, 2=HarmonicMinor, 3=PentatonicMajor,
87. ///                   4=PentatonicMinor, 5=Dorian, 6=Mixolydian, 7=Blues
88. /// * `length`      – number of notes to generate
89. /// * `seed`        – random seed for reproducibility
90. /// * `out_buf`     – caller-allocated buffer of at least `length` bytes
91. ///
92. /// Returns the number of notes written, or -1 on error.
93. #[no_mangle]
94. pub extern "C" fn ear_ring_generate_sequence(
95.     root_midi: c_uchar,
96.     scale_id: c_uchar,
97.     length: c_uchar,
98.     seed: u64,
99.     out_buf: *mut c_uchar,
100. ) -> c_int {
101.     if out_buf.is_null() {
102.         return -1;
103.     }
104.     let scale = match scale_id {
105.         0 => ScaleType::Major,
106.         1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
107.         2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
108.         3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
109.         4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
110.         5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
111.         6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
112.         7 => ScaleType::Blues,
113.         _ => return -1,
114.     };
115.     let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi);
116.     let notes = generate_sequence(root, scale, length, seed);
117.     let out = unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts_mut(out_buf, length as usize) };
118.     for (i, note) in notes.iter().enumerate() {
119.         out[i] = note.midi();
120.     }
121.     notes.len() as c_int
122. }
123. 
124. // ── Android JNI exports ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125. // Exported with the exact symbol names the Kotlin EarRingCoreModule expects.
126. #[cfg(target_os = "android")]
127. mod android_jni {
128.     use jni::objects::{JClass, JFloatArray, JIntArray};
129.     use jni::sys::{jfloat, jfloatArray, jint, jintArray, jlong};
130.     use jni::JNIEnv;
131. 
132.     use super::{detect_pitch, freq_to_note, generate_sequence, staff_position, Note, ScaleType};
133. 
134.     #[no_mangle]
135.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_nativeDetectPitch(
136.         mut env: JNIEnv,
137.         _class: JClass,
138.         samples: jfloatArray,
139.         sample_rate: jint,
140.     ) -> jfloat {
141.         let arr = unsafe { JFloatArray::from_raw(samples) };
142.         let len = match env.get_array_length(&arr) {
143.             Ok(l) => l as usize,
144.             Err(_) => return -1.0,
145.         };
146.         let mut buf = vec![0f32; len];
147.         if env.get_float_array_region(&arr, 0, &mut buf).is_err() {
148.             return -1.0;
149.         }
150.         match detect_pitch(&buf, sample_rate as u32) {
151.             Some(hz) => hz,
152.             None => -1.0,
153.         }
154.     }
155. 
156.     #[no_mangle]
157.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_nativeFreqToMidi(
158.         _env: JNIEnv,
159.         _class: JClass,
160.         hz: jfloat,
161.     ) -> jint {
162.         match freq_to_note(hz) {
163.             Some((note, _)) => note.midi() as jint,
164.             None => -1,
165.         }
166.     }
167. 
168.     #[no_mangle]
169.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_nativeFreqToCents(
170.         _env: JNIEnv,
171.         _class: JClass,
172.         hz: jfloat,
173.     ) -> jint {
174.         match freq_to_note(hz) {
175.             Some((_, cents)) => cents as jint,
176.             None => 0,
177.         }
178.     }
179. 
180.     #[no_mangle]
181.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_nativeStaffPosition(
182.         _env: JNIEnv,
183.         _class: JClass,
184.         midi: jint,
185.     ) -> jint {
186.         staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi as u8)) as jint
187.     }
188. 
189.     #[no_mangle]
190.     pub extern "system" fn Java_com_earring_EarRingCore_nativeGenerateSequence(
191.         mut env: JNIEnv,
192.         _class: JClass,
193.         root_midi: jint,
194.         scale_id: jint,
195.         length: jint,
196.         seed: jlong,
197.     ) -> jintArray {
198.         let scale = match scale_id {
199.             0 => ScaleType::Major,
200.             1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
201.             2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
202.             3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
203.             4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
204.             5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
205.             6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
206.             7 => ScaleType::Blues,
207.             _ => ScaleType::Major,
208.         };
209.         let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi as u8);
210.         let notes = generate_sequence(root, scale, length as u8, seed as u64);
211.         let midi_vals: Vec<jint> = notes.iter().map(|n| n.midi() as jint).collect();
212. 
213.         let arr: JIntArray = match env.new_int_array(midi_vals.len() as i32) {
214.             Ok(a) => a,
215.             Err(_) => return std::ptr::null_mut(),
216.         };
217.         let _ = env.set_int_array_region(&arr, 0, &midi_vals);
218.         arr.into_raw()
219.     }
220. }
221.
```

</details>

218. pub fn staff_position(note: Note) -> i32 {
219.     // Diatonic step offset within an octave for each pitch class (C=0, D=1, E=2, …)
220.     let diatonic = match note.name {
221.         NoteName::C => 0,
222.         NoteName::Cs => 0,
223.         NoteName::D => 1,
224.         NoteName::Ds => 1,
225.         NoteName::E => 2,
226.         NoteName::F => 3,
227.         NoteName::Fs => 3,
228.         NoteName::G => 4,
229.         NoteName::Gs => 4,
230.         NoteName::A => 5,
231.         NoteName::As => 5,
232.         NoteName::B => 6,
233.     };
234.     // Middle C (C4) is at staff position 0
235.     let octave_offset = (note.octave as i32 - 4) * 7;
236.     octave_offset + diatonic
237. }
238. 
239. // ── Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
240.
```

*Now I understand the staff position calculation:

And in MusicStaff.kt:
```kotlin

Looking at treble clef, the lines from bottom to top are E4, G4, B4, D5, F5, so B4 should be on the middle line, not above everything.

Let me reconsider how the staff positions map to actual Y coordinates. If staffTop is the top of the staff and the five lines are spaced lineSpacing apart, then the lines should be at staffTop, staffTop + lineSpacing, staffTop + 2*lineSpacing, staffTop + 3*lineSpacing, and staffTop + 4*lineSpacing from top to bottom. For E4 at staffPos 2, I'm getting noteY = staffTop + lineSpacing, which would be the second line from the top, not the first. I need to recalculate how staffPos actually maps to the treble clef lines.

```

Let me verify this formula works for a few notes. B4 at staffPos=6 should land exactly at staffCenter, C5 at staffPos=7 should be half a line spacing above it, and A4 at staffPos=5 should be half a line spacing below—all matching the treble clef layout correctly.

Now I'm checking what's actually being rendered on the exercise screen with those notes, because the current formula `noteY = staffCenter - staffPos * (lineSpacing / 2f)` is placing them incorrectly. Let me trace through the math: if staffCenter is calculated as staffTop plus 2 line spacings, then A4 ends up way above the staff instead of in the space where it should be.

Looking at the screenshot, all four notes appear in the upper half of the staff, but the user mentioned they look fine in the emulator yet seem centered in the mic test—which suggests there's a mismatch between where I think the notes should be positioned and where they're actually appearing. - meaning horizontally in the center.

1. The note doesn't move when pitch changes (wrong) 
2. OR: The note is always at the horizontal center (correct behavior for 1 note)
3. AND: notes keep getting overwritten as pitch detection fluctuates

Actually, I think the user may be saying:
- The note appears at the center of the staff both horizontally (because 1 note always goes to center-X) AND vertically (if the vertical positioning is wrong)
- "keeps getting overwritten" means the note redraws rapidly as pitch fluctuates, making it look unstable

The staff position seems off. Let me check what the exercise screenshot showed - the notes were at different heights but none were at the middle line. They all looked somewhat high up on the staff. Let me reconsider the treble clef positions. From bottom to top: E4 on the first line, F4 in the first space, G4 on the second line, A4 in the second space, B4 on the middle line, C5 in the third space, D5 on the fourth line, E5 in the fourth space, and F5 on the top line. Middle C (C4) sits below the staff entirely.

Since the staff center is at the third line (B4), and I'm calculating note positions relative to that, I need to adjust my reference point. The formula should account for B4 being at staffPos 6, so the note Y position equals the staff center minus the difference between the note's staff position and 6, scaled by half the line spacing.

Now let me verify this works for adjacent notes—A4 should be half a line spacing above center, and C5 should be half a line spacing below, which checks out. So the fix is to shift the formula to use `(staffPos - 6)` instead of just `staffPos`. But I'm puzzled because the exercise screenshot showed notes at different heights, which shouldn't happen if the original formula was this broken. Let me look more carefully at what's actually displayed there. Looking at the screenshot more carefully, all four notes actually appear within the staff boundaries at different vertical positions, which suggests my calculation might be off—A4 shouldn't be above the staff if it's visible there alongside the others. Let me reconsider whether the staff positioning or the note-to-position mapping needs adjustment. So A4 is placing above the staff when it should be between the second and third lines—the formula is definitely off. Let me reconsider what notes were actually shown in the exercise screenshot. The user mentioned F4, B4, E4, and G4 initially, but I saw different notes in my testing. I should go back and check which notes were actually displayed to understand why they appeared at different heights.

Looking at E4 specifically with the current formula, it calculates to the second staff line from the top, which would visually correspond to D5 in treble clef—so the notes are rendering at different positions on the staff, just at the wrong pitches. The exercise screen showed notes spread across different heights without complaint, but the mic test screen with a single note is where the problem became obvious. The difference might be related to how a single note is positioned versus multiple notes.

When there's only one note in the mic test, it gets centered horizontally in the available space. As pitch detection fluctuates between adjacent notes like A4 and G#4, the note jumps vertically at that same center position, creating the appearance of rapid overwriting. The core issues are the note staying fixed at the horizontal center, the flickering from pitch fluctuations, and potentially incorrect note heights overall. I'm thinking the best approach is to remove the staff visualization from the setup screen entirely since real-time pitch monitoring doesn't really benefit from staff notation, or alternatively add smoothing to reduce the jarring jumps. on the pitch meter + note label text. For the general staff rendering in ExerciseScreen, the notes ARE at different heights (even if the heights might not be perfectly correct musically). The user isn't complaining about ExerciseScreen's staff.

Actually wait - re-reading the user's complaint: "in the mic test the notes are not drawn correctly on the stave, they are in the center and keep getting overwiritten". This confirms:
- The complaint is specifically about SetupScreen (mic test), not ExerciseScreen
- The note(s) appear in the center
- Notes keep getting overwritten (rapid redraws)

My fix:
1. Remove MusicStaff from SetupScreen entirely - just show pitch meter + note label
2. Fix back button 
3. Remove test notes

Actually, I think keeping some visual feedback in SetupScreen is good. Instead of the MusicStaff, I can show a large note name display and the pitch meter. That's cleaner and more informative.

For the back button - let me think about what's actually failing. The TextButton onClick calls `onBack()`. Looking at SetupScreen, the back button stops audio capture and playback before navigating back. The AudioPlayback.cancelPlayback() method handles cleanup by canceling any active sequence jobs and stopping/releasing all active audio players in a thread-safe way. This should be fine.

So `onBack()` should be called and trigger `navController.popBackStack()`. One possibility: if the user navigates from SetupScreen back to HomeScreen but the HOME composable doesn't recompose properly because of shared ViewModel state... but that shouldn't affect navigation itself.

Let me consider another angle. In my edits to ExerciseScreen, I modified the back button to stop listening and cancel playback before navigating back. The `stopListening()` call sets the status to IDLE, which triggers a recomposition via `collectAsState()`. During recomposition, the composable might be trying to clean up resources while navigation is happening simultaneously.

When `stopListening()` is called, it also stops the audio capture, which releases the AudioRecord. This should complete quickly, but on Android 13 with the predictive back gesture, the `popBackStack()` call might be getting intercepted or delayed by the system's gesture handling.

I'm wondering if the real issue is simpler though—when `stopListening()` updates the ViewModel state and triggers a recomposition, maybe the click on the back button just isn't registering properly, especially with how the TextButton is positioned in the Row with vertical centering.

The TextButton should have a 48dp minimum touch target with reasonable padding, making the "← Back" text easily tappable. But I'm suspecting the system gesture zone on the Pixel 7 might be interfering—the left edge 20dp swipe area reserved for Android's gesture navigation could be blocking the tap when the user tries to hit the button.

Looking at the layout, the TextButton starts at 16dp from the left edge with 12dp of internal padding, which puts it right in that 16-20dp overlap zone where the system back gesture takes priority. I'll use `BackHandler` to intercept the system back gesture and handle navigation properly instead of fighting the gesture zone.

Now I'm planning the fixes: add `BackHandler` to the three screens so the system back gesture triggers the same navigation as the text button, remove the test notes section from SetupScreen, and replace the MusicStaff with a simpler live note display showing just the note name and pitch meter. Let me implement these changes.*

125. fun SetupScreen(onBack: () -> Unit) {
126.     val context = LocalContext.current
127.     val audioCapture = remember { AudioCapture() }
128.     val audioPlayback = remember { AudioPlayback(context) }
129. 
130.     var isListening by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
131.     var detectedHz by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(-1f) }
132.     var detectedMidi by remember { mutableIntStateOf(-1) }
133. 
134.     DisposableEffect(Unit) {
135.         onDispose {
136.             audioCapture.stop()
137.             audioPlayback.cancelPlayback()
138.         }
139.     }
140. 
141.     Column(
142.         modifier = Modifier
143.             .fillMaxSize()
144.             .padding(16.dp),
145.         horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally
146.     ) {
147.         Row(
148.             modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
149.             verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
150.         ) {
151.             TextButton(onClick = {
152.                 audioCapture.stop()
153.                 audioPlayback.cancelPlayback()
154.                 onBack()
155.             }) { Text("← Back") }
156.             Spacer(Modifier.weight(1f))
157.             Text("Mic Setup", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleLarge, fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold)
158.             Spacer(Modifier.weight(1f))
159.         }
160. 
161.         Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
162.         Text("Test your microphone — sing or play a note.", style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium)
163.         Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
164. 
165.         // Staff showing currently detected note
166.         val staffNotes = if (detectedMidi >= 0)
167.             listOf(StaffNote(midi = detectedMidi, state = NoteState.ACTIVE))
168.         else emptyList()
169.         MusicStaff(notes = staffNotes, modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth())
170. 
171.         Spacer(Modifier.height(16.dp))
172. 
173.         // Pitch meter
174.         PitchMeter(detectedMidi = detectedMidi, detectedHz = detectedHz)
175. 
176.         if (detectedMidi >= 0) {
177.             Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp))
178.             Text(
179.                 text = "${detectedHz.toInt()} Hz  •  ${MusicTheory.midiToLabel(detectedMidi)}",
180.                 style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge,
181.                 color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary
182.             )
183.         }
184. 
185.         Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
186. 
187.         // Listen toggle
188.         if (!isListening) {
189.             Button(
190.                 onClick = {
191.                     isListening = true
192.                     audioCapture.start(onAudio = { samples ->
193.                         val hz = EarRingCore.detectPitch(samples, 44100)
194.                         if (hz > 0f) {
195.                             detectedHz = hz
196.                             detectedMidi = EarRingCore.freqToMidi(hz)
197.                         } else {
198.                             detectedHz = -1f
199.                             detectedMidi = -1
200.                         }
201.                     })
202.                 },
203.                 modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(52.dp)
204.             ) { Text("🎙 Start Listening", fontSize = 17.sp) }
205.         } else {
206.             Button(
207.                 onClick = {
208.                     isListening = false
209.                     audioCapture.stop()
210.                     detectedHz = -1f
211.                     detectedMidi = -1
212.                 },
213.                 colors = ButtonDefaults.buttonColors(containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error),
214.                 modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(52.dp)
215.             ) { Text("⏹ Stop", fontSize = 17.sp) }
216.         }
217. 
218.         Spacer(Modifier.height(24.dp))
219.         Text("Play test notes:", style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelLarge)
220.         Spacer(Modifier.height(10.dp))
221. 
222.         // Test note buttons
223.         val testNotes = listOf(60 to "C4", 64 to "E4", 67 to "G4", 69 to "A4", 72 to "C5")
224.         Row(
225.             horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(8.dp),
226.             modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()
227.         ) {
228.             testNotes.forEach { (midi, label) ->
229.                 OutlinedButton(
230.                     onClick = { audioPlayback.playNote(midi) },
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 525m 13s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

All three bugs fixed and committed. Summary of changes:

| Bug | Fix |
|-----|-----|
| Back button not working | Added `BackHandler` to all screens — handles system back gesture (left-edge swipe on Pixel 7) AND the `← Back` text button |
| Staff notes in mic test (centered, flickering) | Replaced with a **large 72sp note name** display + 3-frame stability filter so it only shows when the pitch is held steady |
| Test notes on mic screen | Removed entirely |

Once you plug the Pixel 7 back in, install with `adb -s \<serial\> install -r ...` and the mic test will show a big letter like **"A4"** that updates stably as you sing.

<sub>⏱️ 1618m 57s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Based on what's been built in this project:

**Android** ✅ — fully working
- Kotlin + Jetpack Compose
- Rust core (JNI) for pitch detection
- Tested on emulator + Pixel 7
- Build: `./gradlew installDebug`

**iOS** ⚠️ — code written, not tested
- Swift + SwiftUI
- Rust core (C FFI via bridging header)
- All files created but **requires a Mac + Xcode to build**
- Has never been compiled or run

**Web / React Native** ❌ — removed
- The original Expo/RN code is preserved on the `master` branch if you ever want it back

<sub>⏱️ 1637m 51s</sub>

### 👤 User

what options do i have for a native windpws version

---

<sub>⏱️ 1637m 55s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*The user is asking about options for a native Windows version of the Ear Ring app. Let me think about this based on what the project is: an ear training app with Rust pitch detection core, Android (Kotlin/Compose) and iOS (Swift/SwiftUI) native apps.

For Windows native development options:

1. **WinUI 3 / Windows App SDK** - Microsoft's modern native UI framework, C++ or C#, closest to native Windows look
2. **WPF (Windows Presentation Foundation)** - C#/.NET, mature, lots of ecosystem, but older style
3. **MAUI (.NET Multi-platform App UI)** - C#, cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Android, iOS), could share code across platforms


4. **Tauri** - Rust + web frontend (HTML/CSS/JS), great fit since they already have Rust core
5. **egui** - Pure Rust immediate mode GUI, simple, could reuse the Rust core directly
6. **Slint** - Rust-friendly UI framework
7. **Flutter** - Dart, supports Windows natively

Since they've already built a Rust pitch detection core, the most practical paths forward would be using egui for a pure Rust solution that directly integrates the existing code, Tauri if they want a web-based UI with Rust backend, or WinUI 3 for the most native Windows experience.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 1638m 3s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Given the project already has a Rust core, here are the realistic options ranked by how well they fit:

---

**1. egui (pure Rust) ⭐ Best fit**
- Write the entire UI in Rust — no FFI needed, Rust core integrates directly
- Immediate mode GUI, simple to use
- Runs on Windows, macOS, Linux, Web (WASM)
- Not "native" looking but fast and self-contained

**2. Tauri**
- Rust backend + HTML/CSS/JS frontend (could reuse React Native UI logic)
- Rust core plugs in natively
- Produces a small native `.exe`
- Good option if you want a web-style UI

**3. .NET MAUI**
- C# + XAML, runs on Windows, macOS, Android, iOS
- Would call the Rust core via P/Invoke (C FFI — already exists in `ear_ring_core.h`)
- Could potentially replace both Android and iOS native apps too
- Microsoft-supported, good Windows integration

**4. WinUI 3 (Windows App SDK)**
- Most native Windows look and feel (fluent design)
- C# or C++, calls Rust via P/Invoke/FFI
- Windows-only

**5. Flutter**
- Dart + supports Windows natively
- Calls Rust via `flutter_rust_bridge` crate — well supported
- Could also replace Android/iOS apps

---

**My recommendation:** **egui** if you want simplicity and pure Rust, **Tauri** if you want a polished UI quickly. Both reuse your existing Rust core with zero FFI glue code needed.

Want me to start on one of these?

---

<sub>⏱️ 1640m 56s</sub>

### 👤 User

will the ios version run on mac

---

<sub>⏱️ 1641m 0s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Yes — with some caveats:

**Mac Catalyst** (easiest)
- Xcode can compile an iOS app to run natively on macOS with one checkbox: *"Mac Catalyst"* in target settings
- The SwiftUI code we wrote is already compatible — SwiftUI runs on both
- The Rust core needs to be compiled for `x86_64-apple-darwin` and `aarch64-apple-darwin` (Apple Silicon) in addition to the iOS targets
- Result: a real macOS `.app` from the same Swift/SwiftUI codebase

**Native macOS (AppKit/SwiftUI)** (more work)
- SwiftUI is cross-platform within Apple's ecosystem, so most views would just work
- Some iOS-specific APIs (like `AVAudioSession` category setup) need `#if os(iOS)` guards
- Would give a more "Mac-native" feel

---

**What needs changing for Catalyst:**
1. Build Rust as a universal macOS library (`cargo build --target x86_64-apple-darwin` + `aarch64-apple-darwin`, combine with `lipo`)
2. Replace `AVAudioSession.sharedInstance().setCategory(.playAndRecord)` with the macOS equivalent
3. Enable Mac Catalyst in Xcode → should just work

**Bottom line:** The iOS SwiftUI code is ~90% reusable on macOS. The main work is building the Rust XCFramework with macOS slices added. All of this requires a Mac though.

---

---

<sub>⏱️ 1688m 58s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*The user wants to create a Tauri app for Windows, Linux, and Mac. Let me think about what this involves:

1. Tauri uses a Rust backend + web frontend (HTML/CSS/JS)
2. The Rust core (pitch detection, music theory) already exists and can be integrated directly into the Tauri backend
3. For the frontend, we can use React/TypeScript or vanilla JS - given this project had a React Native version before, using React makes sense
4. The audio capture needs to work on desktop - we can use the Web Audio API in the browser side, or use cpal (Rust audio library) on the backend


5. For the architecture, I'll wrap the existing `ear_ring_core` crate as Tauri commands in the Rust backend, handle microphone input with cpal, and build the frontend with React and Vite to reuse the UI patterns from the mobile apps, leveraging Web Audio API for playback.

Now I need to plan out the implementation steps: set up the Tauri project with React and TypeScript, integrate the core library, configure audio capture in the backend, build the UI components matching the existing design, and connect everything through Tauri's command system. Let me start by checking the Tauri setup and project structure. like.

The approach:
- Create a `desktop/` directory within the existing project
- Use `npm create tauri-app` or `cargo tauri init` to scaffold
- The Tauri src-tauri will be a Rust crate that depends on `ear_ring_core`

Let me think about the structure:
```
desktop/
├── src/               # React frontend
│   ├── App.tsx
│   ├── components/
│   └── ...
├── src-tauri/         # Tauri Rust backend
│   ├── Cargo.toml     # depends on ear_ring_core
│   ├── src/
│   │   └── main.rs    # Tauri commands
│   └── tauri.conf.json
└── package.json
```

This is a big task. I'll use a general-purpose agent to handle most of the scaffolding work. For the tech stack, I'm going with React + TypeScript + Vite on the frontend, cpal for audio capture in the Rust backend, and Web Audio API for piano sample playback. The Tauri commands will handle pitch generation, detection, and MIDI conversion, with events streaming detected pitch from Rust to the frontend. Actually, I think the simpler approach is to have the frontend capture audio via Web Audio API and send chunks to the backend for pitch detection, rather than dealing with cpal streaming complexity.*

<sub>⏱️ 1689m 33s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Good. Tauri v1 is installed. Here's the architecture plan:

- **`desktop/`** — Tauri app at the root of the repo
- **Frontend**: React + TypeScript + Vite — Web Audio API captures mic, sends chunks to Rust; Web Audio API plays Salamander piano samples
- **Backend (`src-tauri`)**: Rust crate importing `ear_ring_core` directly; Tauri commands for `generate_sequence`, `detect_pitch`, `freq_to_midi`, `staff_position`
- **Audio**: Mic captured in browser via `getUserMedia` → AudioWorklet → `invoke('detect_pitch', chunk)` → Hz back. Playback via `fetch` + `AudioContext.decodeAudioData` + `playbackRate`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "description": "Create Tauri desktop app",
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "prompt": "Create a complete Tauri v1 desktop app for the Ear Ring ear training project at C:\\work\\ear_ring. \n\n## Context\n- Existing Rust core at `rust/` (crate name: `ear_ring_core`)\n  - `detect_pitch(samples: &[f32], sample_rate: u32) -> Option<f32>`\n  - `freq_to_note(hz: f32) -> Option<(Note, i32)>` where Note has `.midi() -> u8`\n  - `staff_position(note: Note) -> i32`  (C4=0, D4=1, each step = half line)\n  - `generate_sequence(root: Note, scale: ScaleType, length: u8, seed: u64) -> Vec<Note>`\n  - `Note::from_midi(u8) -> Note`, `Note.midi() -> u8`\n  - ScaleType variants: Major, NaturalMinor, HarmonicMinor, PentatonicMajor, PentatonicMinor, Dorian, Mixolydian, Blues\n  - `rust/Cargo.toml` has `crate-type = [\"cdylib\", \"staticlib\", \"rlib\"]`\n- Tauri CLI v1 already installed (`cargo tauri --version` = 1.5.9)\n- npm and node available\n- Target: Windows, Linux, macOS\n\n## Task\nCreate a `desktop/` directory inside C:\\work\\ear_ring containing a complete Tauri v1 app.\n\n### Structure to create:\n```\ndesktop/\n├── package.json\n├── vite.config.ts\n├── tsconfig.json\n├── index.html\n├── src/\n│   ├── main.tsx\n│   ├── App.tsx\n│   ├── styles.css\n│   ├── types.ts\n│   ├── audioWorklet.ts        (inline worklet code as a string, injected as Blob URL)\n│   ├── components/\n│   │   ├── HomeScreen.tsx\n│   │   ├── ExerciseScreen.tsx\n│   │   ├── SetupScreen.tsx\n│   │   ├── ProgressScreen.tsx\n│   │   ├── ResultsScreen.tsx\n│   │   ├── MusicStaff.tsx     (SVG-based)\n│   │   └── PitchMeter.tsx\n│   └── hooks/\n│       ├── useAudioCapture.ts\n│       └── useAudioPlayback.ts\n└── src-tauri/\n    ├── Cargo.toml\n    ├── tauri.conf.json\n    ├── build.rs\n    └── src/\n        └── main.rs\n```\n\n### Frontend details\n\n**package.json** dependencies:\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"ear-ring-desktop\",\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"dev\": \"vite\",\n    \"build\": \"tsc && vite build\",\n    \"tauri\": \"tauri\"\n  },\n  \"dependencies\": {\n    \"react\": \"^18.2.0\",\n    \"react-dom\": \"^18.2.0\",\n    \"@tauri-apps/api\": \"^1.5.0\"\n  },\n  \"devDependencies\": {\n    \"@vitejs/plugin-react\": \"^4.0.0\",\n    \"typescript\": \"^5.0.0\",\n    \"vite\": \"^4.4.0\",\n    \"@types/react\": \"^18.2.0\",\n    \"@types/react-dom\": \"^18.2.0\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**vite.config.ts**:\n```ts\nimport { defineConfig } from \"vite\";\nimport react from \"@vitejs/plugin-react\";\nexport default defineConfig({\n  plugins: [react()],\n  clearScreen: false,\n  server: { port: 1420, strictPort: true },\n  envPrefix: [\"VITE_\", \"TAURI_\"],\n  build: { target: [\"es2021\", \"chrome100\", \"safari13\"], minify: !process.env.TAURI_DEBUG, sourcemap: !!process.env.TAURI_DEBUG },\n});\n```\n\n**src/types.ts** — shared types:\n```ts\nexport type Screen = 'home' | 'exercise' | 'setup' | 'results' | 'progress';\nexport type ExerciseStatus = 'idle' | 'playing' | 'listening' | 'done';\n\nexport interface DetectedNote {\n  midi: number;\n  cents: number;\n  correct: boolean;\n}\n\nexport interface ExerciseState {\n  rootNote: number;   // 0-11\n  octave: number;     // 3-5\n  scaleId: number;    // 0-7\n  sequenceLength: number; // 2-8\n  sequence: number[]; // MIDI values\n  detected: DetectedNote[];\n  status: ExerciseStatus;\n  currentNoteIndex: number;\n  highlightIndex: number;\n}\n\nexport interface SessionRecord {\n  date: string;\n  scale: string;\n  root: string;\n  score: number;\n  length: number;\n}\n```\n\n**src/hooks/useAudioPlayback.ts** — plays Salamander piano samples:\n- Fetch MP3 from `https://tonejs.github.io/audio/salamander/{noteName}.mp3`\n- Cache decoded AudioBuffers in a Map\n- Available samples at MIDI: 21,24,27,30,33,36,39,42,45,48,51,54,57,60,63,66,69,72,75,78,81,84,87,90,93,96,99,102,105,108\n- Note names map: 21→A0, 24→C1, 27→Ds1, 30→Fs1, 33→A1, 36→C2, 39→Ds2, 42→Fs2, 45→A2, 48→C3, 51→Ds3, 54→Fs3, 57→A3, 60→C4, 63→Ds4, 66→Fs4, 69→A4, 72→C5, 75→Ds5, 78→Fs5, 81→A5, 84→C6, 87→Ds6, 90→Fs6, 93→A6, 96→C7, 99→Ds7, 102→Fs7, 105→A7, 108→C8\n- Find nearest sample MIDI, compute playbackRate = 2^(delta/12)\n- `playNote(midi)` and `playSequence(midis[], onEach, onDone)` with 600ms between notes\n- Export as a hook: `useAudioPlayback()` returning `{ playNote, playSequence, cancelPlayback }`\n\n**src/hooks/useAudioCapture.ts** — captures mic audio and sends to Tauri:\n- Use `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true })`\n- Create AudioContext at 44100Hz\n- Use ScriptProcessorNode (bufferSize 4096) to get PCM float samples\n- On each audio chunk: call `invoke('detect_pitch', { samples: Array.from(chunk), sampleRate: 44100 })` which returns `number` (Hz, or -1)\n- Export: `useAudioCapture()` returning `{ start(onHz: (hz: number) => void), stop() }`\n\n**App.tsx** — manages screen routing and exercise state using useState/useReducer. Passes state down to screens. Persists progress to localStorage.\n\n**HomeScreen.tsx**:\n- Title \"🎵 Ear Ring\"  \n- Root note chips (C C# D D# E F F# G G# A A# B) — wrap with flexWrap\n- Scale selector chips (Major, Natural Minor, Harmonic Minor, Pentatonic Major, Pentatonic Minor, Dorian, Mixolydian, Blues)\n- Octave buttons (3/4/5)\n- Sequence length slider (2-8)\n- \"▶ Start Exercise\" button → calls `invoke('generate_sequence', {rootMidi, scaleId, length, seed})` which returns `number[]`\n- \"🎙 Mic Setup\" and \"📊 Progress\" buttons\n\n**ExerciseScreen.tsx**:\n- Back button + current scale/root label\n- MusicStaff component showing sequence notes with state colours\n- Status text based on exercise status\n- PitchMeter showing live Hz\n- Play Sequence button (idle state) → plays audio, highlights notes\n- Start Listening button (idle state) → captures mic, detects pitch with stability logic\n- Stop Listening button (listening state)\n- Note tracker row at bottom showing ○/→/✓/✗ per note\n\n**Pitch stability logic** in ExerciseScreen (same as Android):\n- Require 2 consecutive frames of same pitch class + 450ms hold before confirming\n- On confirm: check if pitch class matches expected note's pitch class\n- Correct → advance to next note, vibrate (skip on desktop)\n- When all notes done or wrong note → navigate to results\n\n**MusicStaff.tsx** — SVG-based treble clef staff:\n- 5 staff lines\n- Treble clef as Unicode \"𝄞\" in SVG text\n- Notes placed by staff position (C4=0): noteY = staffCenter - staffPos * (lineSpacing/2)\n- Colours: expected=grey hollow, active=blue filled, correct=green, incorrect=red\n- Ledger lines above/below staff\n- Width: 100%, height: 160px\n\n**PitchMeter.tsx** — shows current detected note name and Hz, with a simple cents indicator bar (-50 to +50 cents).\n\n**SetupScreen.tsx**:\n- Back button with BackHandler equivalent (just a button)\n- \"Sing or play a note\" instruction\n- Large note name display (current detected note, updates with 3-frame stability)\n- Hz display\n- PitchMeter\n- Start/Stop Listening button\n\n**ResultsScreen.tsx**:\n- Score percentage with emoji\n- Note-by-note breakdown (expected vs detected, ✓/✗)\n- Try Again, New Exercise, View Progress buttons\n\n**ProgressScreen.tsx**:\n- Streak display\n- Session history list from localStorage\n\n**styles.css** — clean Material-inspired CSS:\n- CSS variables for primary colour (#3F51B5), success green (#4CAF50), error red (#F44336)\n- Card-style sections with box-shadow\n- Chip styles for note/scale selection (selected = primary colour background)\n- Button styles (primary filled, outlined)\n- Responsive layout, max-width 600px centered\n\n### Backend details (src-tauri/)\n\n**Cargo.toml**:\n```toml\n[package]\nname = \"ear-ring-desktop\"\nversion = \"0.1.0\"\nedition = \"2021\"\n\n[build-dependencies]\ntauri-build = { version = \"1.5\", features = [] }\n\n[dependencies]\ntauri = { version = \"1.6\", features = [\"shell-open\"] }\nserde = { version = \"1\", features = [\"derive\"] }\nserde_json = \"1\"\near_ring_core = { path = \"../../rust\" }\n```\n\n**build.rs**:\n```rust\nfn main() {\n    tauri_build::build()\n}\n```\n\n**src/main.rs** — Tauri commands:\n```rust\n#![cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), windows_subsystem = \"windows\")]\n\nuse ear_ring_core::{detect_pitch, freq_to_note, generate_sequence, staff_position, Note, ScaleType};\n\n#[tauri::command]\nfn cmd_detect_pitch(samples: Vec<f32>, sample_rate: u32) -> f32 {\n    match detect_pitch(&samples, sample_rate) {\n        Some(hz) => hz,\n        None => -1.0,\n    }\n}\n\n#[tauri::command]\nfn cmd_freq_to_midi(hz: f32) -> i32 {\n    match freq_to_note(hz) {\n        Some((note, _)) => note.midi() as i32,\n        None => -1,\n    }\n}\n\n#[tauri::command]\nfn cmd_freq_to_cents(hz: f32) -> i32 {\n    match freq_to_note(hz) {\n        Some((_, cents)) => cents,\n        None => 0,\n    }\n}\n\n#[tauri::command]\nfn cmd_staff_position(midi: u8) -> i32 {\n    staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi))\n}\n\n#[tauri::command]\nfn cmd_generate_sequence(root_midi: u8, scale_id: u8, length: u8, seed: u64) -> Vec<u8> {\n    let scale = match scale_id {\n        0 => ScaleType::Major,\n        1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,\n        2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,\n        3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,\n        4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,\n        5 => ScaleType::Dorian,\n        6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,\n        7 => ScaleType::Blues,\n        _ => ScaleType::Major,\n    };\n    let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi);\n    generate_sequence(root, scale, length, seed)\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| n.midi())\n        .collect()\n}\n\nfn main() {\n    tauri::Builder::default()\n        .invoke_handler(tauri::generate_handler![\n            cmd_detect_pitch,\n            cmd_freq_to_midi,\n            cmd_freq_to_cents,\n            cmd_staff_position,\n            cmd_generate_sequence,\n        ])\n        .run(tauri::generate_context!())\n        .expect(\"error while running tauri application\");\n}\n```\n\n**tauri.conf.json**:\n```json\n{\n  \"build\": {\n    \"beforeDevCommand\": \"npm run dev\",\n    \"beforeBuildCommand\": \"npm run build\",\n    \"devPath\": \"http://localhost:1420\",\n    \"distDir\": \"../dist\"\n  },\n  \"package\": {\n    \"productName\": \"Ear Ring\",\n    \"version\": \"0.1.0\"\n  },\n  \"tauri\": {\n    \"allowlist\": {\n      \"all\": false,\n      \"shell\": { \"all\": false, \"open\": true },\n      \"http\": { \"all\": true, \"scope\": [\"https://tonejs.github.io/**\"] }\n    },\n    \"bundle\": {\n      \"active\": true,\n      \"targets\": \"all\",\n      \"identifier\": \"com.earring.desktop\",\n      \"icon\": []\n    },\n    \"security\": { \"csp\": null },\n    \"windows\": [\n      {\n        \"fullscreen\": false,\n        \"resizable\": true,\n        \"title\": \"Ear Ring\",\n        \"width\": 480,\n        \"height\": 820,\n        \"minWidth\": 400,\n        \"minHeight\": 700\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Implementation instructions\n\n1. Create the `desktop/` directory and ALL files listed above with complete, working code\n2. All React components must be fully implemented (not stubs)\n3. The CSS must make it look clean and usable — use flexbox, proper spacing, card layouts\n4. The Tauri command names in `invoke()` calls must match the `#[tauri::command]` function names exactly: `cmd_detect_pitch`, `cmd_freq_to_midi`, `cmd_freq_to_cents`, `cmd_staff_position`, `cmd_generate_sequence`\n5. After creating all files, run `cd C:\\work\\ear_ring\\desktop && npm install` to install dependencies\n6. Then run `cargo build` in `desktop/src-tauri` to verify the Rust backend compiles\n7. Do NOT run the full Tauri dev server (it requires a display)\n8. Commit everything: `git add -A && git commit -m \"Add Tauri desktop app for Windows/Linux/macOS\"`\n\n## Notes\n- The `invoke` function is imported from `@tauri-apps/api/tauri`\n- For the ScriptProcessorNode, get inputBuffer.getChannelData(0) as the float samples\n- Staff position arithmetic: staffCenter is at B4 (the middle line of treble clef). Actually use: staffCenter = top of staff + 2 * lineSpacing (third line = B4). noteY = staffCenter - staffPos * (lineSpacing/2). staffPos for B4=5, so noteY = staffCenter - 5*(lineSpacing/2) = staffCenter - 2.5*lineSpacing. That's wrong. Let me clarify: staffPos 0=C4 (middle C, one ledger line below staff). The staff lines from bottom to top are E4(0), G4(2), B4(4), D5(6), F5(8) in half-step units from E4. Middle C (C4) = -2 in this system. Use: staffLine3Bottom = staffTop + 4*lineSpacing (bottom line = E4 = staffPos -2 in Rust's system where C4=0, so E4=2). Actually the Rust staff_position function returns: C4=0, D4=1, E4=2, F4=3, G4=4, A4=5, B4=6, C5=7, B3=-1, etc. So E4=2, G4=4, B4=6, D5=8, F5=10 are the 5 staff lines. The bottom staff line is E4=2. staffBottomY = staffTop + 4*lineSpacing. noteY for staffPos p = staffBottomY - (p - 2) * lineSpacing/2. So E4(p=2) → staffBottomY. G4(p=4) → staffBottomY - lineSpacing. B4(p=6) → staffBottomY - 2*lineSpacing (middle line). D5(p=8) → staffBottomY - 3*lineSpacing. F5(p=10) → staffBottomY - 4*lineSpacing = staffTop. Middle C C4(p=0) → staffBottomY + lineSpacing (one ledger line below).\n- localStorage keys: `ear_ring_sessions` for SessionRecord[]\n- The pitch stability check: track `stableCount`, `stablePitchClass`, `lastConfirmedMs`. PitchClass = midi % 12. Require stableCount >= 2 AND (now - lastConfirmedMs) >= 450ms. On confirm, compare pitchClass of detected with pitchClass of expected sequence note (sequence[currentNoteIndex] % 12).\n",
  "mode": "background"
}
````

that command 'cd desktop && cargo tauri dev' is not valid powershell

---

<sub>⏱️ 1705m 7s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

You're right. In PowerShell the correct way is:

```powershell
cd C:\work\ear_ring\desktop; cargo tauri dev
```

Or equivalently:
```powershell
Set-Location C:\work\ear_ring\desktop
cargo tauri dev
```

The `&&` operator doesn't exist in PowerShell (it was added only in PS 7+, and even then `cargo tauri dev` needs to run in the right directory). Using `;` runs the second command regardless, or just `cd` first then run separately.

C:\work\ear_ring\android\app\src\main\java\com\earring\ui\components\MusicStaff.kt

<details>
<summary>139 lines</summary>

```
1. package com.earring.ui.components
2. 
3. import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxWidth
5. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.height
6. import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
7. import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
8. import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
9. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
10. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.drawscope.DrawScope
11. import androidx.compose.ui.text.TextStyle
12. import androidx.compose.ui.text.drawText
13. import androidx.compose.ui.text.rememberTextMeasurer
14. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.Dp
15. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
16. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.sp
17. import com.earring.DetectedNote
18. import com.earring.EarRingCore
19. 
20. enum class NoteState { EXPECTED, CORRECT, INCORRECT, ACTIVE }
21. 
22. data class StaffNote(
23.     val midi: Int,
24.     val state: NoteState
25. )
26. 
27. @Composable
28. fun MusicStaff(
29.     notes: List<StaffNote>,
30.     modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
31.     lineSpacingDp: Dp = 12.dp
32. ) {
33.     val textMeasurer = rememberTextMeasurer()
34. 
35.     Canvas(
36.         modifier = modifier
37.             .fillMaxWidth()
38.             .height(160.dp)
39.     ) {
40.         val lineSpacing = lineSpacingDp.toPx()
41.         val staffTop = size.height / 2f - 2 * lineSpacing
42.         val leftMargin = 60f
43.         val noteRadius = lineSpacing * 0.45f
44. 
45.         // Draw 5 staff lines
46.         for (i in 0..4) {
47.             val y = staffTop + i * lineSpacing
48.             drawLine(
49.                 color = Color(0xFF333333),
50.                 start = Offset(leftMargin, y),
51.                 end = Offset(size.width - 16f, y),
52.                 strokeWidth = 1.5f
53.             )
54.         }
55. 
56.         // Draw treble clef symbol
57.         drawText(
58.             textMeasurer = textMeasurer,
59.             text = "\uD834\uDD1E",
60.             topLeft = Offset(4f, staffTop - lineSpacing * 1.5f),
61.             style = TextStyle(fontSize = 56.sp, color = Color(0xFF333333))
62.         )
63. 
64.         // Distribute notes horizontally
65.         val noteAreaStart = leftMargin + 20f
66.         val noteAreaWidth = size.width - noteAreaStart - 20f
67.         val noteCount = notes.size.coerceAtLeast(1)
68.         val noteStep = noteAreaWidth / noteCount.toFloat()
69. 
70.         notes.forEachIndexed { index, staffNote ->
71.             val staffPos = EarRingCore.staffPosition(staffNote.midi)
72.             // staffPos: C4=0, D4=1, ... each step = half line spacing
73.             val staffCenter = staffTop + 2 * lineSpacing // line 3 = B4 area
74.             val noteY = staffCenter - staffPos * (lineSpacing / 2f)
75.             val noteX = noteAreaStart + index * noteStep + noteStep / 2f
76. 
77.             val noteColor = when (staffNote.state) {
78.                 NoteState.EXPECTED -> Color(0xFF333333)
79.                 NoteState.CORRECT -> Color(0xFF4CAF50)
80.                 NoteState.INCORRECT -> Color(0xFFF44336)
81.                 NoteState.ACTIVE -> Color(0xFF3F51B5)
82.             }
83. 
84.             // Draw ledger lines if needed (outside staff lines 0-4)
85.             drawLedgerLines(noteX, noteY, staffTop, lineSpacing, noteRadius)
86. 
87.             // Draw note head
88.             drawCircle(
89.                 color = noteColor,
90.                 radius = noteRadius,
91.                 center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
92.             )
93.             // Hollow for expected notes
94.             if (staffNote.state == NoteState.EXPECTED) {
95.                 drawCircle(
96.                     color = Color.White,
97.                     radius = noteRadius - 2.5f,
98.                     center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
99.                 )
100.             }
101.         }
102.     }
103. }
104. 
105. private fun DrawScope.drawLedgerLines(
106.     noteX: Float,
107.     noteY: Float,
108.     staffTop: Float,
109.     lineSpacing: Float,
110.     noteRadius: Float
111. ) {
112.     val staffBottom = staffTop + 4 * lineSpacing
113.     val ledgerWidth = noteRadius * 2.8f
114. 
115.     // Above staff
116.     var y = staffTop - lineSpacing
117.     while (noteY <= y + 1f) {
118.         drawLine(
119.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
120.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
121.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
122.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
123.         )
124.         y -= lineSpacing
125.     }
126. 
127.     // Below staff
128.     y = staffBottom + lineSpacing
129.     while (noteY >= y - 1f) {
130.         drawLine(
131.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
132.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
133.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
134.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
135.         )
136.         y += lineSpacing
137.     }
138. }
139.
```

</details>

1. package com.earring.ui.components
2. 
3. import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Box
5. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.size
6. import androidx.compose.material3.Text
7. import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
8. import androidx.compose.ui.Alignment
9. import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
10. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
11. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.drawscope.Stroke
12. import androidx.compose.ui.text.font.FontWeight
13. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
14. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.sp
15. import com.earring.MusicTheory
16. 
17. @Composable
18. fun PitchMeter(
19.     detectedMidi: Int,      // -1 = no pitch
20.     detectedHz: Float,      // for display accuracy; not required
21.     modifier: Modifier = Modifier
22. ) {
23.     val isDetecting = detectedMidi >= 0
24.     val label = if (isDetecting) MusicTheory.midiToLabel(detectedMidi) else "—"
25.     val borderColor = if (isDetecting) Color(0xFF4CAF50) else Color(0xFFBDBDBD)
26. 
27.     Box(
28.         contentAlignment = Alignment.Center,
29.         modifier = modifier.size(90.dp)
30.     ) {
31.         Canvas(modifier = Modifier.size(90.dp)) {
32.             drawCircle(
33.                 color = borderColor,
34.                 radius = size.minDimension / 2f - 4f,
35.                 style = Stroke(width = 4f)
36.             )
37.         }
38.         Text(
39.             text = label,
40.             fontSize = if (label.length > 2) 16.sp else 20.sp,
41.             fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold,
42.             color = if (isDetecting) Color(0xFF212121) else Color(0xFFBDBDBD)
43.         )
44.     }
45. }
46.
```

112.                 OutlinedButton(
113.                     onClick = { viewModel.audioPlayback.cancelPlayback() },
114.                     modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(48.dp)
115.                 ) { Text("⏹ Stop Playback", fontSize = 16.sp) }
116.             }
117.             ExerciseStatus.LISTENING -> {
118.                 Button(
119.                     onClick = { viewModel.stopListening() },
120.                     colors = ButtonDefaults.buttonColors(containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error),
121.                     modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(52.dp)
122.                 ) { Text("⏹ Stop Listening", fontSize = 17.sp) }
123.             }
124.             ExerciseStatus.DONE -> {
125.                 CircularProgressIndicator()
126.             }
127.         }
128. 
129.         // Note progress display
130.         if (state.sequence.isNotEmpty()) {
131.             Spacer(Modifier.height(20.dp))
132.             Text("Notes:", style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelLarge)
133.             Spacer(Modifier.height(4.dp))
134.             Row(horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(6.dp)) {
135.                 state.sequence.forEachIndexed { idx, midi ->
136.                     val det = state.detected.getOrNull(idx)
137.                     val chip = when {
138.                         det == null && idx == state.currentNoteIndex -> "→"
139.                         det == null -> "○"
140.                         det.correct -> "✓"
141.                         else -> "✗"
142.                     }
143.                     val color = when {
144.                         det?.correct == true -> Color(0xFF4CAF50)
145.                         det?.correct == false -> Color(0xFFF44336)
146.                         idx == state.currentNoteIndex -> MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary
147.                         else -> MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
148.                     }
149.                     Column(horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally) {
150.                         Text(text = chip, color = color, fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold)
151.                         Text(text = MusicTheory.midiToLabel(midi), fontSize = 10.sp, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)
152.                     }
153.                 }
154.             }
155.         }
156.     }
157. }
158. 
159. private fun buildStaffNotes(
160.     sequence: List<Int>,
161.     detected: List<com.earring.DetectedNote>,
162.     currentIdx: Int,
163.     highlightIdx: Int
164. ): List<StaffNote> {
165.     return sequence.mapIndexed { idx, midi ->
166.         val det = detected.getOrNull(idx)
167.         val noteState = when {
168.             highlightIdx == idx -> NoteState.ACTIVE
169.             det == null && idx == currentIdx -> NoteState.ACTIVE
170.             det == null -> NoteState.EXPECTED
171.             det.correct -> NoteState.CORRECT
172.             else -> NoteState.INCORRECT
173.         }
174.         StaffNote(midi = midi, state = noteState)
175.     }
```

</details>

import React from 'react';
import { ExerciseState } from '../types';interface Props {
  exercise: ExerciseState;
  onUpdateExercise: (updates: Partial<ExerciseState>) => void;
  onStart: (rootNote: number, octave: number, scaleId: number, sequenceLength: number) => void;
  onSetup: () => void;
  onProgress: () => void;
}const NOTE_NAMES = ['C','C#','D','D#','E','F','F#','G','G#','A','A#','B'];
const SCALE_NAMES = ['Major','Natural Minor','Harmonic Minor','Pentatonic Major','Pentatonic Minor','Dorian','Mixolydian','Blues'];export default function HomeScreen({ exercise, onUpdateExercise, onStart, onSetup, onProgress }: Props) {
  const handleStart = () => {
    onStart(exercise.rootNote, exercise.octave, exercise.scaleId, exercise.sequenceLength);
  };  return (
    <div className="screen">
      <h1 className="app-title">🎵 Ear Ring</h1>
      <p className="app-subtitle">Train your ear with interactive exercises</p>      <div className="card">
        <h3 className="section-label">Root Note</h3>
        <div className="chip-row">
          {NOTE_NAMES.map((name, i) => (
            <button
              key={i}
              className={`chip ${exercise.rootNote === i ? 'chip-selected' : ''}`}
              onClick={() => onUpdateExercise({ rootNote: i })}
            >
              {name}
            </button>
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>      <div className="card">
        <h3 className="section-label">Scale</h3>
        <div className="chip-row">
          {SCALE_NAMES.map((name, i) => (
            <button
              key={i}
              className={`chip ${exercise.scaleId === i ? 'chip-selected' : ''}`}
              onClick={() => onUpdateExercise({ scaleId: i })}
            >
              {name}
            </button>
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>      <div className="card">
        <h3 className="section-label">Octave</h3>
        <div className="chip-row">
          {[3, 4, 5].map(oct => (
            <button
              key={oct}
              className={`chip ${exercise.octave === oct ? 'chip-selected' : ''}`}
              onClick={() => onUpdateExercise({ octave: oct })}
            >
              {oct}
            </button>
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>      <div className="card">
        <h3 className="section-label">Sequence Length: {exercise.sequenceLength}</h3>
        <input
          type="range"
          min={2}
          max={8}
          value={exercise.sequenceLength}
          onChange={e => onUpdateExercise({ sequenceLength: parseInt(e.target.value) })}
          className="slider"
        />
        <div className="slider-labels">
          <span>2</span><span>8</span>
        </div>
      </div>

      <button className="btn btn-primary btn-large" onClick={handleStart}>
        ▶ Start Exercise
      </button>

      <div className="btn-row">
        <button className="btn btn-outlined" onClick={onSetup}>🎙 Mic Setup</button>
        <button className="btn btn-outlined" onClick={onProgress}>📊 Progress</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
