import React, { useState, useCallback, useRef, useEffect } from 'react';
import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/tauri';
import { ExerciseState, DetectedNote } from '../types';
import MusicStaff from './MusicStaff';
import PitchMeter from './PitchMeter';
import { useAudioCapture } from '../hooks/useAudioCapture';
import { useAudioPlayback } from '../hooks/useAudioPlayback';interface Props {
  exercise: ExerciseState;
  onUpdateExercise: (updates: Partial<ExerciseState>) => void;
  onFinish: (detected: DetectedNote[]) => void;
  onBack: () => void;
}const NOTE_NAMES = ['C','C#','D','D#','E','F','F#','G','G#','A','A#','B'];
const SCALE_NAMES = ['Major','Natural Minor','Harmonic Minor','Pentatonic Major','Pentatonic Minor','Dorian','Mixolydian','Blues'];export default function ExerciseScreen({ exercise, onUpdateExercise, onFinish, onBack }: Props) {
  const [liveHz, setLiveHz] = useState(0);
  const [status, setStatus] = useState<'idle' | 'playing' | 'listening' | 'done'>('idle');
  const [currentNoteIndex, setCurrentNoteIndex] = useState(0);
  const [highlightIndex, setHighlightIndex] = useState(-1);
  const [detected, setDetected] = useState<DetectedNote[]>([]);  const stableCountRef = useRef(0);
  const stablePitchClassRef = useRef(-1);
  const lastConfirmedMsRef = useRef(0);
  const currentNoteIndexRef = useRef(0);
  const detectedRef = useRef<DetectedNote[]>([]);  const { start: startCapture, stop: stopCapture } = useAudioCapture();
  const { playSequence, cancelPlayback } = useAudioPlayback();  useEffect(() => {
    currentNoteIndexRef.current = currentNoteIndex;
  }, [currentNoteIndex]);  useEffect(() => {
    detectedRef.current = detected;
  }, [detected]);  const handlePlaySequence = useCallback(async () => {
    setStatus('playing');
    setHighlightIndex(0);
    await playSequence(
      exercise.sequence,
      (i) => setHighlightIndex(i),
      () => {
        setHighlightIndex(-1);
        setStatus('idle');
      }
    );
  }, [exercise.sequence, playSequence]);  const handleHzDetected = useCallback(async (hz: number) => {
    setLiveHz(hz);
    if (hz <= 0) return;    const midiResult = await invoke<number>('cmd_freq_to_midi', { hz });
    const centsResult = await invoke<number>('cmd_freq_to_cents', { hz });
    if (midiResult < 0) return;    const pitchClass = midiResult % 12;
    const now = Date.now();
    const idx = currentNoteIndexRef.current;    if (pitchClass === stablePitchClassRef.current) {
      stableCountRef.current++;
    } else {
      stableCountRef.current = 1;
      stablePitchClassRef.current = pitchClass;
    }

    if (stableCountRef.current >= 2 && (now - lastConfirmedMsRef.current) >= 450) {
      lastConfirmedMsRef.current = now;
      stableCountRef.current = 0;

      const expected = exercise.sequence[idx];
      const expectedPitchClass = expected % 12;
      const correct = pitchClass === expectedPitchClass;

      const newDetected = [...detectedRef.current, { midi: midiResult, cents: centsResult, correct }];
      setDetected(newDetected);
      detectedRef.current = newDetected;

      if (correct) {
        const nextIdx = idx + 1;
        if (nextIdx >= exercise.sequence.length) {
          stopCapture();
          setStatus('done');
          onFinish(newDetected);
        } else {
          setCurrentNoteIndex(nextIdx);
          currentNoteIndexRef.current = nextIdx;
        }
      } else {
        stopCapture();
        setStatus('done');
        onFinish(newDetected);
      }
    }
  }, [exercise.sequence, stopCapture, onFinish]);

  const handleStartListening = useCallback(() => {
    setStatus('listening');
    stableCountRef.current = 0;
    stablePitchClassRef.current = -1;
    lastConfirmedMsRef.current = 0;
    setCurrentNoteIndex(0);
    currentNoteIndexRef.current = 0;
    setDetected([]);
    detectedRef.current = [];
    startCapture(handleHzDetected);
  }, [startCapture, handleHzDetected]);

  const handleStopListening = useCallback(() => {
    stopCapture();
    setStatus('idle');
    setLiveHz(0);
  }, [stopCapture]);

  const statusText = () => {
    switch (status) {
      case 'idle': return 'Press ▶ to hear the sequence, then 🎙 to sing it back';
      case 'playing': return '🎵 Listen carefully...';
      case 'listening': return `🎙 Sing note ${currentNoteIndex + 1} of ${exercise.sequence.length}`;
      case 'done': return '✅ Done!';
    }
  };

  const noteSymbol = (i: number) => {
    if (i < detected.length) return detected[i].correct ? '✓' : '✗';
    if (i === currentNoteIndex && status === 'listening') return '→';
    return '○';
  };

  return (
    <div className="screen">
      <div className="screen-header">
        <button className="btn-back" onClick={onBack}>← Back</button>
        <span className="screen-title">
          {NOTE_NAMES[exercise.rootNote]} {SCALE_NAMES[exercise.scaleId]}
        </span>
      </div>

      <div className="card staff-card">
        <MusicStaff
          sequence={exercise.sequence}
          currentNoteIndex={currentNoteIndex}
          highlightIndex={highlightIndex}
          detected={detected}
          status={status}
        />
      </div>

      <div className="status-text">{statusText()}</div>

      <div className="card">
        <PitchMeter hz={liveHz} />
      </div>

      <div className="btn-row">
        {status === 'idle' && (
          <>
            <button className="btn btn-outlined" onClick={handlePlaySequence}>▶ Play Sequence</button>
            <button className="btn btn-primary" onClick={handleStartListening}>🎙 Start Listening</button>
          </>
        )}
        {status === 'playing' && (
          <button className="btn btn-outlined" onClick={() => { cancelPlayback(); setStatus('idle'); setHighlightIndex(-
-1); }}>
            ⏹ Stop
          </button>
        )}
        {status === 'listening' && (
          <button className="btn btn-outlined" onClick={handleStopListening}>⏹ Stop Listening</button>
        )}
      </div>

      <div className="note-tracker">
        {exercise.sequence.map((midi, i) => (
          <div
            key={i}
            className={`note-tracker-item ${
              i < detected.length
                ? detected[i].correct ? 'tracker-correct' : 'tracker-incorrect'
                : i === currentNoteIndex && status === 'listening' ? 'tracker-active' : ''
            }`}
          >
            <div className="tracker-symbol">{noteSymbol(i)}</div>
            <div className="tracker-note">{NOTE_NAMES[midi % 12]}</div>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
<exited with exit code 0>
```

</details>

{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",

  "mode": "background",
  "prompt": "Update the Tauri desktop app at C:\\work\\ear_ring\\desktop\\src to exactly match the UI specification in C:\\work\\ear_ring\\AGENTS.md. Do NOT commit anything.\n\n## Key differences to fix vs current code:\n\n### HomeScreen.tsx (C:\\work\\ear_ring\\desktop\\src\\components\\HomeScreen.tsx)\nCurrent problems:\n- Title is \"🎵 Ear Ring\" but spec says \"Ear Ring 🎵\" (emoji at end)\n- Subtitle is wrong text — should be \"Ear Training\"\n- Sections are wrapped in `<div className=\"card\">` — remove cards, use flat layout with section labels\n- Sequence Length uses a `<input type=\"range\">` slider — change to chip row (2 3 4 5 6 7 8)\n- Octave and Scale sections are in wrong order — order must be: Root Note, Octave, Scale, Sequence Length\n- Mic Setup and Progress buttons are in a side-by-side btn-row — they must each be full-width stacked buttons\n- Section label style: small, muted, left-aligned\n\n### ExerciseScreen.tsx (C:\\work\\ear_ring\\desktop\\src\\components\\ExerciseScreen.tsx)\nCurrent problems:\n- Header shows \"RootNote ScaleName\" but spec says \"RootNoteOctave ScaleName\" (include octave number)\n- Status text wording is wrong:\n  - IDLE: \"Press Play to hear the sequence\" (not \"Press ▶ to hear the sequence...\")\n  - LISTENING: \"Sing note N of M: NoteLabel\" (must include note label)\n- Buttons:\n  - IDLE: \"▶ Play Sequence\" (filled primary, full-width, 52dp tall) + \"🎙 Start Listening\" (outlined, full-width, 48dp tall) — currently side by side, must be stacked full-width\n  - PLAYING: \"⏹ Stop Playback\" (outlined, full-width) — currently says \"⏹ Stop\"\n  - LISTENING: \"⏹ Stop Listening\" (filled ERROR/red, full-width) — currently outlined\n  - DONE: show a spinner/loading indicator\n- Note tracker: must show note name below each symbol (10sp/small text), current code only shows NOTE_NAMES[midi%12] which drops the octave\n\n### SetupScreen.tsx (C:\\work\\ear_ring\\desktop\\src\\components\\SetupScreen.tsx)\n- Must show large note name (72px bold) as primary display — NOT a MusicStaff\n- Must show Hz below note name (only when detected)\n- NO test note buttons\n- Pitch stability: 3 frames before updating displayed note\n- PitchMeter circle must be shown\n- Start/Stop Listening buttons full-width\n\n### ResultsScreen.tsx (C:\\work\\ear_ring\\desktop\\src\\components\\ResultsScreen.tsx)\n- Score emoji must be 64px, score percentage 56px bold\n- Score colour: ≥80% green #4CAF50, ≥50% orange #FF9800, <50% red #F44336\n- Note-by-note table: each row shows index, \"Expected: X\", \"Sung: Y\", ✓/✗/— \n- Buttons stacked full-width: 🔄 Try Again (filled), 🏠 New Exercise (outlined), 📊 View Progress (outlined)\n\n### MusicStaff.tsx (C:\\work\\ear_ring\\desktop\\src\\components\\MusicStaff.tsx)\nVerify the staff positioning math matches the spec exactly:\n- Canvas height: 160px\n- lineSpacing: 12 (pixels, since this is web not dp)\n- staffTop = height/2 - 2*lineSpacing = 80 - 24 = 56\n- leftMargin = 60\n- noteRadius = lineSpacing * 0.45 = 5.4\n- staffCenter = staffTop + 2*lineSpacing = 56 + 24 = 80\n- noteY = staffCenter - staffPos * (lineSpacing/2) = 80 - staffPos * 6\n- Treble clef: Unicode 𝄞 (U+1D11E), font-size 56px, at x=4, y=staffTop - lineSpacing*1.5\n- Note colours: EXPECTED=#333333 hollow, note label bold, 20px (16px if 3+ chars), dark #212121 when active, grey when not\n- Shows \"—\" when no pitch\n\n### styles.css (C:\\work\\ear_ring\\desktop\\src\\styles.css)\nAdd/fix these CSS rules DONE: ProgressView() spinner\n- Note tracker below buttons:\n  - \"Notes:\" label\n  - HStack of columns, each showing: symbol (○/→/✓/✗, 16pt bold) above note label (10pt muted)\n  - Colours: ✓=green #4CAF50, ✗=red #F44336, →=primary #3F51B5, ○=muted\n\n### SetupView.swift\n- NO MusicStaff\n- NO test note buttons\n- Large note name: 72pt bold, primary colour when detected, muted when \"—\"\n- Hz label below (shown only when detected): bodyMedium, muted\n- PitchMeter circle (90pt)\n- Full-width Start/Stop Listening buttons\n- Pitch stability: 3 consecutive frames same pitch class before updating displayed note\n\n### ResultsView.swift\n- Score emoji 64pt\n- Score percentage 56pt bold, colour-coded: ≥80% #4CAF50, ≥50% #FF9800, <50% #F44336\n- \"Score\" label titleMedium muted\n- \"RootOctave ScaleName\" bodyLarge\n- Divider\n- \"Note by Note\" section header\n- For each note: HStack with index \"N.\", \"Expected: X\", \"Sung: Y\", ✓/✗/— symbol\n- Buttons stacked full-width: 🔄 Try Again (filled), 🏠 New Exercise (outlined), 📊 View Progress (outlined)\n\n### MusicStaffView.swift  \nVerify and fix the staff positioning math to match spec exactly:\n- Canvas height: 160pt\n- lineSpacing: 12pt\n- staffTop = height/2 - 1. A musical note or staff element
2. An ear or earring shape

I could create an SVG icon that represents this, and then convert it to the various formats needed (PNG at different sizes for Android, iOS, and desktop).


Now I'm settling on a design approach: a white ear silhouette against the app's indigo background with a treble clef or musical note as an earring element to keep it clean and modern.

Now I'm working out the SVG structure—positioning the note head as a rotated ellipse, drawing the stem upward, adding the curved flag, and placing a small circle at the base to complete the earring effect.

Writing SVG code... Building the SVG structure... Writing icon assets... Now I need to update the iOS Contents.json file to make sure it properly references the 1024x1024 icon, though I should verify what the current configuration looks like first.

I'm also thinking through the rounded corners situation—Android and iOS both apply their own system masks, so the icons should be square without pre-rounded corners. For Tauri on desktop, macOS expects rounded corners in the .icns format, but since I'm working on Windows, I'll stick with PNG and ICO formats. For the ICO file, I can use the png-to-ico package to convert.

Before I write the full script, I need to make sure sharp can properly handle SVG input on Windows, since it relies on libvips underneath. I'll set up the SVG file and generation script in the icon directory, then run it from the root where sharp is installed. I also need to grab the `png-to-ico` package to handle the ICO conversion.

For the Android WebP files, I'm thinking the cleanest approach is to replace them with PNG files since sharp can output both formats. I could generate WebP directly with sharp's webp output method, but PNG files are simpler and the Android build system handles them fine.

Looking at the Android adaptive icon structure, I notice the current setup uses simple .webp files in mipmap folders rather than the newer adaptive icon system with `mipmap-anydpi-v26`. These WebP files are probably remnants from the Expo setup, so I'll just replace them with PNGs.

```
1. #![cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), windows_subsystem = "windows")]
2. 
3. use ear_ring_core::{detect_pitch, freq_to_note, generate_sequence, staff_position, Note, ScaleType};
4. 
5. #[tauri::command]
6. fn cmd_detect_pitch(samples: Vec<f32>, sample_rate: u32) -> f32 {
7.     match detect_pitch(&samples, sample_rate) {
8.         Some(hz) => hz,
9.         None => -1.0,
10.     }
11. }
12. 
13. #[tauri::command]
14. fn cmd_freq_to_midi(hz: f32) -> i32 {
15.     match freq_to_note(hz) {
16.         Some((note, _)) => note.midi() as i32,
17.         None => -1,
18.     }
19. }
20. 
21. #[tauri::command]
22. fn cmd_freq_to_cents(hz: f32) -> i32 {
23.     match freq_to_note(hz) {
24.         Some((_, cents)) => cents,
25.         None => 0,
26.     }
27. }
28. 
29. #[tauri::command]
30. fn cmd_staff_position(midi: u8) -> i32 {
31.     staff_position(Note::from_midi(midi))
32. }
33. 
34. #[tauri::command]
35. fn cmd_generate_sequence(root_midi: u8, scale_id: u8, length: u8, seed: u64) -> Vec<u8> {
36.     let scale = match scale_id {
37.         0 => ScaleType::Major,
38.         1 => ScaleType::NaturalMinor,
39.         2 => ScaleType::HarmonicMinor,
40.         3 => ScaleType::PentatonicMajor,
41.         4 => ScaleType::PentatonicMinor,
42.         5 => ScaleType::Dorian,
43.         6 => ScaleType::Mixolydian,
44.         7 => ScaleType::Blues,
45.         _ => ScaleType::Major,
46.     };
47.     let root = Note::from_midi(root_midi);
48.     generate_sequence(root, scale, length, seed)
49.         .iter()
50.         .map(|n| n.midi())
51.         .collect()
52. }
53. 
54. fn main() {
55.     tauri::Builder::default()
56.         .invoke_handler(tauri::generate_handler![
57.             cmd_detect_pitch,
58.             cmd_freq_to_midi,
59.             cmd_freq_to_cents,
60.             cmd_staff_position,
61.             cmd_generate_sequence,
62.         ])
63.         .run(tauri::generate_context!())
64.         .expect("error while running tauri application");
65. }
66.
```

<sub>⏱️ 1740m 46s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Actually, the `tauri::Icon` API in Tauri v1 doesn't work quite like that. Let me check the correct API. In Tauri v1, to set an icon you can use:

```rust
window.set_icon(tauri::Icon::Raw(include_bytes!("../icons/icon.ico").to_vec()))?;
```

Or the path-based approach is different. Let me use `include_bytes!` which is simpler and more reliable:

```rust
window.set_icon(tauri::Icon::Raw(include_bytes!("../icons/32x32.png").to_vec()))?;


```

I'm realizing the distinction between bundle icons in the config file versus runtime window icons—the conf.json `bundle.icon` is for installers, not the actual window. To set the window icon at runtime, I need to use `window.set_icon()` with the proper `tauri::Icon` type, which I'm still verifying the exact API for.

```*

### 💭 Reasoning

*The home screen has the title "Ear Ring 🎵" and subtitle "Ear Training". To add the icon to the UI, I can add an `\<img\>` tag with the icon PNG. But in a Tauri app, the icon is served from the filesystem, not from the web. In dev mode, files in `public/` are served at the root. I could copy the icon to `desktop/public/icon.png` and reference it as `/icon.png`.

Alternatively, I can use the emoji approach or just leave the UI as is and focus on the window icon.

Actually, thinking about this more - the user said "the tauri app doesn't show the icon anywhere." This most likely means:


The taskbar and window title bar are showing a generic default icon instead of the custom one. I need to configure the icon in `tauri.conf.json` and use the `set_icon` call in Rust to fix this. Let me verify the Rust compiles with the new setup, then copy the icon to `desktop/public/` so I can display it in the UI as well.

I'm reconsidering what the user actually needs—they might want the icon visible in the taskbar, the title bar, or both. Since they confirmed the PNG looks good, I should handle both cases: set the runtime icon for the window chrome and also add it to the UI itself.*

*The `tauri::Icon::Raw` variant doesn't exist in this version of Tauri v1. Let me check what variants are available. In Tauri v1, the Icon enum has different variants. Let me look at the Tauri v1 source to find the correct API.

In Tauri v1, the `Icon` type is actually `tauri::image::Image` or it could be `tauri::WindowIcon`. Let me check what version of Tauri is being used.

Actually in Tauri v1, the window icon is set differently. Let me check the Cargo.toml to see which version of tauri is used.


Looking at the actual Tauri v1 source, the `Icon` enum supports `File` for file paths and `Rgba` for raw image data, not `Raw` as the variant name.

The simplest approach is to use `Icon::File` with a path to the icon file, or skip the `set_icon` call entirely and let the `bundle.icon` configuration in `tauri.conf.json` handle it instead. the `setup` block and instead configure the window icon directly in `tauri.conf.json` by adding an `icon` field to the windows array. Let me check what I already have in the config and add the icon path if it's missing.*

### 💭 Reasoning

*The `windows[].icon` field is not supported in this version of Tauri v1. Let me remove it from the conf and instead use the Rust `set_icon` approach with the correct `Icon::Rgba` variant.

To use `Icon::Rgba`, I need to decode the PNG first. I can use the `image` crate or the `png` crate. But simpler: use `tauri::Icon::File` which should work.

Wait, let me check what's in the error again. The error from cargo check was about "unknown field `icon`" in the windows config. Let me remove that. For the Rust approach, let me use `tauri::Icon::File`.
 
So `Icon::File` takes a path that's relative to the current working directory. In dev mode, that's typically the `src-tauri` folder, so `icons/icon.ico` should work fine. In production though, the path would need to be relative to the app's resources directory, which is a different location.

I'm setting up a conditional approach using compile-time flags to handle both cases—in debug mode, I'll use the relative path directly, and in release mode, I'll use the path resolver to get the correct resource location.

Still writing setup code... I need to add the `image` crate as a dependency, but actually I'm realizing Tauri v1 handles window icons through the build system—it reads from `tauri.conf.json` and embeds the `.ico` file during compilation, so I just need to make sure the icon is already listed in the bundle configuration. removing the unsupported `icon` field from the windows config, keeping the bundle icon setup intact, and verifying the icon file is in the correct location relative to the src-tauri directory so the build process can embed it properly.*

1. # Ear Ring — Agent Instructions
2. 
3. ## UI Consistency Rule
4. 
5. **ALL platform versions of Ear Ring must have the same UI design.**
6. 
7. The canonical reference implementation is the **Android app** (`android/` directory).
8. When building or modifying any platform (iOS, desktop/Tauri, web, etc.) you must
9. replicate the Android UI exactly unless there is an explicit per-platform instruction
10. in this file overriding a specific element.
11. 
12. If you are given explicit instructions to make the UI different on a specific platform,
13. document that exception in the **Platform Exceptions** section at the bottom of this file.
14. 
15. ---
16. 
17. ## Screen Inventory
18. 
19. Every platform must implement all 5 screens:
20. 
21. | Screen | Navigation trigger |
22. |--------|--------------------|
23. | Home | App launch / "New Exercise" from Results |
24. | Exercise | "Start Exercise" from Home |
25. | Mic Setup | "Mic Setup" from Home |
26. | Results | Exercise completion (all notes done or wrong note) |
27. | Progress | "Progress" from Home or Results |
28. 
29. Back navigation (system back gesture AND an on-screen "← Back" button) must work on
30. every screen except Home.
31. 
32. ---
33. 
34. ## Screen Designs
35. 
36. ### Home Screen
37. 
38. Layout: vertically scrollable column, 16dp/px padding, centred.
39. 
40. ```
41. [24dp space]
42. "Ear Ring 🎵"          — 32sp bold, primary colour
43. "Ear Training"         — 16sp, muted/secondary colour
44. 
45. [28dp space]
46. Section label: "Root Note"
47. Chip grid (wrapping): C  C#  D  D#  E  F  F#  G  G#  A  A#  B
48.   — 12 chips, must WRAP across multiple rows (FlowRow / flexWrap)
49.   — Selected chip: filled with primary colour
50.   — Unselected: outlined
````

Agent completed. agent_id: agent-4, agent_type: general-purpose, status: completed, description: Fix iOS UI to match spec, elapsed: 2014s, duration: 2011s

Result:
All the changes are complete and correct. Here's a summary of what was changed across all 8 files:

---

**HomeView.swift** — Complete rewrite of styles + view:
- Added `Color.erPrimary/erSuccess/erError/erWarning/erMuted/erDark` brand-colour extension
- `ChipButtonStyle`: unselected now **outlined** (transparent fill + `erPrimary` border + `erPrimary` text), selected = filled `erPrimary` + white text
- `PrimaryButtonStyle`/`OutlinedButtonStyle`/`ErrorButtonStyle`: parameterised with height + fontSize; use brand colours
- `typealias SecondaryButtonStyle = OutlinedButtonStyle` for backward compat
- Title: "Ear Ring 🎵" 32pt bold `erPrimary`; subtitle "Ear Training" 16pt muted
- Section labels: `.caption`, `erMuted`, left-aligned
- Root Note: `LazyVGrid(.adaptive(min: 50))` → wraps into 2 rows
- Octave: `LazyVGrid` with 3 equal `.flexible()` columns
- Scale: `LazyVGrid(.adaptive(min: 120))` → wraps
- Sequence Length: `LazyVGrid` with 7 equal columns
- Buttons: "▶ Start Exercise" / "🎙 Mic Setup" / "📊 Progress", stacked full-width
- `.toolbar(.hidden, for: .navigationBar)` — no nav bar on Home

**ExerciseView.swift** — Complete rewrite:
- Not scrollable (plain `VStack`)
- Custom toolbar: "← Back" leading, "C4 Major" trailing
- Staff at 160pt, always-visible 90pt `PitchMeterView`
- Status text matches spec exactly (IDLE/PLAYING/LISTENING with note label/DONE)
- Buttons: correct per state (▶ Play + 🎙 outlined / ⏹ Stop Playback outlined / ⏹ Stop Listening **red filled** / spinner)
- Note tracker: ○/→/✓/✗ symbols above note labels with correct colours
- Auto-navigates to results via `.onChange(of: model.status)` when `.done`, saves session once

**SetupView.swift** — Complete rewrite:
- Removed MusicStaff, test note buttons, auto-start
- 72pt bold note name (erPrimary when detected, erMuted when "—")
- Hz label shown only when detected
- 90pt PitchMeterView centred
- "🎙 Start Listening" filled / "⏹ Stop" red filled
- 3-frame pitch stability via `model.liveFrameCount` onChange

**ResultsView.swift** — Complete rewrite:
- Score emoji 64pt, percentage 56pt bold colour-coded, "Score" muted label
- "RootOctave ScaleName" bodyLarge
- "Note by Note" section with "N." / "Expected: X" / "Sung: Y" / ✓✗— text
- Buttons: "🔄 Try Again" (filled, calls `resetToIdle()` + pops results) / "🏠 New Exercise" / "📊 View Progress"

**MusicStaffView.swift** — Corrected positioning math:
- `staffTop = height/2 - 2*lineSpacing`, `staffCenter = staffTop + 2*lineSpacing`
- `noteY = staffCenter - (staffPos - 6) * (lineSpacing/2)` — correctly maps B4(6)→middle line, E4(2)→bottom, C4(0)→first ledger below
- Ledger lines via y-coordinate comparison, colour #555555, width `noteRadius*2.8`
- Treble clef at x=4, y=staffTop-lineSpacing*1.5, 56pt
- EXPECTED=hollow dark/white, ACTIVE=erPrimary, CORRECT=erSuccess, INCORRECT=erError
- Accepts `currentNoteIndex` for ACTIVE highlighting

**PitchMeterView.swift** — Rewrite:
- Fixed 90×90pt ZStack (no GeometryReader)
- Ring lineWidth=4, erMuted when no pitch, erSuccess when detected
- Text: bold 20pt (16pt if 3+ chars), erDark when active, erMuted otherwise

**ProgressScreen.swift** — Rewrite:
- Custom "← Back" toolbar button + "Progress" title
- Streak: 🔥 emoji 48pt, "N day streak" 28pt bold
- Session history per spec format, "No sessions yet…" empty state

**ExerciseModel.swift** — Additions:
- `@Published var liveFrameCount: Int = 0` incremented every audio frame in `processAudioLive`
- `func resetToIdle()` for "Try Again" from results
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 1763m 21s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\work\ear_ring\desktop\src\components\SetupScreen.tsx.**

C:\work\ear_ring\desktop\src\components\SetupScreen.tsx

<details>
<summary>90 lines</summary>

```
1. import React, { useState, useCallback, useEffect } from 'react';
2. import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/tauri';
3. import PitchMeter from './PitchMeter';
4. import { useAudioCapture } from '../hooks/useAudioCapture';
5. 
6. interface Props {
7.   onBack: () => void;
8. }
9. 
10. const NOTE_NAMES = ['C','C#','D','D#','E','F','F#','G','G#','A','A#','B'];
11. 
12. export default function SetupScreen({ onBack }: Props) {
13.   const [hz, setHz] = useState(0);
14.   const [noteName, setNoteName] = useState('\u2014');
15.   const [listening, setListening] = useState(false);
16.   const frameNotesRef = React.useRef<number[]>([]);
17.   const { start, stop } = useAudioCapture();
18. 
19.   const handleHz = useCallback(async (detectedHz: number) => {
20.     setHz(detectedHz);
21.     if (detectedHz > 0) {
22.       const midi = await invoke<number>('cmd_freq_to_midi', { hz: detectedHz });
23.       if (midi >= 0) {
24.         const pitchClass = midi % 12;
25.         frameNotesRef.current.push(pitchClass);
26.         if (frameNotesRef.current.length > 3) frameNotesRef.current.shift();
27.         const last = frameNotesRef.current;
28.         if (last.length === 3 && last[0] === last[1] && last[1] === last[2]) {
29.           const octave = Math.floor(midi / 12) - 1;
30.           setNoteName(`${NOTE_NAMES[pitchClass]}${octave}`);
31.         }
32.       }
33.     } else {
34.       frameNotesRef.current = [];
35.       setNoteName('\u2014');
36.     }
37.   }, []);
38. 
39.   const startListening = useCallback(() => {
40.     setListening(true);
41.     start(handleHz);
42.   }, [start, handleHz]);
43. 
44.   const stopListening = useCallback(() => {
45.     stop();
46.     setListening(false);
47.     setHz(0);
48.     setNoteName('\u2014');
49.     frameNotesRef.current = [];
50.   }, [stop]);
51. 
52.   useEffect(() => {
53.     return () => stop();
54.   }, [stop]);
55. 
56.   const active = hz > 0 && noteName !== '\u2014';
57. 
58.   return (
59.     <div className="screen">
60.       <div className="screen-header">
61.         <button className="btn-back" onClick={onBack}>{'\u2190'} Back</button>
62.         <span className="screen-title">Mic Setup</span>
63.       </div>
64. 
65.       <p className="setup-instruction">Sing or play a note to test your microphone.</p>
66. 
67.       <div
68.         className="setup-note-name"
69.         style={{ color: active ? 'var(--primary)' : 'var(--muted)' }}
70.       >
71.         {noteName}
72.       </div>
73. 
74.       <div className="setup-hz">
75.         {hz > 0 ? `${hz.toFixed(1)} Hz` : ''}

13. const NOTE_NAMES_SHARP = ['C','C#','D','D#','E','F','F#','G','G#','A','A#','B'];
14. 
15. function midiToLabel(midi: number): string {
16.   const octave = Math.floor(midi / 12) - 1;
17.   return `${NOTE_NAMES_SHARP[midi % 12]}${octave}`;
18. }
19. 
20. function useStaffPositions(sequence: number[]) {
21.   const [positions, setPositions] = useState<number[]>([]);
22.   useEffect(() => {
23.     if (sequence.length === 0) { setPositions([]); return; }
24.     Promise.all(sequence.map(midi => invoke<number>('cmd_staff_position', { midi }))).then(setPositions);
25.   }, [sequence]);
26.   return positions;
27. }
28. 
29. export default function MusicStaff({ sequence, currentNoteIndex, highlightIndex, detected, status }: Props) {
30.   const staffPositions = useStaffPositions(sequence);

*The MusicStaff component is designed for the Exercise screen - it takes a sequence, currentNoteIndex, highlightIndex, detected array, and status. It's not designed for a single live note display.

For the SetupScreen, I need to either:
1. Create a simpler version of MusicStaff that just shows one note
2. Or repurpose the existing MusicStaff by passing a sequence of [midi] with index 0

Option 2 is simpler. I can pass:
- `sequence={[currentMidi]}` (single note array)
- `currentNoteIndex={0}`


- `highlightIndex={0}`
- `detected={[]}`
- `status="LISTENING"`

But I need the midi value. The SetupScreen currently tracks `noteName` as a string, so I'll add a `currentMidi` state to capture the raw midi value. Then I can update SetupScreen to display the MusicStaff component with the current note while keeping the large note name display below it, and update AGENTS.md to reflect that Mic Setup now shows the staff.

Actually, I'm reconsidering the layout—having both the staff and the large note name creates redundancy. I'll reorganize it to show the MusicStaff at the top, Hz display below that, then the PitchMeter and buttons, removing the large note name since the staff already conveys that information. 1. package com.earring.ui
2. 
3. import androidx.activity.compose.BackHandler
4. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.*
5. import androidx.compose.material3.*
6. import androidx.compose.runtime.*
7. import androidx.compose.ui.Alignment
8. import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
9. import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
10. import androidx.compose.ui.text.font.FontWeight
11. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
12. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.sp
13. import com.earring.AudioCapture
14. import com.earring.EarRingCore
15. import com.earring.MusicTheory
16. import com.earring.ui.components.PitchMeter
17. 
18. @Composable
19. fun SetupScreen(onBack: () -> Unit) {
20.     val context = LocalContext.current
21.     val audioCapture = remember { AudioCapture() }
22. 
23.     var isListening by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
24.     var detectedHz by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(-1f) }
25.     var detectedMidi by remember { mutableIntStateOf(-1) }
26.     // Smoothed midi for display — only update when stable for a few frames
27.     var displayMidi by remember { mutableIntStateOf(-1) }
28.     var stableCount by remember { mutableIntStateOf(0) }
29.     var lastMidi by remember { mutableIntStateOf(-1) }
30. 
31.     fun stopAll() {
32.         audioCapture.stop()
33.         isListening = false
34.         detectedHz = -1f
35.         detectedMidi = -1
36.         displayMidi = -1
37.         stableCount = 0
38.         lastMidi = -1
39.     }
40. 
41.     BackHandler { stopAll(); onBack() }
42. 
43.     DisposableEffect(Unit) { onDispose { audioCapture.stop() } }
44. 
45.     Column(
46.         modifier = Modifier
47.             .fillMaxSize()
48.             .padding(16.dp),
49.         horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally
50.     ) {
51.         Row(
52.             modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
53.             verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
54.         ) {
55.             TextButton(onClick = { stopAll(); onBack() }) { Text("← Back") }
56.             Spacer(Modifier.weight(1f))
57.             Text("Mic Setup", style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleLarge, fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold)
58.             Spacer(Modifier.weight(1f))
59.         }
60. 
61.         Spacer(Modifier.height(24.dp))
62.         Text("Sing or play a note to test your microphone.", style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium)
63.         Spacer(Modifier.height(32.dp))
64. 
65.         // Large note name display
66.         val noteLabel = if (displayMidi >= 0) MusicTheory.midiToLabel(displayMidi) else "—"
67.         val hzLabel = if (detectedHz > 0f) "${detectedHz.toInt()} Hz" else ""
68.         Text(
69.             text = noteLabel,
70.             fontSize = 72.sp,
71.             fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold,
72.             color = if (displayMidi >= 0) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary else MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
73.         )
74.         if (hzLabel.isNotEmpty()) {
75.             Text(hzLabel, style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)
76.         }
77. 
78.         Spacer(Modifier.height(24.dp))
79. 
80.         PitchMeter(detectedMidi = displayMidi, detectedHz = detectedHz)
81. 
82.         Spacer(Modifier.height(32.dp))
83. 
84.         if (!isListening) {
85.             Button(
86.                 onClick = {
87.                     isListening = true
88.                     audioCapture.start(onAudio = { samples ->
89.                         val hz = EarRingCore.detectPitch(samples, 44100)
90.                         if (hz > 0f) {
91.                             detectedHz = hz
92.                             val midi = EarRingCore.freqToMidi(hz)
93.                             detectedMidi = midi
94.                             // Stability: require 3 consecutive frames of same note before displaying
95.                             if (midi == lastMidi) {
96.                                 stableCount++
97.                                 if (stableCount >= 3) displayMidi = midi
98.                             } else {
99.                                 lastMidi = midi
100.                                 stableCount = 1
101.                             }
102.                         } else {
103.                             detectedHz = -1f
104.                             detectedMidi = -1
105.                             stableCount = 0
106.                             lastMidi = -1
107.                             // Keep displayMidi for a moment so it doesn't flicker to "—"
108.                         }
109.                     })
110.                 },
111.                 modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(52.dp)
112.             ) { Text("🎙 Start Listening", fontSize = 17.sp) }
113.         } else {
114.             Button(
115.                 onClick = { stopAll() },
116.                 colors = ButtonDefaults.buttonColors(containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error),
117.                 modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().height(52.dp)
118.             ) { Text("⏹ Stop", fontSize = 17.sp) }
119.         }
120.     }
121. }
122. 
123.

3. struct SetupView: View {

37. 
38.             // ── Pitch meter ───────────────────────────────────────────────
39.             Spacer().frame(height: 24)
40.             HStack {
41.                 Spacer()
42.                 PitchMeterView(midi: model.liveMidi, isActive: model.isCapturing)
43.                     .frame(width: 90, height: 90)
44.                 Spacer()
45.             }
46. 
47.             // ── Start / Stop button ───────────────────────────────────────
48.             Spacer().frame(height: 32)
49.             if model.isCapturing {
50.                 Button("⏹ Stop") {
51.                     model.stopLivePitchDetection()
52.                     resetStabilityState()
53.                 }
54.                 .buttonStyle(ErrorButtonStyle(height: 52, fontSize: 17))
55.             } else {
56.                 Button("🎙 Start Listening") {
57.                     resetStabilityState()
58.                     Task { await model.startLivePitchDetection() }
59.                 }
60.                 .buttonStyle(PrimaryButtonStyle(height: 52, fontSize: 17))
61.             }
62. 
63.             Spacer()
64.         }
65.         .padding(.horizontal, 16)
66.         .background(Color(.systemBackground))
67.         .navigationBarBackButtonHidden(true)
68.         .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(.inline)
69.         .toolbar {
70.             ToolbarItem(placement: .navigationBarLeading) {
71.                 Button("← Back") {
72.                     model.stopLivePitchDetection()
73.                     dismiss()
74.                 }
75.                 .foregroundColor(.erPrimary)
76.             }
77.             ToolbarItem(placement: .navigationBarTrailing) {
78.                 Text("Mic Setup")
79.                     .font(.subheadline.weight(.semibold))
80.             }
81.         }
82.         .onDisappear {
83.             model.stopLivePitchDetection()
84.             model.cancelPlayback()
85.         }
86.         .onChange(of: model.liveFrameCount) { _ in
87.             updateStability()
88.         }
89.     }
90. 
91.     private func updateStability() {
92.         guard let midi = model.liveMidi else {
93.             resetStabilityState()
94.             return
95.         }
96.         let pc = midi % 12
97.         if pc == stabilityPitchClass {
98.             stabilityCount += 1
99.             if stabilityCount >= 3 {
100.                 displayedNote = MusicTheory.midiToLabel(midi)
101.                 let hz = 440.0 * pow(2.0, Double(midi - 69) / 12.0)
102.                 displayedHz = String(format: "%.1f Hz", hz)
103.             }
104.         } else {
105.             stabilityPitchClass = pc
106.             stabilityCount = 1
107.         }
108.     }
109. 
110.     private func resetStabilityState() {
111.         stabilityPitchClass = -1
112.         stabilityCount = 0
113.         displayedNote = "—"
114.         displayedHz = nil
115.     }
116. }
117.
```

<sub>⏱️ 1766m 16s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Now I have the signatures:

**Android MusicStaff:**
```kotlin
@Composable
fun MusicStaff(
    notes: List\<StaffNote\>,
    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
    lineSpacingDp: Dp = 12.dp
)
```
Where `StaffNote(midi: Int, state: NoteState)` and `NoteState` is `EXPECTED, CORRECT, INCORRECT, ACTIVE`.

For SetupScreen, I'd pass:
- When displayMidi \>= 0: `listOf(StaffNote(displayMidi, NoteState.ACTIVE))`


- When no note: `emptyList()`

**iOS MusicStaffView:**
```swift
struct MusicStaffView: View {
    var expectedNotes: [Int]
    var detectedNotes: [DetectedNote]
    var currentNoteIndex: Int = -1
}
```

For SetupView, I need to figure out what `DetectedNote` contains and how to populate these fields—passing the displayed MIDI as `expectedNotes` if a note is detected, leaving `detectedNotes` empty since we're not doing any matching yet, and setting `currentNoteIndex` appropriately.

Now I'm noticing a discrepancy in the iOS staff positioning formula. The current implementation uses `staffCenter - (staffPos - 6) * lineSpacing/2`, which anchors B4 at the center, but the spec indicates C4 should be the reference point at staffCenter. Let me trace through the math to see if this is actually a problem or if I'm misunderstanding the coordinate system. I'm verifying the formula works correctly: B4 at staffPos=6 should land at staffCenter, and C4 at staffPos=0 should be at staffCenter itself, which matches the treble clef layout where B4 is the middle line and C4 sits just below it. I see the issue now—the iOS implementation uses `staffCenter - (staffPos - 6) * lineSpacing/2`, which correctly places B4 at the center and C4 below the staff, whereas the formula I was using would put B4 above the staff. The staffPos offset needs to be relative to 6, not absolute.

Now I need to focus on adding the MusicStaff component to the Android and iOS setup screens. Adding the component to Android...

For iOS, I'm working through how to configure the MusicStaffView with the detected note display. The approach is to pass the currently displayed MIDI note as the expected note, keep detected notes empty for now, and mark the first note as active to show the current state.

I need to extract the MIDI integer from the displayed note label so I can pass it to the staff view properly.

Now I'm planning the implementation: for Android, I'll import and add the MusicStaff component after the instructions, and for iOS, I need to track the MIDI value as state in SetupView, capture it during the stability check, and clear it when resetting.*

<sub>⏱️ 1771m 51s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*The user wants the Mic Setup screen to show notes building up left to right as they're detected, scrolling when the screen is full - like a real-time note history display. This is more like a scrolling piano roll / note history view on the staff.

So instead of showing just one note at a time (current behavior), the SetupScreen should:
1. Accumulate detected notes in a list
2. Display them all on the staff, left to right
3. When the staff fills up, scroll (the newest note stays visible)

This is significantly different from the current approach. I need to:
1. Keep a list/queue of detected midi notes (with some max size)


2. Pass this list to MusicStaff
3. Have the current note index point to the last note (most recently added)
4. When the list exceeds the visible capacity of the staff, the view scrolls or we trim old notes

Now I'm figuring out how many notes can actually fit on the staff - with the treble clef taking up 60dp and the remaining horizontal space available, I need to calculate the spacing between notes to determine the maximum visible capacity before scrolling kicks in. maybe 40dp each, we can fit about 7-8 notes.

For the scrolling approach, I should keep a deque or list of notes with a maximum size, then display only the ones that fit on screen, always positioning the most recent note at the rightmost visible position.

Looking at how MusicStaff works—it takes a sequence and distributes notes evenly—I could keep the full history and pass only the last N notes to it. But simpler would be to just maintain the last N notes and let new ones push old ones off the left as they arrive.

For the implementation, I'm thinking a queue-based approach where I keep up to 8 notes (matching what fits on screen), and when a new note comes in and the queue is full, the oldest note drops off automatically. a new stable note is detected (and it's different from the last, or enough time has passed), add it to the queue
- The queue automatically drops oldest when full
- Pass the queue to MusicStaff as the sequence
- Always highlight the most recent note (last in list) as ACTIVE

For Android, the MusicStaff takes `List\<StaffNote\>` - I can pass the accumulated list.
For Tauri, the MusicStaff takes `sequence: number[], currentNoteIndex, highlightIndex, detected` - I'd pass the accumulated list.
For iOS, similar.

Let me implement this. I need to:
1. Track a list of detected notes (not just the last one)
2. Add to the list when a stable new note is detected
3. Keep only the last 8 notes visible on screen (scrolling/trimming)
4. Pass the full list to MusicStaff

For the logic, I should add a note when it stabilizes after 3 frames AND differs from the last note I added to the list. Looking at the existing Android code, every 3 stable frames triggers a display update, so I'll hook into that same stability check but only append when the pitch class actually changes. I'll keep the most recent note highlighted as active while maintaining the scrolling history. Now I'm implementing the note history tracking across all three platforms. For Android, I'm adding a mutable list that stores the last 8 MIDI notes, comparing full MIDI values to detect new notes, and passing them to MusicStaff with the last note marked as ACTIVE. For Tauri, I'm setting up a ref to track the note history array...

Actually, let me reconsider the spacing—I need to verify that 8 notes will fit reasonably on the staff given the screen widths and treble clef taking up space on the left.

I'm settling on 8 as the maximum visible notes, and when the history grows beyond that, I'll just keep the most recent 8 to simulate scrolling. For the stability detection, I need to track when a note becomes stable for 3 frames and differs from the previously added note, so I don't end up adding the same note multiple times if the user holds it. I'll separate the logic: one variable tracks the current stable MIDI value, and another tracks the last note I actually added to history, so I only push to history when the stable note changes.

Writing the Android code... Writing component setup...

I'm calculating the frequency in Hz from the MIDI note value, then updating the MusicStaffView to display the note history with the current note highlighted. I also need to clear the noteHistory array when listening stops, and update the documentation to explain how the scrolling history works—showing previous notes as hollow dark notes and the current note filled in blue. This should display previous notes as hollow and the current note filled in blue. Now I'm ready to update all three files and the documentation.*

37.   const staffBottomY = staffTop + 4 * lineSpacing;

44.

46.   const noteAreaWidth = svgWidth - noteAreaStart - 20;
47.   const noteCount = Math.max(sequence.length, 1);
48.   const noteStep = noteAreaWidth / noteCount;
49.   const calcNoteX = (i: number) => noteAreaStart + i * noteStep + noteStep / 2;
50. 
51.   const getNoteColor = (i: number): string => {
52.     if (i < detected.length) return detected[i].correct ? '#4CAF50' : '#F44336';

55.     return '#333333';

Also, `noteStep = noteAreaWidth / Math.max(sequence.length, 1)` - so notes spread evenly. With 8 notes, they'll be evenly distributed. Good.

- Pass `status='listening'` (lowercase!) for the current note to be blue
- `currentNoteIndex = noteHistory.length - 1`

For iOS:- `currentNoteIndex = noteHistory.count - 1`
- detectedNotes = [] (no correction coloring)

<sub>⏱️ 1781m 53s</sub>

### 👤 User

i want the first detected note to appear at the left hand end of the staff. The fill the staff left to right until the staff is full, then scroll it. Also do no display anynotes until you have defintievely detecetd a note that passes the detction threshold. And never display a note outside the conficgured octave range

---

<sub>⏱️ 1782m 6s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*The user wants:
1. First note appears at left end of staff (not centered/distributed)
2. Fill left to right until staff is full, then scroll
3. Don't display notes until definitely detected (passes threshold) - the 3-frame stability is already there, but maybe they want it stronger?
4. Never display a note outside the configured octave range

The key issue with the current MusicStaff implementation is that it distributes notes evenly across the full staff width (noteStep = noteAreaWidth / noteCount). So with 1 note, it centers it. With 2 notes, it puts them at 1/4 and 3/4 positions. 

I need to redesign this for fixed positioning instead. Rather than calculating spacing based on how many notes exist, I should use a fixed interval between notes and place them sequentially from the left. Once the staff fills up, I'll scroll by removing the oldest note and keeping the newest ones visible on the right. For something like a 360dp Android screen with a 60dp left margin, I can determine how many notes fit by dividing the available width by a fixed spacing value like 40px per note.

The challenge is that MusicStaff is currently shared between two different use cases—Exercise screens need even distribution across a fixed number of notes, while Setup screens need this fixed-step scrolling behavior. I could add a prop to toggle between these modes, create a separate component, or handle the calculation differently depending on the context.

For the octave filtering, the SetupScreen doesn't currently have access to the octave configuration from HomeScreen, so I need to either pass it down or find another way to determine which notes should be displayed based on the configured range. I need to filter detected MIDI notes to stay within the configured octave range—so if octave is set to 4, notes outside that range (like a stray C3) won't display on the staff. This prevents accidental low rumbles from appearing. The range would span from the root note in the specified octave up through the scale, probably covering a couple of octaves total. I'm working through the MIDI octave numbering to figure out the note range—octave N maps to MIDI notes from (N+1)*12 to (N+2)*12-1, so octave 4 is MIDI 60-71 and octave 5 is 72-83. For the exercise, I'll allow notes spanning two octaves starting from the configured octave, so if it's set to octave 4, I'd include notes from C4 up through B5.

Now I need to implement the changes: add a `fixedSpacing` prop to MusicStaff that places notes at fixed intervals instead of distributing them evenly, calculating how many notes fit in the available width and displaying only the most recent ones. The SetupScreen will pass this prop along with the octave setting and filter detected notes to stay within the allowed MIDI range. I'm thinking through the simplest way to handle this—instead of padding the history with empty slots, I can just add a `fixedSpacing` prop to MusicStaff that uses a constant note step value rather than distributing notes evenly across the width. This way, notes always appear at the same horizontal intervals regardless of how many are in the sequence.

Writing component logic...

For the history capacity, I'd calculate the maximum number of notes that fit based on the fixed spacing. Then for octave filtering, I need to pass the octave value from the exercise state down through each platform—it's already available in the ExerciseViewModel on Android, ExerciseState in Tauri, and ExerciseModel on iOS, so I just need to thread it through to SetupScreen.

My plan is to add a fixed spacing parameter to MusicStaff on each platform, then update SetupScreen to accept the octave, filter the MIDI notes to the appropriate range, and pass the fixed spacing value when rendering.*

1. package com.earring.ui.components
2. 
3. import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxWidth
5. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.height
6. import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
7. import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
8. import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
9. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
10. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.drawscope.DrawScope
11. import androidx.compose.ui.text.TextStyle
12. import androidx.compose.ui.text.drawText
13. import androidx.compose.ui.text.rememberTextMeasurer
14. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.Dp
15. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
16. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.sp
17. import com.earring.DetectedNote
18. import com.earring.EarRingCore
19. 
20. enum class NoteState { EXPECTED, CORRECT, INCORRECT, ACTIVE }
21. 
22. data class StaffNote(
23.     val midi: Int,
24.     val state: NoteState
25. )
26. 
27. @Composable
28. fun MusicStaff(
29.     notes: List<StaffNote>,
30.     modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
31.     lineSpacingDp: Dp = 12.dp
32. ) {
33.     val textMeasurer = rememberTextMeasurer()
34. 
35.     Canvas(
36.         modifier = modifier
37.             .fillMaxWidth()
38.             .height(160.dp)
39.     ) {
40.         val lineSpacing = lineSpacingDp.toPx()
41.         val staffTop = size.height / 2f - 2 * lineSpacing
42.         val leftMargin = 60f
43.         val noteRadius = lineSpacing * 0.45f
44. 
45.         // Draw 5 staff lines
46.         for (i in 0..4) {
47.             val y = staffTop + i * lineSpacing
48.             drawLine(
49.                 color = Color(0xFF333333),
50.                 start = Offset(leftMargin, y),
51.                 end = Offset(size.width - 16f, y),
52.                 strokeWidth = 1.5f
53.             )
54.         }
55. 
56.         // Draw treble clef symbol
57.         drawText(
58.             textMeasurer = textMeasurer,
59.             text = "\uD834\uDD1E",
60.             topLeft = Offset(4f, staffTop - lineSpacing * 1.5f),
61.             style = TextStyle(fontSize = 56.sp, color = Color(0xFF333333))
62.         )
63. 
64.         // Distribute notes horizontally
65.         val noteAreaStart = leftMargin + 20f
66.         val noteAreaWidth = size.width - noteAreaStart - 20f
67.         val noteCount = notes.size.coerceAtLeast(1)
68.         val noteStep = noteAreaWidth / noteCount.toFloat()
69. 
70.         notes.forEachIndexed { index, staffNote ->
71.             val staffPos = EarRingCore.staffPosition(staffNote.midi)
72.             // staffPos: C4=0, D4=1, ... each step = half line spacing
73.             val staffCenter = staffTop + 2 * lineSpacing // line 3 = B4 area
74.             val noteY = staffCenter - staffPos * (lineSpacing / 2f)
75.             val noteX = noteAreaStart + index * noteStep + noteStep / 2f
76. 
77.             val noteColor = when (staffNote.state) {
78.                 NoteState.EXPECTED -> Color(0xFF333333)
79.                 NoteState.CORRECT -> Color(0xFF4CAF50)
80.                 NoteState.INCORRECT -> Color(0xFFF44336)
81.                 NoteState.ACTIVE -> Color(0xFF3F51B5)
82.             }
83. 
84.             // Draw ledger lines if needed (outside staff lines 0-4)
85.             drawLedgerLines(noteX, noteY, staffTop, lineSpacing, noteRadius)
86. 
87.             // Draw note head
88.             drawCircle(
89.                 color = noteColor,
90.                 radius = noteRadius,
91.                 center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
92.             )
93.             // Hollow for expected notes
94.             if (staffNote.state == NoteState.EXPECTED) {
95.                 drawCircle(
96.                     color = Color.White,
97.                     radius = noteRadius - 2.5f,
98.                     center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
99.                 )
100.             }
101.         }
102.     }
103. }
104. 
105. private fun DrawScope.drawLedgerLines(
106.     noteX: Float,
107.     noteY: Float,
108.     staffTop: Float,
109.     lineSpacing: Float,
110.     noteRadius: Float
111. ) {
112.     val staffBottom = staffTop + 4 * lineSpacing
113.     val ledgerWidth = noteRadius * 2.8f
114. 
115.     // Above staff
116.     var y = staffTop - lineSpacing
117.     while (noteY <= y + 1f) {
118.         drawLine(
119.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
120.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
121.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
122.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
123.         )
124.         y -= lineSpacing
125.     }
126. 
127.     // Below staff
128.     y = staffBottom + lineSpacing
129.     while (noteY >= y - 1f) {
130.         drawLine(
131.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
132.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
133.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
134.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
135.         )
136.         y += lineSpacing
137.     }
138. }
139.
```

</details>

1. import React, { useState, useCallback } from 'react';
2. import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/tauri';
3. import { Screen, ExerciseState, SessionRecord } from './types';
4. import HomeScreen from './components/HomeScreen';
5. import ExerciseScreen from './components/ExerciseScreen';
6. import SetupScreen from './components/SetupScreen';
7. import ResultsScreen from './components/ResultsScreen';
8. import ProgressScreen from './components/ProgressScreen';
9. 
10. const SCALE_NAMES = ['Major', 'Natural Minor', 'Harmonic Minor', 'Pentatonic Major', 'Pentatonic Minor', 'Dorian', 'Mixolydian', 'Blues'];
11. const NOTE_NAMES = ['C', 'C#', 'D', 'D#', 'E', 'F', 'F#', 'G', 'G#', 'A', 'A#', 'B'];
12. 
13. const defaultExercise: ExerciseState = {
14.   rootNote: 0,
15.   octave: 4,
16.   scaleId: 0,
17.   sequenceLength: 4,
18.   sequence: [],
19.   detected: [],
20.   status: 'idle',
21.   currentNoteIndex: 0,
22.   highlightIndex: -1,
23. };
24. 
25. export default function App() {
26.   const [screen, setScreen] = useState<Screen>('home');
27.   const [exercise, setExercise] = useState<ExerciseState>(defaultExercise);
28. 
29.   const startExercise = useCallback(async (rootNote: number, octave: number, scaleId: number, sequenceLength: number) => {
30.     // MIDI formula: C4=60, so midi = (octave+1)*12 + rootNote
31.     const midi = (octave + 1) * 12 + rootNote;
32.     const seed = BigInt(Date.now());
33.     try {
34.       const sequence = await invoke<number[]>('cmd_generate_sequence', {
35.         rootMidi: midi,
36.         scaleId,
37.         length: sequenceLength,
38.         seed,
39.       });
40.       setExercise({
41.         rootNote,
42.         octave,
43.         scaleId,
44.         sequenceLength,
45.         sequence,
46.         detected: [],
47.         status: 'idle',
48.         currentNoteIndex: 0,
49.         highlightIndex: -1,
50.       });
51.       setScreen('exercise');
52.     } catch (e) {
53.       console.error('generate_sequence failed', e);
54.     }
55.   }, []);
56. 
57.   const finishExercise = useCallback((detected: ExerciseState['detected']) => {
58.     setExercise(prev => ({ ...prev, detected, status: 'done' }));
59.     const correct = detected.filter(d => d.correct).length;
60.     const score = detected.length > 0 ? Math.round((correct / detected.length) * 100) : 0;
61.     const record: SessionRecord = {
62.       date: new Date().toISOString(),
63.       scale: SCALE_NAMES[exercise.scaleId] || 'Major',
64.       root: NOTE_NAMES[exercise.rootNote] || 'C',
65.       score,
66.       length: exercise.sequenceLength,
67.     };
68.     const existing: SessionRecord[] = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('ear_ring_sessions') || '[]');
69.     existing.unshift(record);
70.     localStorage.setItem('ear_ring_sessions', JSON.stringify(existing.slice(0, 100)));
71.     setScreen('results');
72.   }, [exercise.scaleId, exercise.rootNote, exercise.sequenceLength]);
73. 
74.   const updateExercise = useCallback((updates: Partial<ExerciseState>) => {
75.     setExercise(prev => ({ ...prev, ...updates }));
76.   }, []);
77. 
78.   return (
79.     <div className="app-container">
80.       {screen === 'home' && (
81.         <HomeScreen
82.           exercise={exercise}
83.           onUpdateExercise={updateExercise}
84.           onStart={startExercise}
85.           onSetup={() => setScreen('setup')}
86.           onProgress={() => setScreen('progress')}
87.         />
88.       )}
89.       {screen === 'exercise' && (
90.         <ExerciseScreen
91.           exercise={exercise}
92.           onUpdateExercise={updateExercise}
93.           onFinish={finishExercise}
94.           onBack={() => setScreen('home')}
95.         />
96.       )}
97.       {screen === 'setup' && (
98.         <SetupScreen onBack={() => setScreen('home')} />
99.       )}
100.       {screen === 'results' && (
101.         <ResultsScreen
102.           exercise={exercise}
103.           onTryAgain={() => setScreen('exercise')}
104.           onNewExercise={() => setScreen('home')}
105.           onProgress={() => setScreen('progress')}
106.         />
107.       )}
108.       {screen === 'progress' && (
109.         <ProgressScreen onBack={() => setScreen('home')} />
110.       )}
111.     </div>
112.   );
113. }
114.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 1783m 6s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Now I have everything I need. Let me plan the changes:

**1. MusicStaff - add `fixedSpacing` prop:**
- Android: `fixedSpacingDp: Dp? = null` - when set, use fixed spacing; cap display to fit in staff
- Tauri: `fixedSpacing?: number` - same
- iOS: `fixedSpacing: CGFloat? = nil` - same

When `fixedSpacing` is set:
- `noteStep = fixedSpacing` (not distributed)
- `maxVisible = floor(noteAreaWidth / fixedSpacing)` 


- Show last `maxVisible` notes from the sequence

**2. SetupScreen - pass octave, filter MIDI, use fixedSpacing:**
- Android: `SetupScreen(onBack, octave: Int)` - filter midi to range [octaveMin, octaveMax]
- Tauri: `SetupScreen({ onBack, octave })` - same
- iOS: read `model.selectedOctave` or similar - same

**3. Navigation - pass octave:**
- Android: MainActivity → SetupScreen needs octave from ViewModel
- Tauri: App.tsx → SetupScreen needs octave from exercise state
- iOS: Set up SetupScreen to read from @EnvironmentObject model

For the octave range filtering, I need to think about what "configured octave range" actually means. The user wants notes only within that range, but a single octave is pretty restrictive for singing since voices naturally drift up and down. I'm leaning toward allowing notes within one octave above and below the configured octave—so if octave is 4, that's C3 to B5. Though it's possible they just mean the exact octave specified, which would be C4 to B4. I'll assume the broader interpretation makes more sense for a singing exercise.

Actually, thinking about how scales work—if the exercise is set to octave 4, the sequence will likely span from octave 4 into 5 since scales ascend from the root. So I should allow notes in the selected octave and the one above it. That means valid MIDI notes would be from (selectedOctave + 1) * 12 through (selectedOctave + 3) * 12 - 1, giving me a 2-octave range. For octave 4, that's C4 (60) to B5 (83).

For Android navigation, I'm checking how SetupScreen gets triggered—it looks like it's called through an `onMicSetup` callback in the Home screen. I can see the octave value flows through the state in each platform, so I need to thread it through to SetupScreen and MusicStaff. Rather than getting bogged down in the exact state structure, I'll just make the changes directly—passing octave as a parameter in Android, Tauri, and iOS, then updating MusicStaff to use it for fixed spacing.*

*The user is clarifying that the Mic Setup screen staff display should be identical to the Exercise screen - showing notes as they are detected. In the Exercise screen, there's a sequence of notes expected and the staff shows where each note is. As notes are detected during the exercise, they appear one by one.

So for Mic Setup, it should work the same way: as you sing notes, they appear on the staff from left to right. This confirms the approach we're already implementing.

But wait - the user is also saying "display notes as they are detected" - which might mean don't wait for the 3-frame stability before displaying? Actually re-reading... I think they're saying:
- The behavior should be the same as the exercise screen


- Notes appear as detected after passing the threshold, without waiting for stability confirmation
- The fixed spacing approach we're using is what makes it identical to the exercise screen behavior

The agent is already handling this correctly, so I'll note this clarification for when we review the implementation and update the documentation. I'm realizing the key distinction here—in the exercise screen, notes are pre-defined and displayed all at once with the current position highlighted, whereas in Mic Setup, notes appear dynamically as they're detected. The user wants the visual styling to match (staff lines, treble clef, note colors), but the layout algorithm can differ since one has a fixed sequence and the other builds left to right. I'll let agent-5 finish and review what it produces.*

1. package com.earring.ui.components
2. 
3. import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxWidth
5. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.height
6. import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
7. import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
8. import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
9. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
10. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.drawscope.DrawScope
11. import androidx.compose.ui.text.TextStyle
12. import androidx.compose.ui.text.drawText
13. import androidx.compose.ui.text.rememberTextMeasurer
14. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.Dp
15. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
16. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.sp
17. import com.earring.DetectedNote
18. import com.earring.EarRingCore
19. 
20. enum class NoteState { EXPECTED, CORRECT, INCORRECT, ACTIVE }
21. 
22. data class StaffNote(
23.     val midi: Int,
24.     val state: NoteState
25. )
26. 
27. @Composable
28. fun MusicStaff(
29.     notes: List<StaffNote>,
30.     modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
31.     lineSpacingDp: Dp = 12.dp,
32.     fixedSpacingDp: Dp? = null
33. ) {
34.     val textMeasurer = rememberTextMeasurer()
35. 
36.     Canvas(
37.         modifier = modifier
38.             .fillMaxWidth()
39.             .height(160.dp)
40.     ) {
41.         val lineSpacing = lineSpacingDp.toPx()
42.         val staffTop = size.height / 2f - 2 * lineSpacing
43.         val leftMargin = 60f
44.         val noteRadius = lineSpacing * 0.45f
45. 
46.         // Draw 5 staff lines
47.         for (i in 0..4) {
48.             val y = staffTop + i * lineSpacing
49.             drawLine(
50.                 color = Color(0xFF333333),
51.                 start = Offset(leftMargin, y),
52.                 end = Offset(size.width - 16f, y),
53.                 strokeWidth = 1.5f
54.             )
55.         }
56. 
57.         // Draw treble clef symbol
58.         drawText(
59.             textMeasurer = textMeasurer,
60.             text = "\uD834\uDD1E",
61.             topLeft = Offset(4f, staffTop - lineSpacing * 1.5f),
62.             style = TextStyle(fontSize = 56.sp, color = Color(0xFF333333))
63.         )
64. 
65.         // Distribute notes horizontally
66.         val noteAreaStart = leftMargin + 20f
67.         val noteAreaWidth = size.width - noteAreaStart - 20f
68.         val noteCount = notes.size.coerceAtLeast(1)
69.         val noteStep = if (fixedSpacingDp != null) fixedSpacingDp.toPx() else noteAreaWidth / noteCount.toFloat()
70. 
71.         notes.forEachIndexed { index, staffNote ->
72.             val staffPos = EarRingCore.staffPosition(staffNote.midi)
73.             // staffPos: C4=0, D4=1, ... each step = half line spacing
74.             val staffCenter = staffTop + 2 * lineSpacing // line 3 = B4 area
75.             val noteY = staffCenter - staffPos * (lineSpacing / 2f)
76.             val noteX = noteAreaStart + index * noteStep + noteStep / 2f
77. 
78.             val noteColor = when (staffNote.state) {
79.                 NoteState.EXPECTED -> Color(0xFF333333)
80.                 NoteState.CORRECT -> Color(0xFF4CAF50)
81.                 NoteState.INCORRECT -> Color(0xFFF44336)
82.                 NoteState.ACTIVE -> Color(0xFF3F51B5)
83.             }
84. 
85.             // Draw ledger lines if needed (outside staff lines 0-4)
86.             drawLedgerLines(noteX, noteY, staffTop, lineSpacing, noteRadius)
87. 
88.             // Draw note head
89.             drawCircle(
90.                 color = noteColor,
91.                 radius = noteRadius,
92.                 center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
93.             )
94.             // Hollow for expected notes
95.             if (staffNote.state == NoteState.EXPECTED) {
96.                 drawCircle(
97.                     color = Color.White,
98.                     radius = noteRadius - 2.5f,
99.                     center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
100.                 )
101.             }
102.         }
103.     }
104. }
105. 
106. private fun DrawScope.drawLedgerLines(
107.     noteX: Float,
108.     noteY: Float,
109.     staffTop: Float,
110.     lineSpacing: Float,
111.     noteRadius: Float
112. ) {
113.     val staffBottom = staffTop + 4 * lineSpacing
114.     val ledgerWidth = noteRadius * 2.8f
115. 
116.     // Above staff
117.     var y = staffTop - lineSpacing
118.     while (noteY <= y + 1f) {
119.         drawLine(
120.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
121.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
122.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
123.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
124.         )
125.         y -= lineSpacing
126.     }
127. 
128.     // Below staff
129.     y = staffBottom + lineSpacing
130.     while (noteY >= y - 1f) {
131.         drawLine(
132.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
133.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
134.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
135.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
136.         )
137.         y += lineSpacing
138.     }
139. }
140.
```

<sub>⏱️ 1794m 53s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The root cause: Compose's `drawText`/`TextMeasurer` doesn't do font fallback for Unicode Supplementary Plane characters (U+1D11E, the treble clef 𝄞). Switching to `drawIntoCanvas { canvas.nativeCanvas.drawText(...) }` with `android.graphics.Paint` uses Android's native text system, which automatically falls back to **Noto Sans Symbols** (included since Android 7.0) to render the character.

C:\work\ear_ring\android\app\src\main\java\com\earring\ui\components\MusicStaff.kt

<details>
<summary>143 lines</summary>

```
1. package com.earring.ui.components
2. 
3. import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxWidth
5. import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.height
6. import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
7. import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
8. import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
9. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
10. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.drawscope.DrawScope
11. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.drawscope.drawIntoCanvas
12. import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.nativeCanvas
13. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.Dp
14. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
15. import androidx.compose.ui.unit.sp
16. import com.earring.DetectedNote
17. import com.earring.EarRingCore
18. 
19. enum class NoteState { EXPECTED, CORRECT, INCORRECT, ACTIVE }
20. 
21. data class StaffNote(
22.     val midi: Int,
23.     val state: NoteState
24. )
25. 
26. @Composable
27. fun MusicStaff(
28.     notes: List<StaffNote>,
29.     modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
30.     lineSpacingDp: Dp = 12.dp,
31.     fixedSpacingDp: Dp? = null
32. ) {
33.     Canvas(
34.         modifier = modifier
35.             .fillMaxWidth()
36.             .height(160.dp)
37.     ) {
38.         val lineSpacing = lineSpacingDp.toPx()
39.         val staffTop = size.height / 2f - 2 * lineSpacing
40.         val leftMargin = 60f
41.         val noteRadius = lineSpacing * 0.45f
42. 
43.         // Draw 5 staff lines
44.         for (i in 0..4) {
45.             val y = staffTop + i * lineSpacing
46.             drawLine(
47.                 color = Color(0xFF333333),
48.                 start = Offset(leftMargin, y),
49.                 end = Offset(size.width - 16f, y),
50.                 strokeWidth = 1.5f
51.             )
52.         }
53. 
54.         // Draw treble clef symbol using native canvas for proper font fallback
55.         drawIntoCanvas { canvas ->
56.             val paint = android.graphics.Paint(android.graphics.Paint.ANTI_ALIAS_FLAG).apply {
57.                 textSize = lineSpacing * 4.8f
58.                 color = android.graphics.Color.parseColor("#333333")
59.             }
60.             canvas.nativeCanvas.drawText(
61.                 "\uD834\uDD1E",
62.                 4f,
63.                 staffTop + lineSpacing * 3.8f,
64.                 paint
65.             )
66.         }
67. 
68.         // Distribute notes horizontally
69.         val noteAreaStart = leftMargin + 20f
70.         val noteAreaWidth = size.width - noteAreaStart - 20f
71.         val noteCount = notes.size.coerceAtLeast(1)
72.         val noteStep = if (fixedSpacingDp != null) fixedSpacingDp.toPx() else noteAreaWidth / noteCount.toFloat()
73. 
74.         notes.forEachIndexed { index, staffNote ->
75.             val staffPos = EarRingCore.staffPosition(staffNote.midi)
76.             // staffPos: C4=0, D4=1, ... each step = half line spacing
77.             val staffCenter = staffTop + 2 * lineSpacing // line 3 = B4 area
78.             val noteY = staffCenter - staffPos * (lineSpacing / 2f)
79.             val noteX = noteAreaStart + index * noteStep + noteStep / 2f
80. 
81.             val noteColor = when (staffNote.state) {
82.                 NoteState.EXPECTED -> Color(0xFF333333)
83.                 NoteState.CORRECT -> Color(0xFF4CAF50)
84.                 NoteState.INCORRECT -> Color(0xFFF44336)
85.                 NoteState.ACTIVE -> Color(0xFF3F51B5)
86.             }
87. 
88.             // Draw ledger lines if needed (outside staff lines 0-4)
89.             drawLedgerLines(noteX, noteY, staffTop, lineSpacing, noteRadius)
90. 
91.             // Draw note head
92.             drawCircle(
93.                 color = noteColor,
94.                 radius = noteRadius,
95.                 center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
96.             )
97.             // Hollow for expected notes
98.             if (staffNote.state == NoteState.EXPECTED) {
99.                 drawCircle(
100.                     color = Color.White,
101.                     radius = noteRadius - 2.5f,
102.                     center = Offset(noteX, noteY)
103.                 )
104.             }
105.         }
106.     }
107. }
108. 
109. private fun DrawScope.drawLedgerLines(
110.     noteX: Float,
111.     noteY: Float,
112.     staffTop: Float,
113.     lineSpacing: Float,
114.     noteRadius: Float
115. ) {
116.     val staffBottom = staffTop + 4 * lineSpacing
117.     val ledgerWidth = noteRadius * 2.8f
118. 
119.     // Above staff
120.     var y = staffTop - lineSpacing
121.     while (noteY <= y + 1f) {
122.         drawLine(
123.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
124.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
125.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
126.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
127.         )
128.         y -= lineSpacing
129.     }
130. 
131.     // Below staff
132.     y = staffBottom + lineSpacing
133.     while (noteY >= y - 1f) {
134.         drawLine(
135.             color = Color(0xFF555555),
136.             start = Offset(noteX - ledgerWidth, y),
137.             end = Offset(noteX + ledgerWidth, y),
138.             strokeWidth = 1.5f
139.         )
140.         y += lineSpacing
141.     }
142. }
143.
```

</details>
